overview: Al-Ameen College Malaga offers a tuition fee discount for families who hold valid health care cards, concession cards, or pensioner cards. This financial assistance program recognises families requiring fee support and is available to all enrolled students.
description: Al-Ameen College Malaga provides a 10% discount on tuition fees for parents who furnish their health care card, concession card, or pensioner card to the Accounts Office. This is a means-tested financial assistance program designed to support families experiencing financial hardship. The discount applies to tuition fees only and must be claimed by providing valid documentation to the school's Accounts Office. The discount is ongoing for as long as the family maintains eligible status and continues to provide valid documentation annually.
eligibility_criteria: Parents must hold current health care card, concession card, or pensioner card; Documentation must be furnished to the Accounts Office; Applies to all year levels enrolled at Al-Ameen College Malaga
special_conditions: Valid health care card, concession card, or pensioner card must be provided to the Accounts Office. Discount applies to tuition fees. Families unable to meet fee payment requirements should contact the Accounts Department prior to the due date to discuss payment options.

overview: Albany Senior High School offers a Gifted and Talented Academic Program for exceptionally able students. The program provides an enriched curriculum designed to meet the needs of gifted and talented students, fostering analytical, critical and creative thinking skills while supporting motivation and communication development.
description: The Gifted and Talented Academic Program at Albany Senior High School is available to students entering Year 7 and continuing through Years 8, 9, and 10. Students in the program benefit from a well-rounded curriculum designed to nurture analytical, critical and creative thinking skills, foster motivation and encourage communication. They enjoy a culture of teamwork and support as an integral part of the Albany SHS student community and have opportunities to participate in rich and varied extra-curricular activities. In these subjects, teachers offer a specially designed curriculum to meet the needs of the Gifted and Talented cohort. The selection process is based on achieving the required score in the competitive Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET) administered by the Department of Education. Residential support is available at Albany Residential College for eligible regional students.
eligibility_criteria: Must achieve required score in Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET) administered by Department of Education; Application process begins in Year 5 with testing in Year 6; Placement starts from Year 7; Students are selected based on demonstrated academic ability and test performance
special_conditions: Students selected into the program also have the opportunity to apply to live in residence at the Albany Residential College to enable them to access the program. Enrollment requires meeting selective entry criteria. The Gifted and Talented Boarding Away from Home Allowance of up to $1,424 was available to eligible country students in 2025 for boarding support.

program_name: Tuition Fee Discount - Concession Card Holders
overview: Al-Ameen College offers a 10% discount on tuition fees for families who hold a health care card, concession card, or pensioner card. This financial assistance program helps eligible families reduce their education costs.
description: Al-Ameen College provides a 10% discount on tuition fees for eligible families. Parents must furnish their health care card, concession card, or pensioner card to the Accounts Office to receive this discount. The discount applies to tuition fees only and is available to families meeting the financial assistance criteria. Additionally, a 5% early payment discount is available if full year fees are paid by 28 February. For the fourth child onward in primary school (Pre-primary to Year 6), families will not be charged tuition fees. This program is designed to support families with demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian must hold a current health care card, concession card, or pensioner card; Must provide documentation to the Accounts Office
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only. Early payment discount of 5% available if full year fees paid by 28 February. Fourth child onward in primary school (Pre-primary to Year 6) receives full tuition fee waiver.

school_name: Alta-1 College - Albany Marine VET Campus

overview: All Saints' College offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in incoming students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability as evidenced in the scholarship examination and interview.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic excellence, as evidenced in the student's performance in the scholarship examination and interview. The value of the scholarship is 50% of the College's academic tuition fee. The scholarship is for the duration of enrolment, conditional on maintaining sound academic performance and commitment to academic excellence. The selection process includes a written assessment conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS), typically running over three hours with a 15-minute morning tea break, followed by an interview with the College Principal and/or Director of Academics. Supporting documentation includes copies of the applicant's most recent academic report and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027; Must be currently enrolled in Year 4, Year 6 or Year 9; Open to both current All Saints' College students and students not yet enrolled
special_conditions: Recipients are expected to maintain sound academic performance and remain committed to academic excellence, and contribute positively to College life. Scholarships are conditional on continued commitment and satisfactory progress. If performance falls short, the College reserves the right to review or withdraw the scholarship. Attendance at the scheduled exam is a requirement; alternative dates are not scheduled unless extenuating circumstances apply.

overview: All Saints' College is well known for its exceptional programs in Dance and Drama, and offers dedicated and talented students the opportunity to join the program which is housed in the College's Centre for Performing Arts. Scholarships are awarded following an audition process based on demonstrated talent and commitment to the performing arts.
description: The Drama and Dance Scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2026. The value of the scholarship is generally 50% of the College's academic tuition fee. The scholarship is for the duration of the student's attendance at the College and is conditional upon ongoing excellence in, and commitment to, the All Saints' College Performing Arts program. The selection process involves an audition in their chosen discipline (Drama or Dance), with shortlisted applicants invited to an interview with the College Principal and/or Director of Arts. Applicants must provide a covering letter of up to 300 words addressing their experience, passion and involvement in their chosen Performing Arts area, along with copies of their most recent academic report and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Must demonstrate talent and commitment to drama or dance; Open to both current All Saints' College students and students not yet enrolled; Active membership of cocurricular Performing Arts program is mandatory
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Performing Arts Scholarship recipients are required to be active members of the cocurricular Performing Arts program even if not directly involved as a performer. An annual review may be conducted to discuss the student's attitude, activity and contribution to their scholarship. Recipients must remain committed to the program and contribute positively to College life. The College reserves the right to review or withdraw the scholarship if performance or engagement falls short.

overview: All Saints' College offers the Music Scholarship to recognise outstanding musicians who have attained a high level of performance. The scholarship supports talented musicians entering Years 7 to 12 in the College's comprehensive music program.
description: The Music Scholarship is for students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2026. It recognises an outstanding musician who has attained a high level of performance. The value of the scholarship is generally 50% of the College's academic tuition fee and 100% of the 30-minute music lesson tuition fees. The scholarship is for the duration of the student's attendance at the College and is conditional upon ongoing excellence in, and commitment to, the All Saints' Music Program. An audition with the Director of Music for short-listed candidates is an integral part of the selection process. Applicants must provide copies of awards for musical performance (e.g. Eisteddfods) and a reference letter from their current instrumental teacher detailing practice ethic and progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2026; For Piano: Performance level of at least Grade 8 AMEB standard expected; For Violin, Cello, Viola, Double Bass: Performance level of at least Grade 4 AMEB standard expected; For wind/brass instruments: Evidence of outstanding progress required; For singers: Evidence of outstanding performances in productions or eisteddfods; Open to both current and new students; Active membership in College Choir and at least one other ensemble required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction plus 100% Music Lesson Fees
special_conditions: Music scholarship recipients must be active members of the College Choir and at least one other College ensemble. They should choose Music elective classes at least until the end of Year 9 or Year 10 and be prepared to participate in Music recitals and Music camps. An annual review may be conducted to discuss the student's attitude, activity and contribution to their scholarship. Recipients must maintain commitment to the music program and meet performance expectations or face withdrawal of the scholarship.

overview: All Saints' College offers the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Scholarship to foster future-enabled, creative-thinking entrepreneurs who see problems as opportunities. The scholarship supports students with demonstrated passion for innovation and a strong social conscience.
description: The Innovation and Entrepreneurship Scholarship is designed for students entering Years 5 to 11 who have a demonstrated interest in and passion for innovation, new and creative ways of thinking and doing things, and a strong social conscience. The value of the scholarship is 50% of the College's academic tuition fee. This scholarship is a unique opportunity for students with a demonstrated flair for, and experience in, innovation and entrepreneurship to join the community and be mentored by leading entrepreneurs. The selection process includes review of supporting documentation including two written references that speak to the student's passion for, and skills in, innovation and entrepreneurship. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an interview.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new external students not currently enrolled at All Saints' College; Must be entering Years 5 to 11 in 2027; Must demonstrate demonstrated interest in and passion for innovation and entrepreneurship; Must have demonstrated social conscience; Open to external students only (not current All Saints' students)
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Innovation and Entrepreneurship Scholarship is only applicable for external students who are not currently enrolled at All Saints' College. Recipients are expected to maintain sound academic performance and remain committed to the Innovation and Entrepreneurship program, and contribute positively to College life. Scholarships are conditional on continued commitment and satisfactory progress. The College reserves the right to review or withdraw the scholarship if performance or engagement falls short.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Scholarship
overview: All Saints' College offers the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Scholarship to recognise a student with a demonstrated commitment to their community and who is of Indigenous descent. The scholarship supports students selected on the basis of academic potential, personal commitment and family support.
description: The Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Scholarship exists to recognise a student with a demonstrated commitment to their community and who is of Indigenous descent. Students will be selected on the basis of academic potential, personal commitment and family support for an All Saints' College education. This scholarship is for students entering Year 5 or above in 2026 and can cover up to 100% of tuition fees (dependent on financial statements) and may include other expenses including year level and other charges, textbooks, stationery, uniforms, ICT charges, camp costs and other incidentals. No academic examinations are required for this scholarship. The selection process is based on demonstrated commitment to community, academic potential, and financial need assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Entering Year 5 or above in 2026; Must demonstrate commitment to their Indigenous community; Students selected on basis of academic potential, personal commitment and family support; Open to both current and new students; No academic examination required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship may cover up to 100% of tuition fees depending on financial circumstances and may include additional expenses such as year level charges, textbooks, stationery, uniforms, ICT charges, and camp costs. No academic examination is required. Recipients must maintain commitment to College community and contribute positively to College life. The College reserves the right to review or withdraw the scholarship if performance or engagement falls short.

overview: The Old Saints' Scholarship is offered on behalf of the alumni of All Saints' College to recognise a child of former students (Old Saint). The recipient is recognised as having sound academic results and strong community values, and may be a young person who would otherwise not be able to join the community due to financial constraints.
description: The Old Saints' Scholarship is offered on behalf of the alumni of All Saints' College and may be awarded to a child of former students (Old Saint) entering Year 7 in 2027. The recipient of this scholarship will be recognised as having sound academic results and strong community values. Further, the recipient might be a young person who would otherwise not be able to join this community due to financial constraints. The value of the Old Saints' Scholarship is 50% of the College's academic tuition fee. Supporting documentation required includes two written personal references that speak to the student's experience in community service, leadership skills and commitment qualities in other areas such as sports, club involvements etc., and copies of the student's most recent academic reports.
eligibility_criteria: Open to children of former All Saints' College students (Old Saints) entering Year 7 in 2027; Must have sound academic results; Must demonstrate strong community values and commitment to community service; May be selected based on financial need; Open to both current and new students
special_conditions: Old Saints' Scholarship is for children of former All Saints' College students. Recipients are expected to maintain sound academic performance and contribute positively to College life. The College reserves the right to review or withdraw the scholarship if performance falls short. The scholarship is based on demonstrated community values and service, as evidenced by written personal references.

Renewal is conditional upon continued academic commitment and satisfactory progress.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5, Year 7, or Year 10 in 2027; Demonstrated academic excellence; Performance in scholarship examination and interview; Satisfactory academic progress required for continuation
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the duration of a student's enrolment at the College, provided the conditions of the scholarship continue to be met. The College reserves the right to review or withdraw the scholarship if performance or engagement falls short.

overview: All Saints' College offers Music Scholarships to recognise outstanding musicians who have attained a high level of performance. The scholarship is available to students entering Years 7 to 12 and provides significant support for music tuition alongside academic fee reduction.
description: The Music Scholarship recognises an outstanding musician who has attained a high level of performance. The value of the scholarship is generally 50% of the College's academic tuition fee and 100% of the 30-minute music lesson tuition fees. The scholarship is for the duration of the student's attendance at the College and is conditional upon ongoing excellence in, and commitment to, the All Saints' Music Program. An audition with the Director of Music for short-listed candidates is an integral part of the awarding process. Music scholarship recipients must be active members of the College Choir and at least one other College ensemble.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 to Year 12 in 2026; Outstanding musicians with high level of performance; Piano: Grade 8 AMEB standard expected; Violin, Cello, Viola, Double Bass: Grade 4 AMEB standard expected; Other instruments: Evidence of outstanding progress or performance; Active participation in College Choir and at least one ensemble required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12

Recipients must be active members of the College Choir and at least one other College ensemble. They should choose Music elective classes at least until the end of Year 9 or Year 10. Annual review may be conducted to discuss attitude, activity and contribution to scholarship.

An annual review may be conducted to discuss the student's attitude, activity and contribution to their scholarship.

year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are conditional upon ongoing excellence in, and commitment to the All Saints' College Performing Arts program. Recipients must be active members of the cocurricular Performing Arts program. An annual review may be conducted to discuss the student's attitude, activity and contribution to their scholarship.

overview: All Saints' College offers the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Scholarship to recognise students with demonstrated commitment to their community and of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent. This scholarship significantly supports access to quality education for Indigenous students.
description: The Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Scholarship exists to recognise a student with a demonstrated commitment to their community and who is of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent. No academic examinations are required for this scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5 or above in 2026; Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Demonstrated commitment to community; Academic potential; Personal commitment to education; Family support for All Saints' College education; Financial assessment considered
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12

Coverage can be up to 100% of tuition fees dependent on financial circumstances and may include year level charges, textbooks, stationery, uniforms, ICT charges, camp costs and incidentals. No academic examination required.

overview: The Old Saints' Scholarship is offered on behalf of the alumni of All Saints' College to support children of former students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises recipients who demonstrate sound academic results and strong community values.
description: The Old Saints' Scholarship is offered on behalf of the alumni of All Saints' College and may be awarded to a child of former students (Old Saint) entering Year 7 in 2027. The recipient will be recognised as having sound academic results and strong community values. Further, the recipient might be a young person who would otherwise not be able to join this community due to financial constraints. The value of the Old Saints' Scholarship is 50% of the College's academic tuition fee. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated commitment to community service, leadership skills and involvement in other areas such as sports and club involvements.
eligibility_criteria: Child of former All Saints' College student (Old Saint); Entering Year 7 in 2027; Sound academic results; Strong community values; Demonstrated commitment to community service and leadership; Involvement in sports, club involvements or other extracurricular activities
special_conditions: Scholarship is available to children of former students (Old Saints). Recipients are recognised for having sound academic results and strong community values. Documentation required includes two written personal references addressing experience in community service, leadership skills and commitment qualities.

school_name: All Saints' College - Adelaide Street Campus
overview: All Saints' College offers the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Scholarship to foster future entrepreneurs who demonstrate creative thinking and a strong social conscience. The scholarship supports students entering Years 5 to 11 who show exceptional promise in entrepreneurial pursuits.
description: As part of All Saints' College's commitment to fostering future-enabled, creative-thinking entrepreneurs who see problems as opportunities and who model a 'startup' mindset, the College invites applications for the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Scholarship. Students entering Years 5 to 11 who have a demonstrated interest in and passion for innovation, new and creative ways of thinking and doing things, and with a strong social conscience, are invited to apply. Recipients are offered the unique opportunity to join the community and be mentored by some of the nation's leading entrepreneurs. This scholarship is only applicable for external students who are not currently enrolled at the College.
eligibility_criteria: External students not currently enrolled at All Saints' College; Entering Years 5 to 11 in 2024; Demonstrated interest in and passion for innovation; Strong social conscience; Creative thinking and new approaches to problem-solving
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: This scholarship is only available to external students not currently enrolled at All Saints' College. Recipients will be mentored by leading entrepreneurs. Documentation required includes two written references addressing passion for innovation and entrepreneurship skills.

overview: Alta-1 College offers comprehensive financial assistance to families to ensure cost is never a barrier to access. The college works with families during enrolment to discuss fees, payment options, and available financial support tailored to individual circumstances.
description: Alta-1 College is a not-for-profit independent school committed to removing financial barriers to education. During the enrolment process, families receive full information about fees, payment options, and financial assistance programs. The college works on an individual basis with each family to determine what support may be available. Specific fee amounts and financial assistance details are provided during the enrolment consultation. The college emphasizes that no student should be prevented from attending due to financial constraints. Families are encouraged to discuss their circumstances openly with the college's enrolment team to explore all available options.
eligibility_criteria: All students currently enrolled or applying to enrol at Alta-1 College; Financial need assessment conducted on individual basis; Open to families experiencing financial hardship
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: All financial assistance information is provided during the enrolment process. Payment plans and flexible payment options are available. As a not-for-profit organisation, Alta-1 College prioritises accessibility for all students regardless of family financial circumstances.

program_name: Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Program – Academic
overview: Alkimos College offers the Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Program – Academic to students with exceptional academic ability. The program provides an intellectually stimulating learning environment in the MESH subject areas (Mathematics, English, Science, Humanities and Social Sciences), guided by specialist teachers who receive Gifted and Talented professional learning.
description: The Gifted and Talented Program is an academically rigorous program designed to extend, enrich, and inspire high-achieving students. Students learn alongside like-minded peers in English, Mathematics, Science, and Humanities, with a focus on exploring passions, tackling real-world problems, and taking ownership of learning. The program commenced at Alkimos College in 2022 and is supported by a GAT Coordinator and Education Assistant. There is no financial cost to participate in this program as it is a Department of Education endorsed program within a public school. The program is open to Year 7 and Year 9 intakes. Selection is based on performance in the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET). Students participate in various academic challenges, creative problem-solving experiences, and STEM engagement activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 and Year 9 intakes; Must sit the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET) in Year 6 for Year 7 entry or equivalent for Year 9 entry; Students with exceptional creative and academic abilities; Applications close Friday 6 February 2026
special_conditions: Testing will occur in Term 1 2026 for the 2027 intake. Late applications can only be considered under exceptional and extenuating circumstances. Applications are managed through the Department of Education website www.education.wa.edu.au/gifted-and-talented. The college has been selected as a testing venue for the Year 7 intake.

overview: Alkimos College offers the Alpha/Aspire Academic Extension Program to students who demonstrate desire for academic success and strong work ethic. The program provides streamed classes in Mathematics, English, Science, and Humanities (MESH) with specialist teachers, offering differentiated learning to challenge students daily through authentic, real-world learning experiences.
description: The Alpha Program is an academic extension pathway designed for top-achieving students within specified MESH (Mathematics, English, Science, and Humanities) subjects. Students learn with like-minded peers in streamed classes from specialist teachers and are challenged daily through differentiated learning programs and authentic, real-world learning experiences. Enrichment opportunities are provided including excursions to universities, mentoring from academic coaches, and various engaging incursions. Students can be in Alpha for one or more MESH subjects depending on their strengths and aspirations. Students not able to maintain the required academic rigour within the Alpha program will be placed into the Aspirant class. There is no financial cost to participate in this program. Alpha eligibility is reviewed on a semester basis.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Selection based on primary school data, positive attitude, and teacher recommendations; Students identified as 'Aspirants' in Year 4-6 and inducted into Alpha Program for Year 7 if suitable; Students must maintain high academic standards and positive college conduct; Semester-based eligibility review
special_conditions: Students not able to maintain the required academic rigour within the Alpha program will be placed into the Aspirant class. Alpha eligibility is reviewed on a semester basis by the Assistant Principal. Selection is based on evidence of ability from primary school data, positive attitude, and teacher recommendations.

overview: Alkimos College offers a Specialised Autism Learning Program (SALP) for students with Autism Spectrum Disorder. The program is a Department of Education endorsed specialist educational program designed to support students with ASD to achieve their academic goals while learning within a mainstream school setting.
description: The Specialised Autism Learning Program (SALP) is an evidence-based, Department of Education endorsed specialist program that provides tailored support to students with Autism Spectrum Disorder. The program is designed and resourced for students with recognised ASD diagnosis who have no intellectual impairment and are academically capable of understanding Year 7 content. SALP students learn in a homeroom environment that is accessible, comfortable, supportive, and safe, with access to mainstream classes varying depending on individual student needs. The selection process includes interviews and rating against eligibility criteria. Enrolment is reviewed annually and may be extended to suit the individually tailored support requirements of each student. There is no financial cost for this program. Students from within and outside the local intake area are eligible to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Recognised diagnosis of Autism Spectrum Disorder; No intellectual impairment; Academically capable of understanding Year 7 content; Can manage behaviour independently; Self-management of personal care; Parents ensure safe transport to/from school; Eligibility for enrolment in Western Australian public schools; Students may apply from within or outside local intake area
special_conditions: Limited places available. Students in the Year 6 program at Alkimos Primary School are prioritised for a place if they still meet eligibility criteria. Expression of Interest form must be completed and submitted with enrolment forms by COB Friday 8 May 2026. Any applications after this date will not be accepted. Enrolment in the program is reviewed annually and may be extended to suit individual support requirements. The college follows the Department's Enrolment Policy, Enrolment Procedures, and Enrolment for Students with Disability Guidelines. Supported by School of Special Educational Needs: Disability (SSEND).

school_name: Alta-1 College - Winton Road Joondalup Campus

program_name: Gifted and Talented Visual Arts Program
overview: Applecross Senior High School offers the Gifted and Talented Visual Arts Program, which was established in 1968 and was the first program of its type in Western Australia. The program provides specialised studio disciplines and immersive visual arts education to students who demonstrate exceptional artistic talent and potential. Students participate in Saturday morning workshops conducted by professional artists.
description: The Gifted and Talented Education: Visual Arts program was established at Applecross Senior High School in 1968, making it the first program of its type in Western Australia, and it celebrated 50 years of excellence in 2018. This prestigious Visual Arts program produces graduates who are highly regarded by tertiary institutions and the arts industry due to an established tradition of excellence and reputation for success. The program offers students the opportunity to immerse themselves in a range of highly specialised studio disciplines during regular Saturday morning workshops conducted by professional artists and practising crafts people. Students benefit from exhibiting their artwork and being involved in community art projects. There is no financial cost mentioned for program participation; the program operates within standard school fees. Selection is through testing and interview process managed by the Department of Education Gifted and Talented Selection Unit. Entry is available for Year 7 (with applications closing early February for Year 6 students) and for Years 9, 10 and 11 (with rolling application periods from October to early February). Students accepted into the program are expected to continue from Year 7 to Year 12 to maintain their enrolment at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Students may apply for entry into the Gifted and Talented Program regardless of whether they reside within or outside the Applecross Senior High School boundary; Entry available for Year 7 entry (from Year 6 applicants) or midstream entry for Years 9, 10 and 11; Application and selection process managed by the Department of Education Gifted and Talented Selection Unit; Testing and interview required; Students must maintain enrolment in program from Year 7 to Year 12
special_conditions: All students who have been accepted into the Gifted and Talented Visual Arts program are expected to continue in their program from Year 7 to Year 12 to maintain their enrolment at the school. Application and selection processes are managed centrally by the Department of Education Gifted and Talented Selection Unit.

overview: Applecross Senior High School offers an Approved Specialist Tennis Program for students who have exceptional ability in tennis. The program was the first approved tennis program in Western Australia and offers four hours per week of practical on-court and theoretical instruction. The program caters for tournament players and offers interstate and international travel and competition.
description: The Specialist Tennis program offered at Applecross Senior High School was the first approved tennis program in Western Australia. The school offers the program to students in Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 and Physical Education Studies (Tennis) in Years 11 and 12. Selected students complete four hours per week of practical on-court and theoretical instruction in all aspects of the game. The Specialist Tennis program offers tournament participation, school representation, interstate and international travel and competition, lectures and instruction from former touring professionals and accreditation in coaching. The program has been formally endorsed by the Department of Education and Tennis West and caters for tournament players. Classes are strictly limited to twenty-four students, and entry into the program is extremely competitive. There is no financial cost mentioned for program participation; costs are within standard school fees. The selection process involves a skills appraisal and students must be involved in regular coaching, pennant and tournament participation. Applicants must have their most recent school report approved by the Applecross SHS Principal, with Year 7 entry requiring a copy of the student's Year 5 Semester 2 report.
eligibility_criteria: Students may apply for entry into the Specialist Tennis Program regardless of whether they reside within or outside the Applecross Senior High School boundary; Students must have a passion for and exceptional ability in tennis; Entry available for Year 7 (from Year 5 or 6 applicants); Must pass a skills appraisal; Must be involved in regular coaching, pennant and tournament participation; Most recent school report must be approved by the Applecross SHS Principal; For Year 7 entry, Year 5 Semester 2 report required; Classes strictly limited to twenty-four students
special_conditions: Students who reside within or outside the Applecross Senior High School boundary and who have a passion for, and exceptional ability in, Tennis may apply for entry into this Approved Specialist Program. Entry into the program is extremely competitive. The second part of the selection process involves students having their most recent school report approved by the Applecross SHS Principal. For Year 7 entry, this will need to include a copy of the student's Year 5, Semester 2 report. All students accepted into the Specialist Tennis program are expected to continue in their program from Year 7 to Year 12 to maintain their enrolment at the school.

overview: Applecross Senior High School's Academic Extension Program provides special classes for students who have been selected on the basis of their demonstrated academic performance and talent. The program operates in Mathematics, English, Science and HASS in Years 7 to 10 and offers students rich learning experiences with in-depth studies to develop high-level skills.
description: The needs of academically talented students are specifically catered for through the Academic Extension Program, which operates in Mathematics, English, Science and HASS in Years 7 to 10. Students in the program are offered a wide range of rich learning experiences and are required to complete more in-depth studies to develop high-level skills and competencies to become independent learners. The program provides special classes for students who have been selected on the basis of their performance. Applecross Senior High School's mission is to deliver one of the best Academic Extension programs in the State. There is no financial cost mentioned for program participation; the program operates within standard school fees. Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance in these subject areas. The program includes opportunities for students to develop critical thinking, advanced problem-solving skills, and deeper engagement with curriculum content across the core academic subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate academic talent and performance in Mathematics, English, Science and/or HASS; Selection based on academic performance; Open to Year 7 through Year 10 students; No specific standardised test score mentioned; Selection by school recommendation
special_conditions: Selection is based on demonstrated academic performance and school recommendation. Program operates within standard curriculum and subject areas. No formal application process documented; placement determined through academic assessment.

overview: Applecross Senior High School provides access to the Secondary Assistance Scheme, a government-funded financial assistance program available to eligible families holding concession cards. This scheme provides education allowances and clothing allowances to support students' participation in school.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme consists of two components providing financial support to eligible families. Students whose parents/guardians hold a current concession card (Health Care Card, Pension Concession Card, or Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Card) may be eligible to claim up to $235 Education Allowance which is applied to compulsory charges in the first instance with any remaining funds applied against voluntary contributions. An additional $300 Clothing Allowance is also available and can be paid to the nominated bank account or applied against school charges. The application process requires completing a Financial Assistance Claim Form and presenting it to the Finance Office along with the concession card. Applications close on 28 March each year. Processing is managed by the Department of Education's Financial Planning and Resourcing Directorate. Families experiencing financial difficulties are encouraged to contact the Finance Office to discuss alternative payment arrangements.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a current concession card (Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Card); Applies to all year levels; Application must be submitted by 28 March; Concession card must be current at time of application
special_conditions: Education Allowance of $235 will be applied towards education program charges in the first instance, with any residual to be applied to the voluntary contribution (for Years 7 to 10) or as negotiated with the parent/guardian (for Years 11 to 12). Clothing Allowance of $300 can be paid to parent/guardian or applied to school charges. Applications close strictly on 28 March each year. Processing typically takes 8 to 12 weeks for bank payments.

overview: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is a Western Australian government financial assistance program available to low-income families with students enrolled in secondary schooling. The scheme provides an education program allowance and a clothing allowance to eligible families to help with secondary school costs.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is administered by the Western Australian Department of Education and assists low-income families with secondary schooling costs. The scheme provides two financial components: an Education Program Allowance of $235 paid directly to the school, and a Clothing Allowance of $115 paid to the parent/guardian or the school (for public school students). The allowance is for the entire secondary school period for which a student is enrolled, subject to annual reapplication. Eligibility requires parents or guardians to hold a valid Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs concession card that represents a statement of income for the family.
eligibility_criteria: Children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 studying a full-time secondary course at a Western Australian public school; Parent or guardian must hold a valid Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs concession card; Students must be listed on the concession card holder's card; Student must be eligible up to and including the year they turn 18; Student must be attending school regularly
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Application must be submitted in Term 1 only and applications close 2 April 2026. Applications do not automatically carry forward to subsequent school years and must be reapplied for annually. The Education Program Allowance ($235) is paid to the school. The Clothing Allowance ($115) may be paid to the parent/guardian or directed to the school for other expenses. Students with their own health care card but who are financially dependent on their parent or guardian are not eligible. Foster parents cannot apply.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to reward exceptional students achieving in the top 10% nationally. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and commitment to learning, with automatic enrollment in the Academic Extension Program.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is available to Year 6 students, as well as current and incoming Year 7-10 students. The scholarship offers either full remission (100%) or partial remission (50%) of College fees, at the College's discretion. Selection is based on AGAT, PAT Math and PAT Reading test scores, school reports, positive attitude and willingness to engage and learn. The scholarship is reviewed biannually and concludes at the end of Year 10, though recipients continue in the Academic Extension Program through Years 11 and 12. Scholarship recipients must maintain excellent behaviour, high attendance, minimum B grades, and engage in school events and community stewardship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students; Open to current and incoming Year 7-10 students; Must be currently enrolled or in process of enrolling; Must be in top 10% nationally; Positive attitude and willingness to engage and learn required; Must attend school events and demonstrate school community engagement
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed biannually. Scholarship concludes at end of Year 10 but students continue in Academic Extension Program through Years 11 and 12. Applicants must be enrolled or in process of enrolling. Invitation to sit test is subject to review by Academic Extension Program Coordinator. Test can only be sat once in the year.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College offers select music scholarships to students in all year levels through audition. Music scholarships provide financial compensation for instrumental lessons and recognise students with musical potential and skill on their chosen instrument.
description: The Music Scholarship is available to students entering any year group at the College. Recipients receive financial compensation for instrumental lessons over the course of the academic year. Selection is via audition held in Term 4. Scholarship students must demonstrate musical potential and skill on their chosen instrument, maintain high commitment to classroom and co-curricular programs, and maintain at least a C grade average. Scholarships are reassessed annually and may be withdrawn if commitments are not upheld. Applicants must be enrolled or in process of enrolling.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Must demonstrate musical potential and skill on chosen instrument; Must maintain high level of commitment to classroom and co-curricular program; Must maintain minimum C grade average; Must be enrolled or in process of enrolling
special_conditions: Audition held in late November for the following academic year. Financial compensation is specifically for instrumental lessons. Scholarships reassessed annually. May be withdrawn if student commitments not upheld. Students must maintain minimum C grade average.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College offers scholarships to students in its Specialist Netball Program. The scholarship recognises exceptional netball athletes and supports their development in sport while maintaining academic standards.
description: The Netball Scholarship is available to students applying to the Specialist Netball Program, which has operated for over 20 years. Aranmore offers full and partial scholarships that provide remission of College fees (amount subject to College discretion). The College is committed to assisting scholarship students in gaining standards of excellence in both sports and academic work. Selection considers athletic potential and development prospects. Current College students do not need to complete additional paperwork to apply or re-apply. Support includes assistance with study planning and assessment of progress in challenging subjects.
eligibility_criteria: Open to netball athletes; Particularly Year 7-9 students considered for development potential; Limited playing experience not prohibitive; Current students need not complete additional paperwork; Must meet academic standards; No specific disciplinary issues allowed
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Part of Specialist Netball Program which provides coaching and development. Scholarship may be modified at discretion of College. College provides academic support including study planning and progress assessment. Students do not have to receive scholarship to be included in Netball Program.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College offers scholarships to students in its Specialist Rugby Program. The scholarship recognises exceptional rugby athletes and supports their development in sport while maintaining academic excellence.
description: The Rugby Scholarship is available to students applying to the Specialist Rugby Program for boys and girls, offered from Year 7 to Year 12. The program focuses on developing athletes for both Rugby 7s and Rugby 15s. Aranmore offers full and partial scholarships with remission of College fees (amount subject to College discretion). The College is committed to assisting scholarship students in gaining standards of excellence in both sports and academic work. Selection considers athletic potential and development prospects, particularly in younger year groups. Current College students do not need to complete additional paperwork to apply or re-apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to rugby athletes; Particularly Year 7-9 students considered for development potential; Limited playing experience not prohibitive; Current students need not complete additional paperwork; Must meet academic standards; No specific disciplinary issues allowed
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Part of Specialist Rugby Program which develops athletes for Rugby 7s and Rugby 15s. Scholarship may be modified at discretion of College. College provides academic support including study planning and progress assessment. Students do not have to receive scholarship to be included in Rugby Program. Offered to both boys and girls from Year 7-12.

overview: The Brother Kelly Scholarships are awarded to students entering Year 11 who demonstrate exceptional aptitude in Mathematics and/or Science. The scholarship recognises academic excellence in STEM disciplines.
description: The Brother Kelly Scholarship is available to current Year 10 students from any secondary school who show aptitude in Mathematics and/or Science and are entering Year 11. The scholarships offer remission of up to 100% of all College fees. The scholarship is reviewed annually and is tenable for up to 2 years. Selection is based on demonstrated aptitude in Mathematics and/or Science. Applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents of Australia and enrolled or in the process of enrolling at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current Year 10 students; May be from any secondary school; Must show aptitude in Mathematics and/or Science; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident; Must be enrolled or in process of enrolling
special_conditions: Scholarship reviewed annually. Tenable for up to 2 years. Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia within meaning of Australian Citizenship Act 2007. Applicants must be enrolled or in process of enrolling.

overview: The Moorditj Bidi Program provides scholarships to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program assists with tuition costs for the duration of a student's schooling and provides cultural support and inclusive learning.
description: The Moorditj Bidi Scholarship assists with the cost of annual tuition fees for the duration of a student's schooling at Aranmore Catholic College. The program creates an inclusive and supportive environment that values and respects culture, history and identity. Students are reviewed periodically based on attendance, behaviour and engagement with the program coordinator, relevant Dean of Students, and the Principal. All students in the program are encouraged to apply for ABSTUDY. The program provides connection to culture and community, inclusive learning spaces, and shared cultural experiences. Applicants must be currently enrolled or in the process of enrolling.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students only; Must be currently enrolled or in process of enrolling; Subject to periodic review based on attendance and behaviour; Engagement with program required
special_conditions: Students reviewed based on attendance, behaviour and engagement with Program Coordinator, relevant Dean of Students, and Principal. All students encouraged to apply for ABSTUDY. Program aims to empower Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to achieve full potential academically, culturally and personally. Non-refundable application fee.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College awards the Academic Excellence Scholarship to students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement within the top 10% nationwide. The scholarship recognises students who achieve outstanding results across all academic areas and is designed to foster academic excellence within the College.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship offers either full fee remission (100% of College fees) or partial fee reduction (50% of College fees), with the type determined at the College's discretion and subject to annual modification. Scholarships are tenable from Year 7 to Year 10 and are awarded via written assessment including AGAT, PAT Math and PAT Reading test scores, along with school reports and demonstrated positive attitude toward learning. The scholarship is reviewed biannually and recipients must maintain minimum B grades in all subjects, attend College events, demonstrate excellent behaviour, maintain high attendance rates, and show engagement with the College community through stewardship and leadership. Scholarship recipients are automatically enrolled in the Academic Extension Program (AEP) and will continue in the AEP through Years 11 and 12 even after the scholarship concludes at the end of Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students and current/incoming Year 7-10 students; Must be within top 10% nationally for academic achievement; Academic performance assessed via AGAT, PAT Math and PAT Reading test scores; Must be enrolled or in process of enrolling at the College; Minimum B grade average in all subjects; High attendance rate required; Excellent behaviour and uniform standard; Engagement with College community through stewardship and leadership; Willingness to engage and learn; Subject to biannual review
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed biannually and subject to modification at the College's discretion. A partial scholarship may be upgraded to full scholarship upon significant improvement, or may be reduced or revoked. Scholarship concludes at end of Year 10 but students continue in AEP through Years 11 and 12. Scholarships can be withdrawn following any breach of the College Code of Conduct.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College awards music scholarships to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and potential. The scholarship supports talented musicians across all year levels and recognises commitment to instrumental performance and musical excellence.
description: Music Scholarships provide financial compensation for instrumental lessons over the course of the academic year. Scholarships are awarded via audition and students entering any year group at the College may apply. Auditions take place in Term 4 (late November) with applicants notified of the specific audition date after the Music Scholarship closing date. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate musical potential and skill on their chosen instrument, maintain a high level of commitment to classroom and co-curricular music programs, and maintain at least a C grade average. Scholarships are reviewed annually and may be withdrawn if commitment standards are not maintained.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering any year group at the College; Must be enrolled or in process of enrolling; Must demonstrate musical potential and skill on chosen instrument; Must maintain high commitment to classroom and co-curricular programs; Must maintain minimum C grade average; Subject to annual review
special_conditions: Scholarships are reassessed annually and may be withdrawn if commitment standards are not upheld. Students must maintain at least C grade average across all subjects. Scholarship is conditional upon continued musical participation and demonstrated skill development.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College awards Specialist Netball Program Scholarships to outstanding female athletes demonstrating excellence in netball. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional netball ability and the potential to achieve standards of excellence in both sporting and academic pursuits.
description: Netball Scholarships are full or partial scholarships with the type determined at the College's discretion. The College awards scholarships to deserving students and provides comprehensive support including study and time management plan development and assessment of progress in challenging subjects. Athletes are viewed with their potential for development in mind, particularly in Years 7-9, and limited playing experience should not prohibit potential applicants. Scholarships require students to demonstrate excellence not only in netball but also in academic pursuits. Currently enrolled students are not required to complete paperwork to apply or re-apply for a Netball Scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Female students only; Open to students in Year 7-12; Must demonstrate potential for development in netball; Athletes viewed with development potential in mind; May apply with limited playing experience; Currently at College are not required to complete paperwork to re-apply; Open to new and current students
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: 2026 applications are now closed. Scholarships are awarded as part of the Specialist Netball Program. Support includes assistance with study planning, time management, and academic progress monitoring. Students do not have to receive a scholarship to be included in the Netball Program. For 2027 information, contact acc@aranmore.wa.edu.au.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College awards Specialist Rugby Program Scholarships to outstanding athletes demonstrating excellence in rugby. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional rugby ability and the commitment to achieve excellence in both sporting and academic endeavours.
description: Rugby Scholarships are full or partial scholarships with the type determined at the College's discretion. The College awards scholarships to deserving students and provides comprehensive support including study and time management plan development and assessment of progress in challenging subjects. Athletes are viewed with their potential for development in mind, particularly in Years 7-9, and limited playing experience should not prohibit potential applicants. The Specialist Rugby Program is offered from Year 7 to Year 12 for both boys and girls, focusing on developing athletes for both Rugby 7s and Rugby 15s formats.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7-12; Must demonstrate potential for development in rugby; Athletes viewed with development potential in mind; May apply with limited playing experience; Currently at College are not required to complete paperwork to re-apply; Open to new and current students; Both boys and girls eligible
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: 2026 applications are now closed. Scholarships are awarded as part of the Specialist Rugby Program. Support includes assistance with study planning, time management, and academic progress monitoring. Students do not have to receive a scholarship to be included in the Rugby Program. For 2027 information, contact acc@aranmore.wa.edu.au.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College awards Leadership Scholarships to incoming students from the Intensive English Centre and Year 7-10 who demonstrate exceptional leadership qualities and aptitude. The scholarship recognises students who show ambition to succeed and a willingness to actively contribute to College life.
description: Leadership Scholarships are full scholarships offering 100% remission of all College fees. These scholarships are awarded to incoming students from the Intensive English Centre (IEC) and Year 7-10 students who show an aptitude to become a leader, an ambition to succeed, and a willingness to actively contribute to College life through academic, pastoral, spiritual and creative arts programs. Recipients must be Australian citizens or permanent residents of Australia within the meaning of the Australian Citizenship Act 2007. The incoming Recipient scholarship is reviewed annually and is tenable for up to 6 years.
eligibility_criteria: Incoming students from Intensive English Centre and Year 7-10; Must show aptitude to become a leader; Must demonstrate ambition to succeed; Must show willingness to actively contribute to College life; Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia; Open to students attending Aranmore or another secondary school
special_conditions: Scholarships are reviewed annually and tenable for up to 6 years. Applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents of Australia within the meaning of the Australian Citizenship Act 2007. Currently open to incoming IEC and Year 7-10 students.

overview: Aranmore Catholic College awards scholarships to students who demonstrate excellent leadership skills and a high aptitude and interest in Science and Mathematics. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional ability in STEM disciplines and commitment to academic excellence.
description: Aranmore Catholic College awards full and partial scholarships to secondary students demonstrating a high aptitude and interest in Science and Mathematics. The College recognises the importance of developing talent in STEM fields and provides support to assist scholarship students in attaining standards of excellence in their scientific and mathematical endeavours. The specific structure and financial value of these scholarships is determined at the College's discretion, with full or partial fee remission available.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students; Must demonstrate high aptitude in Science; Must demonstrate high aptitude in Mathematics; Must show interest in STEM disciplines; Open to all secondary year levels
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction

overview: Aranmore Catholic College offers the Moorditj Bidi Program as part of its commitment to further educational outcomes and career opportunities for young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. The program provides financial assistance to support Indigenous students through their schooling at the College.
description: The Moorditj Bidi Program assists with the cost of annual tuition fees for the duration of a student's schooling at Aranmore Catholic College. 'Moorditj Bidi' means 'Solid' or 'Strong Path' in Noongar language and represents the program's vision to empower Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to achieve their full potential academically, culturally, and personally. The program strives to create an inclusive and supportive environment that values and respects culture, history, and identity. All students in the program are encouraged to apply for ABSTUDY. Students on scholarships are reviewed based on attendance, behaviour, and engagement with the program periodically with the Program Coordinator, relevant Dean of Students, and the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students only; Applicants must be currently enrolled or in process of enrolling at the College; Subject to periodic review of attendance, behaviour and engagement; Families encouraged to apply early due to potential waiting lists
special_conditions: Students are reviewed periodically based on attendance, behaviour, and engagement with Program Coordinator, Dean of Students, and Principal. All students encouraged to apply for ABSTUDY. There may be waiting lists due to high demand; families encouraged to apply early. Applicants must be currently enrolled or in process of enrolling.

school_name: Austin Cove Baptist College - California Crescent Campus
Beaconsfield Primary School - Rottnest Island Campus

overview: Beldon Primary School promotes the Little Friends Scholarship to support children and families affected by domestic and family violence. The scholarship is designed to ensure all children have the ability to either further their education or participate in extra-curricular activities that financial constraints may otherwise prevent.
description: The Little Friends Scholarship has been established to ensure all children have the ability to either further their education and/or be able to participate in extra-curricular activities that they may not be able to pursue due to the financial impact of Domestic and Family Violence within their family environment. The scholarship is administered by Friends with Dignity (www.friendswithdignity.org.au). Two scholarships are guaranteed to be awarded per State or Territory, with the remaining balance being awarded on an 'as needs basis'. The program recognises that financial hardship resulting from domestic and family violence impacts a child's capacity to engage fully in their education. This scholarship removes financial barriers to educational participation and personal development. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need resulting from domestic and family violence impact.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship due to domestic and family violence; Students enrolled at Beldon Primary School or seeking enrollment; Children of any year level (Kindergarten to Year 6); Demonstrated need for educational or extra-curricular support
special_conditions: Applications are assessed on an individual basis. The scholarship is specifically designed for students and families impacted by Domestic and Family Violence. Parents should contact Friends with Dignity directly for application information and support details.

overview: Belridge Secondary College offers the Academic Enrichment Program (AEP) for gifted and talented students in Years 7-10. This school-based program is designed for top-performing students within the local intake area and develops higher-order skills including problem-solving, critical thinking, creativity, communication and teamwork.
description: The Academic Enrichment Program provides gifted and talented students with enhanced learning experiences in STEM and Humanities. The program focuses on developing knowledge, skills and core competencies required for future careers through higher-order thinking. Learning experiences extend well beyond the classroom and include university immersion activities, guest speakers, industry partnerships and experiences, competitions across learning areas, specialist tutoring, incursions, excursions and enrichment activities. The program is overseen by a dedicated coordinator with extensive experience in working with gifted students. Selection is based on a written assessment test administered on a designated testing day.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or currently enrolled Years 7-10; Must be within school's local intake area; Must demonstrate gifted and talented ability through selection testing
special_conditions: School-based program for students within local intake area only. Testing day scheduled Monday, 25 May 2026.

overview: Belridge Secondary College offers an Elite Music Program for students demonstrating exceptional musical talent and performance ability. The program supports students to develop as accomplished musicians within a specialist environment that values excellence in music performance.
description: The Elite Music Program provides selected students with specialist music instruction and performance opportunities. Students in this program receive audition-based selection to ensure musical talent and ability are at the required standard. The program operates for multiple year levels and includes ensemble and individual performance opportunities. Applications are submitted through the school's arts portal, with auditions conducted in early June and final offers extended by mid-June. Selection is highly competitive based on musical performance assessed during audition trials.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or continuing through secondary years; Must demonstrate musical talent through audition; Musical instrument proficiency required; Open to all secondary year levels
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications submitted to Belridge.SC.Arts@education.wa.edu.au. Auditions held week commencing 1 June 2026. Places offered by email Friday 6 or 12 June 2026. Acceptance deadline Friday 19 June 2026.

overview: Belridge Secondary College offers an Elite Dance Company program for students with demonstrated dance ability and passion for performance. The program provides structured dance training and performance opportunities for talented dancers across secondary year levels.
description: The Elite Dance Company is a selective program for students demonstrating exceptional dance ability and commitment to dance performance. Students participate in audition-based selection to ensure they meet the program's performance standards. The program includes structured training, choreography opportunities and public performance experiences. Applications are submitted through the school's arts portal with auditions conducted in June. This is a highly competitive program based on dance technique and performance ability assessed during audition trials.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or continuing through secondary years; Must demonstrate dance ability and technique through audition; Dance experience or training background required; Open to all secondary year levels
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications submitted to Belridge.SC.Arts@education.wa.edu.au. Auditions held week commencing 1 June 2026. Places offered by email Friday 6 or 12 June 2026. Acceptance deadline Friday 19 June 2026.

overview: Belridge Secondary College offers a Specialist Fashion Design Program that is WA Department of Education approved. The program is designed for highly creative students with a passion for fashion and an eye for detail. It provides comprehensive training in design, construction and innovation in the fashion industry.
description: The Specialist Fashion Design Program is an intensive, state-approved specialist program that develops students' skills in fashion design, illustration, garment construction and creative innovation. Students engage with highlights including wearable art projects, fashion illustration, design and surface decoration of fabrics using industry-standard tools including sewing machines, pressing stations and overlockers, technology-based design and printing, competition participation in state and national competitions, beading and embellishment techniques, fiber exploration, deconstruction and upcycled fashion studies, and analysis of fashion companies and runway productions. The program runs from Year 7 to Year 12 and accepts students from across Western Australia. Selection is based on application and portfolio review.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 for 2027 intake; May accept applications from outside local intake area; Demonstrated interest and passion for fashion design required; Creative aptitude and design skills required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: WA Department of Education approved Specialist Program. Open to students from across Western Australia, not limited to local intake area. Applications submitted to belridge.sc.arts@education.wa.edu.au. Places offered by email Friday 5 June 2026. Acceptance deadline Friday 19 June 2026.

overview: Belridge Secondary College offers a Specialist Cricket Program that is fully endorsed by the Department of Education and the Western Australian Cricket Association (WACA). The program develops young cricketers with high-level skill development, specialist coaching, mentoring and game play in a professional environment. Both boys and girls are encouraged to apply.
description: The Specialist Cricket Program is a fully approved and endorsed program that provides students with intensive cricket development from Year 7 to Year 12. The program features specialist bowling, batting, wicket-keeping and fielding coaches, international and interstate trips in senior years, community contribution from Year 7, senior school pathways including ATAR Physical Education studies and nationally recognised Certificate III in Sport and Recreation (Cricket Studies) leading to TAFE and Diploma courses. The program operates on the theme of Cricket, Community and Culture. Selection is based on cricket skills trials where students demonstrate technical ability, game understanding and athletic potential. The program accepts students from across Western Australia.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 for 2027 intake or continuing through to Year 12; Both male and female players encouraged to apply; Must pass competitive cricket skills trial; May accept applications from outside local intake area; No prior club experience required but demonstrated cricket aptitude required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: WA Department of Education approved Specialist Program. Fully endorsed by Western Australian Cricket Association. Skills trial day scheduled Tuesday 31 March 2026 for Year 7, 2027 intake. Open to students from across Western Australia. Contact Cricket Coordinator to confirm details and equipment requirements. Year 11/12 students complete Certificate III Sports and Recreation (Cricket Studies).

overview: Belridge Secondary College offers a Specialist Netball Program that is endorsed as a Netball WA Tier 1 Specialist School. The elite program has been supporting students since 2013 and now operates as a comprehensive program from Year 7 to Year 12. Both boys and girls are encouraged to apply and the program provides top-level coaching and personal development.
description: The Specialist Netball Academy is a fully endorsed Netball WA Tier 1 Specialist School program that develops student athletes from Year 7 through Year 12. The program emphasizes skill development, game tactics and strategies, biomechanics, sports psychology and fitness training. Senior school students have the opportunity to complete a nationally recognised Certificate III in Sport and Recreation. The holistic program focuses on teamwork, collaboration and personal health and wellbeing. Entry into the program is highly competitive. Selection is based on netball trials where students demonstrate skill, game understanding and athletic potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 for 2026 intake or Years 8-12 for continuing entry; Both male and female students encouraged to apply; Must pass competitive netball trials; Demonstrated netball ability and commitment required; Students outside local intake area may apply for Years 8-12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Netball WA Tier 1 Specialist School endorsed. Netball trials (second round) scheduled Tuesday 16 September 2025 for Year 7, 2026 intake. Year 11/12 students complete Certificate III in Sport and Recreation. Senior students have opportunity for international tours in Year 11 or 12. Requires register of interest for participation.

overview: Belridge Secondary College provides financial assistance for eligible families to help with school charges and clothing costs. The program is available to families holding Centrelink Family Health Care Cards or Pensioner Concession Cards.
description: The school offers some financial assistance for charges and clothing to eligible families. Most Centrelink Family Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card holders are eligible for this support. Applications should be made during Term 1 of each school year to the Manager of Corporate Services. The rules and eligibility criteria may change from year to year, so families should contact the school directly to determine current eligibility and application requirements. This is a means-tested financial assistance program designed to support families experiencing financial hardship with school-related costs.
eligibility_criteria: Hold valid Centrelink Family Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Application must be made during Term 1 of each school year; Income eligibility based on Centrelink card holder status; Open to all students
special_conditions: Applications must be made during Term 1 of each school year. Rules and eligibility criteria change from year to year. Contact Manager of Corporate Services for current eligibility and application process. For further information contact the school's Manager of Corporate Services.

overview: Belmont City College offers the A Plus Academic Excellence Program as a selective entry program for academically gifted students entering Year 7. The program is delivered in partnership with the University of Western Australia and provides extended academic opportunities for high-achieving students.
description: The A Plus Academic Excellence Program is an academically selective program for Year 7 entry designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. Entry is determined through testing in reading comprehension, mathematics, abstract reasoning and written expression, with consideration of school reports, prior NAPLAN results, references and personal qualities. The program includes orientation via EPIC-Science for Year 6, supported pathways to university studies through partnership with the University of Western Australia, homework and study skills support through a Homework Club with expert tutors, and targeted pastoral care with regular feedback to parents. Students participate in state and national competitions in learning areas including the Australian National Chemistry Quiz, Have Sum Fun, Young Writer's Competition and Science IQ. Duration is for the period of enrolment from Year 7 to Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Entry testing in reading comprehension, mathematics, abstract reasoning and written expression required; School reports, prior NAPLAN results, references and personal qualities considered; Selection based on competitive assessment
special_conditions: Specialist Program and Scholarship Online Application available for Year 7 2026. University of Western Australia partnership provides supported pathways to university studies.

overview: Belmont City College offers the STEM Science Engineering Program as a selective entry program for Year 7 students with strong academic ability in science and engineering. The program provides hands-on, problem-solving based challenges supported by university links and the School Pathways Program.
description: The STEM Science Engineering Program is aimed at academically able students and involves exciting, challenging and hands-on problem-solving based challenges mapped to the Design and Technology Curriculum and Achievement Standards. The program is supported with university links and the School Pathways Program. Year 6 students who have accepted entry into the program for the following year are invited to participate in EPIC-Science for an extra term with a Robotics focus. Students participate in the STEM Excellence elective supported by engineering professional bodies, university links and the School Pathways Program. Each project is underpinned by the processes of research and collaboration, design, building, testing and redesign that characterise the iterative engineering process.
