eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be academically able students; Entry through competitive selection process; Interest in engineering and design problem-solving required
special_conditions: Specialist Program and Scholarship Online Application available for Year 7 2026. EPIC-Science program available for Year 6 students accepted into the program. Supported by university links and the School Pathways Program.

overview: Belmont City College offers the Music Excellence Program as a specialist entry program for students with musical ability entering Year 7. The program provides specialist music instruction including class music, instrumental studies and ensemble performance opportunities.
description: The Music Excellence Program provides specialist music instruction for students in Years 7-12 with a focus on musical excellence. For Year 7 only, students participate in the Instrumental Music School Services (IMSS) Beginner Instrument Program. Students in the Music Excellence Program regularly perform in a variety of concerts, college assemblies and local community events throughout the year and participate in the annual music camp. Ensembles also perform in the following annual state festivals: WA Schools' Senior Concert Band Festival (ABODA) and WA Schools' Junior Concert Band Festival (ABODA). Belmont City College is a local-intake school, with the State Government determining the local-intake area.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate musical ability; Participation in IMSS Beginner Instrument Program available for Year 7 only; Open to students within local-intake area and selectively to those outside local-intake with demonstrated musical talent
special_conditions: Specialist Program and Scholarship Online Application available for Year 7 2026. IMSS Beginner Instrument Program available for Year 7 only. Regular concert and festival performances required. Annual music camp participation expected.

overview: Belmont City College offers the Specialist Contemporary Drama Program as a Department of Education approved selective entry program for students with dramatic arts talent entering Year 7. The program delivers first-rate drama education empowering students to become critical and creative performers.
description: The Specialist Contemporary Drama Program is a Department of Education approved specialist program offering extended and enriched drama education for students demonstrating talent in dramatic arts. The program delivers first-rate drama education that empowers students to become critical and creative performers. The course has produced several successful Western Australian Academy of Performing Arts (WAAPA) applicants and continues to exist at the forefront of Dramatic Arts education in Western Australian public schools. Students develop performance skills through intensive drama study including practical and theoretical components. The program is available for entry at Year 7, with continuation through Year 12. Selection is based on demonstrated talent and interest in drama.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate talent and interest in dramatic arts; Selection through audition and application process; Open to students within local-intake area and selectively to those outside with demonstrated dramatic talent
special_conditions: Department of Education approved specialist program. Specialist Program and Scholarship Online Application available for Year 7 2026. Strong track record of WAAPA applicant success. Produces annual drama productions and performances.

overview: Blue Gum Montessori School offers fee discounts for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. The second child receives a 20% tuition discount, while the third and subsequent children receive a 30% tuition discount.
description: Blue Gum Montessori School provides automatic tuition fee reductions for families with multiple children enrolled simultaneously. When more than one child from the family is enrolled, the second child from that family will be entitled to a 20% discount on the Tuition Fee. The third and subsequent children will be entitled to a 30% discount on the Tuition Fee. This discount applies to all students from Pre-Kindy through Year 6 and continues for the duration of each child's enrolment at the school. The discount is applied automatically and does not require any special application process.
eligibility_criteria: More than one child from the same family must be enrolled at the school; Second child receives discount; Third and subsequent children receive higher discount
special_conditions: Discount applies to the second child from the family. Third and subsequent children receive 30% discount instead. Students cannot change between programs once enrolled for the year.

overview: Booragoon Primary School offers access to the Primary Extension and Challenge (PEAC) program, a part-time supplementary program designed for gifted and talented students in Years 5 and 6 attending public primary schools. The program provides intellectually stimulating and challenging learning experiences beyond the regular classroom curriculum.
description: PEAC is a selective program administered by the Western Australian Department of Education for public primary school students identified as gifted and talented. Students are selected based on their performance in a Year 4 PEAC test, which assesses verbal and mathematical reasoning. This test is administered across all WA public schools, and parents can opt out by notifying the school in writing. Students attend PEAC sessions weekly or in blocks, depending on the region, as a withdrawal program where students leave their regular class temporarily to attend PEAC sessions, usually offsite. The program provides no direct financial benefit but offers enhanced educational opportunities for identified gifted students.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 4 at a WA public primary school; Eligible students identified through PEAC reasoning test performance in Year 4; Placement in Years 5 and 6; Open to co-educational students
special_conditions: Students are selected based on performance in the Year 4 PEAC test which assesses verbal and mathematical reasoning. Parents can opt out of the testing by notifying the school in writing. This is a part-time withdrawal program.

school_name: Boyup Brook District High School - Junior Campus (K-6)

overview: Bridgetown High School offers the Bridgetown Agricultural Society Scholarship, donated and administered directly by the Bridgetown Agricultural Society. The scholarship is presented to recognise students demonstrating merit and achievement.
description: This scholarship is donated and decided by the Bridgetown Agricultural Society. Students are required to apply directly to the Bridgetown Agricultural Society for the scholarship. The exact financial value, selection process details, and duration are determined by the Bridgetown Agricultural Society and available through their direct application process. The scholarship is presented at the annual Presentation ceremony at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Application through Bridgetown Agricultural Society; Merit-based selection
special_conditions: Students must apply directly to the Bridgetown Agricultural Society, not through the school.

overview: Bridgetown High School offers the St John Ambulance Merit Award, donated by the Bridgetown Branch of St John Ambulance. The award recognises students demonstrating merit and commitment to community service values.
description: This award is donated by the Bridgetown Branch of St John Ambulance and is presented by a representative from St John Ambulance at the annual Presentation ceremony. The award is decided by teaching staff and the Principal based on merit. Recipients are recognised for their contributions and values aligned with St John Ambulance's community service mission. The exact financial value and duration are not specified on the school's published information.
eligibility_criteria: Merit-based selection; Decided by teaching staff and Principal
special_conditions: Award selected by teaching staff and Principal without formal application process.

overview: Bridgetown High School presents the Year 9/10 Team Leader Award to recognise outstanding students in Years 9 and 10. The award is selected by teaching staff and the Principal based on merit.
description: The Year 9/10 Team Leader registers interest in this award each year, which is then decided by teaching staff and the Principal. The award is presented at the annual Presentation ceremony and recognises student excellence within the Year 9/10 cohort. Recipients demonstrate achievement and positive contribution to the school community. Financial value and specific selection criteria are not detailed in the school's published information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 and Year 10 students; Merit-based selection by teaching staff and Principal
special_conditions: Award selected through team leader nomination and staff decision without formal application.

overview: Bridgetown High School offers a Co-curricular Award for Year 9 and 10 students who demonstrate excellence across multiple disciplines. The award recognises all-round student achievement and contribution.
description: The Year 9/10 Team Leader registers interest in this award each year, which is then decided by the two Team Leaders and the Principal. The award is presented at the annual Presentation ceremony and recognises students who excel across co-curricular activities. Recipients demonstrate well-rounded participation and achievement in school programs beyond academics. Financial details and specific selection criteria are not published on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 and Year 10 students; Demonstrated excellence in co-curricular activities; Selected by Team Leaders and Principal
special_conditions: Award selected through team leader nomination and principal decision; recognises co-curricular achievement.

overview: Bridgetown High School presents the Rotary Dux Award to the top Year 10 student, recognising exceptional academic excellence. The award is presented as one award for boys and one for girls.
description: The Rotary Dux is presented to the top Year 10 student and merit award – one boy and one girl, decided by teaching staff and Principal and kindly donated by the Bridgetown Rotary Club. The award is presented at the annual Presentation ceremony. This is a merit-based honour recognising the highest-achieving students in Year 10, with recognition given separately to male and female students. The exact financial value is not specified in the school's published information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Highest academic achievers; One award for boys and one for girls; Selected by teaching staff and Principal
special_conditions: Awarded to one boy and one girl; Selected by teaching staff and Principal; Donated by Bridgetown Rotary Club.

overview: Bridgetown High School Alumni Association offers an Environmental Science Scholarship to incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students with demonstrated interest and potential in environmental science, providing financial support for educational costs.
description: Each year, the Bridgetown High School Alumni Association presents an Environmental Science Scholarship to a Year 7 student. Winning students are provided $500 each year from Years 8-10 to help with a range of educational costs, such as uniforms, supplies and other school expenses. The total value over the scholarship period is $1,500 (three payments of $500). The selection process involves evaluation by the Alumni and school staff. This scholarship supports approximately three years of secondary education at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated interest in environmental science; Merit-based selection by Alumni and school
special_conditions: Awarded to one Year 7 student annually; Provides $500 per year for Years 8-10; Recipients must maintain enrolment at Bridgetown High School.

overview: Bridgetown High School Alumni Association offers a Music Scholarship to incoming Year 7 students. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating musical talent and potential, providing financial support for school expenses.
description: Each year, the Bridgetown High School Alumni Association presents a Music Scholarship to a Year 7 student. Winning students are provided $500 each year from Years 8-10 to help with a range of educational costs, such as uniforms, supplies and other school expenses. The total value over the scholarship period is $1,500 (three payments of $500 annually). The selection process involves evaluation by the Alumni and school based on musical merit. This scholarship supports approximately three years of secondary education at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated musical ability or achievement; Merit-based selection by Alumni and school
special_conditions: Awarded to one Year 7 student annually; Provides $500 per year for Years 8-10; Recipients must maintain enrolment at Bridgetown High School.

overview: The Western Australian Department of Education provides the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) to assist eligible low-income families with secondary school costs. Bridgetown High School students and families can apply for this government support program.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a Western Australian government program available to families with children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 at public or non-government schools. The scheme provides an education program allowance of $235 (paid to the school) and a clothing allowance of $115 (2026 rates) for eligible families. The allowance is paid annually and is designed to reduce financial barriers to secondary education. Eligibility is determined based on family income and financial circumstances. Parents and guardians must apply through their child's school or contact the Department of Education.
eligibility_criteria: Family income below eligibility threshold; Children enrolled in Years 7 to 12; Open to students in public or non-government schools; Australian residents
special_conditions: Low-income families only; Application through school or Department of Education; Annual allowance for each eligible student.

overview: The Department of Education coordinates a range of scholarships that provide financial support to secondary students in Western Australia. Bridgetown High School students are eligible to apply for these state-managed scholarships.
description: The Department of Education coordinates a range of scholarships providing financial support to secondary students across Western Australia. Information about the types of scholarships available, application processes and closing dates are available through the Department of Education's official website. These scholarships may include academic merit awards, boarding scholarships, and other programs supporting secondary education. Specific details regarding individual program values, eligibility criteria, and application dates should be obtained directly from the Department of Education scholarship page.
eligibility_criteria: Varies by individual scholarship; Generally open to Australian citizens or permanent residents; Year level requirements vary
special_conditions: Contact Department of Education at SecondarySchoolingScholarships@education.wa.edu.au or (08) 9402 6422 for current information.

program_name: Financial Assistance for Families with Health Care Card
overview: Broome Senior High School assists families with the payment of school contributions and charges through financial assistance offered by the Department of Education. The assistance is available to parents and guardians who are holders of a Centrelink Family Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or a Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Concession Card.
description: The Department of Education offers financial assistance to support families with the payment of contributions and charges. This assistance is available to parents and guardians holding a Centrelink Family Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or a Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Concession Card. The program includes the Clothing Allowance (CLA) which can be paid to the applicant or the school. Families who have applied for Abstudy are not eligible for this allowance. A supplement amount is available to families who have already received the Abstudy school allowance from Centrelink. Application forms are required for both the Education Program Allowance and the Abstudy Supplement Assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of Centrelink Family Health Care Card; OR Holder of Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card; OR Holder of Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Concession Card; Not already receiving Abstudy for general allowance (except for supplement); Open to all students
special_conditions: Applications require submission of relevant application forms. Families must provide documentation of their Centrelink card or Veterans' Affairs card status.

overview: Broome Senior High School offers the Academic Extension Program to extend and support high-level critical and creative learners. The program is an innovative two-year learning experience introduced in 2015 that incorporates the Big History Project (BHP) and West Australian Curriculum in an enriched cross-curricular classroom environment.
description: The Academic Extension Program is designed for exceptional learners and provides a two-year innovative learning experience combining the Big History Project (BHP) with the West Australian Curriculum. The program covers learning from 13.7 billion years of history (from the Big Bang to Modernity), encouraging students to grasp the entire scale of human history through evaluating perspectives and frameworks. Students develop high-level thinking skills through collaborative and individual assessment strategies, inquiry-based learning, and project-based learning experiences. From 2018, the program expanded to include a Year 8 class, and in 2022 it was expanded to encompass delivery across both Science and Humanities and Social Sciences (HaSS) learning areas for both Year 7 and 8. Students conclude the program with a final presentation relating to their local content and the current world, showcased during an evening event.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students entering the program; Demonstrated high-level critical and creative learning ability; Selection based on academic performance and aptitude; Open to all genders and backgrounds
special_conditions: Selective entry based on academic ability. Program is a two-year commitment for both Year 7 and Year 8. Broome Senior High School was chosen to develop this course in conjunction with the Big History Project for Australian and Western Australian based schools.

overview: Follow the Dream is an academic support program for aspiring Aboriginal students at Broome Senior High School. The primary aim of the program is to increase the number of Aboriginal students graduating from Year 12 and continuing with either tertiary education or skilled vocational employment. The program has been operating at the school since 2006.
description: Follow the Dream is an academic program specifically designed for aspiring Aboriginal students, operating at Broome Senior High School since 2006. The primary aim is to increase the number of Aboriginal students graduating from Year 12 and progressing to tertiary education or skilled vocational employment. Many graduates have moved onto productive post-school outcomes across a wide spectrum of vocations and training pathways. The program operates during after school hours Monday to Thursday, providing students with access to tutors, computer and internet access, ongoing advice about careers and school, and refreshments. Students have the opportunity to participate in vocational camps programs and attend excursions. Formal selection of Year 7 students begins at the end of Term 1, though prospective applicants can begin attending on a trial basis early in Term 1. The program is currently supported by the school in partnership with the Department of Education WA and the Polly Farmer Foundation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal students; Must be entering or currently enrolled in Year 7; Must demonstrate aspiration to succeed at high school; Expected to commit to achieving and maintaining good outcomes at school; Selection begins end of Term 1; Trial attendance available from early Term 1
special_conditions: Students are expected to commit to achieving and maintaining good outcomes at school. There is an expectation that they maintain an 'aspiration' to succeed at high school. Formal selection begins at the end of Term 1. Prospective applicants can begin attending from early Term 1 on a trial basis. The program operates in partnership with Department of Education WA and Polly Farmer Foundation.

overview: Broome Clontarf Academy provides flexible learning for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men at Broome Senior High School. The academy is staffed by full-time mentors and is designed to foster positive learning and personal growth for young men in the program, using mentoring and support to improve educational and life outcomes.
description: Broome Clontarf Academy is a mentoring program specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men at Broome Senior High School. The academy rooms are welcoming and inclusive spaces designed to foster positive learning and personal growth. The program is staffed by full-time mentors who provide support and guidance. The Clontarf Foundation operates the academy as part of its national network, using structured support to improve student attendance, engagement, and post-school outcomes. The academy provides access to mentors, resources, and work-ready skill development. Students who complete the program receive recognition for achievements in areas such as dedication to culture, academic performance, attendance, and commitment to employment and training. The program has a strong track record with recent graduates securing employment or training opportunities in areas including university, oil and gas, mining, and various trades.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys; Open to Year 7 students; Demonstrated commitment to learning and personal development; Open to all Year 7-12 students meeting Indigenous criteria
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: For Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys only. The program has a strong mentoring component with full-time staff. Year 7 induction camps are held to introduce students to the program's expectations, routines and values. Work-ready skills development is a significant component of the program.

overview: Broome Stars (formerly known as Girls Academy) is a support program for all Indigenous female students at Broome Senior High School. The program is designed to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls from Year 7 to Year 12 in their educational journey and personal development.
description: Broome Stars is a specialized support program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls at Broome Senior High School, formerly known as the Girls Academy. The program includes all Indigenous female students from Year 7 to Year 12, providing mentoring and support to foster positive learning, engagement, and personal growth. The program operates in partnership with the school's Aboriginal Education Team and other support services. Various workshops and initiatives are delivered throughout the year, focusing on areas including mental health, self-esteem, confidence, goal-setting, bullying prevention, helping others, and dealing with difficult emotions. The program is integrated with the school's culturally responsive education framework and operates in conjunction with the Bagu Warrdarndan Jimbin Centre. The program supports all aspects of student wellbeing and academic success for Indigenous girls at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls; Open to students from Year 7 to Year 12; All eligible Indigenous female students from the school catchment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: For Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls only. Open to Year 7-12 students. Integrated with the school's culturally responsive education framework and Aboriginal Education Team initiatives.

overview: Broome Senior High School offers the Ranger Cadets Program, which incorporates Bush Ranger Cadets WA and Indigenous Ranger Cadetship. The program supports youth to take an active role in the conservation of the natural environment and understand the mechanisms of environmental management while developing leadership, personal growth, and skills.
description: The Ranger Cadets Program at Broome Senior High School is a youth development initiative that incorporates both the Bush Ranger Cadets WA program and Indigenous Ranger Cadetship. The program supports students to take an active role in the conservation of the natural environment and to understand environmental management mechanisms. The Ranger Cadets model promotes leadership, personal growth, and skills development as central key components. Students engage in hands-on environmental and conservation work while developing practical skills applicable to outdoor industries and environmental careers. The program provides opportunities for students to contribute meaningfully to environmental stewardship in their local community. For further information about the program, students can speak with the Deputy Principal, Sandi Passmore.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in environmental conservation; Interest in outdoor pursuits and environmental work; Demonstrated commitment to learning and personal development; Open to all students regardless of background
special_conditions: Students are expected to demonstrate commitment to learning and environmental stewardship. Contact Deputy Principal Sandi Passmore for further information about the program.

overview: Bunbury Senior High School offers academic scholarships through the generosity of the school community. These scholarships are available to currently enrolled students and those who wish to study at the school.
description: Bunbury Senior High School provides academic scholarships supported by the school community to recognize and support student achievement. The exact financial value, selection criteria, duration and specific conditions are detailed on the school's scholarship information page. Interested students should contact the school directly for comprehensive details about eligibility requirements, application procedures and the specific benefits offered.
eligibility_criteria: Open to currently enrolled students or prospective students seeking to study at Bunbury Senior High School
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: The Kingia Scholarship is offered to one student completing an ATAR pathway and one student completing a VET pathway at Bunbury Senior High School.
description: The Kingia Scholarship recognizes academic achievement and supports students pursuing either the ATAR or VET pathway at Bunbury Senior High School. One scholarship is awarded to an ATAR pathway student and one to a VET pathway student each year. Specific details regarding the financial value, selection criteria, application process and eligibility requirements should be obtained directly from the school.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; enrolled at Bunbury Senior High School; pursuing either ATAR or VET pathway
special_conditions: Recipient must remain enrolled until Year 12.

overview: The Kaadajiny Scholarships are offered to two ATAR pathway students and two VET pathway students, selected from Kingia scholarship applicants at Bunbury Senior High School.
description: The Kaadajiny Scholarship provides additional recognition to high-achieving students following either the ATAR or VET pathway. These scholarships are awarded to applicants who have applied for the Kingia Scholarship, with two scholarships allocated to ATAR pathway students and two to VET pathway students annually. Contact the school for specific details about the financial value, selection process and other conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or Year 12 students; must have applied for the Kingia Scholarship; pursuing either ATAR or VET pathway
special_conditions: Selected from Kingia scholarship applicants

overview: The Fogarty Scholarship is awarded to high-achieving students at Bunbury Senior High School who demonstrate academic excellence and excellence in other areas including sports and co-curricular activities.
description: The Fogarty Scholarship recognizes outstanding achievement across academic, sporting and co-curricular domains. Recipients are supported through both undergraduate and postgraduate studies. The scholarship has funded undergraduate programs at the University of Western Australia and continues to support recipients as postgraduate students. Contact the school for details about current eligibility criteria, application procedures and the specific financial benefits available.
eligibility_criteria: High achievers across academic, sporting and co-curricular areas; demonstrated excellence in multiple domains
special_conditions: Scholarship can extend to support postgraduate university studies

overview: Bunbury Catholic College offers an annual scholarship opportunity for gifted and talented music students entering Year 7. The scholarship aims to develop talented students who may have had limited music tuition opportunities.
description: This Music Scholarship is designed to support gifted and talented students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and potential. The scholarship recognises students with advanced music skills who show commitment to developing their musical talents further. Students must participate in the College's music program, which includes opportunities to perform in various ensembles, compete in eisteddfods, and undertake music as an ATAR subject in the upper school. The scholarship covers tuition support and opportunities for musical development throughout their time at the College, fostering a love of music and enabling students to achieve excellence in their musical pursuits.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate exceptional musical talent and ability; Music audition required; Open to all students regardless of previous music qualifications
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are expected to participate actively in College music programs and ensembles. Continued support is contingent on maintaining engagement with music studies.

overview: Bunbury Catholic College offers a bursary program to support students enrolled in external Certificate Courses that exceed $1,000 in cost. This bursary assists Year 11 and 12 students to access vocational education pathways by reducing the financial burden of external course fees.
description: The External Certificate Course Bursary provides financial assistance to Year 11 and Year 12 students who are undertaking external Certificate Courses through registered training providers. The bursary provides up to $1,000 towards school fees to help cover the costs of these external courses, which are split into two equal amounts paid during the year. This opportunity opens doors for students to explore and excel in their chosen vocational pathway without significant financial hardship. Students must provide proof of payment to the training provider and evidence of the course fee to apply. The bursary supports the College's commitment to providing accessible quality education and enabling all families to access the full range of learning opportunities available at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 11 or Year 12; External course fee must exceed $1,000; Must be enrolled in certificate course through registered training provider; Proof of payment to training provider required
special_conditions: Bursary is split into two equal payments. Families must submit proof of payment to the training provider and course fee receipt to bcc.fees@cewa.edu.au to apply. Terms and conditions apply.

overview: Bunbury Catholic College offers an Elite Sports Program for students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and commitment to their sport. The program provides specialist training and support for talented athletes at the College.
description: The Elite Sports Program is designed to support students who demonstrate elite-level sporting ability and are committed to achieving excellence in their chosen sport. The program provides access to specialist coaching, training facilities, and structured development pathways that enable students to balance their sporting commitments with academic responsibilities. Students in the Elite Sports Program benefit from tailored training schedules, mentorship from experienced coaches, and opportunities to compete at regional and state levels. The program recognises that sporting excellence requires dedication, discipline, and support beyond the standard co-curricular offering. Participants are expected to maintain academic standards while pursuing their sporting goals and to represent the College with integrity and sportsmanship. The program supports students across multiple sports and year levels, providing them with a pathway to develop their athletic potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating elite-level sporting ability; Must show commitment to competitive sport; Applicants must be assessed through trials or demonstration of sporting achievement; Open to students in Years 7-12
special_conditions: Participants in the Elite Sports Program are expected to maintain academic standards. Scholarship subject to satisfactory conduct and continued commitment to the College. Continued participation contingent on maintaining training standards and representing the College appropriately.

overview: Academic Challenge and Enrichment Studies (ACES) is a program designed to enhance the education of students who require extension outside of the classroom. Identified students collaborate with like-minded peers in a positive environment to apply 21st Century learning skills.
description: The ACES program is designed for identified students in Years 7-9 who require academic extension and enrichment beyond the standard classroom curriculum. Students engage in two hours per week of structured learning modules that encompass all learning areas and enable them to develop problem-solving and research skills. The program aims to enhance student creativity, critical thinking, and problem-solving abilities through engagement with various academic topics and competitions. Students work with expert staff mentors and have opportunities to participate in competitions and programs with students from other schools, enabling them to practise their skills in differentiated and extensive learning environments. The ACES program provides identified students with a pathway to develop academic excellence and engage in deeper learning that challenges them appropriately. Participants benefit from collaboration with talented peers and exposure to extension learning opportunities that enrich their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to identified students requiring academic extension; Typically Years 7-9 level; Must demonstrate capability for advanced learning; Selection based on academic performance and teacher recommendation
special_conditions: Students identified for ACES are selected through the College's identification process. Program requires commitment to two hours per week of extension learning. Continuation in the program is contingent on engagement and achievement.

overview: Busselton Senior High School offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and potential. These scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 and support academic excellence in secondary education.
description: The Academic Scholarship programme is designed to support students who have demonstrated outstanding academic performance at primary school. Scholarships are offered as part of the school's Year 7 enrolment process, with application packs made available during the annual parent information nights held early in the year. Selection is based on academic merit and achievement. The scholarship provides financial support to families, with specific fee remission details available upon application. The exact financial value and renewal conditions are outlined in individual scholarship agreements with successful applicants. Applicants are assessed through school performance records and demonstrated academic capability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6 at primary school; Demonstrated academic excellence in primary school; Available through school enrolment process
special_conditions: Scholarship applications are included in enrolment packs distributed at parent information nights early in the year. Specific financial benefits and renewal conditions are contained within individual scholarship agreements.

overview: Busselton Senior High School offers Music Scholarships to support talented young musicians through the Geographe Bay Music Program. The programme is designed to nurture musical excellence and provide opportunities for students demonstrating significant instrumental or vocal ability.
description: The Music Scholarship is part of Busselton Senior High School's Geographe Bay Music Program (GBM), which is recognised as the premier music programme in the South West region of Western Australia. The programme is unique as it operates across multiple secondary school sites, including Busselton Senior High School and Cape Naturaliste College. Students can learn instruments including contemporary guitar, bass guitar, saxophone, percussion and others through the Instrumental Music School Services (IMSS). New students have the opportunity to enter the programme at Year 7 level by completing testing. The programme has won numerous awards including the Queens Cup and Silver Award at the Sydney International Music Festival. Financial support details are available from the Music Coordinator.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate musical ability through testing; Participation in instrumental music or vocal performance; Suitable for students involved in the IMSS programme or private instrumental study
special_conditions: Students previously involved in IMSS instrumental lessons in primary school are automatically included in the GBM programme. New students must complete testing to be considered. Applications are included in enrolment packs. For further information contact Liz Yull (Music Coordinator) at liza.yull@education.wa.edu.au.

overview: Busselton Senior High School offers Indigenous Scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in achieving their educational goals. The school is committed to improving outcomes for Indigenous students through the Waalitj Kaaditjin Academy, which provides targeted support and development.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship programme supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from Year 7 through Year 12. The school's Waalitj Kaaditjin Academy provides tailored support to meet the unique needs of Indigenous students, including personal development, activities and experiences in cultural, employment readiness, organisational and leadership development. The Academy offers opportunities for students to grow self-esteem, develop work readiness and employability skills, and strengthen connections to culture and community, particularly on country with local Wadandi custodians. Financial support through scholarships helps eligible students access education at Busselton Senior High School. The programme explores themes of culture, language, environment, conversation, health, wellbeing, sports, recreation, family connections and community.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent; Open to students entering Year 7 through Year 12; Available to local students and those requiring cross-boundary enrolment; Selection based on academic progress, leadership qualities, school attendance and commitment to tertiary study
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded based on current academic progress, leadership qualities, school attendance and interest in tertiary study. The Stars Foundation programme provides additional support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander female students. Contact the school for application details and to apply for the Waalitj Kaaditjin Academy scholarship.

overview: Busselton Senior High School's Waalitj Kaaditjin Academy is a specialised program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students focused on developing leadership, cultural connection and pathways to employment and further education. The academy supports students in achieving their personal and academic goals through targeted mentoring and community engagement.
description: The Waalitj Kaaditjin Academy ('WK' - a reference to the totemic eagle and a quest for knowledge) is a structured support programme designed specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at Busselton Senior High School. The Academy provides comprehensive support across multiple dimensions of student development including personal growth, cultural connection, employment readiness and leadership skills. Students participate in structured activities both within and outside the school environment, with particular emphasis on connection to country and engagement with local Wadandi custodians. The programme explores themes of culture, language, environment, conversation, health, wellbeing, sports, recreation, family connections and community. This comprehensive approach aims to improve educational outcomes and prepare students for successful transitions to further education or employment.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Entry available from Year 7 through Year 12; Students interested in leadership development and cultural engagement; Application through school enrolment process or cross-boundary applications
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: To apply for the Waalitj Kaaditjin Academy please contact busselton.shs@education.wa.edu.au. The programme works in partnership with local Wadandi custodians and community leaders. Students are expected to engage actively in cultural, community and vocational activities.

overview: Busselton Senior High School hosts the Stars Foundation programme, which provides targeted personal development and support specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander female students. The programme offers activities, experiences and mentoring from Year 7 through Year 12 to promote positive education, employment and life outcomes.
description: The Stars Foundation programme at Busselton Senior High School is specifically designed to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls and young women from Year 7 through Year 12. The programme is tailored to meet the unique needs and circumstances of Indigenous female students, with a focus on building life skills, confidence and connections to culture and community. Students participate in personal development activities, community engagement, cultural programmes and volunteering opportunities. Stars Foundation staff work directly with students to support their involvement in activities that develop their capabilities, build their self-esteem and strengthen their educational commitment. The programme recognises the importance of cultural connection and community involvement in supporting positive educational and life outcomes for Indigenous young women.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander female students; Year 7 through Year 12; Commitment to participation in personal development and community activities; Living within school catchment or approved cross-boundary area
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Programme is tailored to the unique needs of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls. Staff support students to build life skills, confidence and cultural connections. Students are expected to participate actively in community, cultural and volunteering activities as part of the programme.

overview: The Geographe Bay Music Program (GBM) is Busselton Senior High School's flagship specialist music programme, recognised as the premier music school choice in the South West region. The programme develops young musicians through instrumental learning, ensemble participation and performance opportunities.
description: The Geographe Bay Music Program (GBM) is a unique cross-school collaborative initiative operating at Busselton Senior High School and Cape Naturaliste College, recognised by the Department of Education's Instrumental Music School Services (IMSS) as the flagship for collaboration in the region. Students have opportunities to learn instruments including contemporary guitar, bass guitar, saxophone, percussion and traditional orchestral instruments. Participants engage in multiple musical ensembles ranging from orchestras and bands to jazz groups. The programme has achieved significant recognition, including Queens Cup (all sections entered), Silver Award at the Sydney International Music Festival, and Top Jazz Band at the State Band Festival. Groups perform regularly at local and state venues, and receive invitations to perform at the OPUS Concert showcasing top performers from public schools throughout Western Australia. Students previously in IMSS primary programmes are automatically included, while new students at Year 7 may apply through testing.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7; Previously involved in IMSS primary instrumental programme (automatic inclusion) OR new students demonstrating musical ability through testing; Interest in learning orchestral or contemporary instruments; Commitment to ensemble participation
special_conditions: Students from IMSS programmes are automatically enrolled. New students must complete testing for entry at Year 7. Application details are available in Year 7 enrolment packs. Students are expected to maintain active participation in ensembles and support the programme's performance schedule. Contact Liz Yull (Music Coordinator) at liza.yull@education.wa.edu.au for further information.

overview: The Academic Extension Program is a challenging selective pathway designed for Year 7-10 students who are curious, motivated and eager to challenge themselves. The program provides an enriched learning experience focusing on extension in Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities and Social Sciences.
description: The Academic Extension Program (AEP) is designed for students in Years 7-10 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and learning capacity. Students engage with enriched curriculum content, complex thinking, problem-solving and deeper exploration of ideas within a supportive and challenging environment. The program features extension in MESH learning areas (Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities and Social Sciences) and includes structured cross-curricular projects that develop leadership, resilience, confidence and community engagement. Selection is based on academic achievement, demonstrated learning capacity and commitment to the program. Continued participation is dependent upon maintaining achievement in line with program expectations and Good Standing. No financial benefit is provided as this is a public school selective pathway program.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Years 7, 8, 9, or 10; Must demonstrate academic achievement; Must demonstrate learning capacity; Must commit to the program; Selection based on performance in Academic Extension Program testing
special_conditions: Continued participation is dependent upon maintaining achievement in line with program expectations and Good Standing. Registrations for Year 7 testing open during 2025 for 2026 entry and ongoing registrations for subsequent years.

overview: The Netball Academy is a Netball WA Tier 1 Endorsed Specialist Program available to Year 7-10 students. Butler College hosts Perth metro's northernmost Tier 1 Netball WA Endorsed Program, one of eight Tier 1 schools in Western Australia.
description: The Netball Academy is a year-long elective program for Years 7-10 designed for highly motivated netball players with a positive attitude and commitment to developing their skills. Students engage in four lessons per week focusing on skill development, fitness, tactical understanding and theory units including nutrition, injury prevention, team culture, leadership, sports psychology and goal setting. The program follows the Netball Australia Skills Development Framework and provides officiating and coaching accreditations. Selection is based on performance in trials held periodically throughout the year. The program is open to all gender identities. No direct financial benefit is provided; however, this is a highly subsidised public school program.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Years 7, 8, 9, or 10; Highly motivated netball player; Positive attitude; Commitment to developing skills; Must pass trials; Open to all gender identities
special_conditions: Trials are run periodically throughout the year. Students must register for trials. Participation involves four lessons per week as a year-long elective. Program includes official Netball WA endorsement and recognition.

overview: The Soccer Academy is a specialist program for Year 7-10 students designed to cultivate well-rounded players with leadership qualities and a deep understanding of the game. The program provides professional training with state-of-the-art resources.
description: The Soccer Academy is a year-long elective program for Years 7-10 offering highly motivated soccer players the opportunity to develop and excel. Students engage in four lessons per week focusing on skill development, fitness, tactical understanding and theory units including nutrition, injury prevention, team culture, leadership, sports psychology and goal setting. The program provides professional training aligned with Football Federation Australia and Football West guidelines, with access to full-sized pitches and advanced facilities. Students can pursue refereeing and coaching certifications. Selection is based on performance in trials held periodically throughout the year. The program is open to all gender identities. No direct financial benefit is provided as this is a public school program.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Years 7, 8, 9, or 10; Highly motivated soccer player; Positive attitude; Commitment to developing skills; Must pass trials; Open to all gender identities
special_conditions: Trials are run periodically throughout the year. Students must register for trials. Participation involves four lessons per week as a year-long elective. Students can pursue refereeing and coaching certifications as part of the program.

overview: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is a Western Australian State Government program that provides eligible families with financial assistance towards school-related costs. Applications are open during Term 1 at Butler College.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is a government-funded financial assistance program available to eligible families with secondary school students. Eligible families can claim up to $350 per child for approved school-related expenses including uniforms, textbooks and other educational costs. Applications are administered through Butler College and must be submitted during specific timeframes. Applications open on the first day of Term 1 and close on the last day of Term 1 with no exceptions. This is a needs-based program administered by the Western Australian Department of Education for eligible families. The financial benefit is up to $350 per eligible child per year.
eligibility_criteria: Families must be eligible as determined by WA Government criteria; Must apply during Term 1 application window; Must be current families at Butler College; Claim value up to $350 per child
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications open on the first day of Term 1 and close on the last day of Term 1 with no exceptions. This is a State Government program managed through the school. Eligible families should contact the school for specific eligibility criteria and application procedures. Claim amount is up to $350 per eligible child per year.

program_name: Westpac Bicentennial Foundation Young Technologists Scholarships
overview: Clarkson Community High School promotes the Westpac Bicentennial Foundation Young Technologists Scholarships for current Year 12 students with a passion for technology and innovation. These merit-based awards aim to strengthen Australia's pool of technology talent by encouraging greater diversity and broader participation in technology-related studies.
description: The Westpac Bicentennial Foundation Young Technologists Scholarships are awarded on merit to current Year 12 school leavers who demonstrate a passion for bringing about change through cutting-edge technology and innovation. Scholarship winners will receive $5,000 per year for the duration of their course. The program is designed to strengthen Australia's pool of technology talent by encouraging greater diversity and broader participation in technology-related studies. These scholarships support students transitioning into tertiary education in technology fields, with selection based on demonstrated merit and commitment to the technology sector.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 12 students; Passion for technology and innovation; Planning to pursue tertiary education in technology-related fields; Selection on merit basis
special_conditions: Scholarship value applies for the duration of the recipient's tertiary course. Award is subject to meeting merit criteria and continuing enrollment in eligible technology program.

school_name: Cocos Islands District High School - Home Island Campus

overview: <cite index="41-12">The Achieve Pathway is an academic enrichment program that brings together like-minded, high achieving students to accelerate their learning across all areas of the curriculum while fostering the development of their social and emotional needs.</cite> <cite index="41-1,41-2">Places in the Achieve Pathway are selective, and places are highly sought after. Students are selected through a school run test, with recommendation from their Primary School teachers and consultation of previous schooling data.</cite>
description: <cite index="41-12,41-13">The Achieve Pathway is an academic enrichment program that brings together like-minded, high achieving students to accelerate their learning across all areas of the curriculum while fostering the development of their social and emotional needs. This pathway will provide students with a learning environment that will both engage and extend them, allowing them to explore the curriculum in more detail.</cite> <cite index="41-14,41-15">Students are asked to be creative and imaginative, to be a problem solver and critical thinker, encouraging them to think outside the box. A range of teaching strategies, such as inquiry based learning, team work, goal setting and having a growth mindset will be used to ensure students are engaged in their learning outcomes.</cite> <cite index="53-3,53-4">Maintaining a place in the Achieve Pathway at Coastal Lakes College is based on ongoing assessment conducted throughout the year. Student performance is reviewed regularly each term with achievement and behaviour expected to be in line with pathway expectations and maintaining Good Standing in the School.</cite> No monetary value is offered with this program; it is an enrichment pathway only. <cite index="53-1">Students are selected through a school run test, with recommendation from their primary school teachers and consultation of previous schooling data.</cite>
eligibility_criteria: <cite index="53-6">For your application to be considered we require copies of your school reports from the last two years, a copy of your Year 5 NAPLAN results and a signature from your current Primary School teacher.</cite> Open to students entering Year 7; High academic performance expected; Interview and testing required; Maintain Good Standing throughout enrolment
special_conditions: <cite index="53-3,53-4">Maintaining a place in the Achieve Pathway at Coastal Lakes College is based on ongoing assessment conducted throughout the year. Student performance is reviewed regularly each term with achievement and behaviour expected to be in line with pathway expectations and maintaining Good Standing in the School.</cite>

overview: <cite index="31-1,31-2">The State Government, through the Department of Education, provides financial support to eligible parents through the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS). The aim of this scheme is to assist low-income families with secondary schooling costs.</cite> This government program is available to students at Coastal Lakes College to help eligible families with schooling costs.
description: <cite index="32-1">The Secondary Assistance Program is available to assist low income families with secondary schooling costs (Year 7 to 12).</cite> <cite index="32-2">Applications must be submitted in Term 1.</cite> <cite index="31-10">Families not in a position to pay charges and contributions all at one time can make alternative arrangements for payment, such as government financial assistance or enter into a payment plan.</cite> The financial value of this scheme varies based on individual circumstances and government eligibility criteria. <cite index="32-9">The card or electronic version must be presented to College Administration to complete an online application.</cite>
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 to Year 12 students; Low-income family eligibility required; Parent/guardian must hold specified government concession cards; Must apply in Term 1; Contact college for eligibility criteria on 9583 2800
special_conditions: <cite index="32-2">Applications must be submitted in Term 1.</cite> Applications must be made in person at the College Administration office.

overview: Clontarf Aboriginal College offers the Noongar Boarding Scholarship to support families living on Noongar Boodja who are unable to access Abstudy's Living Away from Home Allowance. The scholarship assists eligible Indigenous students to reside at the college's Ngulla Mia boarding facility.
description: The Noongar Boarding Scholarship is designed to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from the Noongar region who wish to board at Clontarf Aboriginal College's Ngulla Mia boarding facility. The scholarship recognises that many families within the Noongar area cannot access Abstudy's Living Away from Home Allowance due to the availability of multiple schools in the region. The exact financial value and duration are not publicly specified, but the scholarship covers boarding costs. Scholarships are available for new and existing families of all year groups. Applications are typically invited via the college's website and social media channels, with specific deadlines communicated annually.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Families living on Noongar Boodja unable to access Abstudy Living Away from Home Allowance; Open to all year groups (7-12); New and existing students eligible
special_conditions: Scholarship is for boarding purposes only, intended for families who cannot access Abstudy Living Away from Home Allowance. Applications and specific terms are managed through the college directly.

overview: Clontarf Aboriginal College partners with MADALAH to offer scholarships assisting with tuition and boarding costs for First Nations students. MADALAH is a not-for-profit organisation that supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from regional, rural and remote communities.
description: MADALAH scholarships at Clontarf Aboriginal College assist with both tuition and boarding fees for eligible First Nations students. Administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA), these scholarships are available to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in Years 7-12. The scholarships work in conjunction with Abstudy and parent contributions to cover full costs of tuition and boarding. Selection is based on academic progress, leadership qualities, school attendance, and demonstrated disadvantage (financial and/or geographic). Applications require proof of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent and evidence of eligibility for Abstudy or demonstrated disadvantage.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent with proof; Eligible for Abstudy or demonstrated financial/geographic disadvantage; Good school attendance and behaviour record; Years 7-12; Home location in Western Australia; Recent academic results required; One referee letter required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships administered by MADALAH Limited. Applications for secondary scholarships commencing 2026 closed; applications for 2027 reopened November 3, 2025. Recipients must maintain good academic and behaviour standards. Requires co-application with MADALAH directly.

overview: Clontarf Aboriginal College is approved to offer Abstudy Boarding School Scholarships to assist Indigenous students with tuition and boarding costs. Abstudy is a government-funded scheme providing financial assistance to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students.
description: The Abstudy Boarding School Scholarship is a government-funded scheme that assists Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students attending Clontarf Aboriginal College with tuition and boarding expenses at the college's Ngulla Mia boarding facility. Abstudy provides financial support to eligible Indigenous students living away from home for boarding purposes. The Living Away from Home Allowance is a key component available to students who meet specific residency requirements. The college encourages boarding applicants to apply for Abstudy as soon as possible to expedite processing.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Enrolled at Clontarf Aboriginal College; Meet Abstudy residency and income requirements; Living away from home for boarding purposes
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Administered through Australian government Abstudy scheme. Applications should be submitted as soon as possible to quicken boarding application processing. Separate from school-based scholarships.

overview: Clontarf Aboriginal College offers a limited amount of scholarship funding available at the beginning of each school year to support eligible Indigenous students. The college provides financial assistance to families demonstrating need.
description: Clontarf Aboriginal College maintains a limited pool of scholarship funding available at the beginning of each school year for eligible students. While specific details of these scholarships are not extensively published online, the college indicates that a limited amount of scholarship money is available annually. Prospective families are advised to contact the Principal for detailed information regarding specific scholarship opportunities, eligibility criteria, and application processes. These scholarships are distinct from Abstudy and MADALAH programs.
eligibility_criteria: Indigenous Australian students; Demonstrated financial need or other qualifying circumstances; Years 7-12; Contact Principal for specific criteria
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Limited funding available at the beginning of each school year. Prospective applicants must speak directly with the Principal for further information regarding availability and specific terms.

program_name: Secondary Schooling Scholarship (Kent Street, Como, Cannington, Sevenoaks)
overview: Como Secondary College is one of four eligible Western Australian public secondary schools for the Department of Education's Secondary Schooling Scholarship. This scholarship recognises students with demonstrated academic achievement and provides financial support for secondary education.
description: The Secondary Schooling Scholarship is available to students attending Kent Street Senior High School, Como Secondary College, Cannington Community College or Sevenoaks Senior College. The scholarship provides financial support for eligible students, with the value and specific amount varying based on individual circumstances. The program may be renewed for a second year provided academic and other assessment criteria have been met and the student remains enrolled at one of the eligible schools. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and adhere to school conduct standards throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students attending Como Secondary College; Australian citizenship or permanent resident status required; Currently in Years 6 to 11; Must be attending a public school; Academic performance assessed by selection committee; Subject to financial assessment
special_conditions: Scholarship may be renewed for a second year providing academic and other assessment criteria have been met and the student remains enrolled at one of the eligible schools. Parents or carers must certify financial information is correct. Selection process involves assessment in February and April by a selection committee after school enrolment census.

overview: Cornerstone Christian College offers a sibling discount to reduce tuition costs for families with multiple children enrolled. The program recognises the financial commitment of families educating multiple children at the College by waiving tuition fees for the third and subsequent children.
description: Cornerstone Christian College implements a family-friendly sibling discount program as part of its commitment to making quality Christian education affordable for families in the City of Busselton. Under this program, there is no additional tuition fee charged for the third and subsequent children in each family enrolled at the College. This means that families with three or more children attending the College simultaneously will only pay tuition for their first two children, with all additional siblings receiving free tuition. The program applies to all year levels and campuses. Families must have children concurrently enrolled at the College to qualify for this benefit. This initiative helps reduce the overall financial burden on families with multiple children seeking a Christian education.
eligibility_criteria: Third and subsequent children in each family; Siblings must be concurrently enrolled at the College; Open to all families regardless of year level
special_conditions: Siblings must be concurrently enrolled. Fee remission applies to third and subsequent children only. First and second children pay full tuition fees.

overview: Corpus Christi College offers the Music Bursary to support strong instrumental musicians to fill key positions within the College's existing ensembles. The bursary recognises musical excellence and provides financial assistance to further students' musical journey at the College.
description: The Music Bursary is designed to support passionate instrumental musicians who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment to music. Successful applicants receive financial support to continue their musical development within the College's ensemble programs, which include a range of groups such as symphony orchestra, concert band, guitar ensemble, percussion ensemble, saxophone ensemble, flute ensemble, choir, and various other musical ensembles. The bursaries are offered for specific instruments identified as priority by the College. The selection process involves completing an application form and participating in an audition or interview. Recipients are required to actively participate in the allocated ensemble and contribute to the College's musical community. The financial value and duration of the bursary are determined based on the College's assessment of need and musical merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolling for Years 7 and above; Must demonstrate strong instrumental music ability; Selected bursaries available for specific instruments; Students willing to become involved in College co-curricular activities; Expression of interest form required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Successful applicants must support the College ethos and actively participate in allocated music ensemble. Recipients are expected to demonstrate continued commitment to the music program. The bursary is subject to satisfactory academic progress and good behaviour. Participation in designated music ensembles is mandatory.

overview: Corpus Christi College offers the Aboriginal Student Bursary to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families seeking a Catholic education. The bursary covers the full cost of tuition fees and demonstrates the College's commitment to providing broad career opportunities for young Aboriginal people.
description: The Aboriginal Student Bursary provides comprehensive financial assistance to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students and their families. The bursary covers the full cost of tuition fees, recognising the College's commitment to removing financial barriers and working with Aboriginal communities to provide quality education and career opportunities. Students benefit from dedicated support mechanisms including individual mentoring from an appointed Aboriginal Student Mentor, coordination of individualised education plans where necessary, and direct connections with parents and guardians. The College provides strong links between Aboriginal families and school staff to facilitate successful transitions and ongoing support. Recipients' academic progress and overall participation are reviewed by the relevant Deputy Principal at the end of each semester. The selection process considers financial status, the family and student's desire for Catholic education, and willingness to participate in co-curricular activities. Applications require proof of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent and completion of the dedicated application form with supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Must provide proof of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Open to all year levels; Applicant and family must have strong desire for Catholic education; Financial status of applicant and family may be considered; Applicant should be willing to participate in College co-curricular activities in accordance with College ethos; Parents with Health Care Card should provide copy with application
special_conditions: No application fees apply. Instrumental music tuition is not included in the bursary and remains an additional cost at $64.00 per hour. Uniforms and books are the responsibility of parents/guardians, though the College operates a new and second-hand clothing shop on-site. Compulsory charges such as building levies remain the responsibility of parents. In circumstances of continued bad behaviour or lack of effort in studies, the Student Bursary may be withdrawn following clear warnings to parents/guardians. The College operates a new and second-hand uniform shop on-site to assist families.

overview: The Secondary Assistance Scheme provides financial assistance to low-income families with children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 at Cunderdin District High School. The scheme supports eligible families to meet secondary schooling costs through two components paid directly to support school expenses.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a Western Australian Department of Education program that provides financial support to eligible low-income families with secondary students. For the 2026 school year, the scheme provides an Education Program Allowance of $235 paid directly to the school and a Clothing Allowance of $115 paid to the parent's nominated bank account (or to the school if the parent chooses). Applicants must hold a Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs card that represents a statement of income for the family. Applications are made by the parent or guardian and must be submitted to the school in Term 1 only. The application deadline for 2026 is 2 April 2026. This is an annual scheme and applications do not carry forward to future years.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/guardian must hold a Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs card that represents a statement of income for the family; Child must be enrolled in Years 7 to 12 studying a full-time secondary course; Applications accepted only in Term 1 each year; Students who have their own health care card but are dependent on their parent or guardian are not eligible
value_aud: $235 Education Program Allowance plus $115 Clothing Allowance
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted each school year (annually) and applications do not carry forward to future years. Applicants must provide proof of eligible concession card. For public school students, the clothing allowance can be paid toward the school for other expenses at their option. Speak to the school office to discuss eligibility and obtain application forms.

overview: The WA Student Assistance Payment is a state government initiative providing cost-of-living support to families with children enrolled in Cunderdin District High School. All eligible families with school-aged children can claim financial assistance to help cover school-related expenses.
description: The WA Student Assistance Payment (WASAP) is a Western Australian government initiative providing annual cost-of-living payments to support families with school expenses. For 2025, eligible parents and carers can claim $250 per secondary student (Year 7-12) and $150 per primary student. Claims are made through the ServiceWA app and do not require proof of financial hardship. All WA school students with active enrolment at a public school, non-government school, or registered for home education are eligible. Claims open on 28 April each year with an application deadline of 28 June. The payment is designed to help cover costs such as books, uniforms, stationery, school camps, and other educational expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Child must be enrolled in Kindergarten to Year 12 at Cunderdin District High School; Child must have active enrolment at a public school, non-government school, or be registered for home education; Claimant must be the primary care giver (parent, guardian, or carer) responsible for the eligible student; No income test required
special_conditions: Claims are made through the ServiceWA app or via alternative claiming methods at education.wa.edu.au/wasap/questions-and-support. Payment is annual and applies to the current school year only. No prior application carries forward. This is a cost-of-living payment not tied to academic merit or financial hardship.

overview: PEAC is an integral part of education for academically talented students in WA primary schools. The program provides an opportunity for talented children in specific areas to come together with their peers and undertake extension learning activities.
description: PEAC (Primary Extension and Challenge Courses) programs cater for primary school students from Years 5-6 who display talents in subjects and particular topics. Children attend PEAC classes at various centres once a week to undertake extension learning activities. Year 4 students are tested during Term 2 each year, and these results are used as one source of information about a child's ability to perform at PEAC. Students whose families are nominated receive notification of course outlines and a student nomination form on which they indicate their course choices in preferential order. The cost for PEAC participation ranges from $5 to $300 per year depending on the course selected.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 4 students for nomination and testing; Display talents in specific subjects and topics; Year 5 and Year 6 enrollment for participation; Must be tested during Term 2 of Year 4; Testing results used to determine eligibility
special_conditions: Parents whose children are nominated for PEAC courses receive notification of course outlines and a student nomination form on which they indicate their course choices in preferential order. These are returned to school and processed and forwarded to District PEAC organisers for final selection. Participation involves attending PEAC classes at various centres once a week.

school_name: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus Education Support Centre
program_name: STS Leeuwin II Ocean Adventure Scholarship
overview: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus offers scholarships through the Leeuwin Ocean Adventure Foundation and McCusker Charitable Foundation to selected students from culturally and linguistically diverse (CALD) communities. The scholarship provides an opportunity to experience life at sea on a tall ship adventure.
description: This scholarship program provides full funding for students to participate in a 7-day adventure aboard The STS Leeuwin II, a historic tall ship. The program involves extensive application and selection process, with approximately 9-10 students selected from Cyril Jackson Senior Campus annually. The scholarship covers all costs associated with the ocean adventure experience. Students participate in 'watch' groups and learn teamwork, safety protocols, and maritime skills while supporting the CALD community at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Students from culturally and linguistically diverse (CALD) backgrounds; Currently enrolled at Cyril Jackson Senior Campus; Open to Year 11 and Year 12 students; Selected through extensive application process
special_conditions: Students selected through an extensive application process. Scholarship is sponsored by Leeuwin Ocean Adventure Foundation and McCusker Charitable Foundation. Targeted at CALD community students.

school_name: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus Education Support Centre
program_name: Wesley College Jobs for Jackson Scholarship
overview: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus receives scholarship funding through the Wesley College 'Jobs for Jackson' initiative. Wesley College boys fundraise throughout the year and allocate earnings to support deserving Cyril Jackson students.
description: This scholarship program demonstrates community partnerships between Wesley College and Cyril Jackson Senior Campus.
