Wesley College students actively participate in the scholarships process by reviewing applications and interviewing candidates. The boys fundraise throughout the year via the 'Jobs for Jackson' initiative and donate their earnings to provide scholarships to worthy Cyril Jackson recipients. The specific financial value of individual awards is not specified, but scholarships are awarded based on demonstrated need and merit.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Cyril Jackson Senior Campus; Demonstrated financial need; Selected through application and interview process; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Awarded through partnership between Wesley College and Cyril Jackson Senior Campus. Wesley College students directly participate in selection process through application review and interviews. Recipients must demonstrate gratitude and engagement with the scholarship process.

overview: Court Grammar School offers Academic Scholarships to reward students demonstrating exceptional academic ability. Available to students entering Year 5, 7, and 10, these scholarships recognise outstanding achievement and commitment to learning through rigorous academic testing.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to students entering Year 5, 7 and 10 at CGS at the beginning of the school year and will apply for the duration of the student's time in their relevant sub-School. The scholarship provides up to 50% reduction of tuition fees and is awarded annually to the highest achieving student in each applicable entry level who meets the academic criteria. Applicants will be required to sit an academic test provided by a national testing body designed to identify academically able students. The test assesses skills in interpretation, inference, deduction and critical thinking rather than curriculum knowledge. The scholarship requires ongoing acceptable academic achievement which will be reviewed annually. Application fee is $100 per student.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current Court Grammar School students and potential future students; Students in Year 4, 6, and 9 in 2025 can apply for 2026 entry; Must demonstrate top academic ability; Must meet academic criteria; Ongoing acceptable academic achievement required
special_conditions: Scholarship requires ongoing acceptable academic achievement which will be reviewed annually. Tests are provided by a national testing body and results remain the property of the School with no disputes entered into.

overview: Court Grammar School provides the Nijole Natalija Weedon Bursary to support Indigenous and refugee students commencing secondary education. This bursary recognises the importance of educational access and cultural diversity within the school community.
description: This bursary provides up to $5,000 fee assistance for student fees per year to an Indigenous or refugee student commencing at Court Grammar School in any of the Secondary School years 7-12. The bursary is designed to remove financial barriers to education for students from these communities. Recipients must be commencing new enrolment in Years 7-12. The bursary is awarded based on individual criteria and at the discretion of the Principal. Ongoing assessment and review of the recipient's achievement and attitude to learning is required.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Indigenous or refugee student; Commencing at Court Grammar School; Entering Years 7-12; Secondary school enrolment
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal and are subject to ongoing assessment and review of achievement and attitude to learning.

overview: Court Grammar School offers the Primary Student Instrumental Music Scholarship to recognise and develop talented young musicians. This scholarship supports students entering Year 5 who demonstrate exceptional ability and potential in musical performance.
description: The Primary Student Instrumental Music Scholarship is available to students entering Year 5 at CGS at the beginning of the school year and will apply for the duration of the student's time in Primary School. The scholarship recognises talented young musicians who display exceptional ability in instrumental music. Selection is based on audition, performance assessment and potential. The exact financial value and duration are subject to individual criteria determined by the school. Recipients are expected to actively participate in the school's music program and maintain high standards of musical achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5; Must demonstrate instrumental music ability; Audition required; Open to current and future students; Outstanding musical potential required
special_conditions: Applications are currently open. Audition required for selection. Award is based on individual criteria at the discretion of the Principal and subject to ongoing assessment of musical achievement.

overview: Court Grammar School offers Female Elite Scholarships to recognise outstanding female athletes entering Year 7. These scholarships support girls highly competent in netball, basketball, and athletics, providing pathways to specialist sporting academies.
description: Year 7 girls who are highly competent in netball, basketball and athletics are encouraged to apply for a Female Elite Scholarship. Applicants will be considered for a Scholarship based on performance at trials, reference from their club and/or coach, and an interview with the School Principal. Once accepted, students will also have the option to join the highly sought-after AFLW Academy timetabled class. The scholarship recognises elite sporting achievement and commitment. Recipients must maintain high sporting standards and participate in mandatory co-curricular training sessions. Students are ineligible to hold more than one sports-related scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 girls only; Must be highly competent in netball, basketball or athletics; Currently playing at association representative level (netball), WABL level (basketball), or state level (athletics); Performance at trials required; Coach/club reference required; Interview with Principal required
special_conditions: Students are ineligible to hold more than 1 sports-related scholarship. Netball scholarship recipients must participate in ongoing co-curricular training sessions held before school every Thursday in Terms 1 and 2. Basketball scholarship recipients must participate in basketball co-curricular training sessions on Thursday mornings in Terms 3 and 4. Athletics scholarship recipients must participate in all athletics events including cross-country. Receipt of scholarship allows optional participation in AFLW Academy.

overview: Court Grammar School's AFL/W Academy Scholarships recognise talented male and female students pursuing excellence in Australian Rules Football. These scholarships are awarded across each year cohort on an annual basis to top-performing students.
description: The Court Grammar Cavaliers AFL/W Academy provides a unique opportunity for male and female students to pursue excellence in Australian Rules Football and embark on a journey of personal growth and development. The Specialist AFL Program, established in 2017, gives talented Year 5 to 10 students the opportunity to further develop their footballing ability through skill development, gameplay strategies and tactics and theoretical understanding. In Year 11 & 12 students are provided with the option to further improve their understanding of the AFL and Sport industry through our Certificate II in Sport Coaching & Certificate III in Sport, Recreation & Aquatic. Scholarships are awarded across each year cohort on a 12-monthly basis to students who rank top of their class.
eligibility_criteria: Boys in Year 6 can apply in 2025 for 2026 intake; Girls in Year 6 can apply in 2025 for 2026 intake; Must demonstrate top class ranking; Must show talent and commitment to Australian Rules Football; Selection based on merit
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded annually to students ranking top of their class. Selection based on individual merit and criteria determined by the Principal. Continuation is subject to ongoing assessment of academic and sporting achievement and attitude to learning.

overview: Court Grammar School offers the Ricky Grace Basketball Specialist Program to identify and develop elite basketball players. This program, led by basketball legend Ricky Grace, provides specialised coaching and pathways for highly skilled athletes.
description: The Ricky Grace Basketball Specialist Program provides elite basketball training and coaching for highly skilled basketballers at Court Grammar School. Year 8-10 students can trial for the Ricky Grace Basketball Academy (2025 trial for 2025 onwards). The program is coached by Ricky Grace, a Perth Wildcats legend, who runs elite basketball training sessions year-round, once a week, for the school's highly skilled basketballers. The specialist program aims to support basketballers with goals to become better in their Domestic or WABL competition or to be eventual participants in the American high school, college system, or pro basketball. Recipients receive specialised coaching and are integrated into the school's broader basketball development pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8-10 entry; Must be WABL level player or equivalent; High basketball competency required; Trial-based selection; Must be capable of professional-level development
special_conditions: Year 8-10 trial in Term 2, 2025 ONLY for 2025 onwards participation. Scholarship holders must maintain elite basketball standards and participate in all required training sessions. Continuation subject to ongoing athletic and academic performance.

overview: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus provides financial assistance to eligible students through the Secondary Assistance Scheme. The program supports students from families holding Centrelink Family Health Care Cards, Pensioner Concession Cards, or Veteran's Affairs Pensioner Concession Cards, helping to reduce financial barriers to education.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme provides eligible students with $535 total financial assistance per student for Year 11 and 12 students up to the age of 18. Holders of a current Centrelink Family Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card or Veteran's Affairs Pensioner Concession Card are able to apply for this financial assistance program. Application forms are available from the school's website and also from Reception. The funds are designed to help offset the costs of school materials, stationery, and other educational expenses. Applications must be submitted by the stated deadline for each year.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be in Year 11 or Year 12; Student must be up to the age of 18; Family must hold a current Centrelink Family Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veteran's Affairs Pensioner Concession Card
special_conditions: Eligibility limited to holders of specified government concession cards. Application forms available from school Reception or website. Applications must be submitted by April 11th for the relevant year.

program_name: STS Leeuwin II Ocean Adventure Scholarships
overview: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus offers scholarships providing opportunities for selected students to experience life at sea aboard the STS Leeuwin II tall ship. These scholarships support students from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds and are awarded through an extensive application process.
description: This competitive scholarship program provides carefully selected students and staff with the opportunity to experience a 7-day ocean adventure aboard the STS Leeuwin II, a historic 1850s-style tall ship. Selection occurs through an extensive application process designed to identify worthy candidates from the CALD (Culturally and Linguistically Diverse) community at Cyril Jackson Senior Campus. The program is funded through partnerships with the Leeuwin Ocean Adventure Foundation and the McCusker Charitable Foundation. Students gain valuable leadership and teamwork experience as they engage in the running of the ship, participate in watch groups, and develop independent learning skills in a unique educational environment. The scholarship covers all costs associated with the voyage.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Cyril Jackson Senior Campus students; Preference for students from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds; Selection through extensive application process; Must demonstrate leadership potential and readiness for the experience
special_conditions: Selection is competitive and based on an extensive application process. Program is offered periodically in partnership with external foundations. Participation requires a full week away from school during school term. Preference is given to students from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

program_name: Wesley College Jobs for Jackson Scholarship
overview: Cyril Jackson Senior Campus receives support from Wesley College boys who fundraise throughout the year via the 'Jobs for Jackson' initiative to provide scholarships to deserving students. The program demonstrates community partnership and supports students in financial need.
description: The Jobs for Jackson initiative is a unique fundraising partnership between Wesley College students and Cyril Jackson Senior Campus. Wesley College boys actively fundraise throughout the school year and take a direct role in the scholarships process, including sorting through scholarship applications and interviewing candidates. The funds raised are awarded to worthy scholarship recipients who have demonstrated need and merit. This program highlights the strong community connections between the two institutions and provides financial support to students who might otherwise face barriers to accessing quality education. The exact dollar value of scholarships awarded varies annually based on funds raised by Wesley College students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Cyril Jackson Senior Campus students; Must demonstrate financial need; Must meet selection criteria as determined by Wesley College fundraisers and Cyril Jackson staff; Selection through application and interview process
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are selected through a formal application and interview process. Recipients should be prepared to engage with Wesley College students during award presentations. The number and value of scholarships awarded varies annually based on fundraising success.

overview: Dalyellup College participates in the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) administered by the Department of Education to support low-income families with secondary schooling costs. This government program provides eligible families with financial assistance towards uniforms, contributions, and charges.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is available to low-income families with children enrolled in secondary schooling to assist with school expenses. The scheme provides up to $535 annually comprising two components: $300 Clothing Allowance and $235 Educational Program Allowance. The Clothing Allowance is paid directly to the parent/guardian unless redirected to the school, while the Educational Program Allowance is paid directly to the school and applied toward voluntary contributions for Years 7-10. Applications must be made annually as they do not carry forward to future years. Eligibility requires parents/guardians to hold a valid Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans' Affairs Concession Card (blue card issued annually, valid during First Term). Applications can be submitted to the front office or emailed to dalyellup.col@education.wa.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian must hold a valid Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Concession Card (blue card only); Card must be valid during First Term; Open to secondary school students in Years 7-12; Applications must be submitted annually
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted each school year; applications do not carry forward to future years. The only eligible Veterans' Affairs Card is the blue card issued annually and expiring in December each year. For Years 7-10, the Educational Program Allowance is applied first toward voluntary contributions. Copies of card (front and back) must be provided with application.

program_name: AMPt (Advanced Music Performance and Training Program)
overview: Dalyellup College offers the AMPt (Advanced Music Performance and Training) program as a specialist music offering for students with demonstrated musical ability and passion. The program combines in-depth theoretical instruction with intensive practical training to develop high-level performance skills.
description: The AMPt (Advanced Music Performance and Training Program) is a specialized music program combining theoretical instruction with intensive practical training. Students develop musical skills on brass and woodwind instruments, guitar, keyboard, or drums. The program includes participation in orchestras, concert bands, and chamber music ensembles that perform regularly at school events, regional concerts, and music festivals, providing valuable stage experience. Students receive tuition in music theory and performance across a range of instruments and genres. The program offers opportunities for students to mentor younger musicians as section leaders and to showcase their achievements at annual presentation evenings. No specific financial value or monetary scholarship is provided through this extension program; it is offered as part of standard curriculum enrichment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students who demonstrate musical ability and commitment; Selection based on audition and demonstrated musical performance; Interest in pursuing music study at senior school level
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: No monetary scholarship provided. This is an extension program offered as part of the standard music curriculum. Students are expected to maintain commitment to rehearsals and performances throughout their enrollment. Instrument tuition may incur separate fees not included in this program.

overview: Dalyellup College offers the STUDIO (Specialist Visual Arts) program to develop creative potential in students with demonstrated artistic talent and commitment. The program provides students with exposure to a diverse range of visual arts media and techniques across traditional and contemporary forms.
description: The STUDIO (Specialist Visual Arts) program is designed to awaken and develop creative potential by working with a wide variety of media and techniques. The program encompasses classical drawing and painting to digital art forms, sculpture, and other contemporary forms of expression. Students engage with professional artists through collaborative experiences such as incursions with the Bunbury Regional Art Gallery. Studio extension art classes for Year 7 and 8 students provide an innovative platform for young artists to explore creativity and develop advanced artistic techniques in a supportive environment for experimentation and growth. The program fosters student achievement at local and state art award levels, encouraging students to pursue their artistic passions through the senior school visual arts pathways. No monetary scholarship value is provided; this is an extension program offered as enrichment within the curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students Years 7-12 with demonstrated visual arts ability; Interest in pursuing visual arts at senior school level; Selection based on portfolio review or teacher recommendation
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: No monetary scholarship provided. This is an extension program offered as part of the standard visual arts curriculum. Studio materials and resources may incur separate fees. Students are expected to maintain commitment to the program and participate in school exhibitions and displays.

overview: Dalyellup College's Physical Education Extension Program (PEX) is an aspirational program designed for high-achieving students in Physical Education and across other learning areas. The program further develops knowledge, skills, and understanding in students who demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to sports and physical activity.
description: The Physical Education Extension Program (PEX) was implemented in 2017 for high-achieving students in Health and Physical Education who have also performed well across other learning areas of the school. This aspirational program is designed to further the knowledge, skills, and understanding of students who demonstrate the potential to become leaders in the school and advance through to senior school Physical Education courses. The program enhances and extends students in all aspects of Physical Education including applying sports science concepts in practical format (biomechanics, nutrition, exercise physiology, sports psychology), linking with outside agencies to develop coaching and umpiring qualifications, and developing leadership competencies. The course runs twice weekly and takes the place of general PE class on the timetable. The program aims to improve students' learning capacity and overall performance and success with their academic achievements. No monetary scholarship is provided; this is an extension program offered as curriculum enrichment.
eligibility_criteria: High-achieving students in Health and Physical Education; Minimum C grade or better in Physical Education; Minimum C grade in English; Demonstrated performance excellence across other learning areas; Open to secondary students Years 7-10 primarily
special_conditions: No monetary scholarship provided. This is an extension program offered as part of the standard Physical Education curriculum. Students must maintain grade requirements to continue in the program. The program is designed to prepare students for senior school PE pathways (ATAR PE Studies and General PE Studies) and potential careers in sports and recreation.

overview: Davallia Primary School offers the Primary Extension and Challenge (PEAC) program for identified gifted and talented students. The program provides extension classes delivered through the North Metropolitan Regional Office for high-achieving students in Years 5 and 6.
description: Primary Extension and Challenge (PEAC) Extension classes are offered for identified students through North Metropolitan Regional Office. Students identified through high performance as part of the PEAC Testing during Year 4 will be offered the opportunity to participate in PEAC. Students in the top 3% of all Year 4 students across the Region are typically selected. Other children may be invited to join the program through teacher nomination, should there be a suitable course offered and a course position available. Attendance at PEAC is regarded as an excursion for the students concerned, and students are required to be signed out of school when attending PEAC. The program is non-monetary and focuses on providing challenging extension learning opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 5 and 6; High performance in Year 4 PEAC Testing (approximately top 3% of Year 4 students across the North Metropolitan Region); Or identified through teacher nomination if course position available
special_conditions: Selection based on high performance in Year 4 PEAC Testing. Attendance at PEAC is regarded as an excursion and students are required to be signed out of school when attending. Teacher nomination possible if suitable course available.

school_name: Dianella Secondary College Education Support Centre

overview: Donnybrook District High School provides financial assistance to eligible families of Year 7-10 students through the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS). This government program helps low-income families meet school contribution and charge costs.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a Western Australian Government program administered by the Department of Education that provides financial assistance to eligible families with children enrolled in secondary schooling. The scheme offers assistance with voluntary contributions, charges, and other school-related expenses. Families must hold eligible cards (including but not limited to Centrelink benefits cards) and current in Term 1. Applications must be made in person at the Senior Campus administration office by the end of Term 1. The value of assistance varies depending on family circumstances and the specific charges claimed. This program is designed to ensure that financial barriers do not prevent students from accessing the full educational program and participating in school activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7-10 students; Parents/carers must hold current eligible benefits cards (as defined by Centrelink); Cards must be current in Term 1; Applications must be made in person at Senior Campus administration; Applications close at end of Term 1; Any applications received after Term 1 closure will not be accepted
special_conditions: Applicants must bring their card to the Senior Campus administration office to enable online processing. Financial assistance is calculated based on eligible school charges and contributions. The scheme covers voluntary contributions and compulsory charges for eligible students.

overview: In partnership with Talison Lithium, Donnybrook District High School offers the Year 10 STEM Scholarship to support graduating Year 10 students pursuing science, technology, engineering, and mathematics pathways. This scholarship recognises commitment to STEM education and supports students entering Year 11/12 or TAFE.
description: The Talison Lithium Year 10 STEM Scholarship is offered annually in partnership with Talison Lithium as part of their STEM Outreach Program supporting community development. The scholarship is open to current Donnybrook District High School students who are graduating from Year 10 and entering Year 11/12 or TAFE in the following year. Students pursuing or planning to pursue a STEM pathway are specifically encouraged to apply. The financial value and specific selection criteria (beyond STEM pathway focus) are determined by Talison Lithium annually. The scholarship supports students' progression into senior secondary STEM studies or vocational STEM training through TAFE. Selection is based on demonstrated commitment to STEM education and alignment with the applicant's intended pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Current Donnybrook District High School student; Must be graduating from Year 10; Entering Year 11/12 or TAFE in the following year; Pursuing or planning to pursue STEM pathway (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics)
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered annually and subject to Talison Lithium's STEM Outreach Program funding and priorities. Applicants should have demonstrated genuine interest in STEM subjects and intended pathways in STEM fields.

overview: Dianella Secondary College offers the ECU Pathway Scholarship in partnership with Edith Cowan University (ECU) to provide academic enrichment and university pathway support to select Year 6 students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises students with strong academic potential and provides access to a specialist STEM extension program with mentoring and university engagement activities.
description: The ECU Pathway Scholarship is a university partnership program offered to Year 6 students commencing Year 7 at Dianella Secondary College. Recipients receive support throughout their college journey from Year 7 to Year 12 to assist them in obtaining university entrance after Year 12. The program provides no direct financial fee reduction (as Dianella is a government school with no tuition fees), but offers substantial non-monetary benefits including: enrolment in a specialist STEM extension program; ongoing coaching and mentoring sessions with an allocated ECU mentor on site at Dianella Secondary College; visits to ECU campuses at Mount Lawley and Joondalup locations for tours, guest lectures, workshops, and hands-on activities; participation in activities conducted by ECU at both Dianella Secondary College and ECU campuses. A limited number of scholarships are offered each year. Recipients must maintain appropriate academic effort and engagement with the program.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students commencing Year 7 at Dianella Secondary College; Limited number of scholarships on offer; Students must demonstrate strong academic potential; Selection process involves merit-based consideration
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are enrolled into the specialist STEM extension program. Students are immersed in a creative environment and encouraged to find solutions to real problems both locally and globally while addressing WA and Australian curriculum requirements. A brochure is available; prospective applicants should contact Dianella Secondary College on (08) 9345 9200 or visit the front office admin.

school_name: Donnybrook District High School - Junior Campus (K-6)
overview: Donnybrook District High School, in partnership with Talison Lithium, offers the Year 10 STEM Scholarship to graduating Year 10 students entering Year 11/12 or TAFE. The scholarship recognises students committed to pursuing STEM pathways and supports their senior schooling.
description: This scholarship is offered through a partnership between Donnybrook District High School and Talison Lithium as part of their STEM Outreach Program. The scholarship is open to current Donnybrook District High School students who are graduating from Year 10 and entering Year 11/12 or TAFE in the following year. Students pursuing a STEM pathway are encouraged to apply. The selection process evaluates applications based on specified criteria related to STEM engagement and academic commitment. The scholarship supports students' transition to senior schooling and their pursuit of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics studies. Specific financial value and duration are not publicly detailed on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Current Donnybrook District High School students; Graduating Year 10; Entering Year 11/12 or TAFE; Students pursuing STEM pathway preferred
special_conditions: Partnership with Talison Lithium. Part of STEM Outreach Program.

school_name: Donnybrook District High School - Junior Campus (K-6)
overview: The Masonic Lodge Dux Award is presented by a local Masonic Lodge to recognise exceptional academic achievement at Donnybrook District High School. This award acknowledges students who demonstrate outstanding performance and leadership.
description: The Masonic Lodge Dux Award is an academic honour presented in recognition of exceptional achievement at Donnybrook District High School. The award is facilitated through a local Masonic Lodge and honours students who have demonstrated outstanding academic performance and contribution to the school. This award appears in the school's annual reporting of student achievements and recognises excellence at the conclusion of secondary schooling. The specific financial value, if any, is not detailed in publicly available school materials. The award is presented annually to recognise dux achievements.
eligibility_criteria: Exceptional academic achievement; Demonstrated leadership and contribution to school
special_conditions: Sponsored by local Masonic Lodge. Awarded for outstanding academic achievement and leadership.

program_name: Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Program
overview: Duncraig Senior High School offers the Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Program, providing an academically rigorous and supportive learning environment for exceptionally able students. The program extends and accelerates students from Years 7 to 12 in Mathematics, Science, Humanities and Social Sciences, and English. Students successful in gaining a place in this program do not need to reside within the school's intake area.
description: The Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Program at Duncraig Senior High School provides a comprehensive program extending and accelerating students from Years 7 to 12 in the areas of Mathematics, Science, Humanities and Social Sciences, and English. Entry into this program is only available through a rigorous testing process conducted by the Department of Education. Both within and beyond the classroom, the program provides opportunities for students to participate in an environment that promotes academic rigour within a supportive and enjoyable learning atmosphere. The selection process starts when students are in Year 5, with testing conducted in Year 6, and placement starting from Year 7. Limited vacancies may also be available for entry into Years 9, 10, and 11. There are no financial benefits associated with this program; successful applicants receive access to accelerated and extended academic pathways rather than fee assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Must be in Year 5 when applications open; Testing conducted in Year 6; Entry from Year 7 onwards; Limited vacancies available for Years 9, 10, and 11 entry; Successful applicants do not need to reside within school intake area; Selection determined by rigorous testing process
special_conditions: Parent information evenings for the Gifted and Talented Secondary Program occur between October and November, detailing the application, selection, and placement process. All sessions contain the same content and provide key dates and applicant guidelines for upcoming testing.

overview: Duncraig Senior High School offers the Specialist Triathlon Academy Program, accepting up to 38 in-area high performing athletes annually. The program is designed to help young triathletes conduct a large part of their training during school time, leaving more time for other interests. Triathlon provides opportunities for young people to progress to the top of the sport while developing good lifestyle and exercise habits.
description: The Specialist Duncraig Triathlon Academy Program accepts up to 38 in-area, high performing athletes into the sports program each year. The Triathlon program offers 4 lessons of triathlon structured as swim, bike, run, and general sport spread across the 5-day week. Swimming is held in the afternoon and requires students to finish 30 minutes later than the usual school day. Cycling is held in the morning and requires students to start an hour earlier than the usual school day. Running and general sport are held within the usual school day. The program is designed to help young triathletes conduct a large part of their training during school time, leaving more time after school for other interests. For those athletes already training regularly, it provides additional training during school time. The school has developed an excellent partnership with Triathlon WA. The Duncraig Triathlon Academy has gone from strength to strength and is at the forefront of State and National competitions. There are no financial benefits; the program provides specialised training and competition opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Must be entering Year 7; Must reside within school intake area; Must be a high performing athlete with current or previous competition experience in triathlon, swimming, cycling, or running; Young people who currently, or have in the past, competed in triathlons; Young athletes who currently compete in swimming, cycling or running
special_conditions: When completing the Year 7 Enrolment Form, students must click 'Triathlon' Program on the Welcome Page and complete the Triathlon Application Form. Applicants will be required to attach a copy of their child's most recent school report and a recent headshot photograph. Students must be in-area residents. For more information, contact Triathlon.duncraig.shs@education.wa.edu.au.

overview: Duncraig Senior High School offers a selective Music Program for students entering Year 7, providing free instrumental tuition and enrichment opportunities. The program welcomes students who studied music in primary school and those continuing their musical development. Selected students receive free instrumental tuition and participate in a range of music enrichment activities including class music, instrumental studies, and ensemble performance.
description: The Music Program at Duncraig Senior High School provides free instrumental tuition and the opportunity to participate in a range of music enrichment activities for selected Year 7 students. The program includes class music, instrumental studies, and ensemble performance. Students who studied music in primary school will study band instruments such as flute, clarinet, trumpet, trombone, and guitar. Non-IMSS students entering Year 7 may apply for a beginner place for instruments such as trombone, saxophone, or percussion. Lessons are supplied free of charge to successful applicants provided they are also enrolled in a class music unit and an ensemble. Specialist instrumental tutors and hire instruments are provided to schools through Instrumental Music Schools Services (IMSS), which is a part of the Western Australian Department of Education. The music program runs for two years, enabling tutors to help students achieve their full potential. Students accepted into the music program must remain in the program at least until the end of Year 8, attend all classes and instrumental lessons, practice their instrument on a regular basis, and attend all ensemble rehearsals and performances.
eligibility_criteria: Must be entering Year 7; Must demonstrate previous music study or experience in primary school; Must be willing to commit to the program until at least the end of Year 8; Must attend all classes, instrumental lessons, ensemble rehearsals and performances; Must practice instrument regularly; Selection based on school report and demonstrated musical aptitude
special_conditions: When completing the Year 7 Enrolment application, students must click 'Music' Program on the Welcome Page and complete the Music Application Form. Applicants will be required to attach a copy of their child's most recent school report. Students who are accepted into the music program must remain in the program at least until the end of Year 8. The music program runs for two years. Students must attend all classes and instrumental lessons, practice their instrument on a regular basis, and attend all ensemble rehearsals and performances.

program_name: Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Program (GATE)
overview: East Kimberley College offers the Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Program (GATE) to academically exceptional students who demonstrate high-order thinking skills. The program is designed to provide a specialized, enriched curriculum that challenges gifted learners and allows them to study with like-minded peers in a supportive environment.
description: The GATE program at East Kimberley College is one of 24 select public secondary schools in Western Australia offering Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Programs across Academic, Arts, and Languages streams. Entry is determined through the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET), a standardized assessment measuring Reading Comprehension, Writing, Quantitative Reasoning, and Abstract Reasoning in approximately 2 hours 50 minutes. The program provides an enriched academic curriculum designed specifically for gifted students. Applications open in October each year and close in February the following year, with testing conducted in March. Limited vacancies may be available for entry into Years 9, 10, and 11 in addition to the primary Year 7 intake. Regional students from Western Australia are eligible to apply and may access residential college options if accepted. No tuition fees are charged as this is a government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be currently in Year 6 for Year 7 entry; Open to all students regardless of location in Western Australia; Must sit the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET); Must achieve minimum benchmark score of approximately 209-210 TSS (Total Scaled Score) for academic stream; Limited vacancies for Years 9, 10, and 11 entry
special_conditions: Students test in their local region - no travel to Perth required for regional students. Successful country applicants may be eligible to board at City Beach Residential College or Albany Residential College. Testing is also available for late entry into Years 9 and 10. Placements are finalized by early Term 3. Reserve lists remain active until 1 December in each testing year.

overview: The Follow the Dream Program focuses on high-achieving Indigenous students from Years 7 to 12, identifying those with the skills necessary to obtain their Western Australian Certificate of Education and enter university or pursue traineeships and apprenticeships. The program is funded by the Western Australian Department of Education in partnership with the Graham Polly Farmer Foundation.
description: Follow the Dream (FTD) is an after-school support and mentoring program specifically designed for high-achieving Indigenous students in Years 7-12 at East Kimberley College. The program provides comprehensive support including a dedicated after-school homework and study facility, small group and one-to-one tutoring, mentoring and support for issues between school, parents and teachers, in-class tutoring, attendance at university camps, leadership and careers camps, reward activities, and exposure to career opportunities in engineering, law, science, and other professions. Students also develop friendships with peers who share similar academic interests. The program is funded by the Western Australian Department of Education in partnership with the Graham Polly Farmer Foundation and produces tangible results in student engagement and academic success.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Indigenous (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander); Must be enrolled in Years 7-12; Must demonstrate high academic achievement and potential; Must have skills necessary to obtain Western Australian Certificate of Education and pursue higher education or vocational pathways
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Program is funded by the Western Australian Department of Education in partnership with the Graham Polly Farmer Foundation. Enrollment is based on identification of high-achieving Indigenous students meeting program criteria. Students participate in structured after-school activities and mentoring.

overview: Shooting Stars is an educational program designed to provide comprehensive in-school support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls, helping them overcome barriers that prevent them from completing their education and reaching their full potential. The program uses sport and other engagement tools to encourage greater school engagement amongst young Aboriginal girls and women.
description: Shooting Stars is a comprehensive support program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls at East Kimberley College that works collaboratively with the local community, schools, and service providers to create genuine change in the lives of more than 375 students. The program provides in-school support services, uses sport and other tools to encourage greater school engagement, and is staffed by inspiring, experienced Program Coordinators (85% identified as Aboriginal). More than 25% of participants improve attendance term on term, while 50% of participants maintain an average attendance rate of 80% or above. The program is supported by Netball WA and the National Indigenous Australian's Agency. Outcomes are measured and reported using Indigenous evaluation protocols, ensuring cultural alignment and respect.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must identify as female/girl; Must be enrolled at East Kimberley College; Open to students seeking support in completing their education and overcoming educational barriers
special_conditions: Program is supported by Netball WA and the National Indigenous Australian's Agency. Staff are predominantly Aboriginal (85%) and Board identifies 66% as Aboriginal. Program uses Indigenous evaluation protocols for outcomes measurement. More than 375 students actively participate in the program.

overview: The East Kimberley Clontarf Academy is a program designed to improve the education, discipline, life skills, self-esteem, and employment prospects of young Aboriginal men. The program uses Australian Rules Football and/or Rugby League as a vehicle to attract and retain students in school.
description: The East Kimberley Clontarf Academy exists to improve the education, discipline, life skills, self-esteem, and employment prospects of young Aboriginal men by equipping them to participate meaningfully in society. The Foundation addresses the reality that failure to experience achievement when young, coupled with under-privilege, can lead to alienation and anger with more serious consequences. The programs are delivered through a network of football academies established in partnership with local schools. The Academy uses the existing passion that Aboriginal boys have for Australian Rules Football and/or Rugby League to attract them into school and keep them there, providing them with an opportunity to succeed and raise their self-esteem. The Academy rooms are staffed by full-time mentors and are welcoming, inclusive, and designed to foster positive learning and personal growth for young men in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Must be male; Must be Aboriginal; Any Aboriginal male enrolled at East Kimberley College is eligible to participate; No academic threshold or prior experience with football required
special_conditions: Optional Clontarf participation charge of $25 for Years 9-10 students. Academy produces newsletters throughout the year. Full-time mentors staff the academy rooms. Program focuses on Australian Rules Football and/or Rugby League as engagement vehicles.

overview: The KALS Program is a secondary engagement stream at East Kimberley College designed for students needing re-engagement with education. The program targets key learning areas of Literacy and Numeracy while developing crucial life skills and preparing students for the workforce.
description: The Kimberley Academic & Life Skills (KALS) Program is a specialized secondary engagement stream at East Kimberley College for students requiring academic support and re-engagement. The program focuses on key learning areas of Literacy and Numeracy, as well as developing crucial life skills and preparing students for the workforce. In partnership with external organisations and agencies, the program provides diverse and exciting learning that enhances attendance and fosters lifelong learning values and attitudes. Students are exposed to local organisations including Warringari Aboriginal Art, Mirima Dawang Woorlab-gerring Language and Culture Centre, local police, Ord Valley Aboriginal Health Service, the Clontarf Foundation, and many local businesses. This program-specific charge is obligatory, with payments expected before program commencement as outlined in the provided contract, with a 2020 fee structure of $235 per annum.
eligibility_criteria: Must be secondary student; Must be selected based on need for academic re-engagement; Must be prepared to participate in targeted literacy and numeracy instruction; Must commit to the full program including work-related learning opportunities
special_conditions: Specialist Program charge is obligatory with payments expected before program commencement as outlined in the program contract. Program is unique to East Kimberley College and involves partnerships with local organisations including Warringari Aboriginal Art, Mirima Dawang Woorlab-gerring Language and Culture Centre, Ord Valley Aboriginal Health Service, and Clontarf Foundation. Students develop literacy, numeracy, and life skills while preparing for workforce entry.

school_name: East Victoria Park Education Support Centre

program_name: The Smith Family Learning For Life Scholarship
overview: East Waikiki Primary School partners with The Smith Family to provide the Learning For Life program to support students experiencing disadvantage. This education support program provides financial, social and program assistance to families throughout their children's schooling years.
description: The Smith Family Learning For Life Scholarship is an evidence-based education support program that recognises disadvantaged children need extra support to stay engaged at school. The program provides financial support in the form of a scholarship to help pay for the cost of education, including uniforms, stationary, shoes, school camps, excursions and swimming lessons. Recipients also receive access to targeted learning support programs to help improve reading, numeracy, leadership, computer and finance skills. Additionally, families receive support from a dedicated local Family Partnership Coordinator who links families into services to increase their engagement in education. The scholarship provides ongoing support for eligible students from early primary through to completion of their schooling.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be in receipt of a current concession card (Health Care or Pension Concession Card); Students must be enrolled at East Waikiki Primary School; Family must demonstrate a strong commitment to education; Open to all year levels from Kindergarten to Year 6
special_conditions: Recipients must be in receipt of a concession card. Families must demonstrate a strong commitment to education and commitment to supporting their child's educational engagement. Scholarship funds must be utilised for educational expenses as intended. Long-term support is provided throughout the child's schooling years.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School offers the Academic Scholarship to two Year 6 students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises high academic ability and commitment to excellence in MESH courses (Mathematics, English, Science & HASS).
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 7 students is valued at $235 and will be deducted from the student's course charges and other educational expenses for the following year. The scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating high academic ability through an academic test conducted at the school. Recipients must be nominated by a teacher, provide an academic record, and include a reference from their class teacher. An optional out-of-school reference relating to community involvement may also be provided. The scholarship will be awarded following a panel assessment of each student's application.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students entering Year 7; Nomination by a teacher required; Must complete an academic test at the school; High academic ability in MESH courses (Mathematics, English, Science & HASS); Academic record and class teacher reference required
special_conditions: Academic record needs to be provided together with one reference from a class teacher and one reference from outside the school (optional). Students must demonstrate consistent academic achievement.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School awards Academic Scholarships to the Dux of each year group at the school. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement across all courses throughout the academic year.
description: The Academic Dux Scholarship is valued at $235 and will be deducted from the student's course charges and other educational expenses for the following year. The scholarships acknowledge high academic ability in ATAR courses in Year 11 and MESH courses (Mathematics, English, Science & HASS) in Years 7-10. The Academic Scholarships for the Dux of each year group are automatically awarded by the school based on the academic results for the whole year as reflected in semester 2 reports. No application is required; the award is determined automatically by academic achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Dux of each year group at the school; Highest academic achievement for the full academic year as reflected in semester 2 reports; Automatic award based on academic results
special_conditions: Automatically awarded by the school based on academic results for the whole year as reflected in semester 2 reports. Recognises high academic ability in ATAR courses in Year 11 and MESH courses in Years 7-10.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School offers Enrichment Scholarships to recognise consistent and sustained academic effort combined with school and community participation. One scholarship per year group is awarded to Years 7-9 students demonstrating achievement across multiple curriculum courses.
description: The Enrichment Scholarship is valued at $235 and will be deducted from the student's course charges and other education expenses for the following year. The scholarship recognises consistent and sustained effort over a number of curriculum courses, good standing status, demonstration of satisfactory school attendance, and school and community participation. Achievement in curriculum courses requires nothing less than a 'C' grade. Students can self-nominate or be nominated by a teacher. Recipients must provide an academic record, one reference from a class teacher, and one reference from outside the school. The scholarship will be awarded following a panel assessment of each student's application.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7, Year 8, and Year 9; Consistent and sustained effort over multiple curriculum courses; Achievement of at least 'C' grade in curriculum courses; Good standing status; Satisfactory school attendance; Participation in school and community activities; Self-nomination or teacher nomination
special_conditions: Minimum 'C' grade required in curriculum courses. Students can self-nominate or be nominated by a teacher. One reference from a class teacher and one reference from outside the school are both required. Panel assessment determines the award.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School offers a Music Scholarship to Year 6 students with outstanding instrumental music talent who will be enrolling in Year 7. The scholarship provides financial support for classroom music fees and recognises exceptional musical ability.
description: The Music Scholarship is valued at $300 and will provide financial support for classroom music fees (valued at $60) and other music-related expenses. The scholarship is awarded to students who have been learning a musical instrument and will be enrolling at the school in Year 7. Applications are made to the Principal on the scholarship application form and must include an academic record, a brief handwritten reason for applying, a copy of a recent instrumental tutor's report, and any examination results or awards in music. Applicants must commit to the Music Program at the school for the following three years, involving instrumental studies and participation in an ensemble.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students entering Year 7; Must have been learning a musical instrument; Will be enrolling at Eastern Hills Senior High School in Year 7; Commitment to Music Program for three years mandatory; Academic record required; Recent instrumental tutor's report required; Examination results or awards in music should be provided
special_conditions: Applicants must commit to the Music Program at the school for the following three years involving instrumental studies and participation in an ensemble. Academic record needs to be provided with application. Application form must include brief handwritten reason for applying, recent instrumental tutor's report, and any examination results or awards in music.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School offers Instrumental Bursaries to Year 6 students with musical instrument experience entering Year 7. Two bursaries are awarded each year to provide financial support for instrument-related expenses and music participation.
description: Two Instrumental Bursaries are awarded annually to the value of $150 each to provide financial support for instrument hire, class music fees, music camps, and other related expenses. Applicants must have been learning a musical instrument and be enrolling at the school in Year 7. Applications are made to the Principal on the scholarship application form and must include an academic record, a brief handwritten reason for applying, a copy of a recent instrumental tutor's report, and any examination results or awards in music. Like the Music Scholarship, recipients must commit to the Music Program at the school for the following three years, involving instrumental studies and participation in an ensemble.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students entering Year 7; Must have been learning a musical instrument; Will be enrolling at Eastern Hills Senior High School in Year 7; Commitment to Music Program for three years mandatory; Academic record required; Recent instrumental tutor's report required; Examination results or awards in music should be provided
special_conditions: Applicants must commit to the Music Program at the school for the following three years involving instrumental studies and participation in an ensemble. Academic record needs to be provided with application. Application form must include brief handwritten reason for applying, recent instrumental tutor's report, and any examination results or awards in music.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School offers a VET Scholarship to a Year 10 student who has chosen to complete a VET course in Year 11. The scholarship recognises commitment to vocational education and training pathways.
description: The VET Scholarship is valued at $235 and will be deducted from the student's course charges and other educational expenses for the following year. The scholarship is awarded to a Year 10 student who has selected a VET course for Year 11 enrolment. The scholarship recognises students' commitment to vocational education and training pathways. Selection is based on demonstrated suitability for VET study and commitment to the chosen VET course. The scholarship provides financial support to assist students undertaking VET qualifications.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students; Must have chosen to complete a VET course in Year 11; Demonstrated suitability for VET study; Commitment to the selected VET course
special_conditions: Award is based on student selection of VET course for Year 11 enrolment and demonstrated suitability for VET study.

overview: Eastern Hills Senior High School awards a VET Scholarship to the Year 11 VET Dux. The scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and excellence in vocational education and training courses.
description: The VET Dux Scholarship is valued at $235 and will be deducted from the student's course charges and other educational expenses for the following year. The scholarship is awarded to the student achieving the highest standard in VET courses during Year 11. The award recognises outstanding performance and achievement in vocational education and training. This is typically awarded automatically based on VET course results and achievement demonstrated during the academic year.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 VET Dux; Highest achievement in VET courses during Year 11; Outstanding performance in vocational education and training
special_conditions: Award is based on highest achievement in VET courses during Year 11. Recognises outstanding performance in vocational education and training.

overview: Ellenbrook Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to reward exceptional achievement in secondary students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and commitment to learning, providing significant tuition fee support to eligible applicants.
description: Academic Scholarships offer up to 50% remission of tuition fees and are awarded on the basis of a scholarship exam and successful interview. These scholarships are only available for students entering into Year 7. The award provides fee relief of up to 50% of tuition fees. Selection is based on performance in a standardised scholarship examination and a subsequent interview process. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and engagement with the College's educational program throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 or equivalent; Must sit the scholarship examination; Must participate in the interview process; Open to both domestic and international students
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to provide support for secondary education. Recipients must maintain good standing at the College and continued eligibility may be subject to academic progress reviews. The College reserves the right to determine renewal conditions based on student performance and engagement.

overview: Eastern Goldfields College offers the Goldfields Clontarf Academy, an engagement program targeting Aboriginal male students. The program aims to actively engage students through Year 12, maximising their school attendance and promoting positive participation and achievement in all aspects of their careers and personal engagements.
description: The Goldfields Clontarf Academy (GCA) is an engagement program targeting Aboriginal male students from Years 11 and 12. The aim of the program is to actively engage them through to Year 12, maximising their school attendance and promoting positive participation and achievement in all aspects of their careers and personal engagements. Students who are engaged within the program have the opportunity to improve their health and well-being, develop life skills and build their own self esteem which will enable them to be a proactive positive contributor to their community and their own personal and/or professional relationships. This program operates in partnership with Kalgoorlie-Boulder Community High School and focuses on Indigenous male leadership development through sports and mentoring.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal male students; Year 11 and Year 12 students; Enrolled at Eastern Goldfields College or Kalgoorlie-Boulder Community High School
special_conditions: Program operates in partnership with Kalgoorlie-Boulder Community High School. Focus on Indigenous male students. Mentoring and life skills development.

overview: Eastern Goldfields College offers the Kalgoorlie Girls Academy, a program aimed at improving the life skills and self esteem of both Indigenous and non-Indigenous girls who are selected for the program. The program achieves this through the medium of its basketball program, although the playing of basketball is not essential to being in the program.
description: The Kalgoorlie Girls Academy (KGA) aims at improving the life skills and self esteem of both Indigenous and non-Indigenous girls who are selected for the program. KGA achieves this through the medium of its basketball program although the playing of basketball is not essential to being in the program. A number of girls are in the program to be mentored rather than play sport. KGA support staff are shared between Eastern Goldfields College and Kalgoorlie-Boulder Community High School and the program is overseen by Role Models WA. The program provides mentoring, support and life skills development for girls at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Female students; Both Indigenous and non-Indigenous girls; Selected by the program; Can attend from either Eastern Goldfields College or Kalgoorlie-Boulder Community High School
special_conditions: Selection-based program. Basketball participation not essential. Program operates in partnership with Kalgoorlie-Boulder Community High School. Overseen by Role Models WA.

overview: Eastern Goldfields College hosts the Stars Foundation program, which provides holistic mentoring support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls and young women. The program operates full-time, intensive support services aimed at improving health and education outcomes for this cohort.
description: Stars Foundation uses a highly successful, tailored approach to provide holistic mentoring support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls and young women. Stars operates full-time, intensive support programs for Indigenous girls in schools, with the aim of improving health and education outcomes for this cohort. Stars offers personal development activities and experiences in many different areas, including academic support, personal health and wellbeing, art, cooking and nutrition, and exercise and sport. Students also take part in community, cultural and volunteering activities to build their life-skills and confidence and further develop their links to culture and community. This program operates in partnership with Kalgoorlie Boulder Community High School.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls; Selected for the program; Enrolled at Eastern Goldfields College or Kalgoorlie Boulder Community High School
special_conditions: Selection-based program. Full-time, intensive support program. Operates in partnership with Kalgoorlie Boulder Community High School. Focus on holistic mentoring and cultural development.

overview: Eastern Goldfields College participates in the Follow The Dream program, which provides support services to Indigenous students. The program is designed to improve educational outcomes and provide pathways for Indigenous students.
description: Follow The Dream is a support program available at Eastern Goldfields College that provides assistance to Indigenous students. The program provides academic, personal and community support to help Indigenous students achieve their educational goals and create pathways to further education, employment, or training. The program focuses on improving educational outcomes, building confidence and life skills, and supporting student wellbeing. It is designed to help Indigenous students at the college succeed in their studies and transition to post-secondary pathways. Support includes tutoring, mentoring, and cultural connection opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Enrolled at Eastern Goldfields College; Year 11 and Year 12 students
special_conditions: Indigenous student support program. Includes academic tutoring, mentoring and cultural connection. Focus on post-secondary transitions.

school_name: Emmanuel Christian Community School - Casserley Avenue Campus

overview: Emmanuel Catholic College offers Academic Scholarships to academically able students entering Year 7. Two (or equivalent) scholarships are awarded to recognise outstanding academic achievement and potential at the secondary level.
description: Two (or equivalent) scholarships are offered to academically able students who are entering the College in Year 7. These scholarships cover up to 50% of the tuition fees for Years 7, 8 and 9. Selection is based on performance in written assessment testing conducted with prospective Year 7 students. The scholarship is awarded on the basis of demonstrated academic excellence and commitment to learning. All scholarship recipients must meet College conduct and presentation expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 at Emmanuel Catholic College; Demonstrated academic ability in top percentile; Must sit scholarship examination; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded for Years 7, 8 and 9 only. Recipients must maintain strong academic performance and meet College conduct and presentation standards to retain the scholarship.

overview: Emmanuel Catholic College offers Academic Scholarships to academically able students entering Year 10. Two (or equivalent) scholarships are awarded to recognise outstanding academic achievement demonstrated through Year 9 performance.
description: Two (or equivalent) scholarships are offered to students entering Year 10. These scholarships cover up to 50% of the tuition fees for Years 10, 11 and 12. Students can apply when they are in Year 9, allowing them to demonstrate their academic performance through the previous year. Selection is based on academic ability and commitment to excellence. Recipients must maintain appropriate conduct and presentation standards throughout their scholarship tenure.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 9; Demonstrating academic ability in top percentile; Open to all backgrounds; Must apply in Year 9 for Year 10 entry
special_conditions: Scholarship covers Years 10, 11 and 12. Renewal is conditional upon maintaining strong academic performance and meeting College expectations in conduct, presentation and effort.

program_name: P&F Mission & Values Scholarship – Men and Women of Service
overview: Emmanuel Catholic College offers the P&F Mission & Values Scholarship to recognise students who embody the College's values and contribute meaningfully to all areas of College life. This scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 11 who actively live the College Values and uphold its ethos.
description: One (or equivalent) scholarship of up to 50% of tuition fees for Years 11 and 12 is awarded to a student entering Year 11 at the College, who actively lives the College Values, upholds the ethos of the College and contributes to all areas of College life. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate leadership qualities, community service, and commitment to the Marianist educational philosophy. Selection takes into account the student's overall contribution to the College community across academic, co-curricular, and pastoral dimensions.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 11 at Emmanuel Catholic College; Actively lives the College Values; Upholds the College ethos; Demonstrates contributions to all areas of College life; Open to all students regardless of background
special_conditions: Scholarship covers Years 11 and 12. Applications open 22 April. Recipients must continue to demonstrate commitment to College values and ethos throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Emmanuel Catholic College invites established musicians to apply for Music Scholarships that cover the cost of music lesson tuition fees. One full scholarship per year group (Years 7-12) is awarded annually, along with additional up to 50% scholarships in Years 7-10.
description: The College invites all established musicians to apply for a Music Scholarship to cover Music Lesson tuition fees. A total of one full scholarship per Year Group (Years 7-12) will be awarded each year, along with a range of up to 50% scholarships in Years 7-10. The scholarship covers tuition fees for lessons with a College appointed instrument or vocal tutor. Duration is for one academic year only. All Year 7 to 10 scholarship winners are expected to be members of the College Choir with rehearsals on Monday after school. Recipients must participate in the Catholic Performing Arts Festival and other College events as required.
eligibility_criteria: Established musician with demonstrated proficiency; Years 7-12 eligible; Willing to participate in College Choir (Years 7-10); Must attend rehearsals and College performances; Open to all students
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
number_awarded: One full per year level; multiple up to 50% scholarships in Years 7-10
special_conditions: Full scholarship for one student per year group. Duration is for one academic year only. Scholarship does not include other specific purpose charges, resource fees, books, resources or school uniforms. Vocal scholarship students must attend Vocal Pop Ensemble. Choir participation is mandatory for Years 7-10 recipients. Students must participate in Performing Arts Festival and other College events as required.

overview: Emmanuel Catholic College offers Specialist Academy Scholarships to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional talent and commitment in the College's specialist sports academies. These scholarships recognise excellence across sports programs including AFL, AFLW, Netball, and Basketball.
description: Students entering the College in Year 7 are invited to apply for scholarships for the specialist academies offered at Emmanuel Catholic College, including the Emmanuel Royals AFL/AFLW Academies, Emmanuel Aces Netball Academy, and Emmanuel Knights Basketball Academy. These scholarships cover the individual's Academy kit and 25% of tuition fees for Year 7. Selection is based on a positive reference along with a trial or audition showcasing necessary skills, attitude, and experience. Applicants who have not received access to prior coaching but demonstrate potential will still be considered. All applications are subject to successful entry into the College.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 at Emmanuel Catholic College; Application made in Year 6; Demonstrated talent in relevant sport; Positive reference required; Positive attitude and commitment; Open to all students regardless of background
special_conditions: Applications must be made in Year 6 for Year 7 entry. Scholarships cover Year 7 tuition only and include Academy kit. Selection based on trial/audition performance, skills, attitude and experience. Applicants without prior coaching but showing potential are eligible. Conditional on successful College enrolment. All scholarships subject to positive enrolment reference.

overview: Emmanuel Catholic College offers the First Nations Bursary to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who require financial assistance to access quality Catholic education. The bursary covers 60% of tuition fees for eligible students.
description: The College offers a First Nations Bursary to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to help make an Emmanuel Catholic College education accessible and affordable. This bursary covers 60% of the tuition fee and applies to tuition fees only. The bursary is applicable for the current year and provides meaningful financial assistance to support Indigenous students in accessing quality education. Students can apply for bursary support, and applications are reviewed individually by the College leadership. The bursary recognises the importance of supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to achieve their educational potential.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Application through formal bursary application process; Current or prospective student; Demonstration of genuine financial need; Australian resident
special_conditions: Covers tuition fees only; does not include enrolment fees, levies, laptops, uniforms or books. Bursary is applicable for the current year. Applications made through official First Nations Bursary application form available on the College website.

overview: Emmanuel Catholic College offers Bursaries for families experiencing extreme financial hardship to ensure all students have access to Catholic education. These bursaries provide essential financial assistance to families in acute need.
description: The College offers Bursaries for extreme financial hardship to support families experiencing genuine financial difficulty in meeting the costs of education. The bursary is applicable for the current year and applies to the Tuition Fee only. These bursaries are designed to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent access to quality education at Emmanuel Catholic College. Applications are considered on a case-by-case basis by the College leadership, taking into account individual family circumstances and demonstrated financial need. The College is committed to removing financial barriers to education for worthy students and families.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated extreme financial hardship; Application through formal bursary process; Current or prospective student; Financial assessment required; Genuine financial need demonstrated through supporting documentation
special_conditions: Covers tuition fees only; does not include other specific purpose charges. Bursary is applicable for the current year only and may need to be reapplied for annually. Determined on case-by-case basis considering individual family circumstances.

overview: Exmouth District High School participates in the Western Australian Secondary Assistance Scheme, which provides financial assistance to low-income families with secondary schooling costs. The scheme is available to eligible parents and guardians with children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 who hold an eligible concession card.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is administered by the Department of Education in Western Australia to assist low-income families with secondary schooling costs. The scheme provides up to $350 in financial assistance per eligible student per year. The assistance consists of two components: a $115 clothing allowance and a $235 education program allowance. Applications must be submitted annually during Term 1 of each school year, as applications do not carry forward to future years. Parents and guardians must hold a valid Services Australia (Centrelink) concession card (Pensioner Concession Card or Health Care Card), a Department of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card, or equivalent eligible documentation. The student must be enrolled in Years 7 to 12 studying a full-time secondary course at Exmouth District High School and must be listed on the parent or guardian's concession card.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolled in Years 7 to 12; Parent or guardian must hold a valid Services Australia Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, Centrelink Health Care Card, or Department of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card; Student must be listed on the parent/guardian's concession card; Parent or guardian must not be a foster parent; Application must be submitted in Term 1; Not eligible if student was born prior to 2007; Parents must reapply each school year as applications do not carry forward
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications must be completed annually and do not carry forward to future years. School must witness the application and verify the concession card documentation. Payment consists of $115 clothing allowance and $235 education program allowance. For public school students, the clothing allowance may be directed toward school fees for other expenses. Students must remain enrolled and actively attend school. Concession card must be valid for no less than four weeks and valid within Term 1.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the General Excellence Scholarship to students in Year 6 who wish to attend the school for Year 7 to Year 12. The scholarship is awarded to students who have made a significant contribution to their primary school or wider community while maintaining a sound academic record.
