description: The General Excellence Scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees at EACS for the duration of enrolment from Year 7 to Year 12. This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate both strong academics and leadership. Selection is based on a sound academic record, demonstrated community contribution, and Principal's reference. Students must maintain satisfactory academic progress and high standards of citizenship to retain the scholarship. Annual review ensures recipients continue to meet scholarship conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Year 6; Must have sound academic record; Must have made significant contribution to primary school or wider community; Must provide Principal's reference from current school; Must pass application assessment
special_conditions: Scholarships are not awarded to students currently attending Esperance Anglican Community School. Scholarships and bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students are maintaining satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship and participation within the life of the School. Students not maintaining the expected minimum standards will have their scholarship modified or cancelled.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the Academic Scholarship to qualifying Year 5 and Year 6 students to attend the school from Year 7 to Year 12. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate high level of academic ability as demonstrated in a scholarship examination, through current school reports and via a Principal's reference.
description: The Academic Scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees at EACS for Year 7 to Year 12 enrolment. This scholarship recognises outstanding academic merit and potential. Selection is based on performance in a scholarship examination, current school reports, and a written reference from the applicant's principal. Recipients must demonstrate high academic ability and commitment to learning. Scholarships are reviewed annually and may be modified or cancelled if students do not maintain satisfactory academic progress.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 and Year 6 students; Must demonstrate high level of academic ability; Must provide current school reports; Must provide Principal's reference; Must sit scholarship examination
special_conditions: Scholarships are not awarded to students currently attending Esperance Anglican Community School. Applicants must provide a 250 word personal statement, written reference from current school principal, and copies of two most recent semester reports and any standardised testing results such as NAPLAN, WAMSE or OLNA. Scholarships and bursaries are reviewed annually. Students not maintaining expected minimum standards will have scholarship modified or cancelled.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the Expressive Arts Scholarship to students who display a high level of artistic, dramatic or musical talent. The scholarship recognises students who have performed or exhibited in the public arena with some success and wish to continue developing their talents.
description: The Expressive Arts Scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees at EACS for qualifying students entering Year 7 and beyond. This scholarship supports talented students in drama, visual arts, and music. Selection involves an audition or portfolio presentation as part of the assessment process. Not all categories of scholarships may be awarded in any given year. When there are large numbers of applicants, the school may pre-select applicants for auditions or portfolio reviews. Annual review ensures recipients continue to meet scholarship conditions and maintain high standards of citizenship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with high level of artistic, dramatic or musical talent; Must have performed or exhibited in public arena with some success; Must provide portfolio or prepare for audition; Not currently enrolled at EACS
special_conditions: Scholarships are not awarded to students currently attending Esperance Anglican Community School. Applicants will be required to have an audition or present a portfolio as part of the selection process. In any year where there are a large number of applicants it may not be possible to audition all applicants or see portfolios, in which case applicants will be selected. Scholarships and bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students maintain satisfactory academic progress and high standards of citizenship.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the Tennis Scholarship to students demonstrating high level of sporting ability in tennis. The scholarship is available for qualifying Year 5 and Year 6 students who wish to attend the school from Year 7 onwards.
description: The Tennis Scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees and supports students who demonstrate exceptional ability in tennis. Selection is based on performance in a tennis assessment/trial as part of the application process. Students must demonstrate significant sporting talent and commitment to the sport at a competitive level. Recipients are reviewed annually to ensure they maintain satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship, and active participation in the school's tennis program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 and Year 6 students; Must demonstrate high level of tennis ability; Must perform well in tennis assessment or trial; Not currently enrolled at EACS
special_conditions: Tennis testing takes place as part of the application process. Scholarships are not awarded to students currently attending Esperance Anglican Community School. Scholarships and bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students are maintaining satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship and participation within the life of the School. Students not maintaining expected minimum standards will have scholarship modified or cancelled.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the Netball Scholarship to students demonstrating high level of sporting ability in netball. The scholarship is available for qualifying Year 5 and Year 6 students who wish to attend the school from Year 7 onwards.
description: The Netball Scholarship provides partial remission of tuition fees and supports students who demonstrate exceptional ability in netball. Selection is based on performance in a netball assessment/trial as part of the application process. Students must demonstrate significant sporting talent and commitment to the sport at a competitive level. Recipients are reviewed annually to ensure they maintain satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship, and active participation in the school's netball program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 and Year 6 students; Must demonstrate high level of netball ability; Must perform well in netball assessment or trial; Not currently enrolled at EACS
special_conditions: Netball testing takes place as part of the application process. Scholarships are not awarded to students currently attending Esperance Anglican Community School. Scholarships and bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students are maintaining satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship and participation within the life of the School. Students not maintaining expected minimum standards will have scholarship modified or cancelled.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the McVay Boarding House Scholarship to qualifying Year 10 students to attend EACS in Year 11 and 12. The scholarship provides support for boarding fees at the school's McVay Boarding House.
description: The McVay Boarding House Scholarship provides 25% remission of the Boarding House fees at EACS for Year 11 and Year 12. This scholarship assists students who wish to board at the school while completing their final years of secondary education. Selection is based on demonstrated need, academic performance, and application assessment. The scholarship acknowledges students' commitment to boarding at the school and provides financial assistance with accommodation costs. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and high standards of conduct and participation in the boarding community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students; Must intend to board in Year 11 and Year 12; Must demonstrate need and/or merit; Not currently enrolled at EACS
special_conditions: This scholarship applies to boarding house fees only, not general tuition fees. Scholarships and bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students are maintaining satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship and participation within the life of the School. Students not maintaining expected minimum standards will have scholarship modified or cancelled.

overview: Esperance Anglican Community School offers the School Bursary Program to students and families experiencing financial hardship. The bursaries enable students who would otherwise be unable to attend the school due to financial, social or personal circumstances to access education at EACS.
description: School Bursaries provide partial remission of school tuition fees for students who have the potential to contribute to school life but whose family financial circumstances would otherwise preclude attendance. Bursaries are assessed individually based on the family's financial circumstances and the student's capacity to contribute to school life. The program promotes the school's philosophy of inclusion and community. Bursaries are reviewed annually to ensure students are maintaining satisfactory academic progress and high standards of citizenship and participation. To apply, families should contact the Principal directly at the school email.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Family experiencing financial hardship; Student must have potential to contribute to school life; Individual assessment based on family financial circumstances
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Principal within policy guidelines approved by the School Council. School Bursaries will be assessed individually based on the individual family's financial circumstances and the student's capacity to contribute to the life of the school. Bursaries and scholarships are reviewed annually to ensure students maintain satisfactory academic progress, high standards of citizenship and participation.

overview: Floreat Park Primary School offers placement in the Department of Education's Instrumental Music School Services (IMSS) program. Students are selected based on music aptitude and physical suitability to play their chosen instrument, with places highly sought after at the school.
description: The IMSS program is offered through the Department of Education's instrumental music school, which services over 400 primary and secondary Western Australian public schools. Students are selected from a music aptitude test determined by IMSS teachers and their physical suitability to play their chosen instrument. Testing occurs in Term 4, the year before students commence tuition. The school offers a parent information session during Term 4 to outline program requirements for the coming year. Places may not be confirmed until the beginning of the year classes commence to provide opportunity for new students. The financial value and specific costs are not detailed on the school's website.
eligibility_criteria: Student aptitude in music as determined by IMSS music aptitude test; Physical suitability to play chosen instrument as determined by IMSS teachers; Testing occurs in Term 4 prior to program commencement
special_conditions: Places in this program are highly sought after at Floreat. Places may not be confirmed until the beginning of the year classes commence to provide opportunity for new students. Testing is conducted by IMSS teachers.

overview: Fountain College offers Academic Merit Scholarships to students who demonstrate academic excellence. These scholarships are awarded to students entering any year level between Year 7 and Year 12 and recognise outstanding academic achievement throughout their schooling.
description: Fountain College provides merit-based scholarships to students demonstrating academic excellence. Scholarships are offered to students with academic excellence entering any year level between Year 7-12. The scholarships are available in three tiers: 100% Fee Remission, 50% Fee Reduction, and 25% Fee Reduction. These scholarships are handed out to students at the end of each academic year based on their academic performance. The overall requirement is lower for Year 12 since the standard is much higher for Year 11 subjects. Additionally, students who receive a 100%, 50% or 25% scholarship will receive a $300, $200 or $100 gift card from school respectively as a monetary prize.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 12; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Scholarships awarded at the end of each academic year based on performance
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded at the end of each academic year. Recipients receive additional monetary prizes: $300 gift card for 100% scholarship, $200 for 50% scholarship, $100 for 25% scholarship.

overview: Fountain College offers bursaries to families who may be struggling financially. These discretionary awards are provided at the school's and board's discretion to assist families with educational costs.
description: Fountain College provides financial assistance through bursaries to families facing financial hardship or struggling financially. Bursaries are offered at the school's and board's discretion and are designed to help families with educational costs. Applicants must complete a bursary application form and return it to the office. To request a form, families should contact the front office directly. The bursary program is based on demonstrated financial need and is reviewed at the discretion of the school and board to determine eligibility and support levels.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial hardship; Families struggling financially; Completed bursary application form required; At school and board discretion
special_conditions: Application by form submission to school office. Bursaries are awarded at the school's and board's discretion based on individual family circumstances.

overview: Fountain College offers Academic Merit Scholarships to recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic excellence. Scholarships are awarded at the end of each academic year to students achieving outstanding academic results across their studies.
description: Academic Merit Scholarships at Fountain College are awarded based on academic excellence to students entering any year level between Year 7 and Year 12. The scholarship provides financial benefits ranging from 25% to 100% fee reduction, with recipients receiving gift cards valued at $100 for 25% scholarships, $200 for 50% scholarships, and $300 for 100% scholarships. Scholarships are distributed at the end of each academic year based on student performance. The selection process is based on demonstrated academic merit and overall academic achievement throughout the year, with eligibility requirements varying slightly between year levels.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering any year level between Year 7 and Year 12; Students must demonstrate academic excellence; Academic performance standards vary by year level; Eligibility is based on academic achievement throughout the school year; Overall requirement is lower for Year 12 since the standard is much higher for Year 11 subjects
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: 100% Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships are handed out to students at the end of each academic year. Students who receive 100%, 50% or 25% scholarships receive a $300, $200 or $100 gift card from school respectively.

overview: Fountain College offers Financial Bursaries to support families experiencing financial hardship. These discretionary awards are provided to families who may be struggling financially to afford school fees.
description: Fountain College provides Financial Bursaries at the school's and board's discretion to families who may be struggling financially. These need-based awards are designed to provide financial assistance to eligible families, enabling access to education regardless of financial circumstances. To apply for a bursary, families must complete an application form and return it to the school office. The school evaluates applications on a case-by-case basis, considering the family's financial circumstances and need. Bursaries may provide partial or full fee assistance depending on the level of need and available funds.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Application requires completion of bursary form; Awarded at school's and board's discretion; Financial need assessment required
special_conditions: Bursaries are offered at the school's and board's discretion. Families must contact the front office to request a bursary application form.

overview: Fountain College provides a small monetary prize for students who achieve exceptional academic excellence. This award recognises outstanding academic performance and achievement throughout the school year.
description: Fountain College provides a small monetary prize for students who achieve academic excellence. This award is given to recognise outstanding academic performance and achievement. The prize amount is modest and is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic results. While not a major scholarship, this award acknowledges and celebrates student academic achievement, providing financial recognition alongside academic recognition for high-performing students.
eligibility_criteria: Students who achieve academic excellence; Selection based on demonstrated exceptional academic performance
special_conditions: Award is provided to recognise academic excellence. Amount is small and awarded based on academic performance.

overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Academic Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in secondary students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning during their secondary education.
description: The Academic Scholarship is designed to support students of high academic ability and achievement during their years of secondary education. Scholarship value is 50% of tuition fees for Year 7 recipients for six years (Years 7-12), and 50% of tuition fees for Year 11 recipients for two years (Year 11-12). All other costs, levies and charges remain the responsibility of parents. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of a common test and a student's past and present performance at school. Continuation of the scholarship from one year to the next will be dependent upon a scholarship student's continuing satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution to the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10, or Year 11 in 2026; Must demonstrate high academic ability; Academic performance assessed through common test and past/present school performance
special_conditions: Scholarships are closed for 2026 applications. Continuation of the scholarship from one year to the next will be dependent upon a scholarship student's continuing satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution to the school.

overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Music Performance Scholarship to support students with musical talent and good academic ability. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment during their secondary education.
description: The Music Performance Scholarship is designed to support students with musical talent and good academic ability and achievement during their years of secondary education. Scholarship value is 50% of tuition fees for Year 7 recipients for six years (Years 7-12), and 50% of tuition fees for Year 11 recipients for two years (Year 11-12). All other costs, levies and charges remain the responsibility of parents. Scholarships are awarded on the basis of an instrument or voice performance through audition, and a student's past and present performance at school. Audition requirements include a 10-minute performance of prepared pieces and technical work, 5-minute aural/perception tests, and 5-minute interview. Music Performance Scholarship students are required to participate fully in the school music programme including instrumental lessons, bands, ensembles and performances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 11 in 2026; Must demonstrate musical talent; Good academic ability required; Must be willing to participate fully in school music programme
special_conditions: Applications are now closed for 2026. Audition time approximately 20 minutes. Continuation of scholarship dependent upon satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution to music in the school. Students must participate fully in school music programme including instrumental lessons, bands, ensembles and performances.

overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Kwobadiny Sports Scholarship to recognise students with sporting talent and excellence. The scholarship supports both developing athletes in Years 7-10 and elite athletes in Years 11-12.
description: The Kwobadiny Sports Scholarship was introduced to coincide with the commissioning of the state-of-the-art Kwobadiny Sports Centre. The school offers a part-scholarship in Years 7 to 10 focused on developing potential athletes and a full-scholarship for Years 11 and 12 for those excelling in their field of sport and their contribution to the culture of sport at Frederick Irwin Anglican School. Applications are submitted via letter and portfolio sent as PDF through email. Scholarship value varies between part-scholarships (Years 7-10) and full scholarships (Years 11-12). All candidates are contacted by email regarding application outcomes. Successful recipients are published on the school website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11 in 2026; Open to both current Frederick Irwin students and external applicants; Must demonstrate sporting talent and potential; Years 11-12 applicants must demonstrate excellence and contribution to sport culture at school
special_conditions: Scholarships closed for 2026 applications. Part-scholarships offered for Years 7-10 for developing potential athletes; Full scholarships for Years 11-12 for those excelling in sport and contributing to school sport culture. Continuation subject to satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution to school.

overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Cultural Scholarship to recognise and support students with talent in cultural pursuits. The scholarship reflects the school's commitment to fostering cultural excellence alongside academic and sporting achievement.
description: The Cultural Scholarship is designed to support students with talent and passion for cultural pursuits including performing arts and visual arts. The school acknowledges the importance of cultural development as part of its holistic education approach. Specific financial value and eligibility details for the Cultural Scholarship are not fully documented on the current scholarship pages, indicating the program was offered previously. The scholarship reflects the school's commitment to nurturing cultural pursuits alongside academic excellence and sporting achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students; Must demonstrate talent and passion for cultural pursuits; Selection based on cultural achievement and potential contribution to school community
special_conditions: Scholarships closed for 2026. Program details limited as current offerings not fully published.

overview: Christ's Church Mandurah offers three generous music scholarships of $1,000 each to Frederick Irwin Anglican School students. The scholarship supports musicians and singers to continue pursuing their musical passions.
description: Three generous music scholarships of $1,000 each are being offered by Christ's Church Mandurah for students at Frederick Irwin Anglican School for 2025. These scholarships are provided to support a musician or singer to continue to pursue their passions and provide opportunities to perform at Christ's Church, Mandurah. Each award provides $1,000 in financial support. A condition of the scholarship is that the student is willing to perform on occasion at Christ's Church, Mandurah. Application letters are to be addressed to the Head of Arts, Mrs Jenny Crutchett, and emailed to principalpa@fias.wa.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Frederick Irwin Anglican School students; Must be a musician or singer; Must be willing to perform on occasion at Christ's Church, Mandurah; Application by letter detailing musical background and interest
special_conditions: Offered for 2025 only (external funding). Recipients must be willing to perform on occasion at Christ's Church, Mandurah. Applications emailed to Mrs Jenny Crutchett, Head of Arts.

overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Year 6 Brass Scholarship Programme to provide primary students with access to brass instrumental tuition. The scholarship supports eligible Year 6 students in developing brass performance skills.
description: The Year 6 Brass Scholarship Programme provides brass instrumental music lessons for Year 6 students at no cost for the remainder of the Year 6 school year. The programme is offered through the Performing Arts Department and provides tuition in brass instruments. Scholarship lessons cover the remainder of the Year 6 school year. After one year, students have the option to continue as paying students within the normal structure of the Instrumental/Vocal programme. This programme represents investment in early music development and encourages brass instrument uptake at the primary level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students; Application through Performing Arts Department; Selection based on interest in brass instruments and musical potential
special_conditions: Lessons provided for one year (for remainder of Year 6 school year). After one year, students have option to continue as paying students within normal Instrumental/Vocal programme structure.

overview: The Non-Government Schools Assistance Scheme is an Australian Government program providing financial support to secondary students at Frederick Irwin Anglican School. The scheme assists eligible students and families with school-related expenses.
description: The Non-Government Schools Assistance Scheme provides financial assistance of up to $350 to eligible secondary students in Years 7 to 12 towards school expenses. This is a government-administered program for non-government schools. Students Years 7 to 12 may be eligible for up to $350 towards school expenses, subject to meeting eligibility criteria. The application period closes on 2 April 2026. Eligible families must meet specific income and other criteria determined by the Australian Government scheme. The school provides application support and information to eligible families.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 to Year 12 students; Must meet Australian Government non-government school assistance scheme eligibility criteria; Eligibility determined by income and other factors set by government
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Australian Government administered program. Eligibility subject to non-government schools assistance scheme criteria. Applications close 2 April 2026.

school_name: Fremantle Language Development Centre - Bull Creek Campus
Fremantle Language Development Centre - Beeliar Campus

program_name: Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Academic Program
overview: Fremantle College offers a Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Academic Program for children with exceptional academic ability. The program enables students to engage and flourish in a learning environment that is intellectually stimulating and rigorous, with tailored curriculum to accelerate, extend and enrich learning for each student.
description: Fremantle College has one class of up to 32 students in the Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Academic Program in each cohort, expanding each year. Students are timetabled together as one class for English, Maths, Science and HASS from Year 7 through to Year 10. In Year 11 and Year 12, students will select their own curriculum pathway but remain part of the Gifted and Talented Program. The program does not provide direct financial support, but successful applicants may be eligible to apply for the Olive A Lewis Scholarship (state-funded, ranging $250-$1,500 per year for those demonstrating financial need) or other Department of Education scholarships. Selection is based on performance in the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET), which comprises four components: Reading Comprehension, Communicating Ideas in Writing, Quantitative Reasoning and Abstract Reasoning. City Beach Residential College is available for students who reside remotely.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, 9, 10 and 11; Must sit the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET); Exceptional academic ability required; Australian citizenship or permanent resident status preferred for most related scholarships
special_conditions: All applicants are required to sit the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET). Applicants who complete the ASET receive an individual performance report and, based on their result, may be offered a place in the Gifted and Talented Program. Students remain in the program through Year 12 with flexible curriculum selection in senior years. City Beach Residential College available for remote students.

overview: Fremantle College offers the Financial Secondary Assistance Scheme to support students and families facing financial hardship. The scheme provides assistance to eligible students to help them access educational opportunities at the college.
description: The Financial Secondary Assistance Scheme is a government-funded program administered through the Department of Education to assist families with financial difficulties in supporting their child's secondary education. Students who demonstrate financial need may be eligible for assistance with school contributions, charges and costs. The scheme operates separately from the Student Equity Fund, which is a local college fundraising initiative. Hard copies of application forms are available online and from college reception. Families are encouraged to contact the college early to discuss flexible payment arrangements for contributions and charges. The specific financial value provided varies based on individual family circumstances and eligibility assessment. No formal application fee is required to apply for assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial hardship; Students attending Fremantle College; Australian resident families preferred; Income and circumstance assessment required; Separate from Student Equity Fund eligibility
special_conditions: Application forms available online and from college reception. Families must provide financial documentation and information about circumstances. Decisions made by the college following assessment. Continuation of support is dependent on ongoing financial need assessment. Students may simultaneously receive SAS payments and apply for Student Equity Fund support.

overview: The Olive A Lewis Scholarship is a state-funded program available to students in the Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Academic Program who demonstrate significant financial need. The scholarship provides direct financial assistance to eligible families to support their child's continued participation in the program.
description: Children enrolled in a Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Academic Program in Years 7, 8, 9 or 10 are eligible to apply for the Olive A Lewis Scholarship. The scholarship is provided for students whose further education may be restricted due to financial need. Scholarships are means-assessed and awarded to those families deemed to have the most significant financial disadvantage. Scholarships range between $250 and $1,500 per year. The amount of funding each year varies according to the annual performance of the trust fund managed by Perpetual Trustees based in Sydney. Payments are made by cheque direct to families. Students can apply in consecutive years, provided they remain in the Gifted and Talented program. Families who have little or no difficulty providing for their child's education expenses are not encouraged to apply. Families are required to supply detailed financial information for assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Entrance Academic Program; Years 7, 8, 9 or 10; Demonstrated significant financial need; Means-assessed; Can reapply annually while remaining in program; Families encouraged not to apply if no genuine financial hardship
special_conditions: Means-assessed based on detailed financial information. Selection panel reviews all applications within context of provided documentation. Families required to supply detailed financial information or application removed from consideration. All information kept strictly confidential. Applications must be submitted by deadline; late, faxed or emailed applications not accepted. Amount varies annually based on trust fund performance. Scholarships valid only for students remaining in Gifted and Talented program.

overview: Fremantle College has established the Student Equity Fund to support students facing financial challenges. The fund is supported by donations from the community and allows those who can afford to do so to "Pay It Forward" to help the school support the educational experience of students who may be facing financial difficulties.
description: The Fremantle College Board has established the Student Equity Fund to allow those who can afford to do so to "Pay It Forward" to help the school to support the educational experience of students who may be facing financial challenges. The Student Equity Fund accepts non-tax-deductible donations from local businesses and community members, parents and carers. Donations can be made at the time of payments for Voluntary Contributions and Charges or at any time during the school year, on an ad hoc basis. The school is seeking tax-deductible status for this Fund but there is no guarantee that tax-deductible status will be obtained. Applications for the Student Equity Fund are now open. The fund is different to and separate from the Financial Secondary Assistance Scheme. Students who receive Financial Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) payments are also eligible to apply to the Student Equity Fund.
eligibility_criteria: Students at Fremantle College facing financial challenges; Open to all year levels; Separate eligibility from SAS scheme; Students receiving SAS payments also eligible; Community nominations supported
special_conditions: Applications accepted online via provided form. Donations are currently non-tax-deductible. Fund is managed separately from Student Assistance Scheme. Support depends on available donations and assessed need. Families can apply simultaneously for SAS and Student Equity Fund assistance.

overview: Geraldton Christian College offers Entrance Scholarships to prospective students entering Year 7, Year 9 and Year 11. The Principal invites applications for these merit-based scholarships, which are subject to availability in each year level.
description: Geraldton Christian College invites scholarship applications for students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11 (subject to availability in these year levels) for 2026. Applications are open to prospective Geraldton Christian College students. The selection process involves an application submitted through the online scholarship application form via Operoo. The exact financial benefit details (amount, percentage of fees, or duration) are not publicly listed on the scholarship page. Interested applicants should contact the Enrolments Officer at enrolments@gcc.wa.edu.au or 9938 9100 for complete details regarding scholarship value and selection criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Open to prospective students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Subject to availability in each year level; Applicants must be currently enrolled or transitioning into the specified year level
special_conditions: Scholarship availability is subject to year level and is determined by the Principal. Scholarships are awarded subject to the college's assessment of applicant merit and suitability.

overview: Georgiana Molloy Anglican School offers Secondary Instrumental/Vocal Scholarships to talented and dedicated students of music. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to instrumental or vocal performance.
description: In 2026, four Secondary Instrumental/Vocal Scholarships will be awarded to students entering Years 7 to 12. Each scholarship is valued at $750 per annum for one year and will contribute towards vocal or instrumental tuition. Recipients will be required to contribute to the music program of St Mary's Anglican Church Busselton by participating in at least one Sunday Service per month during the school year, which may be in the form of band membership, choir, small ensemble or as a soloist. It is essential for the applicant to be able to fulfil this commitment. The scholarship application process involves an audition demonstrating musical proficiency.
eligibility_criteria: Current GMAS student; Entering Years 7 to 12 in 2026; Demonstrated musical talent; Must be able to commit to at least one Sunday Service per month at St Mary's Anglican Church Busselton during the school year
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is for one year only. Recipients must support the music program of St Mary's Anglican Church Busselton by participating in at least one Sunday Service per month during the school year. This may be in the form of band membership, choir, small ensemble or as a soloist. It is essential for the applicant to be able to fulfil this commitment.

overview: Georgiana Molloy Anglican School awards All-Rounder Scholarships each year to students who display talent, aptitude and motivation in a variety of areas. These scholarships recognise well-rounded students who excel across multiple disciplines.
description: These all-rounder scholarships are awarded each year to students who display talent, aptitude and motivation in a variety of areas including academia, arts, leadership, physical ability and service to the community. Recipients are selected based on demonstration of sustained commitment to their studies and full, constructive participation in the life of the school across their interests, aptitudes and abilities. The scholarship is valued at 50% of tuition fees for the remaining duration of schooling at GMAS. All scholarship holders are expected to display ongoing sustained commitment to their studies and to take a full and constructive part in the life of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 10 in 2027; Must demonstrate talent, aptitude and motivation in a variety of areas; Must demonstrate commitment to academics, leadership, community service, or physical activities; Must be willing to contribute to the holistic life of the school
special_conditions: Scholarship is for the remaining duration of schooling at GMAS. All scholarship holders must display ongoing sustained commitment to their studies and take a full and constructive part in the life of the school in the areas of their interests, aptitudes and abilities.

overview: Georgiana Molloy Anglican School offers Old Georgian Scholarships to students who display talent, aptitude and motivation in a variety of areas. These prestigious scholarships recognise well-rounded achievers across multiple disciplines.
description: Old Georgian scholarships are awarded to students who display talent, aptitude and motivation in a variety of areas including academia, arts, leadership, physical ability and service to the community. Recipients are selected based on demonstration of sustained commitment to their studies and full, constructive participation in the life of the school across their interests, aptitudes and abilities. The scholarship is valued at 100% of tuition fees for the remaining duration of schooling at GMAS. This represents full fee remission and is one of the school's most prestigious scholarship offerings. All scholarship holders are expected to display ongoing sustained commitment to their studies and to take a full and constructive part in the life of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 to Year 10 in 2027; Must demonstrate talent, aptitude and motivation in a variety of areas; Must demonstrate exceptional commitment to academics, leadership, community service, or physical activities; Must be willing to contribute substantially to the holistic life of the school
special_conditions: Scholarship is for the remaining duration of schooling at GMAS. All scholarship holders must display ongoing sustained commitment to their studies and take a full and constructive part in the life of the school in the areas of their interests, aptitudes and abilities.

overview: Georgiana Molloy Anglican School offers Indigenous Scholarships to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who demonstrate commitment and motivation in areas of academia, leadership and community. The scholarship recognises Indigenous students who display talent and dedication to their studies and school community.
description: These scholarships are open to Indigenous students who demonstrate commitment and motivation in areas of academia, leadership and community. Students who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander who are due to commence Year 7 to Year 10 in 2026 are eligible to apply. The scholarship is valued at up to 100% of tuition fees for the remaining duration of schooling at GMAS. Students must have the support of their family and/or community, which will require emotional support and some contribution to financial support. The level of financial contribution is reviewed on an individual and confidential basis. Recipients must demonstrate sustained commitment to their studies and active participation in school life.
eligibility_criteria: Students who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Due to commence Year 7 to Year 10 in 2026; Must demonstrate commitment and motivation in areas of academia, leadership and community; Must have the support of their family and/or community; Family or community must provide emotional support and some financial contribution reviewed on individual and confidential basis
special_conditions: Scholarship is for the remaining duration of schooling at GMAS. Students must have the support of their family and/or community, which will require emotional support and some contribution to financial support. The level of financial contribution is reviewed on an individual and confidential basis.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Year 6 Brass Scholarship Programme to support students in primary school who demonstrate musical talent on brass instruments. The scholarship provides free instrumental instruction for one year and recognises exceptional musical ability in primary students.
description: The Year 6 Brass Scholarship Programme provides free brass instrument lessons for eligible Year 6 students for the remainder of the school year. The scholarship supports students with demonstrated musical talent and achievement in brass performance. After the scholarship year concludes, students have the option to continue as paying students within the normal structure of the Instrumental/Vocal programme. Selection is based on audition and demonstrated musical ability. This programme is delivered through the Performing Arts Department and fosters musical excellence among primary school students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students; Demonstrated musical talent on brass instruments; Open to both current Frederick Irwin students and external applicants
special_conditions: Scholarship lessons are for one year only (for the remainder of the Year 6 school year). After one year, students have the option to continue as paying students within the normal structure of the Instrumental/Vocal programme.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in secondary students entering Years 7, 10 and 11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning during their secondary education.
description: The Academic Scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees for successful applicants at Years 7, 10 and 11, tenable for six years at Year 7 entry. For Year 11 entry, the scholarship provides 50% tuition fee remission for both Year 11 and Year 12. All other costs, levies and charges remain the responsibility of parents. Selection is based on a common scholarship test and a student's past and present school performance. Scholarships for 2026 have now closed for applications. Continuation of the scholarship from one year to the next depends upon satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, Year 10 or Year 11; Students must demonstrate high academic ability and achievement during secondary education; All school sectors eligible; Eligible students must be ready to contribute to the school community
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 have now closed. Students can apply for both Academic, Music Performance and Sport Scholarships, but if successful will be awarded only one Scholarship. Continuation depends upon satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers Music Performance Scholarships to recognise students with musical talent and good academic ability entering Years 7 and 11. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate excellence in instrumental or vocal performance and are committed to contributing to the school's music programme.
description: The Music Performance Scholarship provides 50% remission of tuition fees for successful applicants. Year 7 scholarships are tenable for six years (Years 7-12), while Year 11 scholarships are for both Year 11 and Year 12 (two years). All other costs, levies and charges remain the responsibility of parents. Selection is based on an audition involving a 20-minute performance assessment comprising: prepared pieces and technical work (10 minutes), aural/perception tests (5 minutes), and an interview assessing music theory knowledge and background (5 minutes). Audition performance must not exceed 10 minutes total playing time. Continuation depends upon satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution to music in the school. Scholarship students must participate fully in the school music programme including instrumental lessons, bands, ensembles and performances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 11; Must demonstrate musical talent with good academic ability and achievement during secondary education; Audition required for all applicants; School Reports required from external applicants; Open to both current Frederick Irwin students and external applicants
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 have now closed. Total audition time approximately 20 minutes. Students can apply for both Academic, Music Performance and Sport Scholarships, but if successful will be awarded only one Scholarship. Continuation of Scholarship depends upon satisfactory performance, conduct and contribution to Music. Scholarship students must participate fully in the School Music programme including instrumental lessons, bands, ensembles and performances.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers the Kwobadiny Sports Scholarship to support elite athletes entering Years 7-11 in secondary school. The scholarship recognises students with exceptional sporting talent and potential, and supports their development as elite athletes at the school.
description: The inaugural Kwobadiny Sports Scholarship is offered in conjunction with the commissioning of the state-of-the-art Kwobadiny Sports Centre. The scholarship provides part-scholarships for Years 7 to 10 focused on developing potential athletes, and full scholarships for Years 11 and 12 for those excelling in their field of sport and their contribution to the culture of sport at Frederick Irwin. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting excellence and potential. Scholarships for 2026 have now closed for applications. Eligibility for Year 7 to 12 scholarships is open to both current Frederick Irwin students and external applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10 and 11; Must demonstrate elite or exceptional sporting talent and potential; Eligible for both current Frederick Irwin students and external applicants; Years 7-10 receive part-scholarship; Years 11-12 receive full scholarship (for those excelling and contributing to sport culture)
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 have now closed. Part-scholarships awarded for Years 7-10 to develop potential athletes. Full scholarships awarded for Years 11-12 for those excelling in their field of sport and contributing to the culture of sport at Frederick Irwin.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Three generous music scholarships are offered by Christ's Church Mandurah to Frederick Irwin Anglican School students who demonstrate musical talent and are willing to support the church community. The scholarships support musicians and singers to continue their musical passions and provide performance opportunities.
description: Three generous music scholarships of $1,000 each are offered by Christ's Church Mandurah for students at Frederick Irwin Anglican School for 2025. These scholarships are designed to support a musician or singer to continue to pursue their passions and provide opportunities to perform at Christ's Church, Mandurah. Each scholarship is valued at $1,000. A condition of the scholarship is that the student is willing to perform on occasion at Christ's Church, Mandurah. These scholarships are designed to support students with musical talent, with good academic ability and achievement during secondary education. Selection is based on instrument or voice performance and student's past and present performance at school.
eligibility_criteria: Students at Frederick Irwin Anglican School; Must demonstrate musical talent with good academic ability and achievement; Must be willing to perform on occasion at Christ's Church, Mandurah; Musician or singer seeking to pursue musical passions
special_conditions: Three scholarships of $1,000 each available. A condition of the scholarship is that the student is willing to perform on occasion at Christ's Church, Mandurah. Application letters must be sent to The Head of Arts, Mrs Jenny Crutchett, emailed to principalpa@fias.wa.edu.au by Monday 7 April 2025. Scholarships are offered for 2025.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School provides information about Non-Government Schools Financial Assistance available to eligible secondary students. This programme offers financial support towards school expenses for families experiencing financial hardship.
description: Frederick Irwin Anglican School provides Non-Government Schools Financial Assistance to eligible secondary students in Years 7 to 12. Students may be eligible for up to $350 towards school expenses under this means-tested financial assistance programme. Applications are assessed based on demonstrated financial need and eligibility criteria set by the Non-Government Schools Financial Assistance scheme. This programme provides support to families experiencing financial hardship to ensure students can continue their education. Applications for this support close on 2 April 2026. Further information about eligibility procedures is available through the school's enrolment office.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 12; Must meet non-government schools financial assistance eligibility criteria; Financial need assessment required; Applications must be submitted by the closing deadline
special_conditions: Means-tested based on demonstrated financial need. Support up to $350 towards school expenses. Applications close 2 April 2026. Eligibility and procedures as determined by Non-Government Schools Financial Assistance scheme.

school_name: Frederick Irwin Anglican School - Halls Head Campus
overview: Frederick Irwin Anglican School offers Cultural Scholarships to recognise students who contribute to cultural pursuits and diversity within the school community. The scholarship supports students demonstrating excellence in cultural activities and performance.
description: Frederick Irwin Anglican School has offered Cultural Scholarships to secondary students. These scholarships were designed to support students with talent and achievement in cultural pursuits. However, the Cultural Scholarship programme has now closed for 2026. The school previously recognised the importance of cultural diversity and cultural pursuits as part of its holistic approach to education. Further information about the structure, value and eligibility criteria for this programme when it reopens would be available through the school's admissions office.
eligibility_criteria: Open to secondary students; Demonstrated talent and achievement in cultural pursuits; Contribution to school's cultural activities; Open to both current Frederick Irwin students and external applicants
special_conditions: These scholarships have now closed for 2026. Further details about reopening dates and specific eligibility criteria should be obtained from the school's admissions office.

overview: Freshwater Bay Primary School offers the Instrumental Music School Service (IMMS) Program, which provides selected students with free instrumental music instruction. The program identifies musically talented students through aptitude testing and offers opportunities to develop instrumental proficiency while participating in enrichment activities.
description: The Instrumental Music School Service (IMMS) Program provides free instrumental music lessons to selected students based on musical aptitude testing. Instrumental tuition is offered in violin beginning in Year 3, cello in Year 4, and trumpet in Year 5. The program offers non-monetary benefits in the form of free specialist instrumental instruction, which would otherwise require private purchase. Students who are selected participate in a range of music enrichment activities and ensemble opportunities. Selection is based entirely on musical aptitude testing conducted by qualified IMMS instructors from the Department of Education Western Australia. The program requires commitment from students to attend lessons reliably, participate in ensemble activities, and maintain consistent practice.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 3, Year 4, and Year 5; Selection based on musical aptitude testing; Must demonstrate musical ability and potential; No financial threshold specified; Open to all enrolled students of the school
special_conditions: Students selected for the IMMS Program must enrol in specialist class music for the duration of the scholarship and maintain a minimum pass grade in this subject. Students must attend the appropriate music ensemble for their instrument regularly. Students are expected to practice consistently to maintain expected progress levels. Lack of attendance or poor engagement may result in loss of scholarship.

overview: Geraldton Senior High School's Academic Program of Excellence (APEX) is designed for high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The program provides an intellectually stimulating and rigorous learning environment where like-minded students extend their learning across all subject areas while attending their local school.
description: APEX, delivered in partnership with the CoRE Learning Foundation, combines academic challenge with real-world STEM applications. The program provides enrichment and extension activities as well as accelerated curriculum in Maths, English, Science and Humanities. Students in APEX have access to tailored learning, mentoring, and extension opportunities. The program includes participation in the Tournament of Minds interschool competition and opportunities to become published authors through the APEX Annual. There is no specific financial value mentioned for this program, as it is an enrichment program for students continuing their regular enrolment. APEX is available for Year 7 to Year 10 students and includes commitment to maintaining high attendance, participating in groupwork and STEM project-based learning, attending excursions and incursions, and maintaining good academic results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Students in Year 6 2025 may apply; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability based on NAPLAN reports or Year 5/6 school reports; Recommendation letter from Primary School Principal or Year 6 Teacher required if NAPLAN results not available; Personal statement from student required; Out of local intake students accepted based on capacity and Department of Education policy; Applications assessed on first come, first serve basis pending capacity
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate commitment to the program throughout Year 7 to retain their position including maintaining high attendance, participating in groupwork and STEM project-based learning, attending excursions and incursions and maintaining good academic results across Maths, English, HASS and Science. Students must support the school's commitment to excellence and academic rigor.

overview: Geraldton Senior High School offers the Accelerated Music Program (AMP) to all students. The program provides instrumental lessons and a dedicated music class where students develop their musicianship and love of music in a specialised and supportive environment.
description: The Accelerated Music Program offers students instrumental lessons alongside a dedicated Music Class. Students build skills in performance, songwriting, theory, and analysis. The program is designed to prepare students for a Certificate II in Music in their senior years at Geraldton SHS and to continue exploring their musical interests as a career, vocation, or hobby throughout their life. The specific financial value of the program is not stated on the website, though it involves instrumental lessons which may have associated costs. Students enter the program at Year 7 and continue through Year 12 if they maintain commitment to the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Open to students entering Year 7; Students must have interest in music; Continuation in the program requires demonstrated commitment and consistent participation
special_conditions: Program provides instrumental lessons and dedicated music class. Students build skills in performance, songwriting, theory, and analysis. Preparation for Certificate II in Music in senior years.

overview: Geraldton Senior High School supports students undertaking the Department of Education WA's Gifted and Talented Online program. This program provides students with exceptional academic ability the opportunity to engage in an intellectually stimulating and rigorous learning environment while living at home and attending their local school instead of relocating to Perth.
description: The Gifted and Talented Online Program is delivered through the Department of Education WA and supported at Geraldton Senior High School. Students with exceptional academic ability can access a rigorous and intellectually challenging curriculum through an online learning environment. The program enables students to access enrichment and extension opportunities tailored to their learning needs. There is no specific financial value as this is a state-based enrichment program. Within the APEX class, students can receive additional support and mentoring to flourish if they are part of the Gifted and Talented Online Program. The program operates continuously throughout the student's school years.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Nomination or application to Department of Education Gifted and Talented Online program required; Acceptance based on demonstrated academic achievement
special_conditions: Program delivered in partnership with Department of Education WA. Students access online learning environment while attending their local school. APEX program provides dedicated classroom support and mentoring for GATE participants.

overview: Geraldton Senior High School provides access to the Clontarf Academy program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men. The program offers flexible learning with full-time mentors in a welcoming, inclusive environment designed to foster positive learning and personal growth.
description: The Clontarf Academy at Geraldton Senior High School provides specialized support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men. The program is staffed by full-time mentors and offers flexible learning opportunities in a dedicated, inclusive space. The program is designed to foster positive learning and personal growth for participants. While specific financial information is not detailed on the school website, the Clontarf Academy typically provides support services and mentoring as part of its non-monetary benefits. The program operates year-round for eligible students from Year 7 through Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Male students only; Must be enrolled at Geraldton Senior High School
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Program specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men. Full-time mentoring available. Designed to support positive learning and personal growth outcomes.

overview: Geraldton Senior High School offers the Daniel Brierty Program for Year 7 students. This program is coordinated at the Year 7 level to support student engagement, leadership development, and positive outcomes for young students entering secondary school.
description: The Daniel Brierty Program is a Year 7 focused initiative at Geraldton Senior High School. The program has a dedicated Program Coordinator for Year 7 who manages and implements the program. While specific details about the program structure and financial benefits are not extensively detailed on the school website, it appears to be an engagement and support program for Year 7 students. The program operates throughout the school year to provide continuity of support and development for students in their first year of senior secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Students must be enrolled at Geraldton Senior High School
special_conditions: Program specifically for Year 7 students. Coordinated at Year 7 level. Supports student engagement and positive outcomes in transition to secondary school.

overview: Geraldton Senior High School participates in the Stars Foundation program for Year 11 and Year 12 students. The program is coordinated at the senior level to support student development, leadership and positive outcomes for students in their final years of secondary education.
description: The Stars Foundation is a program available to Year 11 and Year 12 students at Geraldton Senior High School, with a dedicated Program Coordinator for Years 11 & 12. The Stars Foundation program provides support and development opportunities for senior students. While specific financial details are not extensive on the school website, the program operates as a non-monetary support initiative. The program coordinator works to implement and manage the program throughout the senior school years to support student achievement and wellbeing.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 11; Open to students in Year 12; Students must be enrolled at Geraldton Senior High School
special_conditions: Program specifically for Year 11 and Year 12 students. Coordinated at senior school level. Supports student development and positive outcomes in final years of secondary education.

overview: Geraldton Grammar School offers the Secondary School Scholarship to recognise academic achievement and ability in students entering secondary. The scholarship provides remission of half the tuition fees for eligible years. Selection is based on performance in the school's entrance assessment.
description: The Secondary School Scholarship provides 50% Fee Reduction on tuition fees. Students entering from Year 6 who are successful receive the scholarship for Years 7, 8 and 9. Students entering from Year 7 who are successful receive the scholarship for Years 8 and 9. Additionally, students entering from Year 9 who are successful receive 50% Fee Reduction for Years 10, 11 and 12, while students entering from Year 10 who are successful receive the scholarship for Years 11 and 12. Selection is based on a competitive scholarship test administered by ACER. Applications for scholarships usually close in March of each year, with testing occurring in April/May.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6, Year 7, Year 9, or Year 10; Must achieve success in ACER scholarship test; Applications typically close in March each year
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship duration varies by year level of entry. Scholarships are subject to continued satisfactory academic performance and conduct.

overview: Geraldton Grammar School offers music bursaries to support students demonstrating talent and commitment in specified brass instruments. Bursaries for Trombone recognise musical achievement and ability.
description: Music bursaries and scholarships are available for students participating in the Instrumental Music Program, specifically for Trombone. These bursaries provide financial support to enable talented young musicians to continue their musical development through the school's instrumental program. The program operates for Years 5 to 12 with weekly lessons and ensemble rehearsals. Students must demonstrate commitment to their musical studies and participation in school ensembles. The specific financial value of the bursary and exact duration conditions should be confirmed directly with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Must participate in Instrumental Music Program; Must demonstrate musical ability on Trombone; Contact Director of Music for application details
special_conditions: Bursary applications should be directed to Ewan Stevens, Director of Music. Minimum 80% attendance at lessons required throughout the year.

overview: Geraldton Grammar School offers music bursaries to support students demonstrating talent and commitment in specified brass instruments. Bursaries for Baritone Horn recognise musical achievement and ability.
description: Music bursaries and scholarships are available for students participating in the Instrumental Music Program, specifically for Baritone Horn. These bursaries provide financial support to enable talented young musicians to continue their musical development through the school's instrumental program. The program operates for Years 5 to 12 with weekly lessons and ensemble rehearsals. Students must demonstrate commitment to their musical studies and participation in school ensembles. The specific financial value of the bursary and exact duration conditions should be confirmed directly with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Must participate in Instrumental Music Program; Must demonstrate musical ability on Baritone Horn; Contact Director of Music for application details
special_conditions: Bursary applications should be directed to Ewan Stevens, Director of Music. Minimum 80% attendance at lessons required throughout the year.

overview: Geraldton Grammar School offers music bursaries to support students demonstrating talent and commitment in specified brass instruments. Bursaries for Tuba recognise musical achievement and ability.
description: Music bursaries and scholarships are available for students participating in the Instrumental Music Program, specifically for Tuba. These bursaries provide financial support to enable talented young musicians to continue their musical development through the school's instrumental program. The program operates for Years 5 to 12 with weekly lessons and ensemble rehearsals. Students must demonstrate commitment to their musical studies and participation in school ensembles. The specific financial value of the bursary and exact duration conditions should be confirmed directly with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Must participate in Instrumental Music Program; Must demonstrate musical ability on Tuba; Contact Director of Music for application details
special_conditions: Bursary applications should be directed to Ewan Stevens, Director of Music. Minimum 80% attendance at lessons required throughout the year.

overview: Geraldton Grammar School offers music bursaries to support students demonstrating talent and commitment in specified stringed instruments. Bursaries for Bass Guitar recognise musical achievement and ability.
description: Music bursaries and scholarships are available for students participating in the Instrumental Music Program, specifically for Bass Guitar. These bursaries provide financial support to enable talented young musicians to continue their musical development through the school's instrumental program. The program operates for Years 5 to 12 with weekly lessons and ensemble rehearsals including Rock Band, Jazz Band, and other ensembles. Students must demonstrate commitment to their musical studies and participation in school ensembles. The specific financial value of the bursary and exact duration conditions should be confirmed directly with the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Must participate in Instrumental Music Program; Must demonstrate musical ability on Bass Guitar; Contact Director of Music for application details
special_conditions: Bursary applications should be directed to Ewan Stevens, Director of Music. Minimum 80% attendance at lessons required throughout the year.

program_name: Golden Hill Steiner School High School Scholarship
overview: Golden Hill Steiner School offers high school scholarships to current and future students entering Classes 7–10. The scholarship programme aims to make Steiner education more accessible across the regional community, giving deserving students the opportunity to benefit from this unique educational approach.
description: The Golden Hill Steiner School High School Scholarship provides a substantial financial subsidy to eligible students entering high school. The scholarship provides a subsidy of 75% of annual tuition fees for eligible students for the duration of their high school enrolment at GHSS. The financial benefit applies to tuition fees only and excludes other levies and fees payable by all students (including camp levies, swimming lesson levies, building levy, consumables levy, special electives costs, uniforms and textbooks). Applications for 2026 close on 19 September 2025, and all school fees incurred to date must be paid in full prior to the first year of high school enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and future students; Students entering Class 7, Class 8, Class 9, or Class 10; Students born in 2009–2012 (for 2026 intake); Application form submission required
special_conditions: Scholarship covers tuition fees only and excludes other levies and fees payable by all students (including camp levies, swimming lesson levies, building levy, consumables levy, special electives costs, uniforms and textbooks). All school fees incurred to date must be paid in full prior to the first year of high school enrolment.

overview: Golden Hill Steiner School offers a fee discount to families holding a current Health Care Card. This programme recognises families experiencing financial hardship and provides meaningful support to make Steiner education more accessible.
description: From 2025, Golden Hill Steiner School offers a 5% discount on annual tuition fees to holders of a current Health Care Card. This financial assistance programme is designed to support families experiencing financial hardship and demonstrates the school's commitment to making quality education accessible. The discount applies to tuition fees only and families should contact the school's Bursar to discuss payment options and how this discount can be applied to their specific circumstances. The school recognises that school fees can be a significant financial commitment and is committed to supporting those who choose a Steiner education for their child or children.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a current Health Care Card; Families of all year levels eligible; Application to Bursar required
special_conditions: Must hold current Health Care Card; Discount applies to tuition fees only; Contact school Bursar for application details; Available from 2025 onwards

overview: Golden Hill Steiner School offers a fee discount to families with multiple children enrolled at the school. This programme acknowledges the financial commitment families make when educating multiple children and provides meaningful support.
description: Golden Hill Steiner School offers a discount on tuition fees for families who have more than one child enrolled in the school. This programme is part of the school's commitment to supporting families and making Steiner education accessible to more students. The school recognises that school fees can be a significant financial commitment for families, particularly those with multiple children, and offers various support mechanisms including payment options and fee discounts. Families interested in learning more about the sibling discount and how it applies to their situation should contact the school's Bursar, who can discuss payment options and the specific discount available.
eligibility_criteria: Must have more than one child enrolled in the school; Families of all year levels eligible; Contact school Bursar for application details
special_conditions: Applicable when more than one child is enrolled at the school; Contact Bursar for specific discount details; Discount applies to tuition fees

overview: Girrawheen Senior High School offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to Year 6 students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning. This scholarship recognises students with exceptional academic ability and provides substantial financial support to assist with educational expenses during Year 7.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship provides students with school fees and uniforms to the value of $235, plus school uniform to Year 7 to the value of $325, with an additional $200 to spend on further educational expenses such as excursions, calculators, or sports uniforms. The total value is $760. Recipients are selected through an application process that assesses academic merit and performance. The scholarship covers the cost of educational materials and resources to support student success during Year 7 transition. These benefits cannot be exchanged for cash and are provided as in-kind support for educational purposes.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated academic excellence and merit; Intending to enrol at Girrawheen Senior High School for Year 7; Australian citizen or eligible resident; Open to co-educational students
special_conditions: Scholarships valued at $760 are available to students in Year 6 who will be enrolling in Girrawheen SHS for Year 7. Recipients typically receive scholarships at Year 6 Primary School Graduation Ceremonies. Benefits are provided as school fees, uniforms, and educational expenses and cannot be exchanged for cash.

overview: Girrawheen Senior High School offers the STEM Scholarship to Year 6 students with demonstrated interest and aptitude in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics. This scholarship recognises students committed to pursuing excellence in STEM fields and supports their transition to secondary education.
description: The STEM Scholarship provides recipients with school fees to Year 7 valued at $235, school uniform to Year 7 valued at $325, plus an additional $200 for educational expenses including excursions, calculators, and equipment. The total scholarship value is $760. Students are selected through an application and assessment process evaluating their STEM aptitude and academic performance in science and mathematics. The school is recognised for its strong STEM Club and partnerships including International STEM competition participation. These benefits are non-refundable and cannot be exchanged for cash.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated interest and aptitude in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics; Strong academic performance in STEM subjects; Intending to enrol at Girrawheen SHS for Year 7; Open to co-educational students
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for Year 7 entry. Recipients must demonstrate sustained interest and achievement in STEM subjects. School benefits are provided as fees, uniforms, and educational resources and cannot be exchanged for cash.

overview: Girrawheen Senior High School offers the Performing Arts Academy Scholarship to Year 6 students demonstrating exceptional talent in dance, drama, or music. This scholarship recognises students who have successfully auditioned into the Performing Arts Academy and supports their artistic development during secondary education.
description: The Performing Arts Academy Scholarship provides school fees to Year 7 valued at $235, school uniform to Year 7 valued at $325, plus an additional $200 to spend on further educational expenses including artistic equipment and performance opportunities. The total value is $760. Eligibility requires successful audition into the Performing Arts Academy, which is the primary selection mechanism.
