The school maintains strong performance arts programs including partnerships with WA Youth Theatre Company and Youth Ballet WA, with previous students gaining scholarships to WAAPA. Benefits are provided as fees, uniforms, and educational resources and cannot be exchanged for cash.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Successful audition into Performing Arts Academy required for eligibility; Demonstrated performing arts talent in dance, drama, or music; Intending to enrol at Girrawheen SHS for Year 7; Open to co-educational students
special_conditions: Successful audition into Performing Arts Academy required for eligibility. Scholarships are offered for Year 7 entry only. Recipients must maintain enrolment in the Performing Arts Academy. Benefits cannot be exchanged for cash and are provided as fees, uniforms, and educational expenses.

overview: Girrawheen Senior High School offers the Elite Sports Scholarship to Year 6 students demonstrating exceptional athletic ability and commitment to excellence in sport. This scholarship recognises students selected for the Elite Sports Program and supports their sporting development and education during Year 7.
description: The Elite Sports Scholarship provides school fees to Year 7 valued at $235, school uniform to Year 7 valued at $325, plus an additional $200 to spend on sporting equipment and educational expenses including Elite Sports uniforms and running shoes. The total scholarship value is $760. Students are selected through an assessment process identifying elite athletes with potential for high-level competitive sport. The school partners with Curtin University Athletics Club and other specialist sports providers. Recipients participate in weekly before-school training in athletics and basketball with a focus on fitness, training, growth mindset, and personal best. Benefits cannot be exchanged for cash.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Selected for Elite Sports Program; Demonstrated exceptional athletic ability; Commitment to competitive sport and training; Intending to enrol at Girrawheen SHS for Year 7; Open to co-educational students
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for Year 7 entry. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to the Elite Sports Program and maintain active participation in weekly training sessions. Benefits are provided as fees, uniforms, and sports equipment and cannot be exchanged for cash.

overview: Girrawheen Senior High School offers the Leadership and Citizenship Scholarship to Year 6 students who have demonstrated outstanding leadership qualities and commitment to community service. This scholarship recognises students who embody the school's values of Aspire, Respect, and Unite, and supports their continued development as future leaders.
description: The Leadership and Citizenship Scholarship provides school fees to Year 7 valued at $235, school uniform to Year 7 valued at $325, plus an additional $200 to spend on co-curricular activities and educational expenses. The total scholarship value is $760. Students are selected through an application and assessment process that evaluates demonstrated leadership, community engagement, and personal character. The school actively promotes student and staff leadership opportunities in a supportive environment with comprehensive mentoring and resilience programs. Recipients are expected to model school values and contribute to the school community. Benefits are provided as fees, uniforms, and educational resources and cannot be exchanged for cash.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated outstanding leadership qualities and initiative; Active involvement in community service or school leadership roles; Alignment with school values of Aspire, Respect, and Unite; Intending to enrol at Girrawheen SHS for Year 7; Open to co-educational students
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered for Year 7 entry. Recipients are expected to continue demonstrating leadership and community engagement throughout their enrolment. Benefits are provided as fees, uniforms, and co-curricular activities and cannot be exchanged for cash.

overview: Good Shepherd Catholic School offers scholarships to support students in developing their skills in the classroom and beyond. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate excellence across academic performance, co-curricular activities, and community involvement.
description: Good Shepherd Catholic School provides scholarships that open up opportunities for students to develop their skills in the classroom and beyond. From on-field matches to stage performances and academic competitions, the programs combine development with real-world experiences to support students to achieve their best. The school endeavours to make Catholic education available to all Catholic children within the Parish. Specific financial values, selection processes, and eligibility criteria are not publicly detailed on the school's website and interested families are advised to contact the school directly for complete information regarding scholarship applications, deadlines, and award amounts.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolled or applying to Good Shepherd Catholic School; Students should demonstrate commitment to learning and co-curricular involvement
special_conditions: Interested families should contact the school directly for detailed information about scholarship programs, eligibility requirements, and application processes.

overview: Goldfields Baptist College offers a Year 11 Fee Free Scholarship to all eligible Year 11 students. This scholarship recognises students entering Year 11 and provides full fee remission for their senior secondary studies.
description: Goldfields Baptist College provides full fee scholarships for all Year 11 students at the school. The scholarship offers full fee remission for the duration of Year 11 enrolment. This is a policy-based scholarship available to all students enrolling into Year 11, rather than a merit-based or competitive award. Senior Secondary at GBC is fee free, with this benefit extending to Year 11 students. Enrolment into some external-provider courses or withdrawal from courses prior to completion may result in families incurring some tuition costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 11; Must be enrolling as a Year 11 student at Goldfields Baptist College
special_conditions: Enrolment into some external-provider courses, or withdrawal from courses prior to completion, may result in families incurring some tuition costs. Senior Secondary at GBC is fee free. Note: As of 2026, Goldfields Baptist College will be offering Year 12 only in Senior Secondary (there will not be a Year 11 cohort in 2026).

overview: Great Southern Grammar awards the John Gilmour Academic Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement and positive personal qualities. Scholarships are available to students entering Years 7 to 12 who demonstrate exceptional results in the school's competitive academic scholarship examination and exhibit the school's values of Respect, Integrity, Courage and Compassion.
description: The John Gilmour Academic Scholarship is awarded to students who exhibit the values of Great Southern Grammar, demonstrate positive personal qualities and achieve a good result in the scholarship examination. Great Southern Grammar uses the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER) Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program (CSTP) as its scholarship examination. The selection process includes ACER registration, completion of an online student response form, and shortlisted candidates are invited to attend an interview. The scholarship provides recognition of academic excellence and supports students to pursue their educational goals at an independent school. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not publicly specified on the school's website, though it represents a merit-based award to assist with school costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2027; Must achieve a good result in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program; Must exhibit the values of Great Southern Grammar; Must demonstrate positive personal qualities; Must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Walk-in registrations incur a $180 fee in addition to the $140 application fee. Application fees are non-refundable. Shortlisted candidates will be requested to attend an interview.

overview: Great Southern Grammar offers the Music Scholarship to support students who demonstrate high levels of musicianship and commitment to musical development. The scholarship recognises exceptional musical talent across all instruments and provides assistance to help students maximise their musical education and opportunities at the school.
description: A music scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate a high level of musicianship to assist them to maximise their musical education. Music auditions take place at Great Southern Grammar where students may audition on any instrument and are required to present two contrasting pieces and relevant technical work, scales or exercises appropriate to their developmental level. The selection process evaluates musical proficiency and commitment to ongoing musical development. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress, select Specialist Music as an elective subject in Years 8 to 10, demonstrate exemplary behaviour, and maintain consistent attendance. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not specified on the public website but represents support for musical study.
eligibility_criteria: Must demonstrate a high level of musicianship; Must present two contrasting pieces and relevant technical work in audition; Must be willing to select Specialist Music as elective in Years 8-10; Must maintain satisfactory academic progress in all subjects; Must maintain exemplary behaviour and adherence to Code of Conduct; Must commit to weekly individual music lessons; Must be Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients may be required to undertake annual evaluations consisting of practical assessment, technical work and sight-reading to assess level of development. Parents of scholarship recipients must be fully supportive of school policies and act as advocates for school programmes. Parents remain responsible for uniform costs, personal stationery, outdoor education camps and voluntary activities. Recipients must represent the school for musical events and school activities as requested.

overview: The Spirit of Grammar Scholarship reflects the school's commitment to diversity, inclusion and belonging. It provides students who would not ordinarily have access to independent schooling the opportunity to succeed in their education through financial assistance based on demonstrated financial hardship and academic potential.
description: The Spirit of Grammar Scholarship is designed to support students from Year 5 to Year 12 who demonstrate financial hardship and strong academic potential. Selection criteria include demonstration of family financial hardship, strong academic standing with NAPLAN results at or above state average, active engagement in music, sport or other cocurricular activity, citizenship and positive contribution to school and community, and demonstration of determination, engagement and perseverance across all aspects of schooling. The scholarship covers up to 60% of tuition fees based on individual assessment of the application. The scholarship is available to day students only and is offered to new students to the school. Great Southern Grammar operates two intakes per year for this scholarship, subject to availability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students to the school only; Year 5 to Year 12; Demonstration of family financial hardship; Strong academic standing; NAPLAN results at state average or above; Active engagement in music, sport or other cocurricular activity; Citizenship and positive contribution to activities in and out of school; Demonstration of determination, engagement and perseverance in all aspects of schooling; Day students only; Visa holders may have additional eligibility criteria
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is open to new students to the school only. Available for day students only, not boarding students. GSG has two intakes of scholars per year, subject to availability. A second round may open later in the year if places become available. Visa holder eligibility criteria may apply.

overview: Great Southern Grammar offers Rural Boarding Scholarships to support well-rounded students from remote and rural Australia who would not otherwise have the opportunity to attend the school. The scholarship recognises general excellence across academic merit, personal achievement and community involvement, enabling regional families to access boarding education.
description: Rural Scholarships are provided to well-rounded individuals not already enrolled at Great Southern Grammar who demonstrate general excellence in many pursuits, including academic merit, personal achievement, and community involvement. The scholarship is designed for new boarding families who would be unable to attend Great Southern Grammar due to financial reasons. To be eligible, families must reside at least 85 kilometres from Albany. The scholarship provides financial assistance with boarding costs to ensure regional and rural students can access the school's boarding facilities and educational programs. The exact financial value and percentage of fee remission are determined on a case-by-case basis following individual assessment of applications.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from remote and rural Australia; New students to the school only; Must demonstrate general excellence in academic merit, personal achievement and community involvement; Families must reside at least 85km from Albany; Boarding students only; Unable to attend GSG for financial reasons; Visa holder eligibility criteria may apply
special_conditions: Scholarship is open to new boarding students to the school only. Families must reside at least 85km from Albany to be eligible. For new boarding families unable to attend GSG due to financial reasons. Visa holder eligibility criteria may apply. A second round of applications is expected to open later in the year, subject to availability.

overview: Great Southern Grammar is a partner school of Yalari, a not-for-profit organisation providing full boarding scholarships to Indigenous students for their entire secondary school education. The Yalari Scholarship recognises Indigenous students and provides comprehensive educational support through boarding at one of Australia's highest-achieving schools.
description: Great Southern Grammar is proud to be one of only a few Yalari partner schools in Western Australia. Since 2005, Yalari has provided Indigenous children with full scholarships for their entire secondary school education. Yalari is a not-for-profit organisation supporting Indigenous children's education and provides programmes that bring about generational change by giving Indigenous children the opportunity for a first-class education through full boarding scholarships at some of the highest achieving schools in Australia. The Rosemary Bishop Indigenous Education Scholarship programme invites applications once a year, with applications closing at the end of April for entry the following year. Pastoral care commitments are a major focus for Yalari, and the organisation aims to nurture the whole child. Recipients benefit from full boarding scholarships and comprehensive support services.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous students; Students must be from Years 7 to 12 eligible age; Must submit recent NAPLAN results and last two school reports; Must confirm eligibility for AbStudy or be willing to apply; Shortlisted students will be invited to interview with the Principal; Applications administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA)
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is administered by Yalari on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). Applications must be made to both the School and directly to MADALAH. Students must submit recent NAPLAN results and last two school reports. If successful, the family must complete the application for AbStudy. Shortlisted students are invited to interview with the Principal. Full boarding scholarship for entire secondary school education.

overview: Great Southern Grammar is a valued partner school of MADALAH, a renowned not-for-profit organisation dedicated to providing secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Indigenous students from remote and regional communities. MADALAH scholarships enable Indigenous students to access quality boarding school education and pursue their full potential.
description: Great Southern Grammar is proud to be one of MADALAH's partner schools in Western Australia. MADALAH Limited is a renowned not-for-profit organisation based in Western Australia dedicated to providing secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Indigenous students, primarily from remote and regional communities. MADALAH's aim is to offer learning opportunities and support, allowing students to attend WA's leading boarding schools and prestigious universities from around Australia. MADALAH stands for "Making A Difference And Looking Ahead" and embodies the organisation's commitment to creating positive change through education. MADALAH secondary scholarships provide support to students from Years 7 to 12. These scholarships are administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). The scholarship provides boarding assistance and educational support for Indigenous students from remote and regional areas.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous students from Years 7 to 12; Primarily from remote and regional communities; Must submit recent NAPLAN results and last two school reports; Must confirm eligible for AbStudy or be willing to apply; Shortlisted students invited to interview with Principal; Scholarships administered by MADALAH on behalf of National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA); Partner school applicants may be eligible for this support
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications must be made to both the School and directly to MADALAH. To apply with the School, complete the online Application for Admission form and disregard the $75 application fee. If successful, the family must complete the application for AbStudy. Shortlisted students will be invited to interview with the Principal. Scholarships administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA).

overview: Grace Christian School offers a Fee Assistance Program to help families bridge the gap between tuition costs and their ability to pay. The program is designed so that no family is overburdened financially and is based on assessed family income and demonstrated financial need.
description: Grace Christian School provides tuition fee assistance based on family income and financial need. The program is designed to ensure no family is overburdened financially and encourages a shared financial responsibility among the school community based on biblical principles of supporting one another. The source of funds is contributed by other school families through their tuition fees, making this assistance possible. The school states that assistance is granted one year at a time, with a new application required each year or if family circumstances change. The amount of assistance varies based on individual family circumstances and assessed need. The application process requires families to complete an annual application form and return it to the School Bursar.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all enrolled families; Families must complete annual application; Assistance granted year to year; Application required if family circumstances change
special_conditions: Assistance is granted one year at a time. A new application is needed each year or if family circumstances change. Designed so that no family is overburdened financially. The program is possible because other school families contribute through their tuition fees.

overview: Greenwood College offers a Year 7 Scholarship recognising academic achievement and student potential. The scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating strong academic performance and commitment to their studies.
description: The Year 7 Scholarship is available to students entering Year 7 at Greenwood College. The scholarship is awarded for four years duration. Selection is based on assessment of student academic performance, as evidenced by the student's Semester 1 school report, a completed application form (to be completed in the student's handwriting), and a teacher reference and evaluation sheet submitted directly to Greenwood College. The recipient must remain a student of Greenwood College for the full academic year, and the scholarship value cannot be transferred to cash or another person.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Strong academic performance demonstrated by Semester 1 school report; Application form completed in student's handwriting; Teacher reference and evaluation sheet submission required
special_conditions: The recipient must remain a student of Greenwood College for the full academic year. The value of the scholarship is not transferred to cash or another person. The scholarship is for four years only. The decisions of the judging panel are final and will be based on information contained in the student's Semester 1 school report, application form, and teacher reference and evaluation sheet.

overview: Greenwood College offers a Department of Education approved Specialist Aviation Program for students serious about aviation. The program is designed to connect students with the aviation industry, provide flying experience, and develop comprehensive aviation skills over four years.
description: The Specialist Aviation Program is a Department of Education approved specialist program designed for students who are serious about aviation. Acceptance into the Aviation program is for four years from Year 7 to Year 10. The program prepares students for a rewarding future in aviation through learning to fly fixed-wing aeroplanes and helicopters and connecting with the aviation industry. This in-depth program is perfect for students with a genuine interest in aviation. Prospective students must submit a written application and sit an academic aptitude test. Parents need to register interest before April for enrolment in Year 7 the following year. The program accepts out-of-area applications and successful applicants are enrolled into Greenwood College.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Serious interest in aviation; Must submit written application; Must sit academic aptitude test; Must complete Specialist Aviation application form; Parents must register interest before April for Year 7 enrolment the following year
special_conditions: Acceptance into the Aviation program is for four years from Year 7 to Year 10. Parents must register interest before April for enrolment in Year 7 the following year. Specialist Aviation and Volleyball programs accept out of area applications. Successful applicants are enrolled into Greenwood College.

overview: Greenwood College offers a Department of Education approved Specialist Volleyball Program for students who want to develop high-level performance and technical volleyball skill. The program is supportive and builds character, self-discipline and a love of the sport.
description: The Greenwood College Volleyball Program is a Department of Education approved specialist program designed for students who want to develop high-level performance and technical volleyball skill. Prospective students must complete a Volleyball Trial in Year 6 for acceptance in Year 7. Students may be able to try out later if there are vacancies. Each child in the program will be given the best possible opportunity to play competitive volleyball. The program is supportive while building character, self-discipline and a love of the sport. The program offers integrated indoor and beach volleyball for all students selected. Application is via written application and volleyball trial. Students must demonstrate volleyball ability, commitment to the program, and personal qualities, behaviour and attitude consistent with maximum participation.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Must complete Volleyball Trial in Year 6; Volleyball ability required; Commitment to program required; Personal qualities, behaviour and attitude consistent with maximum participation; Must submit written application; May try out later if vacancies exist
special_conditions: Prospective students must complete a Volleyball Trial in Year 6 for acceptance in Year 7. Students may be able to try out later if there are vacancies. Specialist Aviation and Volleyball programs accept out of area applications. Successful applicants are enrolled into Greenwood College. Note: Students enrolled in the Specialist Volleyball Program in Year 7 and 8 do not study a language, as their training and curriculum commitments take the place of the language learning period.

overview: Greenwood College offers a school-based Elite Music Program for students from Year 7 to Year 12. The program has a long and celebrated history of developing talented musicians, with teaching by experienced and highly skilled staff assisted by visiting instrumental specialists.
description: The Greenwood College Elite Music Program is a school-based program that offers music education to students from Year 7 to Year 12. The program is characterized by great stylistic diversity, covering rock, contemporary, folk, jazz, and classical music genres. The Music program is taught by experienced and highly skilled staff and assisted by visiting instrumental specialists. The program provides students with the opportunity to develop their musical skills and knowledge, as well as to perform in solo, small group and large ensemble contexts in collaboration with the Department of Education's Instrumental Music School Services (IMSS). Enrolment applications are invited from students statewide with places reserved for continuing students. Beginner students are also welcome to apply depending on places remaining. Entry requires an application.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 to Year 12 enrollment; Musical interest and ability; Statewide applications accepted; Beginner students welcome depending on places remaining
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Enrolment applications are invited from students statewide with places reserved for continuing students. Beginner students are also welcome to apply depending on places remaining. The program is made up of three parts, with all Music students participating in Class, Instrumental and Ensemble Music classes concurrently.

overview: Greenwood College offers a school-based Elite Dance Program for students in grades 7 through 12 with dance experience and talent. The program combines the joy of movement with high artistic standards and provides performance opportunities throughout the year.
description: The Elite Dance Program at Greenwood College is aimed at students in Year 7 through Year 12 with dance experience and talent. The program combines the joy of movement with high artistic standards. Classes include contemporary dance, ballet, jazz, hip hop, acrobatics, cultural dance, lyrical, tap, and Bollywood. Students have the opportunity to perform at various events throughout the year. The program covers a study of the full creative process from learning advanced techniques of jazz and contemporary, own choreography as well as public performances, investigations and research on dance genres and reviewing live professional dance performances, and a study of full stagecraft including makeup, costume and theatre etiquette. Entry into this course is by audition only and prior dance experience is required. Students should enter Dance Elite on the Subject Selection form to be registered for the audition.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 to Year 12 enrollment; Prior dance experience required; Dance talent demonstrated; Entry by audition only; Subject Selection form submission required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Entry into this course is by audition only and prior dance experience is required. Students should enter Dance Elite on the Subject Selection form to enable registration for the audition. A willingness to participate in the program including after-hours rehearsals and performances is required. If students join a cheerleading team (Greenwood Knights or Greenwood Titans) attendance is compulsory at all weekly after school training sessions and competitions throughout the year.

school_name: Halls Head College Education Support Centre
Harvey Senior High School - Harvey Senior High School Campus
Helena River Waldorf School - Hazelmere Campus
Indie School Western Australia - Mirrabooka Campus

overview: The Global Achiever Scholarship is a merit-based award designed to recognise and support outstanding students who exhibit academic prowess, collaborative skills, and a commitment to making a positive impact in their communities. The scholarship offers applicants the opportunity to complete the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program, preparing students academically, socially and culturally for a globalised world.
description: The Global Achiever Scholarship at ISWA is a merit-based award designed to recognise and support outstanding students who exhibit academic prowess, collaborative skills, and a commitment to making a positive impact in their communities. The scholarship provides a reduction of 15-50% of the School tuition fee as determined by the Principal after consideration of the family's ability to contribute to the cost of tuition. The Scholarship will continue until the student graduates from ISWA, subject to periodic review and provided satisfactory progress is made. Candidates must have Australian Citizenship or be a Permanent Resident in Australia, maintain a high academic standard, demonstrate the attributes of the IB Learner Profile and contribute fully to the life of the School. The selection process involves submission of an Application Form and comprehensive application portfolio including academic transcripts (past two years and recent standardised test scores such as NAPLAN or MAP), two letters of recommendation from teachers or mentors, a thoughtful essay (300-500 words), and documentation of extracurricular activities and community service.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 to 11 in July 2027; Not already enrolled at ISWA, or current ISWA students entering Year 7 in July 2027 or 2028; Australian Citizenship or Permanent Residency required; Strong academic record with exceptional performance; Proven ability to take initiative and demonstrate teamwork skills; Commitment to embracing diversity and fostering cross-cultural understanding; Active participation in extracurricular activities (sports, arts, community service); Must maintain high academic standard and IB Learner Profile attributes; Expected to complete Year 12 at ISWA
special_conditions: Scholarships are granted with the expectation that a student will complete Year 12 at ISWA. If this does not occur the School is entitled to request that all or part of the value of the scholarship fees are payable to the School. The School reserves the right to review a student's performance at any time and withdraw the scholarship offered in circumstances where the terms of the scholarship have not been met. Parents and guardians must submit a substantial statement of assets and liabilities, and provide copies of Taxation Assessments, and/or Trust Returns, and Centrelink or other assessments for the past two years (if applicable). Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an aptitude test, interview and to participate in team activities. A scholarship offer does not cover other costs such as uniforms, fee-attracting trips and activities and ad hoc expenses. Scholarships should be begun in July (the beginning of the ISWA school year). All decisions will be at the discretion of the Principal. The number and value assigned to each scholarship awarded in a given year may vary at the discretion of the Principal and the Board of Trustees.

overview: ISWA offers bursary assistance to self-funded students who demonstrate financial hardship. A Bursary may be offered at the discretion of the Principal to eligible students who require financial assistance to continue their education.
description: A Bursary may be offered to a self-funded student at the discretion of the Principal. The bursary program is designed to support students experiencing financial hardship or unable to meet the full cost of fees. The exact financial benefit varies based on individual circumstances and Principal discretion. The process involves a meeting with the Principal and submission of statements of proof demonstrating financial need. Inquiries should be directed to the Finance Manager. The bursary is offered on a case-by-case basis, with the Principal making determinations regarding eligibility and the level of support. This financial assistance program operates separately from the merit-based scholarship offerings.
eligibility_criteria: Self-funded student status; Demonstrated financial hardship or inability to meet full cost of fees; Discretionary approval by Principal; Requires meeting with Principal; Requires submission of statements of proof of financial need
special_conditions: Bursary is offered at the discretion of the Principal. The process involves a meeting with the Principal and statements of proof. Bursary does not apply to siblings who receive another form of assistance (e.g. Scholarship). Subject to Principal discretion and available funds.

overview: Irene McCormack Catholic College awards one Academic Scholarship to an incoming Year 7 student. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic achievement and provides 100% tuition fee remission throughout the student's secondary education at the college.
description: The College awards one Academic Scholarship to an incoming Year 7 student, following the Academic Scholarship examination. The Academic Scholarship covers 100% of the student's tuition fee from Years 7 to 12. The scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The selection process involves a comprehensive academic examination. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress throughout their enrolment and remain in good standing with the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 at an Australian primary school; Open to all school sectors; No age restrictions for scholarships
special_conditions: Scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees only from Years 7-12. Recipient must maintain satisfactory academic progress and remain in good standing. College reserves the right to review scholarship continuation annually.

overview: Irene McCormack Catholic College offers three Instrumental Music Scholarships annually to Year 7 entering students. One scholarship is available for vocal students and two scholarships are available for students playing wind instruments. The scholarships cover the cost of instrumental or vocal tuition for the first year.
description: The College offers three Instrumental Scholarships available to students entering the College at Year 7. Scholarships cover students' instrumental or vocal tuition for one year and will be renewed annually if all scholarship criteria are met. One scholarship is available for vocal students and two for students playing the flute, clarinet, saxophone, trumpet, or trombone. The selection process involves audition and assessment of musical ability. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to music and maintain appropriate standards of musical performance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate advanced musical ability; Audition required; Students entering from any primary school sector welcome; Instrumental or vocal proficiency required
special_conditions: Scholarships cover instrumental or vocal tuition for one year only. Renewal is available annually if scholarship criteria continue to be met. Students must participate actively in College music programs and maintain musical standards.

overview: St Andrew's Catholic Parish provides a bursary to students entering Year 7 at Irene McCormack Catholic College. The bursary supports families with genuine financial difficulties and recognises excellence in academic achievement, behaviour, and community contribution.
description: The bursary covers 50% of the student's tuition fees for Years 7 to 12. College levies and non-tuition related expenses are not covered by the bursary and are payable in accordance with the Irene McCormack Catholic College School Fees and Collection Policy. The bursary will be reviewed annually to determine whether the terms are being met and to decide if it will be continued. The selection process involves consideration of students' attitude towards learning, behaviour, and community spirit. The Parish Priest will interview shortlisted candidates and their parents and make a recommendation to the College Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be a member of or have connection to St Andrew's Catholic Parish; Consideration given to financial need; Academic performance and community engagement assessed; No conduct issues; Maintained good attendance
special_conditions: Covers 50% of tuition fees only for Years 7-12. Bursary subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw the bursary if terms are not met. Students must maintain good behaviour, attendance, and academic engagement.

program_name: St Francis of Assisi Catholic Primary School Bursary
overview: St Francis of Assisi Catholic Primary School offers a bursary to students entering Year 7 at Irene McCormack Catholic College. The bursary supports families with financial difficulties and recognises excellence across academic, behavioural and community dimensions.
description: The bursary covers 50% of the student's tuition fees for Years 7 to 12. College levies and non-tuition related expenses are not covered by the bursary and are payable according to the Irene McCormack Catholic College School Fees and Collection Policy. The bursary will be reviewed annually to determine whether the terms are being met and to decide if it will be continued. The selection process involves assessment of students' attitude towards learning, behaviour, and community spirit. Students must demonstrate commitment to their educational journey and active participation in school community life.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled at St Francis of Assisi Catholic Primary School; Consideration given to financial need; Academic performance and character assessed; Good behaviour and conduct record required; Active community participation expected
special_conditions: Covers 50% of tuition fees only for Years 7-12. Bursary subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw the bursary if scholarship terms are not met. Successful application does not include younger siblings in later years.

overview: Djin Djin Bursary is available to students entering Year 7 at Irene McCormack Catholic College. The bursary provides financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship and recognises achievement across academic, behavioural and community service dimensions.
description: The Djin Djin Bursary is available for students entering Year 7. The bursary provides financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial difficulty, enabling access to Catholic education. The program considers students' attitude towards learning, behaviour, and commitment to community service. Financial circumstances and demonstrated need are assessed in the selection process. The bursary supports students throughout their enrolment at the College subject to annual review of circumstances and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Financial hardship demonstrated; Good academic engagement; Positive behaviour and conduct record; Community service commitment valued; Consideration for students from disadvantaged backgrounds
special_conditions: Financial circumstances and need are primary considerations. Bursary value and duration subject to annual review. College takes a pastoral approach towards families experiencing genuine financial hardship.

program_name: Partner Catholic Primary School Sibling Discount Scheme
overview: Irene McCormack Catholic College offers a 10% tuition fee discount for the oldest child to families with siblings attending partner Catholic Primary Schools. This scheme aims to support families transitioning from primary to secondary education within the Catholic system.
description: Families with students attending Irene McCormack Catholic College and who have siblings attending St Francis of Assisi Catholic Primary School, St Andrew's Catholic Primary School, or St John Paul II Catholic Primary School are eligible for this 10% discount on tuition fees only for their oldest child at the College. The discount applies to tuition fees only and does not extend to student levies or other charges. Eligible families must apply for the discount through their primary school and complete the required documentation by the specified deadline.
eligibility_criteria: One or more children attending partner primary schools (St Francis of Assisi, St Andrew's, or St John Paul II); Student entering Irene McCormack Catholic College; Applies to oldest child only; Families paying full tuition fees eligible; Completed application form required
special_conditions: 10% discount applies to tuition fees only. Discount applies to oldest child only at the College. Families must apply through their primary school. Form must be received by College by deadline. Does not apply to student levies, building levy, or other charges.

overview: Irene McCormack Catholic College offers significant fee discounts to families holding a current means-tested Concession Card. The College provides tuition fee reductions and building levy reductions in accordance with Catholic Education Western Australia policies.
description: In accordance with the policies of Catholic Education Western Australia Ltd, the College offers a Concession Card Discount to families holding a means tested Concession Card. Discounts are provided for tuition fees (billed per student) and building levies (billed per family). The College requires a copy of the eligible Concession Card (front and back) along with the Concession Card Application Form. Concession Card eligibility is reviewed annually at the commencement of each new school year. For families holding a current Concession Card, the Department of Education provides a Secondary Assistance Scheme with a Clothing Allowance of $115 per student for Years 7-12 for 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Current means-tested Concession Card holder; Card must be valid to April 2025 or beyond; Application form completed and submitted; Valid to March 31st for application eligibility; Annual review of eligibility required; Students in Years 7-12
special_conditions: Requires current means-tested Concession Card valid to April or beyond. Discounts reviewed annually. Building levy discount applies per family only. Additional user-pays levies for VET, camps, music, and activities may not qualify for discount. Department of Education Clothing Allowance of $115 per student available.

overview: John Forrest Secondary College offers a Cricket Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding ability in cricket. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional talent and commitment in this endorsed specialist program.
description: John Forrest Secondary College's Cricket Scholarship is available to Year 7 students entering the college who demonstrate outstanding ability in cricket. Recipients receive a $300 voucher to cover specialist course charges. The cricket program is endorsed by the Western Australian Cricket Association and includes four hours per week of class time, regular competitions, interstate and international cricket tours. Selection is based on demonstrated outstanding cricket ability and commitment to the sport. This specialist program allows selected students from outside the local-intake area to gain entry to the college through the approved specialist program pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding ability in cricket; Students living outside the local-intake area may be accepted into the school if selected into this specialist program
special_conditions: Scholarship is a voucher to cover specialist course charges. Cricket program includes four hours per week of class time, regular competitions, interstate and international cricket tours. Students play on a turf wicket at school grounds and travel to the WACA Ground. Program is endorsed by the Western Australian Cricket Association.

overview: John Forrest Secondary College offers a Music Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding ability in music. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional musical talent and commitment in this approved specialist program.
description: John Forrest Secondary College's Music Scholarship is available to Year 7 students entering the college who demonstrate outstanding musical ability. Recipients receive a $300 voucher to cover specialist course charges. The music program is approved by the Department of Education and requires students to audition for entry. Students in the program undertake music theory, weekly instrumental lessons, ensembles outside class time and various performances and concerts. Selection is based on audition performance demonstrating outstanding musical ability. This specialist program allows selected students from outside the local-intake area to gain entry to the college through the approved specialist program pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding ability in music; Must pass audition for music program; Students living outside the local-intake area may be accepted into the school if selected into this specialist program
special_conditions: Scholarship is a voucher to cover specialist course charges. Entry to the music program requires audition. Students undertake music theory, weekly instrumental lessons, ensembles outside class time and various performances and concerts. Students can choose to do Music ATAR in Years 11 and 12. Program is approved by the Department of Education.

overview: John Forrest Secondary College offers a Tennis Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding ability in tennis. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional talent and commitment in this endorsed specialist program.
description: John Forrest Secondary College's Tennis Scholarship is available to Year 7 students entering the college who demonstrate outstanding ability in tennis. Recipients receive a $300 voucher to cover specialist course charges. The tennis program is approved by the Department of Education and endorsed by Tennis West. The program includes four hours per week of class time and participation in competitions. Selection is based on demonstrated outstanding tennis ability and commitment to the sport. This specialist program allows selected students from outside the local-intake area to gain entry to the college through the approved specialist program pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding ability in tennis; Students living outside the local-intake area may be accepted into the school if selected into this specialist program
special_conditions: Scholarship is a voucher to cover specialist course charges. Tennis program includes four hours per week of class time and participation in competitions. Program is approved by the Department of Education and endorsed by Tennis West.

overview: John Forrest Secondary College offers a Netball Scholarship to Year 7 students who demonstrate outstanding ability in netball. The scholarship recognises students who show exceptional talent and commitment in this approved specialist program.
description: John Forrest Secondary College's Netball Scholarship is available to Year 7 students entering the college who demonstrate outstanding ability in netball. Recipients receive a $300 voucher to cover specialist course charges. The netball program is approved by the Department of Education. Students in the program have four hours per week of class time and are involved in competitions. Selection is based on demonstrated outstanding netball ability and commitment to the sport. This specialist program allows selected students from outside the local-intake area to gain entry to the college through the approved specialist program pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate outstanding ability in netball; Students living outside the local-intake area may be accepted into the school if selected into this specialist program
special_conditions: Scholarship is a voucher to cover specialist course charges. Netball program includes four hours per week of class time and participation in competitions. Program is approved by the Department of Education.

overview: Iona Presentation College offers the Year 4 General Excellence Scholarship to recognise all-round excellence in incoming Year 4 students. The scholarship rewards students who demonstrate strong academic ability combined with enthusiasm and reliable contribution to all facets of school life.
description: The Year 4 General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 4 who are considered 'all-rounders' by their peers and teachers. Successful applicants receive a remission of either 25% or 50% of tuition fees for Years 4, 5 and 6. The selection process includes a written assessment held at the Junior School campus in Term 3, followed by interviews with parents in September for shortlisted candidates. Applicants must provide Semester 1, 2025 school reports, a handwritten letter from the student, and 2025 NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 4 in 2027; Must be entering Year 4 in 2026 or later; Demonstrates strong academic ability; Shows enthusiasm and reliable contribution to all facets of life; Currently enrolled in Year 3 or external students welcome
special_conditions: Scholarship tenable for Years 4, 5 and 6. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to attend an interview with parents in September. Parents are invited to tour the Junior School Campus whilst their child sits the scholarship test.

program_name: Sister Patricia Downey Music Scholarship for Aptitude - Suzuki Strings Program
overview: Iona Presentation College offers the Sister Patricia Downey Music Scholarship for Aptitude targeting students in the Suzuki Strings Program. This scholarship recognises musical aptitude and supports students beginning string instrument study at Pre-primary and Year 1 levels.
description: The Sister Patricia Downey Music Scholarship for Aptitude is available for students in Pre-primary and Year 1 undertaking the Suzuki Strings Program. Music Scholarships cover music tuition fees. The selection process requires students to attend an audition at the College and complete an application. Students must be either future enrolled or current students and must be available for an interview following the application close date. Applications are accepted in all instruments including voice, and auditions are held in the student's strongest instrument.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Pre-primary or Year 1; Part of or entering Suzuki Strings Program; Must be future enrolled or current student; Available for audition and interview; Students from Catholic, Independent and Government schools whose values align with Iona
special_conditions: Students applying for Music Scholarship must be prepared to attend an audition at the College. A piano accompanist can be provided if notice is given with application. Sound system available for backing tracks on Bluetooth or USB drive.

program_name: Sister Patricia Downey Music Scholarship for Aptitude - Year 5 Beginner Band Program
overview: Iona Presentation College offers the Sister Patricia Downey Music Scholarship for Aptitude for students entering the Year 5 Beginner Band Program. The scholarship supports students demonstrating musical aptitude in instrumental music.
description: The Sister Patricia Downey Music Scholarship for Aptitude is available for students entering Year 5 who are beginning the Beginner Band Program. Music Scholarships cover music tuition fees. The selection process requires students to attend an audition at the College and complete an application. Students must be either future enrolled or current students and must be available for interview following the application close date. Applications are accepted in all instruments including voice.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5; Part of or entering Beginner Band Program; Must be future enrolled or current student; Available for audition and interview; Students from Catholic, Independent and Government schools whose values align with Iona
special_conditions: Students applying for Music Scholarship must be prepared to attend an audition at the College. Candidates who play more than one instrument should audition with their strongest instrument. A piano accompanist can be provided if notice is given with application.

overview: Iona Presentation College offers the Nano Nagle Bursary to support families experiencing genuine financial hardship. The bursary is available to holders of means-tested concession cards and assists families in accessing quality Catholic education.
description: The Nano Nagle Bursary is available to any parent in genuine financial hardship who holds a means-tested concession card. Application forms are available from the College Accounts Office. Various criteria are applicable for the assessment of these applications, and parents are informed of the outcome of their assessment. The bursary provides fee assistance based on individual family circumstances and financial need assessment. Parents are required to re-apply for consideration on an annual basis to maintain the bursary.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian in genuine financial hardship; Must hold means-tested concession card; Re-application required annually; Subject to financial assessment by College
special_conditions: Application forms available from College Accounts Office. Annual reapplication required. Bursary recipients are not eligible to receive One Payment discount on tuition fees. Application requires completion of financial assessment with various criteria applicable.

overview: John Paul College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement in students entering Year 7. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to their learning at the College.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 at John Paul College. The scholarship provides financial support in the form of partial remission of tuition fees for eligible students who demonstrate strong academic achievement and commit to the College's values. Selection is based on performance in an academic examination or test assessment. Recipients are expected to maintain exemplary conduct and engagement with both curricular and co-curricular activities throughout their enrolment. The scholarship is reviewed annually to ensure recipients continue to meet the standards set out by the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate above-average academic achievement; Must provide NAPLAN results and recent school reports; Must commit to at least two co-curricular programs annually; Must show exemplary effort and conduct; Must comply with College values and expectations
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered subject to annual review. Recipients must actively demonstrate College values and meet the standards set for each scholarship category. Scholarship tenure extends for the duration of study at JPC, until the end of Year 12, subject to annual reviews. Failure to maintain academic standards or conduct may result in withdrawal of the scholarship.

overview: John Paul College recognises that Catholic education should not be withheld due to financial reasons. The College offers confidential financial assistance to families experiencing genuine financial hardship who wish to enrol their child at the College.
description: John Paul College, in accordance with Catholic Education Western Australia principles, provides financial assistance to families who are experiencing financial difficulty and may otherwise be unable to afford the school fees. Parents or guardians who are facing financial hardship are encouraged to contact the Principal in confidence to discuss assistance options. The College understands that financial situations may change and requests for assistance must be made each year. All contact regarding financial hardship is kept in strict confidence and is managed sensitively by the College leadership. The specific value and terms of assistance are determined on a case-by-case basis depending on individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to enrolled or prospective students; Evidence of genuine financial hardship; Must contact Principal directly; Annual reapplication required; Confidential assessment of financial circumstances
special_conditions: All enquiries and applications are kept in strict confidence. Financial situations may change, so requests for assistance must be renewed each year. Contact must be made directly with the Principal for assessment and discussion of available support.

overview: John Paul College provides automatic fee discounts to families who hold a current means-tested Concession Card. This scheme ensures that families receiving government support can access reduced tuition fees.
description: Families holding a current means-tested Concession Card are eligible for automatic discounted fees at John Paul College. The discount is applied automatically upon presentation of the valid card at the College. The specific discount amount and application to tuition fees is determined by the eligibility status of the Concession Card holder. This scheme provides a structured way for low-income families to reduce their financial burden of school fees. The discount applies to tuition fees only and does not cover additional costs such as uniforms, books, camps, excursions, or private music tuition. The card must be current during the billing period for which the discount is being claimed.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a current means-tested Concession Card; Card holder must be the person legally responsible for fees; Card must be current during the billing period; Applies to tuition fees only
special_conditions: Discount is automatic upon presentation of valid Concession Card. Card must be current on any day of the billing period for which discount is being applied. Does not cover uniforms, books, camps, excursions, or private music tuition. Families must pay the balance of any account by due date.

school_name: John Tonkin College Education Support Centre
overview: John Tonkin College offers scholarships through university partnerships to reward academic achievement and provide support to students whose socio-economic, cultural, geographic or personal circumstances may affect their capacity to succeed. The scholarships are designed to recognise student achievement and support access to higher education.
description: Through partnerships with universities, John Tonkin College makes scholarships available to students demonstrating academic excellence and those facing socio-economic, cultural, geographic or personal barriers to success. The scholarships are intended to reward outstanding academic performance and support students whose circumstances may impact their educational access and outcomes. Specific financial values, selection procedures, and application deadlines are not publicly detailed on the college website. Students should contact the University Partnerships Coordinator directly for comprehensive information about available scholarships, eligibility requirements, application procedures, and financial benefits.
eligibility_criteria: Academic achievement required; Open to students whose socio-economic, cultural, geographic or personal circumstances may affect their capacity to succeed

school_name: John Tonkin College Education Support Centre
overview: Follow the Dream is a support program for Aboriginal students at John Tonkin College who show an aptitude for learning and demonstrate a positive approach towards their education. The program provides one-on-one tuition, mentoring, emotional and wellbeing support to help students achieve their educational goals.
description: Follow the Dream is a long-standing program operating at John Tonkin College designed specifically for Aboriginal students demonstrating aptitude for learning and positive educational engagement. The program provides personalised pathway planning for each participant, including one-on-one tuition, mentoring services, emotional and wellbeing support, and participation in inspirational excursions and camps. Students selected for the program are expected to attend after-school tuition hours and demonstrate commitment to their personal educational goals. The specific financial value and benefits of this support program are not detailed on the publicly available pages, but it operates as a comprehensive support initiative rather than a traditional scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal student; Shows aptitude for learning; Demonstrates positive approach towards education; Selected based on academic results and attendance records; Expected to attend after-school tuition hours; Must demonstrate commitment to educational goals
special_conditions: Students are selected for the program based on their academic results and attendance. Students must attend after-school hours tuition and demonstrate a commitment to their goals. The program is designed around a personalised pathway plan for each student.

school_name: John Tonkin College Education Support Centre
overview: The Western Australian Government provides the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) allowance to assist eligible families with secondary schooling costs at John Tonkin College. The scheme provides two components to eligible parents/guardians whose families hold a Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs card.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a government-supported financial assistance program administered through John Tonkin College for families facing secondary schooling costs. The scheme provides two financial components: a $115 Clothing Allowance paid directly to the parent/guardian or school, and a $235 Education Program Allowance paid directly to the school, totalling $350 per eligible student annually. Parents and guardians must apply for the SAS each school year; previous applications do not carry forward to future years. To be eligible, the parent/guardian must hold a Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs card representing a family income statement. Applications are accepted for students enrolled in Years 7-12 studying full-time secondary courses at Western Australian public schools.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/guardian must hold a Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs card that represents a statement of income; Student enrolled in Years 7-12; Must be studying a full-time secondary course; New application required each school year; Application does not carry forward from previous years
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Parents/guardians must apply for the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) each school year (annually) – applications do not carry forward to future years. The allowance consists of two components: $115 Clothing Allowance paid directly to the parent/guardian or the school; $235 Education Program Allowance paid directly to the school.

overview: John XXIII College offers a Foundation Bursary which aims to provide access to a John XXIII College education to students who otherwise may not be able to afford it. The bursary is a needs-based program funded through donations made to the College's Bursary Fund and represents the College's commitment to distributive justice and educational equity in the local community.
description: The John XXIII College Foundation Bursary is a needs-based financial assistance program that covers tuition fees for the duration of the student's education at the College. The allocation may include tuition fees, resource fees, elective fees, books and uniforms. The bursary covers the student's standard fees and charges for the complete duration of their schooling at John XXIII College. Applications are assessed based on financial need and are available to both current and future families. The bursary program commenced in 2022 and has expanded with community donations. Recipients must be Australian citizens or permanent residents entering Year 7 or above. The program is administered by the John XXIII College Foundation with funds managed by the Foundation Investment Committee adhering to the Foundation Board's Investment Policy.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 or above; Must be Australian citizens or permanent residents; Must demonstrate financial need; Available to current and future families; Academic performance and capacity to benefit from a John XXIII education considered
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Bursary covers tuition fees for the duration of the student's education at John XXIII College. The allocation of additional resources including resource fees, elective fees, books and uniforms may also be considered by the Foundation. Non-monetary support and pastoral care are provided as part of the program.

overview: John XXIII College's Magis Gifted and Talented Program is designed to assist gifted students to develop their potential into performance and maximise their talents to better serve their community and the world. The program operates across multiple year levels and strives for excellence in the Ignatian tradition of pursuing 'the more' in all endeavours.
description: The Magis Gifted and Talented Program at John XXIII College provides enriched educational opportunities for identified gifted and talented students across secondary years. Entry to the program is initially via results from MYAT (Multi-Year Ability Test) testing, but students can also qualify by demonstrating excellent academic performances as they progress through the school. There is also a sub-strand called Altiora (meaning higher things) which provides opportunities for other gifted and enthusiastic students to participate in Magis events. The program provides some events exclusively for Magis students while many are open to all students. Highlights include prestigious interschool competitions such as WADL Debating, Da Vinci Decathlon, Creative Edge, Tournament of Minds, Opti-Minds, Quest, Ethics Olympiad, Jesuit National Debating Championship, International Debating and World Scholar's Cup including global rounds and the Tournament of Champions in Yale. Students receive access to a large range of academic resources, four major publications annually (Magis Handbook, Magis Chronicle, Magis Resources Handbook and Magis Australian Competitions Handbook), Academic Seminar Breakfasts with motivational speakers, special programs including USA Space Camp, Academy Conferences, Imaginarium, TED-X talks, and Notre Dame University's Cultural Decoding Program. The program also includes specific competitions in Public Speaking, Creative Writing, Chess and an Academic Challenge House program covering Academic Spelling, Mental Mathematics, Arts and Humanities, STEM and Language-Literature. Awards are presented at the Magis Awards Breakfast which recognises excellent performances including the Magis Champion Award for students who gain points from gifted and talented events, school academic performance and service to the program.
eligibility_criteria: Identified gifted and talented students; Initial entry via MYAT test results; Alternative entry through demonstrated excellent academic performance as student progresses through school; Open to secondary year levels; Altiora sub-strand available for gifted and enthusiastic students
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Entry is initially via MYAT testing but students can also qualify through excellent academic performance. Some events are exclusive to Magis students while others are open to all students. A Magis student committee assists with organisation and serves the community through tutoring, running competitions and speaking at breakfasts and camps. Recipients must support the College's Ignatian ethos and educational mission.

overview: John Wollaston offers Academic Scholarships for entry into Year 4, recognising outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through merit-based selection.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 4 entry provides a 50% Fee Reduction on tuition fees for the duration of the scholarship. Applicants enter via a scholarship examination process assessed by their academic performance. The scholarship is awarded based on academic excellence and successful interview with the Principal. Please note that Year 4 scholarship applicants do not sit an examination; selection is based on application materials and interview. The scholarship is of three years duration, subject to good standing being maintained, and recipients must support the school's Christian ethos and values.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 3 students from government and independent schools; students applying for entry into Year 4; must demonstrate strong academic performance; current John Wollaston students eligible to apply
special_conditions: Year 4 scholarship applicants do not sit an examination. Scholarships are of a three-year duration, subject to good standing being maintained. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and support the school's Christian ethos. Late applications accepted with late fee from Thursday 30 April 2026.

overview: John Wollaston offers Academic Scholarships for entry into Year 5, recognising outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through merit-based selection.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 5 entry provides a 50% Fee Reduction on tuition fees for the duration of the scholarship. Applicants participate in a scholarship examination held on Saturday 9 May 2026, with assessment based on academic excellence as evidenced in the examination performance and interview with the Principal. The scholarship is of three years duration, subject to good standing being maintained. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress throughout their scholarship period. Application materials include copies of most recent academic report and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 4 students enrolled in government or independent schools; current John Wollaston students eligible; must provide copy of most recent academic report; must provide copy of most recent NAPLAN results
special_conditions: Scholarships are of a three-year duration, subject to good standing being maintained. Academic excellence assessed through examination performance and Principal interview. Recipients must support the school's values. Late applications accepted with late fee from Thursday 30 April 2026.

overview: John Wollaston offers Academic Scholarships for entry into Year 7, recognising outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through merit-based selection.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 7 entry provides a 50% Fee Reduction on tuition fees for the duration of the scholarship. Applicants participate in a scholarship examination held on Saturday 9 May 2026, with assessment based on academic excellence as evidenced in the examination performance and interview with the Principal. The scholarship is of three years duration, subject to good standing being maintained. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress throughout their scholarship period. Application requires copies of most recent academic report and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students enrolled in government or independent schools; current John Wollaston students eligible; must provide copy of most recent academic report; must provide copy of most recent NAPLAN results
special_conditions: Scholarships are of a three-year duration, subject to good standing being maintained. Academic excellence assessed through examination performance and Principal interview. Recipients must support the school's values. Late applications accepted with late fee from Thursday 30 April 2026.

overview: John Wollaston offers Academic Scholarships for entry into Year 11, recognising outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning through merit-based selection.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 11 entry provides a 50% Fee Reduction on tuition fees for the duration of the scholarship. Applicants participate in a scholarship examination held on Saturday 9 May 2026, with assessment based on academic excellence as evidenced in the examination performance and interview with the Principal. The scholarship is of three years duration or for the duration of enrolment at the school. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress throughout their scholarship period. Application requires copies of most recent academic report and NAPLAN results.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10 students enrolled in government or independent schools; current John Wollaston students eligible; must provide copy of most recent academic report; must provide copy of most recent NAPLAN results
special_conditions: Scholarships are of three years duration or for the duration of enrolment at the school, subject to good standing being maintained. Academic excellence assessed through examination performance and Principal interview. Recipients must support the school's values. Late applications accepted with late fee from Thursday 30 April 2026.

overview: School Council Bursaries offer financial assistance to existing school families who support the values of John Wollaston Anglican Community School. The bursaries provide educational opportunities for students in good standing who would not otherwise be able to remain at the school for financial or family reasons.
description: School Council Bursaries provide financial assistance in the form of one semester's tuition fee remission to eligible current students. These bursaries are available to current students entering Years 5 and 6, and Years 7 to 12. Recipients are selected based on financial need and good standing (excellent attendance, compliance with school rules, satisfactory academic record). Each year, eligible students can apply, including those who have previously received the bursary. The bursary recognises students' contributions to the school community through co-curricular activities, awards earned, and positions held. Recipients' names are kept confidential. Selection involves written application with supporting documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Current students of John Wollaston; Students in Years 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12; Must be in Good Standing (excellent attendance, comply with all School Rules, maintain satisfactory academic record); Students must be attending the school at time of application; Open to current and returning recipients
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Bursaries are for one semester's tuition fees. Recipients must maintain good standing throughout the year including excellent attendance, compliance with school rules and satisfactory academic record. Application involves written essay identifying ways school is helping student reach potential and future plans. Recipients' names kept confidential. Eligible students can apply each year.

overview: John Wollaston acknowledges word-of-mouth referrals from current families by providing a fee credit for enrolments. This incentive recognises the contribution of current families in promoting the school to their networks.
description: The Referral Credit provides a fee credit for families referred by current John Wollaston students and families. The maximum value of the fee credit is one term's tuition fees for new enrolments. This policy applies to enrolments from 2020 onwards. The credit acknowledges the significant role existing families play in promoting John Wollaston Anglican Community School within the community and provides a tangible benefit for those making referrals. The credit is applied to the enrolment of the referred student.
eligibility_criteria: Family must be referred by current John Wollaston families; Applying for new enrolment at the school; Enrolment from 2020 onwards
special_conditions: Maximum value equivalent to one term's tuition fees of the youngest enrolled student. Applies to new enrolments from 2020 onwards. Families should refer to the Referral Credit Policy on the school website for full details and application process.

overview: John Wollaston provides an enrolment discount for children of past students (Old Wollastonians). This policy acknowledges the contribution made by Old Wollastonians to the school community.
description: The Old Wollastonians Discount provides an enrolment discount equivalent to up to one term's tuition fees for children of past John Wollaston students. This policy recognises the ongoing connection between the school and its alumni, and acknowledges the contribution Old Wollastonians have made to the school. Many children of past students choose to attend John Wollaston, creating a legacy of family connection to the school. The maximum enrolment discount is equivalent to one term's tuition fees of the youngest enrolled student in the family.
