eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian must be a past student (Old Wollaston/alumnus); Child applying for enrolment at John Wollaston
special_conditions: Maximum enrolment discount equivalent to one term's tuition fees of the youngest enrolled student. Policy acknowledges the contribution made by Old Wollastonians (past students). Applicants should refer to the school's enrolment policies for specific application procedures.

overview: Follow the Dream is a highly regarded support program for Aboriginal students who demonstrate an aptitude for learning and a positive approach towards their education. The program provides academic support, mentoring, and wellbeing support to selected Aboriginal students across all year levels at John Tonkin College.
description: Follow the Dream is a comprehensive support program specifically designed for Aboriginal students at John Tonkin College. Students are selected based on their academic results and attendance records. The program is designed around a personalised pathway plan for each student and provides one-on-one tuition, mentoring, emotional and wellbeing support as well as attending various inspirational excursions and camps. Students are expected to attend after-school hours tuition and demonstrate a commitment to their goals. The program has been operating successfully from John Tonkin College for many years.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students; Must demonstrate aptitude for learning; Must show positive approach towards education; Selection based on academic results; Selection based on attendance records
special_conditions: Students are expected to attend after-school hours tuition and demonstrate a commitment to their goals. Program includes personalised pathway planning.

overview: Deadly Sista Girlz is a culturally-appropriate mentoring program delivered by the Waalitj Foundation to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls at John Tonkin College. The program aims to empower girls to make informed decisions about their personal health, wellbeing, and future education and career pathways.
description: Deadly Sista Girlz (DSG) is a valuable program offered at John Tonkin College, delivered by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander mentors to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls in years 7-12. The mentors aim to empower and enable girls to make informed decisions about their personal health and wellbeing to lead a positive and healthy lifestyle. The program is designed to improve school attendance and education attainment amongst First Nations girls. It is led by strong First Nations role models who provide culturally appropriate mentoring and support. A goal is to give these girls more opportunity to expand their thinking and become active members and leaders in their community and create a brighter future for themselves, their families and generations to come.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander girls; Year levels 7-12; Must be enrolled at John Tonkin College
special_conditions: Delivered by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander mentors. Includes mentoring sessions focused on personal health, wellbeing, and future planning.

overview: Surf Science is a flagship selective entry program at John Tonkin College offering a focus on Marine and Outdoor Education to selected students in years 7, 8 and 9. The program combines academic excellence with practical environmental and marine learning experiences.
description: Surf Science is a flagship program at John Tonkin College which offers a focus on Marine and Outdoor Education to selected students from years 7, 8 and 9. It is an academically challenging pathway with an integrated curriculum developed across English, Mathematics, Society and Environment, Science, Physical Education and Outdoor Education. Year 7, 8 and 9 students are taught the WA Curriculum in all subject areas with a focus on Outdoor and Marine Science Education. Students participate in Swimming, Sailing, Snorkelling, Kayaking, Surfing and many other water based activities. Students attend excursions and camps to enhance these skills and obtain various water qualifications including Bronze Medallion, First Aid and a Skipper's ticket. The program provides access to world-class educational facilities including the John Tonkin College Marine and Maritime Centre at Dawesville with well-equipped Science Laboratories, IT ready classrooms, and field work opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, 8, or 9; Academic performance with at least C grade achievement in all subjects; Higher grades preferred (confirmed by final report); NAPLAN results reflecting achievement at standard or higher; Strong records of positive and appropriate behaviour; Regular attendance; Leadership qualities such as Student Councillors, Faction Captains or Head Boy/Girl preferred; References from staff; Interest in marine and outdoor education; Appropriate self-management skills
special_conditions: Access to the program is based on academic performance, interest and appropriately developed self-management skills. An interview process may be used in determining student suitability for the program. Students continue to Year 10 where they may choose specialised courses in readiness for senior school.

overview: The University Preparation Program is a specialized pathway for Years 10-12 students at John Tonkin College designed to inspire and motivate students to strive for excellence in preparation for the university pathway. The program supports students working towards ATAR qualification.
description: The University Preparation Program (UP) is offered to Year 10-12 students at John Tonkin College's MET Campus and is designed to prepare students for an ATAR pathway. The program inspires and motivates students to strive for excellence so they are prepared to tackle the university pathway in senior school. It develops skills such as effective research and study habits. Students who continue with an ATAR pathway will be well-equipped to complete at high levels. The program is integrated with the MET Campus co-location with South Metropolitan TAFE and Murdoch University on the Peel Education Campus, providing students with access to university-level learning opportunities and facilities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10, 11, and 12 students; Demonstrated commitment to academic excellence; Interest in pursuing university pathway; Enrolled at John Tonkin College MET Campus
special_conditions: Program available at MET Campus location. Co-located with Murdoch University and South Metropolitan TAFE providing access to higher education learning opportunities.

overview: The Learning Support Program provides differentiated teaching and learning support to students with diagnosed disabilities and learning difficulties across years 7-12. The program ensures all students can access the curriculum on an equal basis as their peers.
description: The Learning Support Program at John Tonkin College supports staff to plan, deliver, monitor, assess and evaluate differentiated teaching and learning programs that meet the diverse educational needs of students across years 7 to 12. The Learning Support Coordinator works with parents/carers and external agencies to ensure relevant Individual Education Plans are in place for students with diagnosed disabilities and learning difficulties, so that they are able to access the curriculum on the same basis as their peers. Targeted literacy and numeracy support is offered to assist students as they progress toward achieving national benchmarks in NAPLAN and OLNA testing. Students with a disability are integrated into mainstream classes, where they are supported by their teachers, Student Services staff and experienced Education Assistants as they work towards achieving success in their chosen educational pathway.
eligibility_criteria: Students with diagnosed disabilities; Students with identified learning difficulties; Must be enrolled at John Tonkin College; Students across years 7-12; Requires documentation of disability or learning difficulty for Individual Education Plan development
special_conditions: Requires external agency involvement and parent/carer collaboration. Individual Education Plans are developed for each eligible student. Support provided across mainstream classes with Education Assistants.

overview: Through university partnerships with Murdoch University and other institutions, John Tonkin College offers scholarships and support programs to Years 7-12 students to reward academic achievement and provide support to those whose socio-economic, cultural, geographic or personal circumstances may affect their capacity to succeed.
description: JTC students across years 7-12 engage in fun, hands-on learning activities with on-campus experiences, school-based workshops and student mentoring programmes through the University Partnerships program. Students are encouraged to identify their interests and passions, aspire to higher education, and plan a career pathway so that they feel empowered to make decisions about their life after school. Through university partnerships, scholarships are available to reward academic achievement and provide support to those whose socio-economic, cultural, geographic or personal circumstances may affect their capacity to succeed. The program facilitates successful transitions to higher education through hands-on learning activities and mentoring from university partners including Murdoch University.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in years 7-12; Academic achievement; Demonstrated interest in higher education; Students whose socio-economic circumstances may affect their capacity to succeed; Students with cultural, geographic or personal circumstances requiring support; Enrolled at John Tonkin College
special_conditions: Scholarships vary by university partner and circumstance. Program includes on-campus experiences, workshops and student mentoring. Links to Murdoch University and other tertiary institutions on the Peel Education Campus.

overview: John Tonkin College has a dynamic Aboriginal Education Team that provides comprehensive support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students including facilitation of successful school transitions, academic support, mentoring with cultural aspect, mental health and wellbeing support, and post-secondary pathway planning.
description: John Tonkin College's Aboriginal Education Team is managed by Andrea Tacko with Denysha Marshall, Clinton Jetta and Sam Stack working part-time as Aboriginal & Islander Education Officers (AIEOs). The team provides a comprehensive focus including facilitating successful transition into JTC, academic support including through the Follow the Dream program, mentoring with a cultural aspect, mental health and wellbeing support, and support for senior school students including assistance with ASBTs (Australian School-based Apprenticeships and Traineeships), post-secondary pathway planning and provision of external agency support. The program works collaboratively with parents/carers and external support providers to ensure Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students feel part of a supportive community.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students; Enrolled at John Tonkin College; Years 7-12; Seeking academic support; Requiring mentoring or wellbeing support; Planning post-secondary pathways
special_conditions: Delivered by dedicated Aboriginal & Islander Education Officers. Includes cultural mentoring and external agency partnerships. Coordinated with other support programs including Follow the Dream and Deadly Sista Girlz.

school_name: John Septimus Roe Anglican Community School

overview: Kalamunda Christian School offers the Music Scholarship Scheme to Year 4 students as a special feature of the school. This program provides students with the valuable opportunity to access a year's worth of group instrumental lessons at no cost, recognising the importance of music education in developing the whole child.
description: The Music Scholarship Scheme is a special feature of Kalamunda Christian School that provides Year 4 students with a year's worth of group instrumental lessons for free. Nearly one in three students benefit from music tutoring at the school, where students learn an instrument with trained music tutors. Instruments available vary depending on demand but can include woodwind and brass instruments such as clarinet, flute, trumpet, percussion including drums and keyboards, piano, strings including violin, guitar and bass, and vocal instruction. This scholarship enables greater access to music education and supports the school's strong performing arts program which includes Year 4 Training Band, Year 5 and 6 Performing groups, String Ensemble, and school choir. The program is designed to encourage participation in the school's vibrant music culture.
eligibility_criteria: Must be in Year 4; Music orientation for Year 3 students entering Year 4 is held during the year
special_conditions: Students must attend music orientation sessions held for Year 3 students entering Year 4. Participation requires commitment to group lessons throughout the year. The scholarship value covers one year of group instrumental tuition only.

school_name: John Curtin College Of The Arts
overview: John Curtin College of the Arts offers the Robin Pascoe Scholarship to recognise students who demonstrate respect, care, compassion and leadership qualities. This scholarship honours the values established by Robin Pascoe, who was President of the College Board and a driving force behind the College of the Arts.
description: The Robin Pascoe Scholarship is awarded to Year 8 to 12 students at John Curtin College of the Arts who demonstrate exceptional respect, care, and compassion towards others. Recipients are recognised for contributing to the wellbeing of others, showing mutual respect through cooperation and collaboration, acting with integrity and positive character, and demonstrating leadership qualities. The scholarship is credited towards the student's school-related expenditure (contributions and charges). The amount varies depending on the number of scholarships endorsed by the College. Scholarships are selected by the Principal, Associate Principal – Arts and Innovation and Board representative. Students may self-nominate or be nominated by parents, teachers, other students or community members, and must submit a response no more than one page (1,800 characters). The scholarship will be applied to the student's account and any credit balance on completion of education will be refunded. If a student leaves during the school year of receiving the scholarship, no refund will be provided.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8 to Year 12 students; Open to all John Curtin College of the Arts students; Demonstrated respect, care, and compassion; Contribute to wellbeing of others; Show mutual respect through cooperation and collaboration; Act with integrity and positive character; Demonstrate potential leadership qualities
special_conditions: Scholarships are endorsed by the Board and funded by the Parents and Citizens association. The scholarship will be applied to the student's account and if there is a credit balance on the student's completion of education at John Curtin College of the Arts, the balance will be refunded. If the student leaves the school during the school year of receiving the scholarship, no refund will be provided. Non-refundable courses such as Uniready will not be refunded. Applied to all College Fees and Charges. No discounts applicable. Students are able to apply for this scholarship more than once during their time at the College. Students who have been previous recipients however are unable to reapply for any of the scholarships.

school_name: John Curtin College Of The Arts
overview: John Curtin College of the Arts presents the WAAPA Excellence Award to recognise outstanding Year 12 graduates. This award is donated by John Cowdell, a past student of the college who is heavily involved in the Arts in Western Australia.
description: The WAAPA Excellence Award is presented annually at Year 12 graduation to recognise exceptional achievement in the performing arts. The award is donated by John Cowdell, the first Friend of John Curtin College of the Arts, who was President of the WA Legislative Council and Chairman of the National Trust. Cowdell regularly attends college performances and contributes significantly to the college community. The award recognises students who have demonstrated excellence in the performing arts and are recognised for their graduation achievements. Recipients are selected based on their outstanding performance and contribution to the college's arts programs.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 students; Exceptional achievement in performing arts; Recognised at year 12 graduation
special_conditions: Award is donated by John Cowdell and presented annually at Year 12 graduation to recognise graduating students who have demonstrated excellence in the performing arts.

school_name: John Curtin College Of The Arts
overview: John Curtin College of the Arts offers Arts Excellence Scholarships to selected Year 8 students in the Gifted and Talented Education programs. These scholarships recognise students demonstrating exceptional excellence in Dance, Drama, Music, and Visual Arts.
description: Arts Excellence Scholarships are awarded to selected Year 8 students who have been accepted into the Gifted and Talented Education programs in Dance, Drama, Music, and Visual Arts. These scholarships recognise students who have demonstrated exceptional artistic ability and commitment to their chosen arts discipline. The scholarships are designed to support talented young artists as they progress through their secondary education. The exact financial value varies depending on the programs and courses selected by the student. Recipients must meet the entry requirements for the Gifted and Talented Arts programs and be recommended for the scholarship based on their demonstrated excellence in their chosen arts discipline. The scholarship supports students' continued participation and achievement in their specialised arts program.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8 students; Accepted into Gifted and Talented Education programs in Dance, Drama, Music, or Visual Arts; Demonstrated exceptional artistic ability; Commitment to chosen arts discipline
special_conditions: Available to Year 8 students in Gifted and Talented Education programs in Dance, Drama, Music and Visual Arts. Selection based on exceptional artistic ability demonstrated through the GAT selection process.

school_name: John Curtin College Of The Arts
overview: John Curtin College of the Arts provides Arts Equity Scholarships to support students whose further education may be restricted due to financial need. These scholarships ensure that talented students can access the college's programs regardless of financial circumstances.
description: Arts Equity Scholarships are available for selected students at John Curtin College of the Arts whose further education may be restricted due to financial hardship or need. These need-based scholarships are designed to ensure that gifted and talented students have equitable access to the college's specialist arts programs regardless of their family's financial circumstances. The scholarships provide financial assistance to eligible students to help cover school fees and charges. The specific value of the scholarship is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on the student's financial need and circumstances. Applications are assessed based on demonstrated financial need and the student's commitment to their arts program. The scholarship is applied to the student's account for school-related expenditure.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels at John Curtin College of the Arts; Financial need or hardship; Further education restricted due to financial circumstances; Merit in arts discipline or academic subjects
special_conditions: Available to selected students whose further education may be restricted due to financial need. Assessment based on demonstrated financial need and commitment to programs. Contact college for application process and requirements.

overview: Kalamunda Senior High School's Academic Excellence Program supports top academic students in achieving excellence across all areas of study. The program has been running since 2004 and develops academic talent alongside leadership, resilience and persistence. Students are identified through competitive testing and progress through the school.
description: The Academic Excellence Program provides selective entry for high-achieving students entering Year 7. The program fee is $40 per stream for 2026 (cost for 2027 to be advised). Students are streamed together for English, Mathematics, Science and Society and Environment, allowing them to work at a pace suited to their needs. The program includes excursions, guest speakers, special programs and links to university programs. Selection is based on student Academic Excellence testing performance. Students may be selected for one or both of the available streams: English/Humanities and Mathematics/Science. The program runs from Year 7 through Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Selection based on Academic Excellence testing performance; Must be attending school in local intake area or have cross-boundary approval; Australian citizen or permanent resident status required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Proof of residential address required. Places offered according to priority order in School Education Regulations 2000. Testing date: Wednesday 4 March 2026. Siblings of students gaining enrolment from outside local intake area are not guaranteed enrolment. Annual continuation in program subject to ongoing academic performance.

program_name: Gifted and Talented Visual Arts Program
overview: Kalamunda Senior High School offers a selective Gifted and Talented Visual Arts Program open to students with natural creative artistic ability. The program allows students to develop their abilities through visual inquiry, studio work, exhibition and critical reflection. This is an integrated program delivered across Years 7-12 and has operated since 1981.
description: The Gifted and Talented Visual Arts Program is a Department of Education endorsed selective program requiring competitive application and testing. Students participate in workshops held on Saturday mornings conducted by specialist art tutors who serve as mentors. The program includes visual inquiry, studio work, exhibitions and critical reflection. Students interact with new mediums and technologies and have opportunities to exhibit work in competitions, auctions and annual school exhibitions. The program runs from Year 7 through Year 12 for successful applicants. Students can cross school boundaries to attend if selected. Workshop tutors provide ongoing mentorship and support within the art community. There are no specific program fees listed, but extra charges apply for specialist art materials and activities.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with natural creative artistic ability; Must be applying for Year 7, 9, 10 or 11 entry; Australian citizen or permanent resident status required; Application through Department of Education Gifted and Talented Programs website; Must sit ASET (Academic Selective Entrance Test) or request exemption; Testing conducted in Year 6 for Year 7 entry
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications made online only through Department of Education GT portal. Students granted ASET exemption (if disability accommodations apply) selected based solely on arts workshops and interview scores. Places offered January/February following testing. Siblings of students gaining enrolment from outside local intake not guaranteed enrolment. Saturday workshop participation required.

overview: Kalamunda Senior High School's Outdoor Adventure Program is an endorsed program unique to this school, commenced in 2007. The program is open to all students in Years 7-10 who are enthusiastic about outdoor education and want to enrich their understanding in this context. Students complete the core program by Year 10 and can continue with Outdoor Education courses in Years 11-12.
description: The Outdoor Adventure Program is designed to enhance learning across all learning areas in an outdoor education context. The class is selected in Year 7 and students complete the program at the end of Year 10. Students are able to continue their studies into Years 11 and 12 through a range of Outdoor Education courses and Certificate courses. Students in senior years enhance their leadership skills by acting as mentors and leaders to incoming Year 7 groups. The program includes camps, expeditions, excursions and special experiences. Extra charges apply for program activities. The program has been identified as an endorsed program by the Department of Education and Kalamunda SHS is the only school offering this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in Year 7-10; Must be enthusiastic about outdoor education; Selection based on application and interview; Open to all school sectors; Australian citizen or permanent resident status required; Students must demonstrate commitment to outdoor learning
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Extra charges apply for camps, expeditions and excursions. Year 7 closing dates refer to individual program pages. Programs form part of elective courses for these year levels. Leadership pathway built in for senior years. Endorsed by Department of Education.

overview: Kalamunda Senior High School offers a Performing Arts Program that promotes and develops excellence across the performing arts. The general stream includes Dance, Drama and Music studies and has been running since 2012. The program provides pathways through Middle and Senior School for students who wish to enrich their learning through arts development.
description: The Performing Arts Program (General stream) develops excellence across the three performing arts disciplines: Dance, Drama and Music. The school has access to a 250-seat Performing Arts Centre and excellent music facilities used for both lessons and performances. Students work across all three disciplines initially and are able to specialise in one or more as they progress through the school. Potential candidates should have a passion for Performing Arts but do not need to show proficiency in all three disciplines initially. The program emphasizes development of arts ideas, skills and processes and creative and critical thinking. Extra charges apply for program activities including performances, excursions and special productions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must have passion for performing arts; Do not need prior proficiency in all three disciplines; Selection through application process; Australian citizen or permanent resident status required; Must be able to participate in performances and productions
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Audition/demonstration required. Extra charges apply for performances, camps and excursions. Year 7 closing dates available on specialist programs page. All students in program automatically placed for following year. Contact Ms Anna Head – Performing Arts Program Coordinator on 9293 6400 or anna.head2@education.wa.edu.au for information.

overview: Kalamunda Senior High School offers a specialised Kalamunda Performing Arts Music stream (KPAM) that focuses on crafting musicianship and performance skills to higher levels. Entry is by audition. This program was introduced in 2017 and builds on the school's vibrant contemporary and classical music traditions.
description: The KPAM program is a specialized music stream within the performing arts offerings that focuses on developing musicianship and performance skills to advanced levels. Entry is by audition only. The school's music program features a strong IMMS instrumental program and supporting class music curriculum. Students are regularly involved in performances including ANZAC service, mid-year concerts, Rock Night, KPA showcases and Twilight Concerts. The program includes two concert bands, string orchestra, guitar ensembles, choirs, rock bands, quartets and other instrumental ensembles. The program emphasizes both contemporary music and traditional classical ensembles. Extra charges apply for the specialist music stream.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with demonstrated musical ability or commitment; Entry by audition; Must demonstrate musicianship potential; Australian citizen or permanent resident status required; Prior instrumental experience beneficial but not always required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Entry by audition. Students must commit to instrumental lessons (private or through school). All students in Music must learn an instrument or voice. Extra charges apply for instrumental instruction and performances. Contact Ms Michelle Vivian on 9293 6400 or michelle.vivian@education.wa.edu.au for music program information. Instrumental lesson times rotate to minimize subject class disruption.

overview: Kalamunda Senior High School offers a Soccer Program started in 2016 that is now open to all students enrolling in Years 7-10 from 2025 onwards. The program is open to students with any level of soccer experience, from first-time players to those seeking to improve and take the game to the next level.
description: The Kalamunda SHS Soccer Program provides opportunities for students to develop soccer skills and passion for the game. From 2025, the program is open to all students enrolling at the school in Years 7-10. The program is inclusive and welcomes any student interested in soccer, whether playing for the first time or wanting to improve their game and advance to a higher level. Entry for 2026 is through a selection process for students currently in Year 7 and for Year 6 students enrolling at the school for 2027. Trials include a variety of tests targeting physical, technical, tactical and overall gameplay ability. Successful applicants are offered a position in the program for the following year. Extra charges apply for program participation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students enrolling in Years 7-10; Interest in soccer required; Can be beginners or advanced players; Selection through trials for entry; Australian citizen or permanent resident status required; Physical fitness and tactical understanding assessed
special_conditions: Selection through trials testing physical, technical, tactical and overall gameplay ability. Successful applicants notified via email. Extra charges apply. Opportunities to gain Mini-Roos Certificate Coaching Course and coach at neighbouring primary schools. Contact school on 9293 6400 or kalamunda.shs@education.wa.edu.au for selection details. Students automatically placed in program for following year once accepted.

overview: Karrinyup Primary School offers instrumental music scholarships provided by IMSS. The scholarships enable students to learn musical instruments from Year 5 onwards, continuing through to Year 12. The program recognises students with musical aptitude and commitment to instrumental performance.
description: Karrinyup Primary School provides instrumental music scholarships through IMSS (likely a music provider) for students demonstrating musical ability. The scholarships cover lessons in trumpet, trombone, flute, clarinet, and classical guitar. Students participate in 30-minute group lessons each week, with Year 6 IMSS students forming the school's Concert Band Ensemble which rehearses weekly for 40 minutes. The exact financial benefit and cost of these scholarships is not specified on the school website. Lessons begin in Year 5 and continue through to Year 12, providing long-term musical development. Selection and enrollment procedures are not detailed on the publicly available website.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 5 students; May continue through Year 12; Demonstrated musical ability or interest; Enrollment at Karrinyup Primary School or progression to secondary school
special_conditions: Scholarships are provided by IMSS. Lessons begin in Year 5 and continue through to Year 12. Students participate in 30-minute group lessons each week. Year 6 IMSS students form the Concert Band Ensemble and rehearse weekly for 40 minutes.

overview: Karratha Senior High School, in partnership with Yara Pilbara, offers an Academic Excellence Scholarship providing $20,000 annually to support students pursuing tertiary education. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and assists with the cost of higher education for Karratha secondary students.
description: Yara Pilbara provides $20,000 each year in the form of an Academic Excellence Scholarship to Karratha secondary students. The funds are specifically designed to assist with the cost of tertiary education. The scholarship is open to students going down the university pathway as well as those pursuing apprenticeships and traineeships. Applications are assessed based on academic merit and commitment to further study. The scholarship is awarded annually and recipients are supported in their transition to post-secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Karratha secondary students; Must demonstrate academic excellence; Open to students pursuing tertiary education pathways; Eligible for university, apprenticeship, and traineeship pursuits
special_conditions: Funds are designated to assist with the cost of tertiary education. Scholarship guidelines document available for applications.

overview: Karratha Senior High School supports access to GATE programs through the Department of Education's selective entry process. The school facilitates participation in gifted and talented academic programs for eligible students, recognising exceptional intellectual ability and academic potential.
description: Karratha Senior High School supports students interested in selective academic programs through the Department of Education's GATE (Gifted and Talented Education) framework. Students must sit the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET) administered by the Department of Education for consideration into gifted and talented programs. The selection process is competitive and assesses intellectual ability, reasoning, and academic potential. GATE programs provide enriched, academically rigorous curricula designed for high-achieving students. Entry is determined centrally through the Department of Education based on standardised testing and selection criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Must sit the Academic Selective Entrance Test (ASET); Selection based on demonstrated intellectual ability; Assessment administered by Department of Education; Open to eligible students across Western Australia
special_conditions: Selection process managed by Department of Education. Testing occurs in Year 6 with placement from Year 7. School provides full support for selected students in gifted and talented programs.

program_name: SIDE (Selective International Deaf Education) / GT Online
overview: Karratha Senior High School fully supports the Gifted and Talented Selective Academic Program Online (GT Online) for students in Years 7-10. This program provides enriched online learning opportunities for gifted students, allowing them to access advanced curriculum while remaining enrolled at their home school.
description: The Gifted and Talented Selective Academic Program Online (GT Online) is an online distance education program fully supported by Karratha Senior High School for eligible students in Years 7 to 10. This program allows gifted and talented students to access advanced academic content through online delivery while maintaining their enrolment at the school. Students participate in specialist, accelerated curriculum designed for high-achieving learners. The program combines online learning with support from the school to ensure student success. Further information and enrolment details are available on the SIDE website at www.side.wa.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Year 7 to Year 10; Must be identified as gifted and talented; Eligibility determined through selection process; Support available at Karratha Senior High School
special_conditions: Program delivered online with school-based support. Students must maintain engagement with both online and school-based learning. Selection based on identified gifted and talented status. Refer to SIDE website for detailed eligibility and enrolment information.

overview: Karratha Senior High School offers the Stars Foundation program, a holistic support program specifically for Indigenous girls and young women. The program supports student engagement, Year 12 completion, and successful transitions to employment or further study.
description: Stars Foundation at Karratha Senior High School provides a comprehensive support program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls and young women. The program aims to help students attend school regularly, remain engaged in their education, complete Year 12, and successfully transition into full-time work or further study. The school employs full-time Stars Foundation staff including mentors and transitions managers who provide intensive support both during and after school. Students receive holistic support including academic mentoring, personal development, and career pathway guidance. The program has been shown to significantly improve school attendance, Year 12 completion rates, and post-school outcomes for Indigenous girls.
eligibility_criteria: Identification as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander girl or young woman; Open to all year levels attending Karratha Senior High School; Nomination and selection by school and program staff
special_conditions: Program provides holistic support including mentoring, academic assistance, and transitions management. Full-time staff dedicated to student support. Program manager: Jodi Bampton (jodi.bampton@education.wa.edu.au). Students receive support before and after graduation for employment, training, or tertiary study transitions.

overview: Karratha Senior High School hosts a Clontarf Academy program supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men. The academy uses Australian Rules Football as an engagement tool to improve education, discipline, life skills, and employment prospects.
description: The Karratha Clontarf Academy, established in 2010, operates within Karratha Senior High School to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys and young men from Years 7 to 12. The Clontarf Foundation uses the sport of Australian Rules Football as a vehicle to engage young men with education, helping them develop discipline, positive lifestyles, self-esteem, and practical life skills. The academy is staffed by full-time mentors who create welcoming, inclusive learning environments fostering personal growth and educational achievement. Students participate in football training and mentoring while accessing academic support and pathway planning. The program is designed to improve school attendance, academic outcomes, and post-school employment and education prospects.
eligibility_criteria: Identification as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander boy or young man; Open to students in Year 7 to Year 12; Selection through nomination and program intake
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Program uses Australian Rules Football as engagement strategy. Full-time mentors provide ongoing support and mentoring. School-based academy with dedicated staff. Students participate in training, mentoring, and academic support programs. Contact Clontarf Foundation website for additional information and program details.

overview: Kennedy Baptist College offers a limited number of scholarships to assist new full-time students experiencing financial hardship to achieve their full potential at the College. Scholarships cover either partial or full amount of a student's tuition fees and are only available to families who are unable to provide this due to financial hardship.
description: Kennedy Baptist College provides scholarships primarily based on financial needs of the family, however the College will also consider student performance and community involvement. Financial hardship is defined as a difficulty in coping with financial commitments due to unforeseen circumstances such as family tragedy, financial misfortune, serious illness, impacts of natural disaster and other serious or difficult circumstances. Scholarships cover either partial or full amount of a student's tuition fees. The selection process includes submission of an application form, independent support/evidence validating financial position (bank statements, tax statements, payroll summary), a curriculum vitae detailing academic achievement, sporting activities and achievements, community service, and leadership positions. The Scholarship Selection Panel will consider applications and may interview short-listed applicants. The members of the Scholarship Selection Panel are the Principal and the Director of Finance and Operations.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new full-time students; Must be experiencing financial hardship; Must submit curriculum vitae including academic achievement, sporting activities, community service, and leadership positions; Must provide independent support/evidence of financial position; Must remain enrolled as a full-time student and make satisfactory progress in line with the College
value_aud: Partial Fee Remission or Full Fee Remission
special_conditions: Applications are called for by the College and must be submitted to the Principal. The decision to offer a scholarship is at the sole discretion of the College. The allocation of scholarships is entirely at the sole discretion of the Scholarship Selection Panel, who are under no obligation to give explanation for the acceptance or rejection of an individual. Remain enrolled as a full-time student (unless there are exceptional circumstances) and make satisfactory progress in line with the College.

overview: Kennedy Baptist College offers a selective entry academic extension program for Year 7 students. KEEP is designed to provide an environment where academically advanced students can be challenged and extended to attain the highest levels of excellence alongside peers who share similar aspirations, abilities and interests.
description: The Kennedy Extension and Enrichment Program (KEEP) is a selective entry academic talent program for Year 7 students. Students selected for KEEP will participate in the full Year 7 Program, which includes Physical Education, Christian Education, French or Chinese, Computing, Health Education and Electives. For Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities, KEEP students will be in a class together with other KEEP peers. Selection is based on testing conducted during the year preceding entry to Year 7, which normally takes place in May. All students enrolled for Year 7 can participate in testing for KEEP. Students are taught by educators with expert skills and knowledge for this particular program. The program extends and enriches students throughout Years 7 to 10, exposing them to higher order cognitive concepts and skills. From Semester One, Year 9, formalised examinations start for the Extension stream.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Students must be enrolled in Year 6 at time of testing; Testing conducted during the year preceding entry to Year 7 (normally in May); All students enrolled for Year 7 can participate in testing
special_conditions: Testing occurs the year prior to Year 7 entry. Families are invited for testing and will be notified via email if their child has been selected. Alongside peers who share similar aspirations, abilities and interests, students in KEEP are taught by educators with expert skills and knowledge for this particular program.

overview: Kennedy Baptist College offers a Specialist Basketball Program to assist talented student athletes to achieve at the highest level of performance in basketball. The program aims to develop the basketball skills of students to their fullest potential in an environment that caters to their abilities.
description: The Specialist Basketball Program is designed to assist talented student athletes to achieve at the highest level of performance in basketball. Students are provided with a range of opportunities to grow and excel in an environment that caters to their abilities, allowing individuals to maximise their development and take their game to the next level. The program includes morning training sessions on Kennedy's campus courts and competition in various tournaments including the ACC Champion Schools and School Sport WA competitions. The program is led by Andy Stewart, who is the current Head Coach of Perth Lynx (Australian women's professional basketball team) and former Perth Wildcats Assistant Coach, and Jessica Van Schie, WSBL Player/Coach. Students will be required to attend two morning sessions per week, running from 6:45am–7:45am, and the program commences Week 2, Term 1. Selection is based on trials conducted during the year preceding entry or throughout the school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to talented student athletes; Must participate in selection trials; Must demonstrate basketball ability at elite level; Must be able to commit to two morning training sessions per week; Must remain enrolled as full-time student
special_conditions: Specialist Sports Program withdrawal may take place after the end of the initial probation period or at the end of the school year. Students are selected based on trials and must commit to the program schedule. Specialised program focuses on enhancing basketball skills and teaching team values and healthy competition.

overview: Kennedy Baptist College offers a Specialist Cricket Program developed in partnership with Elite Cricket Training and Athlete X to assist talented student athletes to achieve at the highest level of performance in cricket. The program provides students with exposure to professional coaching, elite facilities and opportunities to help them maximise their potential.
description: The Specialist Cricket Program has been developed in partnership with Elite Cricket Training and Athlete X Gym in Bibra Lake, offering Kennedy students the opportunity to gain exposure to professional coaching, elite facilities and a variety of opportunities to help them maximise their potential. During the year students will have access to state-of-the-art indoor facilities at Elite Cricket Training, where they will be able to utilise the ProBatter bowling machine and Merlyn Spin bowling machine. Students will all take part in a strength and conditioning program conducted by staff at Athlete X Gym. Students receive professional coaching to enhance their technique and develop their skills which are put into practice through various competitions held throughout the season, including Southern Associated Schools and the Kim Hughes Shield run by the WACA. Students will be required to attend one morning session per week, running from 6:30am–7:30am.
eligibility_criteria: Open to talented student athletes; Must participate in selection trials; Must demonstrate cricket ability at elite level; Must be able to commit to one morning training session per week; Must remain enrolled as full-time student
special_conditions: Specialist Sports Program withdrawal may take place after the end of the initial probation period or at the end of the school year. Students are selected based on trials and must commit to the program schedule. Program provides access to elite facilities typically available only to professional cricketers.

overview: Kennedy Baptist College offers an Instrumental Music Program providing students the opportunity to learn one or more instruments during College hours from highly trained and experienced music tutors. Students can choose from a range of instruments and are encouraged to participate in ensemble performances.
description: Kennedy Baptist College students have the opportunity to learn an instrument during College hours from highly trained and experienced music tutors. Whether continuing to learn an instrument or a beginner, specialist staff are matched to student ability. Classes are held on a rotating time schedule so students can participate in Kennedy's Instrumental Program and not miss any one particular class on a recurring basis. All students enrolled in the Instrumental Program are expected to be involved in an ensemble as soon as they reach the appropriate level of performance. Students are allocated to an appropriate ensemble that rehearse either before school, lunchtime or after school. Kennedy sponsors the Ensemble Program and there are no fees attached to ensemble classes. Students have the opportunity to choose from instruments including: Cello, Clarinet, Drums/Percussion, Flute, French Horn, Guitar (Acoustic, electric, bass), Piano/Keyboard, Saxophone and others. Tuition occurs once a week with each student receiving a 30 minute lesson with a qualified instrumental teacher, averaging nine lessons per term. Some instruments may have shared lessons available as advised by the tutor and Instrumental Music Coordinator. Private lesson cost $34; Shared weekly lesson cost $22.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Can be beginner or continuing musician; Instruments can be rented or purchased; Payment required before commencement of lessons each term
special_conditions: Students must purchase their own instruments (tutors can provide advice). Kennedy sponsors ensemble program with no additional fees. All students enrolled must be involved in an ensemble once appropriate performance level is reached. Payment required before commencement of lessons each term; if not received by tutor within two weeks of invoice due date, students may be refused lessons. Application closes mid-January each year.

overview: Kearnan College accepts applications for the Secondary Assistance Scheme, a Western Australian government program designed to assist families with secondary schooling costs. This scheme provides financial support to eligible parents and guardians of students enrolled in Years 7 to 12.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is a state government program administered through the Department of Education to assist low-income families with secondary schooling costs. Eligible parents or guardians with children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 at Kearnan College can apply for this assistance. The scheme provides financial support comprising an Education Program Allowance paid to the school and a Clothing Allowance paid to parents. Applications must be submitted before the end of Term 1. Recipients must hold an eligible concession card to qualify. The specific financial amounts vary based on student year level and concession card type held by the parent or guardian.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian must hold an eligible concession card; Student must be enrolled in Years 7 to 12; Student must be enrolled at Kearnan College; Application must be submitted before end of Term 1
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Recipients must hold an eligible concession card. The scheme comprises two components: an Education Program Allowance paid to the school and a Clothing Allowance paid to parents or guardians. Applications are due before the end of Term 1. Non-government school recipients receive different amounts than public school students.

overview: Kingsway Christian College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise gifted and talented students entering Years 7 to 12. The scholarships are awarded based on outstanding performance in the ACER Scholarship examination and provide opportunities for students to develop their academic ability and maturity at the College.
description: Kingsway Christian College offers Academic Scholarships via the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER) for students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2027. Successful applicants are not required to reapply and the scholarship lasts until the student completes Year 12. Students are not required to maintain certain grades or marks to keep their scholarship, but are expected to model good behaviour. The College does not have a set number of scholarships and they will vary from year to year. The specific financial value of the scholarship is not publicly disclosed on the College website, and applicants should contact the College directly for details on the exact fee reduction or assistance provided. Selection is based entirely on performance in the ACER Scholarship examination conducted at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 12 in 2027; Must be a domestic student (International Students are not eligible to apply); Must sit and qualify on the ACER Scholarship examination; Selection based on ACER exam performance
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Successful applicants are not required to reapply. The scholarship lasts until the student completes Year 12. Students are expected to model good behaviour. Selection is based on ACER exam performance and the College reserves discretion in awarding scholarships.

overview: Kingsway Christian College advises families about the government-funded Secondary Assistance Scheme available to eligible parents or guardians of Year 7-12 students. This scheme provides financial assistance of up to $350 per year to eligible families who hold specific Centrelink or Veterans Affairs cards.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is a government program administered by the State Education Department that provides financial assistance of up to $350 to parents or guardians of Year 7 to Year 12 students who hold an eligible Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card. The assistance is available on an annual basis. To access this scheme, families must obtain application forms from the College reception. The application form must be witnessed by a member of staff from the Finance Department. Proof of eligibility (the relevant card) must be provided. This is a need-based government support program, not a College-administered scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7-12 students; Parents or guardians must hold an eligible Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card; Must provide proof of eligibility; Application form must be witnessed by College Finance Department staff
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: This is a government scheme, not a College scholarship. Application forms must be obtained from College reception and witnessed by Finance Department staff. Proof of eligibility (Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card) must be provided.

overview: Kiara College supports eligible families through the Secondary Assistance Scheme, a government subsidy available to families holding concession cards. The scheme provides educational program assistance and clothing allowances to reduce financial barriers to secondary education.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a government-funded support program available during Term 1 only to families holding eligible concession cards. The program provides $235 in Educational Program assistance which is paid directly to the school to cover voluntary contributions in Years 7-10 and charges in Years 11-12. Additionally, eligible families receive a $300 clothing allowance which can be paid either directly to parents/guardians or to the school towards charges. Applications are made online with assistance from the school's Administration office or can be submitted via email to kiara.college@education.wa.edu.au with a copy of both sides of the concession card. The application deadline is typically Friday 11 April in 2025. This program is widely publicised by the school at enrolment and via the website.
eligibility_criteria: Family must hold one of the eligible concession cards; Available during Term 1 only; Applicant must present card when applying to enable authorisation
special_conditions: Applications close Friday 11 April each year. Card must be presented when applying to enable authorisation. Financial assistance is available as both Educational Program assistance and clothing allowance.

overview: The Polly Farmer Foundation Follow the Dream program at Kiara College supports the aspirations of Aboriginal students to successfully complete their secondary schooling. The program provides individualised after-school tutoring, mentoring, and enrichment activities to help students achieve academic success.
description: Follow the Dream is a comprehensive support program that helps Aboriginal students complete their secondary schooling through individualised after-school tutoring and mentoring. The program's core focus is achieving academic success, with tutors providing targeted support that improves performance across all areas of the curriculum. Beyond academics, students experience enrichment activities that broaden horizons, celebrate culture, and develop knowledge, skills, behaviours and dispositions to assist students in living and working while pursuing their aspirations. The program emphasises developing well-rounded students who can succeed both in education and future employment. Participation is non-monetary in nature but provides substantial non-monetary support through tutoring time and mentoring relationships.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander student; Must be enrolled at Kiara College; Seeking to complete secondary schooling
special_conditions: Program operates year-round to support student progression. Tailored support is provided based on individual student needs. Program focuses on both academic achievement and holistic student development.

overview: Clontarf Academy helps young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander men at Kiara College to attend school, finish Year 12 and enter employment. The program uses supportive relationships and a welcoming environment to develop self-esteem, confidence and positive life outcomes.
description: Clontarf Academy at Kiara College is a dedicated support program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander boys that helps participants attend school consistently, complete Year 12, and transition into employment. The program creates a supportive, welcoming environment where young men develop improved self-esteem and confidence. These qualities enable participants to engage positively in education, employment and broader society. The program uses a relationship-based approach, combining structured activities and mentoring to support young men's development. Through participation, students gain the confidence and skills needed to pursue post-secondary employment pathways. The program is non-monetary but provides substantial in-school support and mentoring.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander male student; Must be enrolled at Kiara College; Seeking to complete Year 12
special_conditions: Program operates year-round. Participants have different uniform requirements from standard college uniform. Emphasis on sustained engagement and attendance to support Year 12 completion.

overview: Shooting Stars empowers Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls at Kiara College to make informed choices about their education and employment journey. The program combines advocacy, support, engagement activities, health and wellbeing sessions to strengthen confidence and cultural identity.
description: Shooting Stars is a comprehensive support program specifically designed for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander girls at Kiara College. The program combines advocacy and tailored support with engagement activities and health and wellbeing sessions. These elements work together to strengthen participants' confidence, cultural identity, and positive attitudes while promoting overall health and wellbeing. The program operates with a minimum attendance target of 80% at school, with a goal to increase this to over 90%. Individual school attendance data is tracked weekly and summarised at the end of each term to monitor program effectiveness. Program coordinators analyse data weekly to identify improvements and provide additional support to students who need it. Tailored attendance plans are developed in collaboration with the school and key Aboriginal community corporations to support each student. The program is non-monetary but provides substantial in-school support services.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander female student; Must be enrolled at Kiara College; Minimum 80% school attendance target
special_conditions: Attendance is tracked weekly and summarised termly. Minimum 80% attendance target with goal to reach 90%. Tailored attendance plans developed for each student. Program coordinators provide individualised support based on weekly data analysis.

program_name: Kinross College Year 11 Scholarships - RISE Value Categories
overview: Kinross College offers scholarships for students entering Year 11 in 2026. There are four different scholarship categories, each underpinned by one of the school's RISE values: Respect, Inspire, Support and Excel. The scholarships are designed to recognise and support student talent and achievement.
description: Kinross College is delighted to offer scholarships to students entering Year 11 in 2026. Four distinct scholarship categories are available, each reflecting one of the school's core RISE values (Respect, Inspire, Support, and Excel). Each scholarship provides financial support of $1000 to be used for educational resources such as textbooks, school supplies, school excursions, leavers jackets, school ball tickets, incidental fees or uniform across both Years 11 and 12, provided the student remains enrolled at the College. Each scholarship category has an individual focus, and students may apply for more than one scholarship type. The selection process involves submission of an application and a college-based referee who must attest to the student's claims. A panel will evaluate applications to determine successful recipients. Up to 6 scholarships are available in total.
eligibility_criteria: Must be in Year 10 (in 2025) when applying; Must be continuing into Year 11 at Kinross College to complete their Western Australian Certificate of Education; College-based referee required; Must respond to the focus of each scholarship value category
special_conditions: Financial support of $1000 to be used for educational resources such as textbooks, school supplies, school excursions, leavers jackets, school ball tickets, incidental fees or uniform across both Years 11 and 12, provided the student remains at the College. Late applications cannot be accepted. A College-based referee must be provided to attest the student's claims in their application.

overview: Kolbe Catholic College is committed to providing opportunities for gifted music students in an environment that promotes excellence within the Performing Arts Learning Area. The College offers multiple scholarships to students who demonstrate an affinity for music and whose enrolment is confirmed for commencement in Year 7. Kolbe's Music Scholarships are fully funded by the College, with no money received from outside agencies.
description: New Music Scholarships are offered annually to students entering Year 7. The scholarships cover the full cost of group music tuition fees and are tenable for Years 7, 8 and 9, providing the student's conduct, overall contribution to the College, and their progress, both in music and other academic areas, are satisfactory. The cost of hiring a College instrument, if required, will also be covered during the first year of the scholarship, though offers are based on the current needs of the College Music Program. In the first year, recipients attend weekly group lessons with a tutor in a group of three. In the second year, as students develop their skills and at the Music Department's discretion, the weekly lesson scheme will change to one group lesson and one solo lesson in rotation. Recipients must attend regular 30-minute lessons (no less than 30 per year for each year of the scholarship) with a College music tutor for the instrument initially offered; participate fully in at least two music ensembles for the duration of the scholarship; maintain high standards of effort, practice and performance; and maintain enrolment in the Year 7 Specialist Music Program and achieve at least a "B" grade for the year.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 student with enrolment at the College confirmed for Year 7; Demonstrate ability on at least one musical instrument or voice; Provide most recent Music report and brief reference from music tutor, class music teacher or related organisation; Provide evidence of competition or school awards, or adjudication feedback relating to music; Provide certificates of academic or cultural achievements; Available to take part in a scholarship audition and interview; Willing to fully participate in the College's co-curricular activities in accordance with the ethos of Kolbe Catholic College
special_conditions: No scholarship application fees apply. The selection panel meets to select applicants who will be offered an audition. The selection panel consists of the Music Coordinator and representative of the College Executive. Submitting an application does not guarantee an audition. Successful applicants are to be advised of their audition date and time via email approximately two weeks before auditions are scheduled. Scholarships are tenable for Years 7, 8 and 9 subject to satisfactory conduct, overall contribution to the College, and progress in music and other academic areas. In the first year, cost of hiring a College instrument, if required, will also be covered. Recipients must participate in at least two music ensembles for the duration of the scholarship and participate in major College music events. Unsuccessful scholarship applicants will be offered the opportunity to enrol in the music tuition program at normal cost.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship
overview: At Kolbe Catholic College, we are committed to providing opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from Year 7 to 12 to achieve outstanding educational outcomes. The College offers annual Aboriginal Scholarships, allowing us to work with and assist families in giving broad career opportunities to young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. Kolbe's Aboriginal Scholarship is fully financed by the College, with no funds received from outside agencies.
description: At least two Aboriginal Scholarships are awarded each year to students commencing Year 7. Each scholarship is awarded for a period of six years and is renewed on an annual basis, subject to a satisfactory review. As long as the student's performance is acceptable, the scholarship covers half the cost of the annual tuition fee for the duration of the student's time at the College. The College recognises that Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students may experience cultural isolation and require academic support. To ease the transition from primary to secondary school, the College provides teaching assistance through an Aboriginal Liaison Officer, and offers an annual camp designed to strengthen bonds between students and deepen their connection to culture and heritage. Instrumental music tuition is not covered by the Aboriginal Scholarship, as music tutors are paid in addition to class teachers.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 students scheduled for enrolment into Year 7 in 2028 at time of application; Student must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Both family and student must have a strong desire for a Catholic education; Student must live within the College's catchment area (Rockingham, Kwinana or Serpentine/Mundijong); Student should be willing to become involved in the College's co-curricular activities in accordance with the ethos of Kolbe Catholic College; Completion of the Aboriginal Scholarship Application Form; Attachment of a copy of the prospective student's full birth certificate; Attachment of copies of the student's two most recent academic school reports
special_conditions: No application fees apply. The Selection Panel will review the information provided by families to shortlist candidates. Successful applicants will be invited to the College for an interview. All Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarships are reviewed annually by the College Principal. Continuing to demonstrate a positive representation of the College's values as a Kolbe student is required. Feedback will be provided to each recipient and their family on an annual basis. In circumstances of continued unacceptable behaviour or continued lack of effort in studies, the Scholarship may be withdrawn. Parents are expected to meet the costs of compulsory charges such as subject levies, camp fees and excursions. Families eligible for other concessions should note that only one discount is applicable. Scholarship covers half the cost of annual tuition fee only; other compulsory charges, uniforms, stationery and iPad lease are the responsibility of parents.

program_name: Department of Education School of Instrumental Music Scholarship
overview: Kyilla Primary School offers free instrumental music tuition through the Department of Education's School of Instrumental Music Scholarship Program. Students in Year 4 are assessed on their musical aptitude, and selected students receive complimentary weekly tuition from peripatetic music tutors.
description: Year 4 students at Kyilla Primary School are assessed on their musical aptitude. A number of students are offered a scholarship of free weekly tuition by peripatetic music tutors through the Department's School of Instrumental Music. Tuition is provided to children in Years 5 and 6 only. Instruments offered through this program include clarinet, brass and guitar. Successful scholarship recipients are automatically members of the school choir, and students who play clarinet or brass also participate in the Mt Hawthorn Area Band. This scholarship provides full tuition fee remission for instrumental music lessons and includes participation in co-curricular music ensembles.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 4; Assessed musical aptitude during Year 4 assessment period; Continuing enrollment in Years 5 and 6; Selection by musical aptitude assessment
special_conditions: Tuition is only offered to children in Years 5 and 6, despite assessment occurring in Year 4. Successful students are automatically members of the choir. Students playing clarinet or brass participate in Mt Hawthorn Area Band.

overview: Lake Monger Primary School students may access a complimentary weekly dance class scholarship offered through Humphreys Dance Schools. The scholarship introduces young people to dancesport and promotes dancing as a family activity.
description: The Humphreys Dance Schools Program offers a scholarship to Lake Monger Primary School students that entitles each recipient to attend a weekly complimentary class held on Saturdays at Humphreys Dance Studio in Victoria Park. Classes are held during the school term and the scholarship is effective for one year from the date of commencement. The program has been running for over 20 years and aims to introduce interested young people to dancesport, encourage a love of dance, and promote dancing as a family affair. The offer extends to siblings who may not be learning in a schools program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Lake Monger Primary School students; Students who participate in the Humphreys Dance Schools Program at their school are eligible; Siblings of participating students are also eligible
special_conditions: Complimentary weekly class held on Saturdays at Victoria Park location. Classes are held during school term only. Scholarship is effective for one year from date of commencement. Offered by Humphreys Dance Schools in partnership with the school.

overview: Lake Joondalup Baptist College offers the Academic Excellence Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic merit. The scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, with a limited number awarded annually to eligible secondary students.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship at Lake Joondalup Baptist College is designed to recognise and reward students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement. A limited number of scholarships will be awarded annually to students. Applicants are expected to demonstrate a high level of ability and uphold the College values: seek wisdom, act justly, love mercy and walk humbly, and to be committed to involvement and participation in the life of the College community. The scholarship typically offers a 25% discount on applicable tuition fees, with the possibility of up to 50% discount in exceptional circumstances, at the discretion of the College. Music and Academic Scholarships remain for the duration of Secondary School, subject to the criteria and expectations indicated.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Must uphold College values and be committed to involvement in the College community
special_conditions: Applications are only open to Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents. Scholarships operate within the College's Enrolment Policy and are dependent on enrolment places being available. All external applicants are required to complete a Registration of Interest prior to an application being considered. Recipients must display the values of the College and be consistent in their endeavour towards their educational pursuit, and demonstrate their willingness to contribute to the life of the College community. Non-public, confidential awards; identity of recipients will not be disclosed in any public forum.

overview: Lake Joondalup Baptist College offers the Music Excellence Scholarship to students who display excellence in music performance and ability. This scholarship recognises outstanding musical achievement and commitment to the college music program, with a limited number awarded annually.
description: The Music Excellence Scholarship at Lake Joondalup Baptist College is awarded to students who display excellence in music and demonstrate exceptional musical ability. A limited number of scholarships will be awarded annually for those who display excellence in music. Applicants are expected to demonstrate a high level of ability and uphold the College values: seek wisdom, act justly, love mercy and walk humbly, and to be committed to involvement and participation in the music program and the broader College community. The scholarship typically offers a 25% discount on applicable tuition fees, with the possibility of up to 50% discount in exceptional circumstances, at the discretion of the College. Music and Academic Scholarships remain for the duration of Secondary School, subject to the criteria and expectations indicated.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7-11; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must demonstrate excellence in music; Must uphold College values and be committed to involvement in the College community and music program
special_conditions: Applications are only open to Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents. Scholarships operate within the College's Enrolment Policy and are dependent on enrolment places being available. All external applicants are required to complete a Registration of Interest prior to an application being considered. Recipients must display the values of the College and be consistent in their endeavour towards their educational pursuit, and demonstrate their willingness to contribute to the life of the College community. Non-public, confidential awards; identity of recipients will not be disclosed in any public forum.

overview: Lake Joondalup Baptist College offers Opportunity Bursaries to provide financial opportunities for students who would otherwise be unable to attend or continue at the College due to their family's financial circumstances. The College particularly encourages applications from children of past students.
description: Lake Joondalup Baptist College offers a limited number of Opportunity Bursaries, which have been created to provide opportunities for students for whom the College believes would benefit from joining the LJBC community for their education. These bursaries are available to a limited number of students who would perhaps otherwise be unable to attend or continue at Lake Joondalup Baptist College due to their family's financial circumstances. A limited number of Opportunity Bursaries are available to new as well as currently enrolled students from Kindy through Year 12. All recipients will be expected to display the values of the College: seek wisdom, act justly, love mercy and walk humbly, to be consistent in their endeavour towards their educational pursuit, and to demonstrate their willingness to contribute to the life of the College community. Bursaries typically offer a 25% discount on applicable tuition fees, with the possibility of up to 50% discount in exceptional circumstances. Opportunity Bursaries remain for the recipient's schooling, subject to criteria.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Kindergarten through Year 12; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Financial need demonstrated; Must uphold College values and be willing to contribute to the College community; Past student families encouraged to apply
special_conditions: Bursaries and scholarships operate within the College's Enrolment Policy and are dependent on enrolment places being available. All external applicants are required to complete a Registration of Interest prior to an application being considered. The College's expectation is that applicants and their families will be able to express and establish their dedication to actively supporting the mission, purpose and values of the College. Opportunity Bursaries remain for the recipient's schooling, subject to criteria. Non-public, confidential awards; identity of recipients will not be disclosed in any public forum. Applications are accepted at any time and will be kept on file until an opportunity becomes available.

overview: Lake Joondalup Baptist College offers Financial Assistance Bursaries to families who experience unforeseen changes in financial circumstances after entering the College. The bursary is available to eligible families who require support and is subject to a needs assessment.
