description: Lake Joondalup Baptist College recognises that families may encounter unexpected financial challenges that could not have been anticipated at the time of entry into the College. The granting of a Financial Assistance Bursary would be subject to a needs assessment to be conducted by the College in collaboration with the family. This assessment allows the College to understand the specific circumstances and needs of the family, ensuring that assistance is provided where it is most needed. Families are welcome to submit a Financial Assistance Bursary application at any time. Financial Assistance Bursaries are available to families who are eligible to be considered. Bursaries typically offer a 25% discount on applicable tuition fees, with the possibility of up to 50% discount in exceptional circumstances. Financial Bursaries are reviewed annually based on personal circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Kindergarten through Year 12; Australian Citizens or Permanent Residents only; Must demonstrate unexpected change in financial circumstances; Subject to College needs assessment; Currently enrolled families
special_conditions: Financial Assistance Bursary applications may be submitted at any time. The granting of a Financial Assistance Bursary is subject to a needs assessment conducted by the College with the family. Bursaries operate within the College's Enrolment Policy and are dependent on enrolment places being available. The College's expectation is that applicants and their families will be able to express and establish their dedication to actively supporting the mission, purpose and values of the College. Financial Bursaries are reviewed annually based on personal circumstances. Non-public, confidential awards; identity of recipients will not be disclosed in any public forum.

overview: La Salle College offers scholarships to families in need for students who display academic excellence and support the faith and service values of St John Baptist de La Salle. The scholarship programme recognises students demonstrating both financial hardship and academic merit.
description: La Salle College offers scholarships to families in need for students who display academic excellence, and support faith and service values of St John Baptist de La Salle. The scholarship process begins with completion of an online application form. A shortlist of families will then be invited to interview for scholarship places. Successful applicants will have their tuition fees covered for the time they study at the College. The exact financial benefit is determined on a case-by-case basis during the interview process. Applicants must demonstrate both financial need and academic excellence. The scholarship is renewable annually, with reviews conducted each year for each recipient.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial need; Students displaying academic excellence; Support for faith and service values of St John Baptist de La Salle; Families in need seeking assistance with tuition
special_conditions: Successful applicants will have their tuition fees covered for the time they study at the College. A review will be conducted every year for each scholarship recipient. Scholarship holders must support the faith and service values of St John Baptist de La Salle and maintain academic excellence.

overview: La Salle College is committed to working with First Nations people to support and foster quality education opportunities. The First Nations Bursary Programme is funded by the College and contributes to assisting First Nations families with the education of their children.
description: La Salle College is committed to working with First Nations people to support and foster quality education opportunities. The First Nations Bursary Programme is funded by the College and contributes to assisting First Nations families with the education of their children. Opportunities to apply for bursaries occur each year during Term 4. Successful applicants will have their tuition fees covered for the time they study at the College. The bursary is renewable annually, with a review conducted every year for each bursary recipient. Prior to commencing the First Nations Bursary application process, applicants are encouraged to contact the Director of Pedagogy (Special Projects) for guidance.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations heritage; Families seeking educational opportunities for their children; Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Commitment to education and College values
special_conditions: Opportunities to apply for bursaries occur each year during Term 4. A review will be conducted every year for each bursary recipient. Successful applicants will have their tuition fees covered for the time they study at the College. Prior to commencing the First Nations Bursary application process, applicants are encouraged to contact the Director of Pedagogy (Special Projects) Ms Jade Lori by email or call 9449 0601.

overview: La Salle College offers a two-year Band Scholarship Program for Year 7/8 students that nurtures young musicians by providing subsidised instrumental tuition for Band Instruments, Piano and Voice. Selected students participate in Year 7/8 band and rotational joint instrumental lessons during school hours.
description: The Year 7/8 Band Scholarship Program at La Salle College is a two-year program that nurtures young musicians by providing subsidised instrumental tuition for Band Instruments, Piano and Voice. Selected students participate in Year 7/8 band as well as rotational joint instrumental lessons during school hours, guided by expert tutors. This pathway provides students with a comprehensive and immersive musical education, combining technical skill development with performance experience, ensemble collaboration, and theoretical knowledge. The scholarship provides a partial fee reduction or subsidy on instrumental lesson fees for the two-year duration (Year 7 and Year 8). All students must participate in the Year 7 Band ensemble and the College ensembles are a core requirement for scholarship holders.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 or Year 8; Interest in learning Band Instruments, Piano or Voice; Commitment to participate in Year 7/8 band and ensemble activities; Willingness to engage in comprehensive musical education
special_conditions: Scholarship is a two-year commitment (Year 7 and Year 8). All scholarship holders must participate in Year 7 Band and college ensembles. Participation in College ensembles is a core requirement for scholarship holders. No prior experience is required. Unlike the Instrumental Tuition Pathway, these lessons are subsidised through the scholarship.

overview: La Salle College offers 2026 Year 9-12 Music Scholarships to current music students at the College. These scholarships provide a 50 percent discount on instrumental lesson fees for one year and recognise ongoing commitment to musical excellence.
description: La Salle College is accepting applications for 2026 Year 9-12 Music Scholarships. These scholarships are open to current music students at the College and offer a 50 percent discount on instrumental lesson fees for one year. Auditions will take place during Weeks 3 and 4 of Term 4. The scholarship is valued at 50% reduction in instrumental lesson fees and is renewable for one academic year. Students must maintain their musical studies and participation in ensembles. This is an opportunity to support students continuing to develop their musical skills and passion under the guidance of experienced tutors.
eligibility_criteria: Current music students at La Salle College; Year 9 to Year 12 students; Demonstrated musical ability and commitment; Participation in College music program
special_conditions: Open to current music students at the College only. Auditions will take place during Weeks 3 and 4 of Term 4. Applications must be completed online and close on Monday 3 November 2025. Scholarship provides 50% discount on instrumental lesson fees for one year. Students must continue their musical studies and maintain participation in College ensembles.

overview: Landsdale Christian School offers Academic Scholarships to students entering Year 7 who have demonstrated academic excellence. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning.
description: Landsdale Christian School awards academic scholarships to Year 7 entry students who have demonstrated exceptional academic excellence and commitment to their studies. The scholarship has a value of 50 percent (50%) of the school tuition fees and is tenable for the duration of the student's education at Landsdale Christian School; however, it is subject to annual review. Successful applicants will be committed to their studies and can demonstrate involvement in other interests and their community. While academic application is important, the school is interested in students who have a sense of who they are and who they can be. Selection involves submission of academic records, a character reference from someone who has known the student for not less than two years, and a hand-written letter to the Landsdale Christian School Council outlining reasons for scholarship consideration. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview with the Principal and Head of Secondary.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2025; Must be Australian residents; Applicants can be current students or those interested in entering the school; Demonstrated academic excellence required; Commitment to studies and involvement in other interests and community
special_conditions: Scholarship is subject to annual review. Recipients must be committed to their studies and demonstrate involvement in other interests and their community. Scholarship is tenable for the duration of the student's education at Landsdale Christian School.

overview: Landsdale Christian School offers Academic Scholarships to students entering Year 10 who have demonstrated academic excellence. The scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and commitment to learning.
description: Landsdale Christian School awards academic scholarships to Year 10 entry students who have demonstrated exceptional academic excellence and commitment to their studies. The scholarship has a value of 50 percent (50%) of the school tuition fees and is tenable for the duration of the student's education at Landsdale Christian School; however, it is subject to annual review. Successful applicants will be committed to their studies and can demonstrate involvement in other interests and their community. While academic application is important, the school is interested in students who have a sense of who they are and who they can be. Selection involves submission of academic records, a character reference from someone who has known the student for not less than two years, and a hand-written letter to the Landsdale Christian School Council outlining reasons for scholarship consideration. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to an interview with the Principal and Head of Secondary.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10 in 2025; Must be Australian residents; Applicants can be current students or those interested in entering the school; Demonstrated academic excellence required; Commitment to studies and involvement in other interests and community
special_conditions: Scholarship is subject to annual review. Recipients must be committed to their studies and demonstrate involvement in other interests and their community. Scholarship is tenable for the duration of the student's education at Landsdale Christian School.

program_name: Lakeland Empowering Athlete Program (LEAP) in Touch Football & Sport Science
overview: Lakeland Senior High School offers the Lakeland Empowering Athlete Program (LEAP), a specialist program designed to meet the highest educational standards while fostering athleticism, leadership, and teamwork. The program provides a dynamic blend of skills development in Touch Football combined with comprehensive Sport Science education, nurturing athletes to new heights of excellence.
description: LEAP is an Approved Specialist Program tailored to the highest standards of the Western Australian curriculum, offering a transformative journey for students passionate about Touch Football and Sport Science. The program encompasses both practical skills in invasion games and spatial awareness, plus in-depth Sport Science components exploring anatomy, physiology, nutrition, and cutting-edge training methodologies. Students in Years 7-10 complete officiating qualifications in various sports and can earn payment for officiating services at Lightning Carnivals, SSWA competitions, and Country Week. Students also complete Bronze Medallion and First Aid certifications to enhance employment opportunities at venues such as Cockburn Arc and Cockburn Ice Skating. In upper school, students select either a VET or ATAR pathway depending on their future goals. The program is led by expert coaches and sport science professionals in a supportive environment with strong partnerships to local associations and governing bodies.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 and above; Demonstrated passion and aptitude for Touch Football and Sport Science; Must attend trials and auditions; Interview required; Selection based on athletic ability and commitment to excellence
special_conditions: Students attending LEAP may also access the academic enrichment program ACHIEVE and Specialist Music Technology where they meet selection criteria for these programs. Non-refundable program based on annual review. Parents will be notified about interview and audition times. Conduct and attendance expectations must be maintained.

overview: Lakeland Senior High School offers the Specialist Music Technology Program, a cutting-edge Approved Specialist Program renowned as a benchmark for excellence in music technology and contemporary music production. The program is specifically designed to nurture the next generation of recording artists, producers, and music industry professionals.
description: The Specialist Music Technology Program is a comprehensive Year 7-12 offering that caters to both academically-inclined and vocationally-focused students with a strong passion for music and its underlying technologies. Students gain hands-on experience in creating, producing, and performing music using the latest music industry technologies, including recording in professional studios, sound editing, music mastering using industry-standard hardware and software, and information technology applications for music distribution. The program equips students with critical creative thinking skills to become innovators in the music industry. Students studying Music in Year 11 and 12 receive an automatic ATAR of 70, with pathways established through to a Certificate IV in Music (Performance). Essential extracurricular components include regular workshops, rehearsals, and concerts; annual projects producing high-quality music albums and organizing major community music events; and developing production, management, and promotional skills.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated musical ability and/or passion for music technology; Commitment to practicing and improving musical skills independently and collaboratively; Growth mindset striving for excellence; Flexibility and willingness to participate in extra-curricular performances requiring varied timetable; Good behaviour and regular attendance; Commitment to upholding school values and code of conduct; Must sign code of conduct agreement
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Approved Specialist Program allowing students to apply from anywhere across Western Australia. Students can access ACHIEVE program where they meet selection criteria. Non-refundable program based on merit. Annual review of student progress and conduct. Attendance at extra-curricular performances is required. Varied timetable may be required.

overview: Lakeland Senior High School offers the ACHIEVE Program, an innovative school-based program designed to provide academic enrichment and curriculum differentiation. The program is designed to challenge and inspire students, fostering a love for learning and academic excellence while focusing on the whole child with a future-focused education.
description: ACHIEVE is a comprehensive academic enrichment program offered across Years 7-10 that provides curriculum differentiation and enrichment opportunities beyond standard classroom learning. Students participate in educational experiences including community competitions, excursions, and after-school masterclasses with expert teachers, with an emphasis on ICT use. The program offers targeted goal-oriented support and individual career pathway planning to develop resilient young adults with a sense of purpose and self-worth. Students benefit from a personalized and supportive educational environment within a small school setting, learning from experienced and dedicated educators passionate about student success. Community engagement through project-based learning and civic responsibility initiatives are integral components. Year 6 students entering Year 7 must complete and submit an application form. From Years 7-10, students are identified by Heads of Learning Area (HoLAs) for selection based on merit, with no quota per year group.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated commitment to academic excellence and personal growth; Year 6 students entering Year 7 must submit application form; Years 7-10 students identified by Heads of Learning Area based on merit; Aspirational mindset; No quota per year group
special_conditions: School-based program (not an Approved Specialist Program). Merit-based selection with no quota limitations per year level. Annual review of student progress and commitment to academic excellence. Students must maintain good standing and commitment to school values. Selection process may include teacher recommendations and academic performance review.

overview: Lakeland Senior High School offers a Department of Education Approved Education Support Program designed to cater for students with disabilities. The program focuses on a student-centred planning model that encompasses academic programs as well as social-emotional wellbeing of students with a range of physical and intellectual disabilities.
description: The Education Support Program is a state-of-the-art facility established from 2023 with a purpose-built building designed to meet the needs of students with a range of physical and intellectual disabilities. The program focuses on progression and achievement starting at each student's level with specialized instruction programs implemented step-by-step. The building is equipped with a specialist sensory room and independent living skills room, with easy access to sensory spaces for breaks. All classrooms have access to technology including iPad and smart boards to support student learning. Visual communication resources, prompt cards, and visual timetables support learning environments tailored to meet the sensory and social/emotional needs of students. Staff have specialized training to cater for individual needs with focus on Social Emotional Wellbeing, Protective Behaviours, Independent Living skills, and Individual Behaviour analysis. Curriculum programs include ASDAN-Towards Independence and ASDAN-Workright to improve students' employability skills. Therapists can attend school through a Therapy in Schools Service Agreement.
eligibility_criteria: Students with eligibility for Individual Disability Allocation for Intellectual Disability; Students with eligibility for Individual Disability Allocation for Autism Spectrum Disorder and demonstrated high educational need; Students with eligibility for Individual Disability Allocation for Severe Mental Disorder or Physical Disability and demonstrated high educational need; Placement reviewed on a yearly basis
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Department of Education Approved Program. Placement reviewed annually. Therapy programs delivered at school must be aligned with Individual Education Plan (IEP) goals. Families and therapists must meet with teacher/Learning Support Coordinator to discuss goals and proposed therapy. Education staff welcome additional programs and strategies to support students meeting their goals.

school_name: Leeming Senior High School Education Support Centre
program_name: Science and Technology Academy - Specialist Member
overview: Leeming Senior High School offers the Science and Technology Academy (STA) Specialist Member program for academically gifted students in STEM fields. The program provides students with exceptional science, mathematics and technology abilities the opportunity to learn from specialist teachers who are experts in their fields. The program is designed to foster academic achievement, higher-order thinking, and participation in cutting-edge STEM disciplines.
description: The Science and Technology Academy (STA) Specialist Member category is an Approved Specialist Program within the WA Department of Education designed for students selected through academic testing. The program provides academically gifted students with the opportunity to learn from specialist Science, Mathematics and Technology teachers, experts in their fields with a passion for teaching. Students experience an extensive co-curricular program of enrichment and extension activities facilitated by specialist program coordinators, designed to foster academic achievement and greater interest in STEM. The program includes incursions, excursions, competitions, after-school activities, and term challenges. Entry is by merit selection through written application and academic testing, with testing taking place in early June utilising diagnostic tests in science understanding, numeracy, literacy and general capabilities. For Year 6 entry into Year 7, applications open in February and close mid-May. For Years 8-10, entry is based on student achievement in school-based assessments, teacher recommendations and position availability, with students achieving consistent marks over 80% in relevant subjects considered suitable. The program is offered at no additional financial cost beyond standard school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Gifted and talented students with high academic ability in science and mathematics; Open to students entering Year 7 from Year 6; Applications submitted in February-May period; Selected through academic testing; For mid-year entry to Years 8, 9, 10: achieving consistent marks over 80% in relevant subjects; positive teacher recommendations; availability of positions
special_conditions: Successful selection into a Specialist Program is a privilege. Remaining in the program requires students to maintain the academic and behavioural expectations set by the school and adhere to the Specialist Programs Policy and Code of Conduct at all times. Students who have not met these expectations will engage in a Performance Review Mentoring Process.

school_name: Leeming Senior High School Education Support Centre
program_name: Science and Technology Academy - Associate Member
overview: Leeming Senior High School offers the Science and Technology Academy (STA) Associate Member program to all mainstream students interested in exploring STEM fields. This category provides students with an opportunity to develop their STEM interests and abilities through collaboration with like-minded peers and exposure to cutting-edge research and technology without requiring advanced academic testing.
description: The Science and Technology Academy (STA) Associate Member category is available to all mainstream students interested in exploring STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics) fields. The program welcomes students including those with academic gifts and those interested in developing STEM skills and knowledge. Associate Members gain access to enrichment and extension activities, collaborative learning opportunities with like-minded peers, exposure to cutting-edge research and technology, and competitive advantages in STEM preparation. This category offers a flexible entry pathway for students who demonstrate interest in STEM subjects but may not have qualified for Specialist Member selection through academic testing. The program fosters academic achievement, critical thinking, and deeper engagement with STEM disciplines through varied co-curricular activities. Entry is available to all mainstream students without additional selection testing or restrictions. The program is offered at no additional financial cost beyond standard school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all mainstream students at Leeming Senior High School; Interest in STEM fields; Students in Years 7-10; No academic testing requirement for entry; Self-selection option available
special_conditions: Associate Members are not subject to the same conduct and academic expectations as Specialist Members but are expected to participate positively in program activities. Participation is voluntary and ongoing.

school_name: Leeming Senior High School Education Support Centre
overview: Leeming Senior High School offers the Academic Extension Program (AEP), a school-based enrichment program in English and Humanities and Social Sciences for academically talented students. The program aims to inspire and challenge students through intellectual inquiry, fostering excellence and a collaborative learning environment that develops deep critical thinking and analytical skills.
description: The Academic Extension Program (AEP) is a school-based enrichment program designed for academically talented students in English and Humanities and Social Sciences (HASS). The program aims to inspire and challenge students through intellectual inquiry, fostering excellence and creating a collaborative learning environment. The AEP enhances students' critical thinking, analytical and communication skills while developing deep understanding of literary and historical contexts to foster a well-rounded perspective. Entry is by merit selection through written application and academic testing process, with applications opening in February and closing mid-May for Year 6 entry into Year 7 the following year. Academic testing takes place in early June utilising a full range of diagnostic tests in literacy, numeracy, and general capabilities relevant to English and HASS disciplines. For mid-year entry into Years 8, 9 and 10, selection is based on student achievement in school-based assessments, teacher recommendations and availability of positions, with students achieving consistent marks over 80% in English and HASS considered suitable. The program is offered at no additional financial cost beyond standard school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Academically talented students with demonstrated excellence in English and Humanities; Open to students entering Year 7 from Year 6; Applications submitted February-May; Selected through academic testing and application; For mid-year entry to Years 8, 9, 10: achieving consistent marks over 80% in English and HASS subjects; teacher recommendations; availability of positions
special_conditions: Successful selection into the AEP is a privilege. Remaining in the program requires students to maintain academic and behavioural expectations set by the school and adhere to the Specialist Programs Policy and Code of Conduct. Students not meeting expectations will engage in a Performance Review Mentoring Process.

school_name: Leeming Senior High School Education Support Centre
overview: Leeming Senior High School offers the Japanese Specialist Program, one of only 31 approved programs in Western Australia. The program is designed to equip students with essential Japanese language communication skills and deep understanding of Japanese culture, traditions and the role of language in human communication through innovative and challenging learning.
description: The Japanese Specialist Program is one of only 31 approved Specialist Programs in Western Australia, offered by Leeming Senior High School as an Approved Department of Education program. The program is designed to equip students with essential communication skills in Japanese and foster understanding of the role of language and culture in human communication. Students participate in two hours of Japanese classes per week, with curriculum designed to cater for both students who have previously studied Japanese at primary school and beginner learners. Students experience immersion in a Japanese classroom environment supported by native assistant teachers. Program highlights include a two-week immersion program with visiting Japanese high school students, a biannual two-week Japan tour (open to Years 9-11), after-school enrichment workshops, cultural activities exploring Japanese cuisine and traditions, and various excursions throughout the year. Students can pursue Japanese studies through Year 12 as either an ATAR subject or General subject. Entry is by merit selection through written application and academic testing process, with applications opening in February and closing mid-May for Year 6 entry into Year 7. Academic testing takes place in early June. For mid-year entry to Years 8, 9 and 10, selection is based on school-based assessments, teacher recommendations and position availability. The program is offered at no additional financial cost beyond standard school fees, though Japan tour participation involves additional costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 from Year 6; Interest in Japanese language and culture; Applications submitted February-May; Selected through written application and academic testing; For mid-year entry to Years 8, 9, 10: school-based achievement in relevant subjects; teacher recommendations; availability of positions; Interest in language and cultural immersion
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Successful selection into the Japanese Specialist Program is a privilege. Students must maintain academic and behavioural expectations and adhere to the Specialist Programs Policy and Code of Conduct. Participation in the biannual two-week Japan tour (Years 9-11) involves additional costs not covered by the school. Students are supported by native Japanese-speaking assistant teachers within the program.

school_name: Leeming Senior High School Education Support Centre
overview: Leeming Senior High School Education Support Centre provides the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) to support families experiencing financial hardship. The scheme is available to parents, guardians and independent students who hold eligible and valid Centrelink concession cards or veteran affairs cards and meet specific criteria to assist with school-related expenses.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a government assistance program available to parents, guardians and independent students of school-aged children experiencing financial hardship. The scheme provides financial support to help families purchase essential school items such as books, uniforms and pay school fees. Eligible applicants must hold an eligible and valid concession card with the student's name listed, which could be a Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, Centrelink Health Care Card (family card only for medication purposes, including Child Disability Allowance card), or Department of Veteran Affairs Pensioner Concession Card (Blue card only). Students must be enrolled in Years 7 to 12 studying a full-time secondary course at a Western Australian school or registered with WA Department of Education Regional Office for home-schooling. Applicants must not have previously claimed for the same student in the same year and must not be foster parents (except in specific circumstances). Independent students may apply if they hold their own eligible concession card. Applications are completed online and must be submitted in person at the ESC Administration office with supporting documentation during Term 1, after the child's first day of school and before the last day of term. Financial assistance is paid to the school for public school students or to the applicant's nominated bank account, with public school students having the option to request payment to the school.
eligibility_criteria: Parent, guardian or independent student of enrolled secondary student; Hold an eligible and valid concession card (Centrelink Pensioner Concession, Health Care Card-family card, CDA, or VA Pensioner Concession Card Blue); Student enrolled in Years 7-12 full-time secondary study in WA or registered for home-schooling; Have not previously claimed the same student in the same year; Not a foster parent; Applications completed online and submitted in Term 1 only
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications can only be submitted in Term 1 after child's first day of school and before the last day of term. Supporting documentation (valid concession card) must be brought in person to ESC Administration office. Payment is made directly to school for public school students unless applicant nominates bank account. Scheme is available only to those who meet strict financial hardship criteria and hold eligible concession cards.

overview: Lesmurdie Senior High School offers two LEAP Scholarships valued at $250 each to reward exceptional achievement and aptitude in the Lesmurdie Enrichment and Acceleration Program (LEAP). The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate superior ability in Mathematics and Science and are selected into this specialist program.
description: The LEAP program awards two scholarships valued at $250.00 each. One scholarship is offered to the top-ranked student selected into the LEAP program for Year 7 entry. The second scholarship is available via application to other qualifying students accepted into the LEAP program. Selection is based on performance in standardised testing, NAPLAN results, school reports, and assessment by Heads of Mathematics and Science. The selection process includes primary school reports on academic performance, behaviour and commitment, and may include interviews. Only students who meet rigorous benchmarks are accepted into the program. The scholarship value is a one-time monetary award of $250. Continuation in the program requires maintaining high academic performance and exemplary behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in the following year; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6 at a primary school within Western Australia; Demonstrate superior aptitude and ability in Mathematics and Science as indicated by standardised testing; NAPLAN scores must meet rigorous benchmarks; Selection based on school reports regarding academic performance, behaviour and commitment; Open to all students across Western Australia; Maximum 64 placements made at Year 7 entry level each year
special_conditions: Continuation in the LEAP program is conditional upon the student maintaining high academic performance and exemplary behaviour at all times. The scholarship is awarded to students selected into the LEAP program. Interviews may be held as part of the selection process. Standardised testing takes approximately one and a half hours and consists of recognised tests in literacy, numeracy and non-verbal concepts.

overview: Living Waters Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 7 or Year 10. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability as determined by their performance in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program (CSTP).
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic excellence as determined by individual student results in the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program (CSTP) examination, which is held at Living Waters Lutheran College. Scholarships offered can cover full or partial remission of tuition fees. The number of scholarships offered is dependent upon the number of applicants and their ability to meet the selection criteria. Successful applicants' eligibility is reviewed annually to ensure scholarship winners continue to meet required standards. Scholarships will be awarded when results are made available to schools.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Demonstrated academic excellence based on ACER CSTP examination results; Willingness to adhere to the college ethos
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on an annual basis. Successful applicants' eligibility is reviewed annually to ensure scholarship winners continue to meet required standards. Scholarships for the 2025 academic year have now closed.

overview: Living Waters Lutheran College is offering Music Scholarships as part of the college's strategic initiative to grow the Music Program. The scholarships recognise students with a love of music who will be in Years 7-10, and students in other year levels who meet the criteria are also invited to apply and will be considered on an individual basis.
description: The Music Scholarship may be offered to a student who is a capable musician and enthusiastic to undertake or continue music lessons on their chosen instrument or voice. The scholarship covers the cost of individual and specialised tuition for one year on a chosen instrument or voice of successful applicants. Selection involves completion of the application package by the candidate, followed by an audition on the designated instrument or voice and an interview with a panel held late in Term 3. The audition consists of: performance of one piece of prepared repertoire; aural testing; and an interview with selected questions before a panel. Students must display a history of commitment to their current chosen instrument or voice, a willingness to participate in extra-curricular music activities associated with the college, and a willingness to adhere and uphold the ethos of the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-10; Students in other year levels who meet the criteria are also invited to apply and will be considered on an individual basis; Must be a capable musician; Must be enthusiastic to undertake or continue music lessons on chosen instrument or voice; History of commitment to chosen instrument or voice; Willingness to participate in extra-curricular music activities; Willingness to adhere to the college ethos; Current scholarship recipients may reapply
special_conditions: All scholarships will be at the Music Coordinator's recommendation and determined by the Principal. Scholarship covers individual and specialised tuition for one year on chosen instrument or voice. Students who are already recipients of the scholarship may reapply. Applications open in Term 3.

overview: Living Waters Lutheran College offers scholarships for the Netball Program as part of their Netball WA Endorsed Specialist School status. The college works with Netball WA to produce a comprehensive program in accordance with the Netball Australia Long Term Athlete Development Plan.
description: Scholarships are available for the Netball Program at Living Waters Lutheran College. Students selected into the program receive two netball coaching lessons per week with a focus on fitness, strength and conditioning, skill acquisition and court intelligence with the main goal being to improve athlete's technique and performance on the netball court. The Specialist Netball Program enables students engaged in high-level competitive netball to develop specific technical skills and tactical applications in preparation for Association, State, and State League Trials. Further details regarding scholarships are available in the Netball Program Scholarship FAQ document. There is an application and trial process for all programs. Successful applicants will receive a contract that must be completed and returned to the College.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students Years 10-12 for Specialist Netball Program; Open to students Years 7-9 for Development Netball Program; Open to students Years 5-6 for Junior Development Netball Program; Places are limited and offered to athletes with best emerging talent; Must complete application and trial process; New students to the College must also complete a School Enrolment Application
special_conditions: Scholarships are available for this program. Further information can be obtained by viewing the Netball Program Scholarship FAQs document. There is an application and trial process for all programs. Successful applicants will receive a contract that must be completed and returned to the College. Places are limited.

overview: Living Waters Lutheran College offers scholarships for the Basketball Academy, a program designed for skilled and passionate basketball players in Years 5-12. The Basketball Academy provides students with the unique opportunity to combine quality education with physical exercise through expert coaching in practical, theoretical and strength conditioning sessions.
description: Scholarships are available for the Basketball Academy program at Living Waters Lutheran College. Students selected into the Basketball Academy receive two basketball theory and practical lessons per week to develop their skills, techniques, tactics and decision making specifically for basketball. Players will represent Living Waters Lutheran College at interschool, RKAS (Rockingham Kwinana Associated Schools) and independent college competitions, preparing them to be nominated to trial for the Western Australian Basketball League (WABL). The application and assessment process occurs after submission of the Basketball Application Form. Students will be assessed individually, pending positions available. Successful applicants will receive a contract that must be completed and returned to the College. New students to the College will also need to fill in an Enrolment Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Must be skilled and passionate about basketball; Each student assessed individually pending positions available; Must complete Basketball Application Form; New students must complete Enrolment Application Form; Application must be submitted to basketball@livingwaters.wa.edu.au
special_conditions: Scholarships are available for this program. Further information can be obtained by viewing the Basketball Frequently Asked Questions regarding scholarships. Trials are held and applicants will be assessed individually pending positions available. Successful applicants will receive a contract that must be completed and returned to the College.

overview: Lynwood Senior High School offers scholarships for the Specialist Soccer Academy to support talented football players from Years 7 to 12. The program is designed to develop individual skills, knowledge and attitude while allowing students to play at the highest level possible.
description: The Specialist Soccer Academy has been in operation since 2004 and is endorsed as part of Gifted and Talented Education (GATE) by the WA Department of Education. Scholarships are available to assist with costs associated with the program. Students receive specialized coaching from experienced football teachers with internationally recognized accreditations from the Asian Football Confederation, Football Federation Australia and the United European Football Association. The program provides opportunities for leadership development, civic responsibility, team representation, and participation in interstate and international tours and competitions. Students have the opportunity to compete in the Champions Schools Cup, zone carnivals, and international competitions in Singapore and the United Kingdom. The program also streams into upper school WACE units and VET courses specific to football for university or TAFE placements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7-12; Must demonstrate football (soccer) ability; Selection determined by trials; Applicants must submit application form and permission forms
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Students must attend selection trials. Extra costs are incurred for camps and interstate or overseas competitions. All students are expected to wear standard PE or Soccer Academy uniforms during school hours. Students must receive a Letter of Invitation before trialing. Scholarships are available to assist with program costs.

program_name: Environment and Life Sciences (EaLS) Selective Entry Program
overview: Lynwood Senior High School offers a selective entry Approved Specialist Program in Environment and Life Sciences (EaLS) for Years 7 to 10. This accelerated academic pathway enriches the learning experience of gifted students, enabling many to achieve excellent results in WACE and gain university entrance.
description: The EaLS program is a dynamic and engaging academic extension program providing cross-curricular environmental education. Students explore actions to minimize environmental impact and develop understanding of ecological footprints. The program forms a discrete class for four core areas: English, Mathematics, Science and Humanities and Social Sciences. Students are accelerated in all core curriculum areas and study a range of environmental issues to prepare them for a dynamic future. This is the only recognized sustainability curriculum course approved in Western Australia. Over the program's history, many ATAR Dux students and four out of six past School Captains were EaLS graduates. A majority of students receiving 95 or above in ATAR results were EaLS graduates, demonstrating the academic excellence of the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Selective entry based on testing and interview; Applicants must submit last two semester reports and last NAPLAN report; Must attend testing day (Saturday); Must attend interview; Statewide applicants eligible
special_conditions: Selective entry program. Testing day held on Saturday with four assessments: Mathematics, Reading Comprehension, Writing and Science. Applicants must attend interview. Application form must be completed and returned to Lynwood Senior High School. This is the only recognized sustainability curriculum course approved in Western Australia. Students must maintain academic performance to remain in program.

overview: Lynwood Senior High School participates in the Secondary Assistance Scheme, a government financial assistance program for families with eligible concession cards. The scheme provides support toward school fees and educational costs for students in Years 7 to 12.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is administered through the Department of Education and provides financial assistance to eligible families with secondary school students. Parents or guardians holding a current concession card (such as Health Care or Pension cards) may be eligible to claim support toward school fees. The Educational Program Allowance of $235 is paid to the school, and the Clothing Allowance of $115 is paid to the parent or guardian. For public school students, parents and guardians have the option to pay the clothing allowance to the school for other expenses. The scheme requires annual application in Term 1, with applications closing early April. Eligibility requires the parent or guardian to hold a Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs card representing a statement of income for the family.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled in Years 7-12; Parent or guardian must hold current Centrelink concession card (Health Care, Pension) or Veterans' Affairs card; Card must be valid during Term 1; Student must be listed on claimant's card; Must be enrolled in full-time secondary course at Western Australian public school
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications must be made by parent or guardian in Term 1 each school year. Parents must provide copy of signed concession card (front and back). Educational Program Allowance of $235 paid directly to school; Clothing Allowance of $115 paid to applicant's nominated bank account or can be paid to school. For public school students, clothing allowance can be directed to school for other expenses. Applications do not carry forward to future years - must reapply annually.

overview: Manea Senior College offers Academic Scholarships to support exceptional students. The scholarship recognises academic achievement and merit. Prospective students and their families should contact the college directly for detailed information about this program.
description: Manea Senior College provides Academic Scholarship opportunities as part of its sponsorship program aimed at supporting and developing youth to reach their full potential. This scholarship is mentioned as available within the college's sponsorship framework but specific details regarding the exact financial value, eligibility criteria, application process, and selection timeline are not publicly documented on the college website. Students and families interested in pursuing this scholarship are encouraged to contact the college directly via the sponsorship enquiries email to obtain comprehensive information about application procedures, value, and requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolled at Manea Senior College; Academic achievement required; Direct application to college required
special_conditions: Sponsorship opportunities are described in relation to college partnerships. Applicants should contact Natalie Rykers, College Marketing Manager, at natalie.rykers@education.wa.edu.au for details.

overview: Manea Senior College offers Equity Scholarships as part of its commitment to supporting students from diverse backgrounds and circumstances. The scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance and remove barriers to education. Specific details require direct contact with the college.
description: Manea Senior College provides Equity Scholarship opportunities within its broader sponsorship program intended to support and develop youth to reach their full potential and become resilient, flexible people who can chart their own course. The equity scholarship framework aligns with the college's commitment to inclusion and student wellbeing. However, detailed information regarding financial value, specific eligibility criteria beyond equity considerations, application deadlines, and selection processes is not publicly available on the college's main website. Interested applicants must contact the college directly for comprehensive program details and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolled at Manea Senior College; Equity/need considerations; Direct application to college required
special_conditions: Part of the college's sponsorship program aimed at supporting student development and potential. Applicants should contact Natalie Rykers, College Marketing Manager, at natalie.rykers@education.wa.edu.au for specific details about equity-based support.

overview: Mandurah Catholic College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward outstanding academic achievement. The scholarship is available to students commencing at Year 7 and other entry points, with selections made from both feeder schools and the wider community.
description: Mandurah Catholic College's Academic Scholarship program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The scholarship is awarded to selected students based on academic merit and performance. Several scholarships are presented at the annual Speech & Awards Evening to current students or students commencing their education at MCC at Year 7 level. Additional opportunities are advertised to the wider community, inviting all interested persons to apply when applications open. The exact financial value, duration, and selection criteria are determined by the College and released during application periods. Recipients are expected to maintain the College's academic standards and contribute positively to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or other year levels; Current students eligible for internal awards; Open to students from feeder schools and wider community; Academic performance required
special_conditions: Several scholarships are awarded at the annual Speech & Awards Evening. Applications for 2026 scholarships and bursaries are now closed.

overview: Mandurah Catholic College offers Sport Scholarships to exceptional athletes entering Year 8 and Year 11. These scholarships recognise outstanding sporting ability and the student's potential to represent the College at elite levels.
description: Sport Scholarships are offered for entry to Year 8 and Year 11 at Mandurah Catholic College. The Year 8 Sports Scholarship is tenable for a duration of up to three years, and the Year 11 Sports Scholarship is tenable for a duration of up to two years. Part remission of tuition fees will be awarded commensurate with the value of the bursary. Remissions offered to bursary awardees are applicable to tuition fees only. Recipients are selected based on sporting excellence and potential, and must actively contribute to the life of the College beyond their sporting expertise. All Sports Bursaries are tenable for one year, and the same students can reapply each year. The College expects bursary students to maintain high standards of behaviour and meet the same expectations as other students.
eligibility_criteria: Entry to Year 8 or Year 11; Students must be able to commit to ACC competition level when required; Demonstrated sporting excellence required; Must meet College standards of behaviour and conduct; Broad range of outcomes expected during schooling beyond sporting expertise
special_conditions: Year 8 Sports Scholarship tenable for up to three years; Year 11 Sports Scholarship tenable for up to two years. All Sports Bursaries are tenable for one year. Recipients can reapply each year. Recipients are expected to support the college Christian ethos and actively contribute to the life of the College beyond their sporting expertise. No student will be awarded a bursary unless the Principal believes the student will gain a broad range of outcomes during schooling.

overview: Mandurah Catholic College offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support talented musicians. The scholarship is available to students at various year levels who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and commitment to the performing arts.
description: Mandurah Catholic College offers Music Scholarships as part of its commitment to nurturing talented performers in the arts. The Instrumental Program at the College attracts over 100 students who participate in private lessons. Music scholarships are advertised to the wider community when applications open. The College provides in-depth learning opportunities with specialist music teachers and pathways beyond Senior School courses. Students can engage in both practical and theoretical experiences in music. The exact financial value and specific eligibility criteria are released during application periods. Recipients are expected to contribute to the College's performing arts program and maintain high academic and behavioural standards.
eligibility_criteria: Musical ability and achievement required; Open to students at various year levels; Application to wider community; Participation in College music programs expected
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 scholarships and bursaries are now closed. The Instrumental Program requires an application form submitted to the Head of Learning Area Music. Enrolments are processed in Term 4 for the following year, though late enrolments are accepted throughout the year.

overview: Mandurah Catholic College offers Arts Scholarships to recognise and support talented performers in drama, dance, and visual arts. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate exceptional artistic ability and commitment to the performing and visual arts.
description: Mandurah Catholic College offers Arts Scholarships covering performance arts disciplines including Drama, Dance, and Visual Arts. The College's comprehensive arts program provides tuition in Contemporary Dance, Jazz, Hip Hop, Drama, and Theatre Studies. Students can engage in both practical and theoretical experiences. The visual arts program promotes self-expression and creative exploration in a well-resourced studio space. Arts scholarships are advertised to the wider community when applications open. The College annually celebrates student artwork and artistic achievements in community events. The exact financial value and specific selection criteria are determined by the College and released during application periods. Recipients are expected to contribute actively to the College's arts program and maintain high standards.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated artistic ability in performing or visual arts; Open to students at various year levels; Application to wider community; Active participation in College arts programs expected
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 scholarships and bursaries are now closed. Visual arts students' work is showcased in annual student art exhibitions celebrating creative talents across Kindergarten to Year 12. Performing arts students can participate in live productions and performances developed within the school.

overview: Mandurah Catholic College offers scholarships specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to support their educational journey. The scholarship recognises the importance of providing equitable access to quality Catholic education.
description: Mandurah Catholic College offers Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander Scholarships as part of its commitment to supporting Indigenous students in achieving their educational potential. The scholarship is advertised to the wider community when applications open. The College provides a supportive learning environment that celebrates and respects Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander cultures, values, and perspectives within its Catholic education framework. The exact financial value, duration, and specific eligibility criteria are determined by the College and released during application periods. Recipients are expected to maintain the College's academic and behavioural standards while contributing to the broader school community.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to students at various year levels; Application to wider community
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 scholarships and bursaries are now closed. Eligibility requires documented Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage.

overview: Lumen Christi College offers full and part-time Music Scholarships to recognise and support students demonstrating excellence in music performance. The scholarships are available to currently enrolled students in Years 7-9 and are designed to foster music excellence within the College.
description: Lumen Christi College is committed to the development of music excellence and offers a variety of full and part-time Music Scholarships for Years 7-9 students enrolled at the College. The full Music Scholarship covers the cost of 32 x 30-minute individual instrumental lessons per year, while a part Music Scholarship covers 50% of the cost of 32 x 30-minute individual instrumental lessons. Both scholarships include free instrument hire if applicable. Scholarships are valid for one year, and students have the opportunity to reapply for the same scholarship in the following year. Recipients must make a leading contribution to music at the College, maintain a high level of achievement on their chosen instrument, enrol in Specialist Music classes for the duration of the Scholarship, and participate in at least one College ensemble suitable to their Scholarship instrument for the duration of the Scholarship. The application process requires completion of an Audition Application Form and includes an audition on the chosen instrument(s), sight-reading exercise, and interview.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Lumen Christi College; Year 7, 8, or Year 9; Must audition on chosen instrument(s); Must prepare and perform two contrasting pieces; Must demonstrate commitment to making a leading contribution to music at the College; Must maintain high level of achievement on chosen instrument; Must enrol in Specialist Music classes for scholarship duration; Must participate in at least one College ensemble suitable to scholarship instrument for scholarship duration
special_conditions: Scholarships are valid for one year and students have the opportunity to reapply for the same scholarship in the following year. Auditions will take approximately 25 minutes and take place on Monday, 20 October 2025. Upon receipt of application, the College Music Administrator will contact families by phone regarding audition details. Students are welcome to arrange their own accompanist for audition pieces or perform with backing tracks provided in mp3 format on USB. There is no requirement to perform audition pieces along to accompaniment.

overview: Lumen Christi College offers the Aboriginal Secondary Bursary to support Aboriginal youth from families seeking enrolment at the College. The bursary recognises commitment to schooling and provides a significant contribution to assisting Aboriginal families with the education of their children.
description: Lumen Christi College has a clearly articulated commitment to work in partnership with Aboriginal people to provide assistance to increase positive life choices and help further possible career opportunities for Aboriginal youth. The Aboriginal Secondary Bursary Program is funded by the College itself and there are limited bursaries awarded annually. The purpose of awarding the Bursary is to recognise and reward commitment to schooling, evidenced by regular attendance at school, a commitment to learning, and participation in College and community activities. The College is committed to provide a very real and significant contribution to assisting Aboriginal families with the education of their children. This is a financial assistance program available for students considering enrolment at the College. The exact value of the bursary is not publicly specified on the official pages but is described as 'significant' and designed to assist with educational costs.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal student or family identifying as Aboriginal; Considering enrolment at Lumen Christi College; Demonstrated regular school attendance; Commitment to learning; Participation in College and community activities; Prospective or new enrolment status
special_conditions: The Aboriginal Secondary Bursary Program is funded by the College itself and there are limited bursaries awarded. The College is committed to provide a very real and significant contribution to assisting Aboriginal families with the education of their children. Bursary is now available for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander (ATSI) students as of program review in 2022.

overview: Manjimup Senior High School administers the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) to assist low-income families with secondary schooling costs. This government program provides financial support to eligible families of students in Year 7 to Year 12. The assistance is provided through two allowances designed to offset the costs of uniforms and educational programs.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a Western Australian government program designed to assist low-income families with secondary schooling costs for students in Year 7 to Year 12 who are studying a full-time secondary course at a Western Australian public school. The scheme provides two allowances: a $115 Clothing Allowance (CLA) paid directly to the parent/guardian or the school, and a $235 Educational Program Allowance (EPA) paid directly to the school. Parents and guardians must apply for the SAS each school year (annually) as applications do not carry forward to future years. Hard copy application forms can be obtained from the school's front office. Parents and guardians must meet the eligibility criteria set by the Department of Education before applying.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be enrolled in Year 7 to Year 12; Students must be studying a full-time secondary course at Manjimup Senior High School; Family must meet low-income eligibility criteria set by Western Australian Department of Education; Annual application required; Applications must be submitted each school year
special_conditions: Annual application required; applications do not carry forward to future years. The two allowances consist of $115 Clothing Allowance (CLA) and $235 Educational Program Allowance (EPA). Parents/guardians must meet eligibility criteria before applying.

overview: Manjimup Senior High School provides information about the ABSTUDY Supplement Allowance for eligible families who are holders of valid Services Australia (Centrelink) cards. This allowance supplements the Centrelink ABSTUDY Fees Allowance payment and assists eligible families with secondary schooling costs.
description: The ABSTUDY Supplement Allowance provides an allowance to assist eligible families with secondary schooling costs. The allowance is available to valid Services Australia (Centrelink) card holders and supplements the Centrelink ABSTUDY Fees Allowance payment. To be eligible for the supplement allowance, the parent/guardian must hold a Centrelink or Veterans' Affairs card that represents a statement of income for the family. Additionally, the school or parent must have received the School Fee Allowance of $78 or $156 from Centrelink as part of the student's ABSTUDY School Fees Allowance. The Department of Education ABSTUDY Supplement Allowance provides $79 Educational Program Allowance paid directly to the school. Parents and guardians must apply for this allowance each school year (annually) as applications do not carry forward to future years.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/guardian must hold a valid Services Australia (Centrelink) card or Veterans' Affairs card representing statement of income for the family; School or parent must have received School Fee Allowance of $78 or $156 from Centrelink as part of student's ABSTUDY School Fees Allowance; Students must be enrolled at Manjimup Senior High School; Annual application required; Cannot be claimed in conjunction with Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS)
special_conditions: Annual application required; applications do not carry forward to future years. The Secondary Assistance Scheme cannot be claimed as well as this allowance. Families must have already received ABSTUDY Fees Allowance from Centrelink to be eligible.

school_name: Mary MacKillop Catholic Community Primary School

overview: Margaret River Senior High School provides access to the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) for eligible families. This government-funded financial assistance program helps low-income families with secondary school costs for students in Years 7-12. Eligible parents and guardians who hold a Health Care Card or other concession card may receive support toward school expenses.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a financial assistance program available to eligible families with children attending secondary school in Western Australia. Students at Margaret River Senior High School can access this program if their families meet the eligibility criteria. The scheme provides up to $350 towards school expenses. Applications are submitted in Term 1 each year and are processed according to the school's timeline. The program is designed to help families experiencing financial difficulty meet secondary school costs including uniforms, books, and educational materials. Families interested in applying should contact the school's front office in Term 1 to collect an application form or download it from the school website. Documentation such as current Health Care Cards or other concession cards must be provided with the application.
eligibility_criteria: Parents or guardians with children enrolled in Years 7-12 at Margaret River Senior High School; Must hold a Health Care Card or other concession card from Services Australia (Centrelink) or Veterans' Affairs; Child must be studying a full-time secondary course
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications can only be submitted in Term 1. Applicants must provide current applicable concession card with application form. For public school students, parents have the option to direct the clothing allowance component to the school for other expenses instead of receiving it as a direct payment.

school_name: Margaret River Montessori School - Margaret River campus
overview: Margaret River Montessori School offers scholarships to support students and families who may face substantial financial barriers to accessing or continuing a Montessori secondary education. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate enthusiasm in their studies and resilience.
description: The Adolescent Program Scholarship is designed to provide financial support to students enrolling in the secondary program at Margaret River Montessori School. The school seeks students who exhibit enthusiasm in their studies and demonstrate resilience. This scholarship supports access to the Montessori secondary education program. Detailed application information for 2026 is available through the school's scholarship application portal. The school assesses applicants based on demonstrated financial barriers and student characteristics that align with the Montessori educational philosophy.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering the Adolescent Program (secondary level); Demonstrate enthusiasm in studies; Demonstrate resilience; Families facing substantial financial barriers to accessing or continuing Montessori secondary education
special_conditions: Scholarship applications require submission of detailed financial information and student portfolio. Recipients must maintain enrolment at the school and meet academic standards.

school_name: Margaret River Montessori School - Metricup campus
overview: Margaret River Montessori School offers a Scholarship Program designed to support students and families who face substantial financial barriers to accessing or continuing a Montessori secondary education. The school seeks students who exhibit enthusiasm in their studies, demonstrate resilience, excel in their areas of strength, and contribute positively to school dynamics.
description: The Margaret River Montessori School Adolescent Program Scholarship is designed to provide financial support to students and families who may face substantial financial barriers to accessing or continuing a Montessori secondary education at the new Metricup campus. The program accepts applications for commencement in Term 1, 2026. In offering scholarships, MRMS seeks students who demonstrate enthusiasm in their studies, resilience in overcoming challenges, excel in their areas of strength, and consistently contribute positively to school dynamics both academically and socially. The selection process involves a comprehensive application including demonstration of financial need and personal qualities. The exact financial benefit amount is not specified on the public website, though the school indicates it provides substantial support to eligible families.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering the Adolescent Program (Years 7-12); Families facing substantial financial barriers to accessing Montessori secondary education; Students demonstrating enthusiasm in studies; Students showing resilience in overcoming challenges; Students excelling in their areas of strength; Students contributing positively to school dynamics academically and socially; Eligible for commencement Term 1, 2026
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Detailed application information is referenced on the school website but specific conditions regarding duration, renewal, and exact financial benefits are not publicly available. Applicants should contact the school directly for comprehensive application details and conditions. The scholarship is specifically for the Adolescent Program at the Metricup campus.

overview: Mater Dei College offers full academic scholarships to reward exceptional academic achievement in incoming Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Mater Dei College awards full academic scholarships that cover the student's tuition fee only for three years. The scholarship is designed to recognise individual strengths and to support the needs of the growing community. Selection is based on performance in the ACER Academic Scholarship Test, which assesses academic ability. Students are required to sit the test at the College on a nominated Saturday in May. Candidates must be current Year 6 students applying for Year 7 entry, or current Year 9 students applying for Year 10 entry. Students successful in gaining a Year 7 scholarship will need to reapply for the Year 10 scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students commencing Year 7; Open to students commencing Year 10; Current Year 6 or Year 9 students; Must sit ACER Academic Scholarship Test; Students successful in gaining Year 7 scholarship must reapply for Year 10
special_conditions: Full scholarship covers tuition fee only for three years. Does not include specific purpose charges or levies. Scholarship is conditional upon recipient maintaining satisfactory academic progress. Students must remain enrolled at the College throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Mater Dei College offers half academic scholarships to recognise strong academic achievement in incoming Year 7 and Year 10 students. The scholarship supports students demonstrating solid academic ability and commitment to their studies.
description: Mater Dei College awards half academic scholarships that cover 50% of the student's tuition fee only for three years. These scholarships are designed to recognise individual strengths and to support the needs of the growing community. Selection is based on performance in the ACER Academic Scholarship Test. Students sit the test at the College on a nominated Saturday in May. Candidates must be current Year 6 students applying for Year 7 entry, or current Year 9 students applying for Year 10 entry. Students successful in gaining a Year 7 half scholarship will need to reapply for the Year 10 scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students commencing Year 7; Open to students commencing Year 10; Current Year 6 or Year 9 students; Must sit ACER Academic Scholarship Test; Students successful in gaining Year 7 scholarship must reapply for Year 10
special_conditions: Half scholarship covers 50% of tuition fee only for three years. Does not include specific purpose charges or levies. Scholarship is conditional upon recipient maintaining satisfactory academic progress. Students must remain enrolled at the College throughout the scholarship period.

overview: Mater Dei College offers music scholarships to students who demonstrate musical excellence through instrumental or vocal performance. The scholarships recognise students currently receiving tuition at the College or those commencing Year 7.
description: Mater Dei College offers several music scholarships to students demonstrating musical excellence. Successful applicants receive free instrumental tuition and instrument hire (if applicable) for one year in the student's nominated instrument. Music scholarships are offered to students in Years 7 to 11 who are currently receiving instrumental or vocal tuition at Mater Dei College, and to students commencing Year 7 who are currently receiving instrumental tuition in specified instruments. Selection is based on audition performance, which includes performing two contrasting pieces, basic music scales, sight-reading a short extract of music, and answering questions about musical knowledge. Following the audition and interview, parents are notified of the outcome typically within four weeks.
eligibility_criteria: Current students in Years 7 to 11 receiving instrumental or vocal tuition at Mater Dei College; Students commencing Year 7 receiving instrumental tuition; Must successfully audition; Students must be pursuing instrumental or vocal music study
special_conditions: Scholarship provides free instrumental tuition and instrument hire (if applicable) for one year only in the nominated instrument. Recipients must maintain satisfactory progress in their music studies and demonstrate commitment to the College music program.

overview: Mater Dei College offers AFL Academy scholarships to students who have demonstrated excellence in Australian Football and gained entry into the Hawks AFL Academy. The scholarship supports exceptional athletes pursuing excellence in AFL at the secondary level.
description: Mater Dei College offers AFL Academy scholarships that cover 50% of the Year 7 tuition fee only. The scholarship does not include any other specific purpose charges or levies. Scholarships are only open to students currently in Year 6 who have gained entry into the Hawks AFL Academy and are commencing Year 7. Selection is based on physical skill and fitness testing held during Term 2, combined with academic performance, attendance, and behaviour. Students must attend a skills and gameplay trial, typically held after school on a nominated date in May or June. Parents are notified of the outcome within four weeks of the trial date.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Current Year 6 students only; Must have confirmed enrolment at Mater Dei College or be subject to normal enrolment acceptance; Must successfully complete AFL Academy trial; Must demonstrate academic performance, good attendance and behaviour
special_conditions: Scholarship covers 50% of Year 7 tuition fee only and does not include specific purpose charges or levies. For all subsequent years, full tuition fee applies. Recipients must maintain excellent attendance and behaviour, display positive attitude towards College ethos, participate consistently in AFL and other sports, maintain positive attitude towards academic studies, and participate in College co-curricular activities. Scholarship holders must sign an Academy Agreement committing to these expectations.

overview: Mater Dei College offers Basketball Academy scholarships to students who have demonstrated excellence in basketball and gained entry into the Hawks Basketball Academy. The scholarship supports talented athletes pursuing basketball excellence at secondary level.
description: Mater Dei College offers Basketball Academy scholarships that cover 50% of the Year 7 tuition fee only. The scholarship does not include any other specific purpose charges or levies.
