Scholarships are only open to students currently in Year 6 who have gained entry into the Hawks Basketball Academy and are commencing Year 7. Selection is based on skill and fitness testing held during Term 2, combined with academic performance. Applicants must attend a skills and gameplay trial, typically held after school on a nominated date in May or June. All registered applicants are emailed trial details. Parents are notified of the outcome within four weeks of the trial date.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Current Year 6 students only; Must have confirmed enrolment at Mater Dei College or be subject to normal enrolment acceptance; Must successfully complete Basketball Academy trial; Must demonstrate academic performance and physical skill levels
special_conditions: Scholarship covers 50% of Year 7 tuition fee only and does not include specific purpose charges or levies. For all subsequent years, full tuition fee applies. Entry is subject to successful application, trial and selection into the Basketball Academy, and subsequent enrolment acceptance into Mater Dei College. Selection is based on academic performance and physical skill levels.

overview: Mater Dei College offers the Ballaruk Bursary to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students seeking quality Catholic education. The bursary recognises the College's commitment to improving educational experiences and career opportunities for Aboriginal youth.
description: Mater Dei College holds a commitment to providing accessible quality education and works in conjunction with Aboriginal people to improve the educational experiences and career opportunities of Aboriginal youth. The Ballaruk Bursary is available to current and prospective students at the College and will cover the cost of tuition fees. The bursary is intended to recognise the student's commitment to schooling. Applicants must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander and have a desire to be educated in the Catholic Education system. This commitment will be evident through the student's attendance at school, a commitment to learning, and involvement in College activities and community events. Applications are called for from prospective students, and parents and guardians are invited to lodge applications as early as possible, as the number of bursaries is limited.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must have a desire to be educated in the Catholic Education system; Evidence of commitment to schooling through attendance; Commitment to learning; Involvement in College activities and community events; Available to current and prospective students
special_conditions: Bursary covers full cost of tuition fees. Number of bursaries is limited, and applications are encouraged to be lodged as early as possible. Recipients must maintain commitment to schooling through good attendance, engagement with learning, and participation in College activities and community events. Bursary is subject to annual review and may be withdrawn if conditions are not maintained.

overview: Mazenod College offers two tuition scholarships per year level to reward outstanding academic achievement and significant contribution to the College community. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to both learning and community engagement.
description: Mazenod College awards two scholarships per year level, each valued at approximately 50% Fee Reduction of tuition fees. These scholarships are available for Years 7-10 and are tenable for one academic year at a time, though recipients may receive them more than once during their time at the College. Students who achieve the highest average across the five core subject areas (Religion, Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities and Social Sciences) are considered for Year 7-10 awards, with the final grade average over the student's entire program of study also considered. The Year 12 scholarship is awarded to the Year 11 student with the highest projected Australian Tertiary Admission Rank, with satisfactory performance in Religious Education also considered. Each scholarship is named after a past Rector of the College and reflects the Oblate value of pursuing personal excellence and contributing meaningfully to the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Mazenod College students in Years 7-10; Achievement of highest average across Religion, Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities and Social Sciences (Year 7-10); For Year 12: Year 11 student with highest projected ATAR; Satisfactory performance in Religious Education; Demonstrated significant contribution to the Mazenod College community; Annual renewal based on continued performance and engagement
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the period of one academic year and recipients may receive them more than once in their time at the College. Awarded Scholarships apply to Annual Tuition Fees only. Early payment and Direct Debit discounts are not applicable to Scholarship recipients.

program_name: Boarding Bursaries for Year 7 and Year 10 Entry
overview: Mazenod College offers Boarding Bursaries to external students demonstrating general excellence who are commencing in Year 7 and Year 10. These bursaries provide financial support to enable rural and regional students to access quality boarding education at the College.
description: Boys who demonstrate general excellence are encouraged to apply for Boarding Bursaries valued at $3,600 per annum, tenable for the duration of the student's education at the school. These bursaries are open to external students only and are designed to support students from regional, remote and rural Western Australia. All applicants will be required to attend an interview with the College Principal as part of the selection process. The bursary is a commitment to the College's mission to serve students from across the state by providing access to the Mazenod boarding experience and quality education in the Oblate tradition.
eligibility_criteria: External students only; Commencing in Year 7 or Year 10; Demonstration of general excellence in academics and character; Interview with College Principal required; Rural and regional student preference
special_conditions: Tenable for the duration of the student's education at the school. External students only. Applicants must attend interview with College Principal. Recipients must demonstrate ongoing general excellence and commitment to boarding and College involvement.

program_name: Current Boarder Bursary for Year 10 Progression
overview: Mazenod College offers continuation bursaries to current Year 9 boarding students demonstrating general excellence and strong commitment to boarding and College life. This program supports deserving boarders to progress to Year 10 and continue their education at the College.
description: Current Year 9 Mazenod College boarding students displaying general excellence and a strong capacity for boarding and College involvement are eligible to apply for a bursary valued at $3,600 per annum for the duration of their education at the College (Years 10-12). Applicants will be required to attend an interview with the College Principal. This bursary recognises students who have demonstrated excellence in their current boarding experience and commitment to contributing to the broader College community.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 9 Mazenod College boarding students; Demonstration of general excellence; Strong capacity for Boarding and College involvement; Interview with College Principal required; Must be re-enrolled for Years 10-12
special_conditions: Available to current Year 9 boarders only. Duration from Year 10-12. Recipients must attend interview with College Principal. Tenable for the duration of stated years only. Recipients must maintain general excellence and commitment to boarding and College involvement.

overview: Mazenod College offers a prestigious two-year music scholarship program to students entering Year 8, beginning as a bursary in Year 8 followed by a full scholarship in Year 9. This elite program supports dedicated music students who demonstrate commitment to musical excellence and development.
description: This is Mazenod College's most prestigious music scholarship program, offered to select dedicated music students. The program begins as a bursary in Year 8, followed by a full scholarship in Year 9. Students selected for this program will be provided with a musical instrument, individual tuition, and a structured music program with at least 4 group performance opportunities per year. Selection into this prestigious program is determined in Year 7, and students are committed to the two-year program under the tutelage of the College's expert music staff. All students selected will not necessarily have had previous music experience but must demonstrate a willingness to learn and have a strong commitment to practice. The program provides comprehensive musical development within a supportive and rigorous framework.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 selection for Year 8 entry; Willingness to learn and strong commitment to practice; No previous music experience required; Selection based on musical potential and dedication; Commitment to two-year program (Year 8-9)
value_aud: Bursary in Year 8, Full Fee Remission in Year 9
special_conditions: Selection determined in Year 7 for Year 8 entry. Two-year commitment required. Instrument provided. Individual tuition included. Minimum 4 group performance opportunities per year. Year 8 = bursary (partial support), Year 9 = full scholarship. Students must maintain commitment to practice and musical development throughout the program.

overview: Mazenod College partners with MADALAH, a not-for-profit organisation providing secondary scholarships for Indigenous students from remote and regional communities. These scholarships support students across Years 7-12 to access quality education in the Oblate tradition.
description: MADALAH Limited is a not-for-profit organisation that offers secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Indigenous students, primarily from remote and regional communities, to Western Australia's leading boarding schools and Australian universities. MADALAH secondary scholarships provide support to students from Year 7-12. These scholarships are administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). Mazenod College is a partner school with MADALAH, and therefore enrolled students may be eligible to apply. Students must be able to provide proof of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent and meet ABSTUDY eligibility requirements or provide proof of disadvantage to qualify for the program.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent with proof required; Year 7-12 enrollment; Eligible for ABSTUDY or able to provide proof of disadvantage; Recent academic results required; Good school attendance and behaviour record; Recommendation letter from referee required; Home location in Western Australia; Proof of offer or enrolment at Mazenod College required
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications administered by MADALAH, not directly by the College. Applicants must apply to MADALAH via their website (www.madalah.com.au). ABSTUDY eligibility must be verified or disadvantage must be documented. Recipients must demonstrate good attendance and behaviour. Application requires recent academic results and referee letter.

school_name: Mel Maria Catholic Primary School - Pater Noster Campus
Mel Maria Catholic Primary School - St Joseph Pignatelli Campus

overview: Medina Primary School offers the Bunning Crescendo Scholarship to recognise and support outstanding young musicians learning cello or violin. The scholarship supports a student in their final three years at primary school with specialised tuition, AMEB exam guidance, and enhanced music education opportunities.
description: The Bunning Crescendo Scholarship is awarded annually to an eligible Year 4 student from Medina Primary School who is learning cello or violin as part of the WASO Crescendo program. Recipients receive specialised tuition including extra-curricular lessons, guidance through Australian Music Education Board (AMEB) exam preparation, practice technique coaching, and instruction on what it means to be a 21st Century musician. The scholarship supports the student for their final three years at primary school (Years 4, 5 and 6), offering a pathway for talented emerging musicians to pursue further musical studies. The program offers non-monetary benefits in the form of enhanced music education and mentoring, designed to help students develop into professional musicians.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a Year 4 student at Medina Primary School; Must be learning cello or violin as part of the WASO Crescendo program; Must be selected through an application process; Must demonstrate musical talent and potential
special_conditions: Scholarship awarded following an application process. Recipients must be learning cello or violin. Scholarship provides access to specialised tuition, AMEB exam preparation guidance, practice technique coaching, and 21st Century musician training. The program aims to develop talented musicians for potential careers in music.

program_name: The Smith Family Learning for Life Program
overview: The Smith Family offers the Learning for Life Program to support students in financial hardship. This program recognises that some children need extra support to stay at school and go on to further studies or employment. The program provides financial assistance, case coordination, and access to educational support services.
description: The Learning for Life Program provides comprehensive support to eligible families, including financial assistance from a sponsor to help cover the cost of the child's education, support from a Learning for Life Program Coordinator to connect the child and their family to local learning opportunities and other supports, and access to Smith Family educational programs to help children get the most out of their school years. Applications are open to students in Pre-Primary to Year 6. Families must meet specific eligibility criteria including that the Primary Carer holds a Pensioner or Health Care Card. The school will provide a digital referral application link once the family has been assessed as meeting the criteria, with paper copy options also available.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Pre-Primary to Year 6; Primary Carer must hold a Pensioner or Health Care Card
year_levels: Pre-Primary, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Family must meet eligibility criteria assessed by school. Requires completion of digital or paper referral application. Support is ongoing through Program Coordinator throughout child's involvement.

overview: Middle Swan Primary School endeavours to access Access to All Areas vouchers for families experiencing financial difficulty. These vouchers assist families in paying for school activities and programs including swimming lessons and other school-based activities.
description: Access to All Areas vouchers are utilised by the school to support families who are finding it difficult to pay for the full cost of school activities and programs. The school actively pursues these vouchers from the Department of Education to help eligible families access swimming lessons and other co-curricular activities that would otherwise be unaffordable. The vouchers provide a subsidy that reduces or covers the cost of participation in these school-organised activities. The school's newsletter indicates they 'will endeavour to access more Access to All Areas vouchers in 2025 for families who are finding it difficult to pay for the full cost', demonstrating an ongoing commitment to supporting financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students; Family must be experiencing financial hardship or difficulty affording school activities
special_conditions: Vouchers are accessed on a per-activity basis by the school on behalf of eligible families. Families must communicate their financial need to the school to be considered for vouchers.

overview: Mercedes College offers the Mercy Scholarship to provide full tuition coverage for students from low-income families. The scholarship reflects the college's commitment to inclusivity and empowering young women to access an outstanding Catholic education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: The Mercy Scholarship provides full tuition coverage for the duration of a student's secondary education at Mercedes College. The scholarship is awarded to students entering Years 8 to 10 who demonstrate exceptional talent or potential in academic achievement, the arts, sports, community service, or leadership. Applicants must meet low-income thresholds and demonstrate alignment with Mercy values of compassion, justice, excellence, integrity, hope and service. The selection process includes submission of a personal statement, academic achievements, extracurricular activities, certificates, and letters of recommendation, followed by interview with the Principal for shortlisted candidates. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and personal conduct, subject to annual review.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 8 to 10 in 2027; Not currently enrolled at Mercedes College; Families must meet low-income threshold; Must demonstrate values alignment with Mercy principles; Academic and character excellence required; Must submit completed application with all required documents by deadline
special_conditions: Full tuition coverage subject to annual review of academic and personal progress. Scholarship subject to renewal based on continued academic performance and alignment with College values. Students who withdraw may be required to reimburse fees to the school.

overview: The Sisters of Mercy Perth offer a Music Bursary to encourage students to pursue music studies at Mercedes College. The bursary supports students who demonstrate outstanding musical potential but whose family circumstances make music tuition difficult to access.
description: The Sisters of Mercy Music Bursary covers two years of tuition through the College instrumental music program for students who demonstrate outstanding musical potential but face financial barriers to music education. Students must achieve a minimum standard of AMEB Grade 2 (or equivalent) in their audition and are required to study elective Music as a classroom subject and participate in the co-curricular music program to remain eligible. The bursary covers hire and service costs for an instrument if required during the bursary period. Applications are due at the end of Term 3 each year, with auditions taking place in Term 4. Bursaries are awarded at the discretion of the Director of Music and Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Minimum AMEB Grade 2 standard (or equivalent) on chosen instrument; Must be current student or accepted for enrolment for following year; Must study elective Music as classroom subject; Must participate in co-curricular music program; Demonstrate outstanding musical potential; Financial circumstances make music tuition difficult to access
special_conditions: Hire and service costs for instruments covered by Mercedes College for duration of bursary. After bursary ends, instrument hire/purchase becomes family responsibility. All other instrument-related costs (books, accessories) are family responsibility. Prolonged absence from lessons or withdrawal from instrumental program requires fee reimbursement to school. Applicants should demonstrate genuine love for music and willingness to learn instruments in areas of need (oboe, horn, trombone, double bass).

overview: The Louise Allen-Arbuckle Music Scholarship was established in 2014 to nurture music students with exceptional musical talent and passion for music. Administered by the Mercedes College Alumni since Louise's passing in 2016, the scholarship honours the legacy of a dedicated musician and music teacher.
description: The Louise Allen-Arbuckle Music Scholarship covers two years of tuition through the College instrumental music program and is awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding musicianship and musical aptitude on their chosen instrument. Students must achieve a minimum standard of AMEB Grade 4 (or equivalent) in their audition and must study elective Music as a classroom subject and participate in the co-curricular music program to be eligible. The scholarship requires applicants to present two contrasting pieces for audition demonstrating minimum Grade 4 standard, with a parent or guardian present, and attend an interview with the Principal as part of the selection process. Applications are due at the end of Term 3 each year with auditions in Term 4.
eligibility_criteria: Minimum AMEB Grade 4 standard (or equivalent) on chosen instrument; Must study elective Music as classroom subject; Must participate in co-curricular music program; Demonstrate outstanding musicianship and musical aptitude; Present two contrasting pieces in audition; Attend interview with Principal
special_conditions: Hire and service costs for instruments covered by Mercedes College for duration of scholarship. After scholarship ends, instrument hire/purchase becomes family responsibility. All other instrument-related costs (books, accessories) are family responsibility. Prolonged absence from lessons or withdrawal from instrumental program requires fee reimbursement to school. Scholarship awarded at discretion of Director of Music and Principal.

overview: The MCA Scholarships have been established to encourage and support the education of an ex-student's daughter, granddaughter or great-granddaughter at Mercedes College. Two partial scholarships of $2,500 each are awarded to incoming Year 7 students.
description: The MCA Scholarships provide partial financial support of $2,500 each to two incoming Year 7 students who have accepted a position to enter Year 7 the following year. These scholarships are designed specifically for daughters, granddaughters or great-granddaughters of Mercedes College alumni. The scholarship recognises the College's commitment to supporting students connected to the school's alumni community. Applications require a completed application form submitted to the MCA scholarship coordinator. Applications for 2027 entry open in Term 2, 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be daughter, granddaughter or great-granddaughter of Mercedes College ex-student; Must be entering Year 7 the following year; Must have accepted position to enter Year 7; Not currently enrolled at Mercedes College
special_conditions: Awarded to incoming Year 7 students only. Two scholarships of $2,500 each awarded annually.

overview: Mercedes College is a partner school with MADALAH, offering scholarship opportunities for Indigenous students from Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent. The college provides access to Catholic school education through this partnership with MADALAH Limited, a not-for-profit organisation.
description: Families of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent are given the opportunity to engage in a Catholic school environment through scholarship opportunities for the education of their daughters. MADALAH Limited administers secondary education scholarships for Indigenous students, primarily from remote and regional communities, to WA's leading schools and Australian universities. MADALAH secondary scholarships provide support to students from Years 7 to 12 and are administered on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). Scholarships are offered annually and renewed on an annual basis, subject to satisfactory review by the Principal. Mercedes College is a partner school with MADALAH and therefore enrolled students may be eligible.
eligibility_criteria: Must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Island descent; Years 7 to 12 eligible; Must apply through MADALAH; Primarily from remote and regional communities; Subject to annual satisfactory review by Principal
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). Offered annually and renewed on annual basis subject to satisfactory review by Principal. Contact MADALAH directly for application and eligibility details.

overview: MLC offers Academic Scholarships to girls demonstrating outstanding academic excellence. These scholarships reward exceptional achievement and commitment to learning across all academic disciplines. Successful candidates are invited to interviews with College leadership after passing the initial scholarship test.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to girls entering Years 5, 6, 7 and 10 who demonstrate academic excellence. The scholarships provide a tuition fee reduction of varying values. A further reduction of tuition fees may be provided on a needs-based approach through financial assessment of individual circumstances. Applicants indicate on the application form whether they wish to be considered for additional needs-based support. Candidates entering Years 5 and 6 hold their scholarship until the end of Year 6, while successful candidates entering Years 7 to 10 hold their scholarship until graduation at the end of Year 12. Retention of a scholarship is conditional upon the holder maintaining high standards in academic achievement and conduct throughout schooling at MLC.
eligibility_criteria: Girls entering Years 5, 6, 7 or 10; Must sit the Academic Assessment Services (AAS) academic scholarship test; Demonstrate academic excellence; Open to current and future MLC students; Students living more than 400km from Perth may apply for remote testing; Applicants must list MLC as first preference to be considered in first-round offers
special_conditions: Retention of scholarship is conditional upon maintaining high standards in achievement and conduct throughout schooling at MLC. Scholarships extend through to Year 12, subject to scholarship holders maintaining academic and behavioural standards and participating fully in College life. Non-refundable application fees apply.

overview: MLC offers Music Scholarships to girls entering Years 5 to 10 who demonstrate musical excellence and potential. The College boasts an outstanding music program with an international reputation for excellence in performance. Successful audition candidates receive tuition fee reduction and 100% coverage of music tuition fees for one instrument.
description: Music Scholarships are awarded to girls entering Years 5 to 10 based on musical excellence and potential. All scholarship applicants must sit the academic scholarship test as well as audition. The scholarship provides a tuition fee reduction of varying values and covers 100% of music tuition fees for one instrument. A further reduction of tuition fees may be provided on a needs-based approach through financial assessment of individual circumstances. Applicants can indicate on the application form whether they wish to be considered for additional needs-based support. Candidates entering Years 5 or 6 hold their scholarship until the end of Year 6, while candidates entering Years 7 to 10 hold their scholarship until graduation at the end of Year 12. Candidates may audition on orchestral instruments, band instruments, or piano, typically presenting both principal and secondary instruments. Candidates may offer voice as principal study for entry to Year 9 or above.
eligibility_criteria: Girls entering Years 5 to 10; Must demonstrate musical excellence and potential; Must sit the academic scholarship test; Must audition (shortlisted candidates audition in person); Open to orchestral instruments, band instruments, piano, or vocal; Candidates must present a principal and secondary instrument (except piano specialists); Vocal candidates eligible for Year 9 entry and above; Successful candidates must maintain high academic standards throughout schooling
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: All applicants must sit the academic scholarship test in addition to audition. Candidates must submit video audition with candidate's full name, instrument and title of piece being played. Shortlisted candidates will be invited to audition in person during late January to mid-February. Retention of scholarship is conditional upon maintaining high standards in achievement and conduct throughout schooling at MLC.

overview: MLC invites girls who live in regional Western Australia to apply for Boarding Scholarships. These scholarships provide boarding fee reductions for students seeking a transformative boarding experience at the College's Boarding House. Successful candidates entering Years 7 to 10 hold their scholarship until graduation at the end of Year 12.
description: Boarding Scholarships are available to girls who live in regional Western Australia and are entering Years 7 to 10. All scholarship applicants must sit the academic scholarship test. The scholarship provides a boarding fee reduction of varying values. A further reduction of boarding fees may be provided on a needs-based approach through financial assessment of individual circumstances. Applicants can indicate on the application form whether they wish to be considered for additional needs-based support. Successful candidates entering Years 7 to 10 hold their scholarship until graduation at the end of Year 12. The Boarding House provides a nurturing environment and is home to girls from across regional Western Australia and internationally. Retention of scholarship is conditional upon maintaining high standards in academic achievement and conduct throughout schooling at MLC.
eligibility_criteria: Girls entering Years 7 to 10; Must reside in regional Western Australia; Must sit the academic scholarship test; All scholarship applicants must sit the AAS academic scholarship test; Open to current and future MLC students; Students living more than 400km from Perth may apply for remote testing; Applicants must list MLC as first preference to be considered in first-round offers
special_conditions: Scholarships extend through to Year 12, subject to scholarship holders maintaining academic and behavioural standards and participating fully in College life. Retention of scholarship is conditional upon maintaining high standards in achievement and conduct throughout schooling at MLC.

overview: The MLC Foundation Community Scholarship is a needs-based scholarship for girls who would otherwise be unable to attend MLC due to financial hardship. This scholarship recognises versatile girls demonstrating wide-ranging skills, interests and passions who plan to contribute meaningfully to the community. It provides full or partial fee remission for selected years of schooling.
description: The MLC Foundation Community Scholarship is a needs-based scholarship available to girls who are otherwise unable to afford an MLC education. It is not based on academic excellence alone but rather on a girl's versatility, demonstrated skills, interests, passions and commitment to contributing meaningfully to the community. Recipients are expected to make the most of all that an MLC education offers and to participate fully in College life. The scholarship provides financial assistance with tuition fees and is awarded at the discretion of the College following a rigorous application process. Applications require comprehensive documentation including birth certificate or citizenship papers, handwritten letter to the Principal, personal introduction video or recording, two most recent school reports, NAPLAN results, parental financial profile, and statutory declaration. Applications are due by 10 July annually.
eligibility_criteria: External applicants not currently enrolled at MLC; Girls demonstrating versatility and wide-ranging skills, interests and passions; Must be otherwise unable to afford an MLC education; Must demonstrate commitment to College values; Must plan to contribute meaningfully to community; Must be motivated to fully participate in College life and co-curricular opportunities; All required documentation must be complete and submitted by 10 July
special_conditions: Only open to external applicants not currently enrolled at MLC. Not an academic scholarship but a community scholarship. Applications due by 10 July. Comprehensive documentation required including parental financial profile and statutory declaration. Applicant must provide handwritten letter of at least 250 words to the Principal outlining why she wishes to attend MLC and how she demonstrates College values.

overview: MLC awards Specialist Instrumental and Vocal Prizes annually to current MLC students in Years 6 to 9 to support musical development and acknowledge progress and potential. These prizes are awarded by audition or application to recognise excellence in instrumental and vocal performance.
description: Specialist Instrumental and Vocal Prizes are awarded annually to support musical development and acknowledge progress and potential in students learning a musical instrument or voice. Eligibility is limited to current MLC students in Years 6 to 9. Auditions are open to all achievement levels, recognising that prizes acknowledge progress as well as excellence. Applications are encouraged from students learning with an MLC music staff member or private tutor. Prizes are awarded for the following year of study, so students receive recognition and support for their continued musical development. The prizes provide financial assistance with music tuition costs.
eligibility_criteria: Current MLC students in Years 6 to 9; Must audition or apply; Open to all achievement levels; Learning with MLC staff or private tutor encouraged; Students learning any instrument or voice eligible; Audition not required to apply
special_conditions: Prizes awarded for the following year. Open to all achievement levels. Auditions open to all achievement levels. Applications encouraged from students learning with MLC or private tutor.

overview: MLC Sports Prizes were introduced in 2026 to support elite athletes with costs for travel, coaching and equipment. These prizes recognise outstanding sporting achievement and potential in current and confirmed future MLC students across Years 6 to 11.
description: MLC Sports Prizes were introduced in 2026 to support elite athletes with the financial costs associated with competitive sport, including travel, coaching and equipment. The prizes are available to students in Years 6 to 11 (as of 31 December 2025), including confirmed future enrolments at MLC. These prizes provide financial assistance to enable talented athletes to pursue their sporting interests and development while attending MLC. The prizes recognise elite sporting achievement and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 6 to 11 as of 31 December 2025; Confirmed future enrolments eligible; Must demonstrate elite sporting achievement or potential; Must participate in competitive sport; Prize supports costs for travel, coaching and equipment
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Applications for 2026 Sports Prizes are now closed.

overview: Mindarie Senior College acknowledges students who have been selected to represent the state or nation in a sport or activity with a Certificate and a monetary scholarship. The scholarship recognises exceptional achievement in state or national representation.
description: The College proudly acknowledges students who have been selected to represent our state or nation in a sport or activity by presenting them with a Certificate and a $100 scholarship which is deducted from the student's College charges. For students to be eligible to receive the scholarship they must complete the application form and return to the Front Office with the supporting documentation. This award is available to students in Year 11 and Year 12 who achieve state or national selection in their respective sports or activities.
eligibility_criteria: Must be selected to represent the state or nation in a sport or activity; Must complete the State or National Representation Application Form; Must provide supporting documentation of selection; Open to Year 11 and Year 12 students
special_conditions: Scholarship is credited as a deduction from the student's College charges. Students must submit an application form with supporting documentation to the Front Office to be eligible.

overview: Mindarie Senior College presents Honour Board and Course Awards to Year 11 students who demonstrate exceptional performance and achievement in their coursework. Recipients receive a certificate and financial credit towards College charges.
description: Students are selected based on performance and achievement in their courses and receive a certificate and $150 credit for College charges. Recipients also have their name displayed on the respective Honour Board outside each Learning Area. This award recognises subject-specific excellence among Year 11 students and provides tangible financial assistance through a reduction in College charges. The selection process is based solely on academic performance and course achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled as a Year 11 student; Must demonstrate exceptional performance and achievement in coursework; Selection based on academic excellence in specific subject areas
special_conditions: Award is credited as a reduction in College charges. Recipients have their name permanently displayed on the Honour Board outside their respective Learning Area.

school_name: Mosman Park School For Deaf Children

overview: Mother Teresa Catholic College offers the Arts Scholarship to students who demonstrate a profound dedication to advancing and enriching the College's commitment to the Arts. This prestigious award recognises and supports individuals who exhibit exceptional aptitude, passion, and commitment across diverse arts disciplines and contribute to the vibrant cultural life of the College.
description: The Arts Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate an exceptional level of talent, passion, and commitment to advancing the College's artistic endeavours. Candidates must showcase exceptional aptitude and commitment across diverse arts disciplines while displaying a genuine passion for the arts and contributing to the vibrant cultural life of the College. The scholarship recognises individuals who actively contribute to the growth and prominence of artistic endeavours within the community. Selection is based on demonstrated artistic ability, commitment to the arts, and contribution to the College's cultural life. The exact financial benefit is not specified on the public website. Applications for 2026 are now open, with selection based on demonstrated excellence and potential in performing and visual arts disciplines.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate exceptional aptitude and commitment across diverse arts disciplines; Display genuine passion for the arts; Contribute to the vibrant cultural life of the College; Evidence of artistic achievement required
special_conditions: Selection based on portfolio, interview/audition, cover letter and copies of certificates, awards or media articles outlining performing arts achievement.

overview: Mother Teresa Catholic College awards the Music Tuition Scholarship to students who demonstrate an exceptional level of talent, passion, and commitment to the field of music. The scholarship recognises individuals who actively contribute to the growth and prominence of music within the College, helping to cultivate a thriving musical community.
description: The Music Tuition Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional talent, passion, and commitment to music. Candidates must show a high level of musical ability and commitment, and contribute meaningfully to the music culture at the College through performance, leadership, or other artistic endeavours. The scholarship seeks to support and encourage students dedicated to advancing their musical journey and enhancing the artistic culture at Mother Teresa Catholic College. The exact financial benefit is not specified on the public website. Selection involves submission of a cover letter and copies of certificates, awards or media articles outlining musical achievement, followed by an audition. Applications for 2026 are now open.
eligibility_criteria: Show a high level of musical ability and commitment; Contribute meaningfully to the music culture at the College through performance, leadership, or artistic endeavours; Evidence of musical achievement required
special_conditions: Selection based on audition, cover letter and submission of certificates, awards or media articles demonstrating musical achievement. Recipients are contacted if shortlisted for audition/interview stage.

overview: Mother Teresa Catholic College offers the First Nations Student Scholarship to support Indigenous and Torres Strait Islander students pursuing a Catholic secondary education. The scholarship demonstrates the College's commitment to providing broad career opportunities for young First Nations people through quality Catholic education.
description: The First Nations Student Scholarship is available to students of First Nations (Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander) descent who seek a Catholic education. Recipients must demonstrate regular attendance of at least 80% per semester, possess a desire to attend a Catholic secondary college with intentions to pursue a career upon Year 12 graduation, have a positive attitude towards learning with sound literacy skills, and be able to share their First Nations heritage with peers. The exact financial benefit is not specified on the public website. Applications must be endorsed by a member of the Catholic Education First Nations Committee (CEAC) or First Nation Corporation to confirm First Nations status. The scholarship complements current schemes assisting families with financial difficulties and supporting students with special needs.
eligibility_criteria: Must be of First Nations (Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander) descent; Seek a Catholic education; Demonstrate minimum 80% attendance per semester; Possess desire to attend Catholic secondary college with career intentions post-Year 12; Have positive attitude towards learning with sound literacy skills; Share First Nation heritage with peers; Have application endorsed by CEAC member or First Nation Corporation; Parents must be supportive of child's learning endeavours
special_conditions: Application must be endorsed by a member of the Catholic Education First Nation Committee (CEAC) or First Nation Corporation. Parents must demonstrate support for their child's learning endeavours. Selection criteria available upon request.

overview: Mother Teresa Catholic College offered Academic Scholarships for Year 7 and Year 10 entry. These scholarships recognised outstanding academic achievement. However, applications are currently closed.
description: Mother Teresa Catholic College offered Academic Scholarships for students entering Year 7 and Year 10. These scholarships were designed to recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The exact financial benefit and other specific details are not publicly available. Applications for this scholarship are currently marked as 'NOW CLOSED' on the College website, indicating this program is not currently accepting applications. This scholarship may reopen for future intake years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Open to students entering Year 10; Academic performance criteria not specified on public website
special_conditions: Applications are currently CLOSED. This program may reopen for future intake years.

overview: Morley Senior High School offers the Performance Plus Program to enrich the learning experience of high performing students in Years 7, 8 and 9. The program is designed for students who demonstrate sustained high achievement across Mathematics, English, Humanities and Science, and encourages them to become critical thinkers through enrichment opportunities.
description: The Performance Plus Program is a selective school-based program open only to enrolled Year 7 students at Morley Senior High School. Students are selected through submission of a portfolio of evidence demonstrating sustained high achievement in primary school, including recent NAPLAN results and school reports. The program enriches learning in Mathematics, English, Humanities and Science, with regular opportunities for cross-curricular activities. Students develop critical thinking skills and participate in student-led learning opportunities to develop problem-solving skills addressing real-life issues. The program continues from Year 7 through Years 8 and 9, provided students continue to demonstrate high performance and meet criteria. Students are closely monitored and supported to ensure the best possible outcomes. Selection is entirely at the discretion of Morley Senior High School. The program provides no direct financial benefit but offers enriched academic opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Year 7 at Morley Senior High School; Portfolio must demonstrate sustained high achievement in primary school; Recent NAPLAN results in top performance bands; Recent outstanding school reports; Evidence of appropriate behaviour respecting safety and learning needs of others
special_conditions: This school based program is only open to enrolled students. The enrolment of a child into the Performance Plus Class is entirely at the discretion of Morley Senior High School. Students are selected upon confirmation of enrolment into Year 7 through submission of a portfolio. Compulsory documentation includes copy of most recent NAPLAN results, copy of most recent school report, and evidence of appropriate behaviour. Scholarships are not offered; however, enriched academic opportunities are provided to high-performing students.

overview: Morley Senior High School offers the Aviation Academy, one of only four approved specialist aviation programs in Western Australia. This program is available to select students from Year 7 through to Year 12, with advanced aviation studies in Years 11 and 12. The program has been successfully running for over 30 years and is taught by an experienced flying instructor.
description: The Aviation Academy is an approved specialist program at Morley Senior High School that provides select students with comprehensive aviation education from Year 7 to Year 12. Aviation studies are offered from Years 7 to 10, with advanced aviation studies available in Years 11 and 12. The program covers numerous learning areas including mathematics, technologies, science, humanities and social science and design. Students receive the exciting opportunity to learn to fly a lightweight training aircraft, which enhances their understanding of aircraft and the environment in which they operate. The program has successfully operated for more than 30 years and is taught by an experienced flying instructor with extensive involvement in the aviation industry. Many students who have completed the course have gone on to careers as airline pilots, drone operators, flying instructors, maintenance engineers and in airport management. No financial award is provided, but the program offers specialised aviation training and hands-on learning opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students wishing to apply for Year 7 entry; Must complete an entry test at Morley Senior High School; Test covers literacy and numeracy skills and general knowledge questionnaire about aviation interest; Recent academic records reviewed; Must be enrolled in or approved for enrolment at Morley Senior High School; Successful applicants expected to remain in program until at least end of Year 10
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: A child's residential address is not a factor in the selection process. All applicants must complete an entry test at Morley SHS. Tests are held twice a year but applications can be submitted anytime. Students who are successful in receiving a placement are expected to remain in the program until at least the end of Year 10. Following successful test completion, students must attend an interview with the Deputy Principal where recent academic records and the student's school timetable will be reviewed to determine eligibility. Completion of Aviation Academy test is not an automatic entry into Morley Senior High School; an enrolment form must also be completed. First round of testing takes place in Term One.

overview: Morley Senior High School offers the Media Arts Design Academy (MADA), an approved specialist program for students with passion and interest in creative arts. The program enables students to be involved in numerous creative fields and utilises industry standard technologies. Students develop practical creative skills across art, digital design, photography, media, multimedia, television and graphics.
description: The Media Arts Design Academy (MADA) is an approved specialist program at Morley Senior High School designed for Year 6 students wishing to apply for Year 7 entry. The program enables students to be involved in numerous creative fields including art, digital design, drawing, graphics, media, multimedia, photography, TV production and presenting. MADA students utilise a wide range of industry standard technologies to plan, create and share learning in meaningful ways, including Adobe Creative Cloud Suite (Photoshop, Premiere Pro, InDesign, Illustrator). Photography instruction teaches students digital SLR use and creative photographic projects. In upper school, MADA students are encouraged to select from specialised courses in creative arts and complete nationally accredited courses including Certificate III in Visual Art and Certificate III in Screen and Media. Completing a certificate leads to direct entry pathways into TAFE and prepares students for university degrees in art and creative industry fields. The program provides no direct financial award but offers enriched creative learning opportunities and industry-standard training.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students wishing to apply for Year 7 entry; Must complete a testing process demonstrating creativity; Test involves simple drawing and design tasks; Must address in writing why the student wishes to join MADA; May submit portfolio of creative arts work; A child's residential address is not a factor in selection; Must be enrolled in or approved for enrolment at Morley Senior High School
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: A child's residential address is not a factor in the selection process for the Media Arts Design Academy. All applicants must complete an entry test at Morley SHS. Tests are held twice a year but applications can be submitted anytime. Year 6 students wishing to apply must demonstrate creativity through simple drawing and design tasks and address why they wish to join MADA. Students may also submit a folio of creative arts work highlighting creativity in drawing, painting, cartooning, photography, video or film production, animation and advertising. Completion of Media Arts Design Academy test is not an automatic entry into Morley Senior High School; an enrolment form must also be completed.

overview: Morley Senior High School offers the Soccer Academy as a specialist program for students with passion and ability in soccer. Year 7 applicants must demonstrate high standards of personal behaviour, consistent satisfactory academic performance, prior soccer experience and enthusiasm for the sport.
description: The Soccer Academy at Morley Senior High School is a school-based specialist program for Year 7 entry. Year 7 parents and students apply to the Soccer Academy by completing an application form. In their responses, students must demonstrate high standards of personal behaviour and consistent satisfactory academic performance. Applicants must have some prior soccer experience and demonstrate that they are an enthusiastic and capable sports person. The program provides specialist soccer coaching and training as part of the school curriculum. The program provides no direct financial award but offers specialist coaching and competition opportunities for talented young soccer players. Students benefit from structured training, competitive opportunities and integration of soccer skills development within the school's broader curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 entry; High standards of personal behaviour; Consistent satisfactory academic performance; Some prior soccer experience required; Must be an enthusiastic and capable sports person; Must apply via completed application form at Morley Senior High School
special_conditions: This is a school-based program for Year 7 entry. Application must be completed at the school. Selection is based on demonstrated personal behaviour, academic performance and soccer ability and enthusiasm. Students accepted into the program are expected to maintain high standards of behaviour and academic performance.

overview: Morley Senior High School offers Automotive Trade Training as a two-year vocational program for Year 11 and 12 students. The program is designed for students who want to gain direct entry into the automotive industry, apprenticeships or training while working towards their Secondary Graduation. Students complete the AUR20520 Certificate II in Automotive Servicing Technology.
description: Automotive Trade Training at Morley Senior High School (RTO 50638) is available to Year 11 and 12 students as a two-year program designed for those seeking direct entry into the world of work, apprenticeships or training while working towards Secondary Graduation. The program is delivered in state-of-the-art industry standard facilities in the school's Trade Training Centre and is suitable for students who have a passion and commitment to the automotive industry with clear focus on pursuing a career in this field. Students who successfully complete the national qualification AUR20520 Certificate II in Automotive Servicing Technology will have their indentured period of an automotive apprenticeship reduced by 6 months. The program requires students to undertake three days of schooling per week (Monday-Wednesday) studying Maths Essentials, English General, Career and Enterprise and Physical Education General, one day per week of automotive industry placement (Thursday), and one day per week in the Morley SHS Trade Training Centre (Friday). No financial award is provided, but students gain nationally recognised qualifications and accelerated apprenticeship entry.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 and 12 entry; Demonstrated passion and commitment to automotive industry; Clear focus on pursuing a career in automotive field; Consistently high school attendance; Good standing at school; Must meet training applicant selection criteria; Recent academic records reviewed
special_conditions: This is a two-year program offered to Year 11 and 12 students. Training applicants are required to attend an interview and meet other selection criteria, including consistently high school attendance and good standing. All students who successfully complete this national qualification will have their indentured period of an automotive apprenticeship reduced by 6 months. The program complements the Trade Training Pathway at Morley SHS. Students gain direct entry pathways into work, apprenticeships, further TAFE training or full-time employment upon completion.

school_name: North East Metropolitan Language Development Centre
North East Metropolitan Language Development Centre - Beechboro Campus
North East Metropolitan Language Development Centre - North Balga Campus

overview: North Woodvale Primary School offers the Instrumental Music School Services scholarship to students who demonstrate musical aptitude and interest. The program provides free specialist instrumental music lessons from qualified IMSS teachers in partnership with the Department of Education.
description: The IMSS Instrumental Music scholarship at North Woodvale Primary School offers free weekly instrumental lessons conducted during school hours by specialist-trained teachers from the Department of Education. The school offers scholarships in trumpet, trombone, guitar, clarinet and flute. Selection is based on a musical aptitude test undertaken in Year 4, along with demonstrated enthusiasm for music as shown in classroom music lessons. The program includes opportunities to participate in school ensembles and perform at various school functions. Recipients are expected to commit to weekly 30-minute lessons, regular practice, and participation in music ensembles.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 5 and Year 6; Must demonstrate musical aptitude through aptitude testing; Must show enthusiasm and positive attitude toward music in classroom settings; Must demonstrate willingness to practice regularly; Must commit to attending weekly lessons and ensemble participation
special_conditions: Students must participate in classroom music for the duration of their scholarship. Weekly practice is required to maintain progress and scholarship eligibility. Selection is made in consultation between the music specialist, IMSS teachers and classroom teachers, taking into account attitude and behaviour in classroom and music lessons. Scholarship is contingent on maintaining satisfactory attendance and progress.

overview: Northshore Christian Grammar School offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and reward exceptional academic achievement. Each year, three academic scholarships are awarded for Year 7 entry and a further three for Year 10 entry. These scholarships provide fee remission to support outstanding students in their continued educational journey.
description: Academic scholarships provide fee remission for a period of three years, with extensions offered, providing academic standards are maintained and the student fulfils all scholarship requirements, such as school representation. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate outstanding academic ability and a strong commitment to learning. Selection is based on the ACER Cooperative Scholarship Testing Program (CSTP). Recipients must maintain academic standards and participate in school representation activities to retain and extend their scholarship beyond the initial three-year period. Each scholarship application is considered individually and is at the discretion of the principal.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Must demonstrate exceptional academic achievement; Selection based on CSTP performance
special_conditions: Scholarship is offered on a three-year basis with potential extensions. Recipients must maintain academic standards and fulfil all scholarship requirements, including school representation. Extensions are offered providing academic standards are maintained.

overview: Northshore Christian Grammar School offers Sports Scholarships to students who have achieved state or national representation in their chosen sport. The scholarship recognises and supports sporting excellence and acknowledges the significant commitment and costs families invest in developing their children's athletic potential.
description: Sports scholarships are offered to any student who obtains state or national representation in their chosen sport or sports. Like academics, sports are highly valued at Northshore and encouraging health and physical activity is very important. The scholarship comes with fee remission to support families who go to great lengths and often great costs to enable their children to pursue sporting excellence. Selection is at the discretion of the principal, and each application is considered individually based on demonstrated state or national sporting achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Must have achieved state or national representation in chosen sport; Open to students at any year level; Each application considered individually and at discretion of principal
special_conditions: Students must have achieved state or national representation in their chosen sport. Each scholarship application is considered individually and is at the discretion of the principal.

overview: Northshore Christian Grammar School recognises students for their ongoing commitment to serving their communities. The Community Service Scholarship acknowledges students who have made significant contributions to the wider community through service and leadership. Applications are considered for fee remission to recognise this valuable contribution.
description: Northshore recognises many students for their ongoing commitment to serving their communities. Examples of service can take many different forms and applicants should speak to the school if they are unsure as to their suitability for the scholarship. Applications may be considered for fee remission to acknowledge the student's contribution to the wider community. The scholarship recognises the value of community service and leadership demonstrated by students. Each scholarship application is considered individually and is at the discretion of the principal, who will assess the nature and extent of the student's community service contributions.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated ongoing commitment to serving the community; Evidence of significant community service contributions; Open to students at any year level; Each application considered individually
special_conditions: Students should speak to the school to discuss suitability for the scholarship before applying, as community service takes many different forms. Each scholarship application is considered individually and is at the discretion of the principal.

overview: Northshore Christian Grammar School offers Arts Scholarships to students who are recognised in their chosen artistic field. The scholarship supports students who demonstrate excellence in visual arts, music, drama, or performing arts. It acknowledges the significant investment families make in developing their children's artistic talents.
description: If a student is recognised in their chosen artistic field, they should apply for a Northshore Arts Scholarship. The scholarship comes with fee remission to support families who go to great lengths and often great costs to enable their children to pursue artistic excellence. Artistic achievement can be demonstrated through various disciplines including visual arts, music, drama, dance, and other performing arts. Each scholarship application is considered individually and is at the discretion of the principal. Recognition in the artistic field is the primary criterion for eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Must be recognised in chosen artistic field; Demonstrated excellence in visual arts, music, drama, or performing arts; Open to students at any year level; Each application considered individually
special_conditions: Students must be recognised in their chosen artistic field. Each scholarship application is considered individually and is at the discretion of the principal. Recognition in the artistic field is demonstrated through portfolio, audition, or performance evidence.

program_name: Laurence Armstrong Trust - Great Beginnings Scholarship (Year 7)
overview: Northam Senior High School offers the Great Beginnings Scholarship to incoming Year 7 students from catchment primary schools. This scholarship recognises exceptional academic achievement and provides sustained financial support through secondary schooling to students entering from local feeder primary schools.
description: The Great Beginnings Scholarship supports 2 or more incoming Year 7 students from catchment primary schools enrolling at Northam Senior High School. The scholarship provides $1,000 per year for Years 7 to 10, increasing to $2,000 per year for Years 11 and 12, for the duration of enrolment. Recipients are selected based on academic performance and continue to receive the scholarship for each year they remain at the school. The selection process involves assessment of school reports and references from primary school principals. Applicants must be from designated catchment primary schools and can apply while in Year 6.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Year 6 at a catchment primary school; Must be applying to enter Northam Senior High School as an incoming Year 7 student; Must be from designated catchment primary schools within the district; Australian citizenship or permanent resident status required; Not eligible if holding another scholarship valued at $450 or more
value_aud: $1,000 per year for Years 7 to 10, increasing to $2,000 per year for Years 11 and 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more students and continues for each year they remain at Northam Senior High School. A change of school can affect eligibility if the scholarship is tied to the school; if a change of school occurs, the scholarship is forfeited. Grants may be renewed in subsequent years provided satisfactory academic and general progress is made.

overview: Northam Senior High School offers the Continuing Scholarship to Year 11 students who continue their studies from Year 10. This scholarship recognises sustained academic achievement and commitment to secondary education, supporting students in completing their final years.
description: The Continuing Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more students who continue at Northam Senior High School from Year 10 through to Year 11 and 12. This scholarship provides financial support during the final years of secondary education. Recipients are selected based on academic performance and commitment to their education. The selection process involves assessment of school reports and principal references. This scholarship supports retention of strong students at the school and recognises their ongoing academic achievement throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at Northam Senior High School at the end of Year 10; Applying to continue to Year 11; Australian citizenship or permanent resident status required; Not eligible if holding another scholarship valued at $450 or more; Must demonstrate satisfactory academic progress in Year 10
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more continuing students for Years 11 and 12. A change of school can affect eligibility; if a change of school occurs, the scholarship is forfeited. Grants may be renewed in subsequent years provided satisfactory academic and general progress is made.

program_name: Laurence Armstrong Trust - District High School Scholarship (Year 11)
overview: Northam Senior High School offers the District High School Scholarship to Year 11 students enrolling from district high schools. This scholarship supports regional students transitioning from smaller district high schools to complete their secondary education at a larger regional school.
description: The District High School Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more students enrolling in Year 11 at Northam Senior High School from district high schools within the region. This scholarship provides financial support plus residential costs subsidy for students who transition from district high schools. The scholarship value includes out-of-pocket residential costs (once all eligible subsidies have been applied) plus $2,000 per year for Years 11 and 12. Recipients are selected based on academic performance and the need to support regional access to secondary education. The selection process involves assessment of school reports and principal references from district high schools.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled at a Western Australian district high school; Applying to enter Year 11 at Northam Senior High School; Intending to reside at Northam Residential College or require residential support; Australian citizenship or permanent resident status required; Not eligible if holding another scholarship valued at $450 or more; May require residential college eligibility assessment
value_aud: Out of pocket Residential costs plus $2,000 per year for Years 11 and 12
special_conditions: Scholarship value includes residential costs plus $2,000 per year. Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more students. A change of school can affect eligibility; if a change of school occurs, the scholarship is forfeited. Grants may be renewed in subsequent years provided satisfactory academic and general progress is made. Requires commitment to reside at or utilise Northam Residential College.

program_name: Laurence Armstrong Trust - Residential College Scholarship (Year 11)
overview: Northam Senior High School offers the Residential College Scholarship to Year 11 students who reside at Northam Residential College. This scholarship recognises the commitment of regional students living away from home to complete their secondary education at Northam Senior High School.
description: The Residential College Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more Year 11 students who will live at Northam Residential College to complete Year 11 and 12 at Northam Senior High School. This scholarship provides financial support for residential costs plus additional funding for living expenses. The scholarship value includes out-of-pocket residential costs (once all eligible subsidies have been applied) plus $2,000 per year for Years 11 and 12. Recipients are selected based on academic performance and demonstrated need for residential accommodation while attending secondary school. The selection process involves assessment of school reports and residential college placement requirements. Applicants can apply in Year 10.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 10; Applying to enter Year 11 at Northam Senior High School; Intending to reside at Northam Residential College during Years 11 and 12; Australian citizenship or permanent resident status required; Not eligible if holding another scholarship valued at $450 or more; Must meet Northam Residential College admission requirements
value_aud: Out of pocket Residential costs plus $2,000 per year for Years 11 and 12
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded to 2 or more Year 11 students living at Northam Residential College. Scholarship value includes residential costs plus $2,000 per year. Recipients must reside at Northam Residential College for the duration of Years 11 and 12. A change of school or residential status can affect eligibility; if a change occurs, the scholarship is forfeited. Grants may be renewed in subsequent years provided satisfactory academic and general progress is made.

school_name: One Arm Point Remote Community School

overview: Ocean Reef Primary School participates in the centrally supported Primary Extension and Challenge (PEAC) program, which provides supplementary gifted and talented education for Years 5 and 6 students. PEAC caters to the special learning needs of gifted and talented students through a wide range of extension and specialist courses in community and educational settings.
description: Primary Extension and Challenge (PEAC) is a centrally supported supplementary program for gifted and talented government school students in Western Australia. PEAC in the North Metropolitan Education Region caters to the special learning needs of gifted and talented students in Years 5 and 6. Students gain access to a wide range of extension courses as well as specialist courses in a variety of community and educational settings. PEAC courses are self-funding with course charges ranging from a minimum of $10 up to $100 depending on course requirements such as use of external venues and incursions/excursions. To qualify for PEAC, children are tested in Year 4 and often only the top 1% are selected for the program by the North Metro PEAC team.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5 and 6; Must be identified as gifted and talented; Must score in top 1% on PEAC selection testing in Year 4
special_conditions: PEAC courses are self-funded with parents paying course fees. There is also an early years extension program available.

overview: Ocean Reef Primary School offers instrumental music tuition through IMSS (formerly known as SIM) to students in Years 5 and 6. The school provides instruction in guitar and flute to selected students who demonstrate musical aptitude and meet selection criteria.
description: Instrumental Music Services for Schools (IMSS) provides instrumental music services to Ocean Reef Primary School in Years 5 and 6. The school currently offers 5 guitar and 5 flute places to students starting in Year 5 which continue into Year 6. In addition to weekly small group instrumental lessons, students have opportunities to participate in enrichment music activities which combine students working collaboratively across year levels and with students from other schools. Students must score on or above the threshold mark (generally 20 or more) in the Musical Aptitude Indicator test. Tuition is free but costs are associated with learning an instrument including hiring flutes through the school for the first year and purchasing classical guitars. Additional costs include music stands, books, and tuners.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students starting in Year 5; Must score on or above threshold mark (20+) on the Musical Aptitude Indicator; Screening by instrumental music teachers on physical factors influencing instrument selection; Expected commitment until at least end of Year 10
special_conditions: Aptitude testing is arranged at the students' schools and administered by school staff. Information evenings for parents of beginner students are organised by IMSS in Week 8 of Term 4. Students have access to Department instruction on one instrument only. Students learning from a private teacher may not learn the same instrument from a Department teacher. However, students may learn an instrument privately and play in ensembles conducted by Department teachers after audition.

overview: Ocean Reef Primary School has established a Drum Corps through its association with the Royal Australian Navy. This selective group of approximately 30 students learns drumming and flag patterns under the direction of Band Master Peter Cobb.
description: Through its association with the Royal Australian Navy, Ocean Reef Primary School has established a Drum Corps. This group of approximately 30 students, led by Band Master Peter Cobb, learn drumming and flag patterns. They represent the school at a range of events throughout Perth, including Celebrate Maritime Day for the Navy. The group rehearses every Wednesday morning before school. Students are able to apply to join the Drum Corps from Year 4. There is a termly fee of $70 to cover the cost of instruction. Students accepted into the Drum Corps develop their drumming and marching skills with opportunities to perform at significant community events.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Year 4; Application required to join; Must commit to Wednesday morning rehearsals before school
special_conditions: Termly fee of $70 covers cost of instruction. Students must demonstrate commitment to Wednesday morning rehearsals before school. Full complement typically participates each year under expert tutelage of Band Master Peter Cobb.

program_name: School Leadership Awards (Commanders and School Captains)
overview: Ocean Reef Primary School recognises student leadership through the appointment of School Captains and Commanders. These student leaders take responsibility for steering fellow junior crewmates on the right course within the school's maritime-themed environment.
description: Ocean Reef Primary School appoints School Captains and Commanders as part of its leadership program, consistent with its maritime theme. Rather than traditional prefects, the school designates these crew leaders who take responsibility and steer their fellow junior crewmates on the right course. Leadership programs provide optimum opportunities for students to develop their leadership skills, model school values, and display pride in themselves and the school community. These appointed leaders participate in special activities such as visits to Parliament House and other significant community events representing the school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in leadership positions; Selected by school staff based on demonstrated leadership qualities and alignment with school values
special_conditions: Commanders participate in special events and responsibilities throughout the school year. Selection based on demonstrated commitment to school values and leadership capacity.

overview: Ocean Reef Primary School participates in the City of Joondalup Citizenship Awards program which recognises young members of the school community who show outstanding dedication and commitment to their school. Two awards are presented annually.
description: The City of Joondalup aims to recognise young members of the school community who show outstanding dedication and commitment to their school. Ocean Reef Primary School awards two City of Joondalup Citizenship awards annually to students demonstrating exceptional commitment to their school community. These awards are selected by Year 6 teachers based on established criteria that consider student contributions to school life and community engagement. The awards recognise outstanding citizenship and service to the school community within the maritime-themed environment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students at Ocean Reef Primary School; Assessed on outstanding dedication and commitment to school; Selection by Year 6 teachers
special_conditions: Two awards are given annually. Selection is made by Year 6 teachers using specified criteria. Presented in recognition of outstanding citizenship.

overview: Ocean Reef Primary School presents an annual award for outstanding commitment to the Drum Corps. This award recognises the student who has displayed exceptional commitment, leadership and support to their role and the corps during their membership.
description: One award is given annually for outstanding commitment to the Drum Corps at Ocean Reef Primary School. This award is identified by Band Master Peter Cobb and recognises the student who, during their membership of the Drum Corps, has displayed commitment, leadership and support to their role and the corps. The selection process recognises exceptional dedication to the group's performance and contributions beyond basic participation. The award acknowledges students who have demonstrated leadership within the Drum Corps and supported fellow members.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Drum Corps members; Selected by Band Master Peter Cobb; Based on demonstrated commitment, leadership and support to the corps
special_conditions: Award identified by Band Master Peter Cobb. Recognises students who display commitment, leadership and support to their role and the corps.

overview: Orana Catholic Primary School presents the Loreto Award to recognise students who exemplify and live out the school values of Generosity, Respect and Excellence.
