The award is selected by Year Five students and honours those who consistently demonstrate these values in their daily interactions with the school community.
description: The Loreto Award is presented annually to a student from the school community who demonstrates exceptional commitment to the school values. Recipients are chosen by Year Five students, who make the selection based on observing peers who consistently display generosity, respect, and excellence in their conduct. The award comes with a tuition fee scholarship that pays for tuition fees for the following school year. The award is presented at the Loreto Mass, a significant religious celebration within the school calendar. The recipient is expected to continue embodying the school motto 'To Give is to Receive' and the Catholic values that underpin the Loreto charism upon which the school is founded.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in the school; Must consistently demonstrate the school values of Generosity, Respect and Excellence; Selected by peer nomination from Year Five students; Character-based selection focused on living school values
special_conditions: Award is based on peer selection by Year Five students. The scholarship covers tuition fees for the following school year following the award presentation at Loreto Mass. Recipient must have demonstrated consistent alignment with school values throughout their time at the school.

overview: OneSchool Global offers the Fee Relief Program to support families who may be experiencing financial difficulty and need extra assistance with school fees. The program is available to any family in genuine need and is designed to help ensure all students can access quality education.
description: The OSG Fee Relief Program is a financial hardship assistance program available to all OneSchool Global families experiencing difficulty meeting school fees. Parents experiencing financial hardship or difficulty paying tuition can apply for fee relief support. The program is administered by OneSchool Global and information regarding specific fee relief amounts and application processes can be obtained by contacting the school directly. While the exact financial benefit amount varies by individual circumstances, the program provides flexible fee relief options for families in genuine need. The school emphasizes that families should use this support only when genuinely required, as it represents a significant cost to the institution.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty; Families experiencing hardship meeting school fee obligations; Current or prospective OneSchool Global students
special_conditions: Families are asked to access this assistance only if required, as it represents a significant cost to the school. Application and enquiries regarding fee relief can be directed to the campus administration.

school_name: Our Lady of Mercy Primary School
Our Lady of Mount Carmel School
overview: Our Lady of Mount Carmel School offers a K-12 Sibling Discount Program in collaboration with Seton Catholic College. The program provides financial benefits to families with multiple children enrolled across both institutions, supporting families in managing the costs of Catholic education.
description: The K-12 Sibling Discount Program allows eligible families to receive financial benefits when children are enrolled at both Our Lady of Mount Carmel School (primary) and Seton Catholic College (secondary). Families who have siblings at Seton Catholic College are eligible for this discount. The school is committed to collaborating with Seton Catholic College through this program, which aims to strengthen the bond between the primary school and secondary college while offering enhanced educational opportunities. Eligible families should complete the K-12 Sibling Program Discount Form and return it to the school administration. The discount amount and specific financial value were not detailed on the accessible pages.
eligibility_criteria: Families with children enrolled or planning to enroll at both Our Lady of Mount Carmel School and Seton Catholic College; Form must be completed and submitted by specified deadline
special_conditions: Families must have children at both Our Lady of Mount Carmel School and Seton Catholic College. The discount is offered as part of a collaborative K-12 program. Eligibility is confirmed upon submission of the completed K-12 Sibling Program Discount Form.

school_name: Our Lady of Good Counsel School
Our Lady of Mount Carmel School

overview: Our Lady of Mercy College offers Academic Scholarships to students entering Year 7 in 2026. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and ability in entrance examinations.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 who achieve outstanding results in the scholarship examination. Applicants must sit a scholarship examination on Saturday, 17 May 2025, from 8:20am to 12:30pm. A copy of the most recent school report must be included with the application. The exact financial value and duration are to be determined on individual review. Scholarship holders are expected to maintain a high level of commitment to the whole school program, including school attendance, being a positive influence in all classes, completion of work, excellent citizenship, wearing the uniform correctly, attendance at school functions, and active contribution to co-curricular activities. All scholarship holders are subject to the College's Code of Conduct and policies.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 in 2026; Must sit scholarship examination on Saturday, 17 May 2025; Most recent school report required; Applications closed Friday, 9 May 2025
special_conditions: Scholarship holders are expected to maintain high commitment to the whole school program including school attendance, positive influence in classes, completion of work, excellent citizenship, correct uniform wearing, attendance at school functions, active contribution to co-curricular house and voluntary school activities. Acceptance of the Scholarship is required within ten business days of the offer being made. The offer is conditional upon acceptance of a place at the College.

overview: Our Lady of Mercy College offers Music Scholarships to Year 7 students who demonstrate musical excellence and talent. These scholarships support students who wish to further their musical abilities.
description: Music Scholarships are available to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional musical ability and talent. The College encourages musical endeavours by offering these scholarships to recognise and support musical achievement. Applicants may be required to undergo audition assessment as part of the selection process. The exact financial value varies based on individual assessment and merit. Recipients must maintain good academic standing and active participation in the College's music programs. Books, stationery, uniforms and other charges such as camps or overseas trips are not included in the scholarship value.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated musical ability and talent required; Audition may be required; Most recent school report required
special_conditions: Acceptance of the Scholarship is required within ten business days of the offer being made. The offer is conditional upon acceptance of a place at the College. Books, stationery, uniforms, and other charges are not included in the Scholarship value.

overview: Our Lady of Mercy College offers limited Mercy Values Scholarships to current students entering Year 11 in 2026. These scholarships recognise students who consistently demonstrate the College's Mercy values of Compassion, Respect, Hospitality, Service, Courage and Justice in all aspects of their school life and community.
description: The Mercy Values Scholarship is awarded to current College students entering Year 11 in 2026 who have demonstrated consistent commitment to and embodiment of the College's Mercy values throughout their school career. These values include Compassion, Respect, Hospitality, Service, Courage and Justice. Students must demonstrate these values in the classroom, within the College, and in the broader community. The selection process involves submission of an application form and assessment of the student's demonstrated values. The closing date for applications is Friday, 13 June 2025. Financial benefits and exact values are to be determined on individual review.
eligibility_criteria: Current students at the College entering Year 11 in 2026; Consistent demonstration of Mercy Values required; Demonstrated commitment in classroom, College and community; Application due Friday, 13 June 2025
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available. Scholarship holders are expected to maintain high commitment to the whole school program including school attendance, being a positive influence in classes, completion of work, excellent citizenship, correct uniform wearing, attendance at school functions, and active contribution to co-curricular activities. Acceptance of the Scholarship is required within ten business days of the offer being made. The College reserves the right not to award any or all of the available Scholarships.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Bursary
overview: Our Lady of Mercy College offers bursaries to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students through partnership with MADALAH. These bursaries are open to Indigenous students who demonstrate commitment and motivation in learning, leadership and community.
description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who enrol at the College for 2026, or who are already enrolled, can apply for bursaries in partnership with MADALAH (a not-for-profit organisation offering secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Indigenous students from remote and regional WA communities). These bursaries are open to Indigenous students who demonstrate commitment and motivation in areas of learning, leadership and community. The level of financial contribution is determined on an individual and confidential basis. The value of the bursary is 50% to 100% of tuition fees for the remaining duration of schooling at Our Lady of Mercy College. Bursaries are renewable every year up to Year 12, subject to positive application and effort. MADALAH scholarship funds can be utilised for school fees and tuition, boarding and accommodation, uniforms, textbooks and stationery, IT equipment, school leadership, cultural or sporting camps and tours, tutoring, mentoring or counselling, and personal expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Students who identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Demonstrated commitment to learning, leadership and community; Applicable to enrolment in 2026 or current students; Years 7-12 eligible
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
value_aud: 50% Fee Reduction, Up to 100% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Bursaries are renewable every year up to Year 12, subject to positive application and effort. The level of financial contribution is determined on an individual and confidential basis. Scholarship holders must display ongoing commitment to studies and evidence of Mercy values of Compassion, Respect, Hospitality, Service, Courage and Justice. Books, stationery, uniforms and other charges such as camps or overseas trips are not included in the bursary value.

overview: Our Lady of Mercy College offers bursary support to families experiencing financial hardship. These bursaries assist families facing financial challenges due to a change in circumstances who are not eligible for an HCC card or concessions.
description: For families experiencing financial hardship, the College invites applications for bursary support. This program is designed to assist families facing financial challenges due to a change in circumstances and/or who are not eligible for an HCC card or concessions. Families must complete an application form and will be contacted by a College representative to arrange a meeting with members of the Executive Team to discuss the application. Appointments can be made with the Finance Department and a member of the College Executive. The level of financial contribution and support is determined on an individual and confidential basis through discussion with the family.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Change in circumstances or ineligible for HCC card or concessions; Must complete application form; All year levels eligible
special_conditions: Level of financial contribution determined on individual and confidential basis. Applications require completion of bursary application form. Families will be contacted to arrange meeting with Executive Team to discuss application. Scholarship holders must display ongoing commitment to studies and evidence of Mercy values.

overview: The Parents and Friends association provides bursaries to assist families with the transition to secondary school at Our Lady of Mercy College. These bursaries help eligible families meet the costs of entering Year 7.
description: The Parents and Friends (P&F) organisation at Our Lady of Mercy College provides bursaries to assist families with the transition to secondary school. These bursaries are designed to help families manage the financial impact of enrolling their child at secondary level. The specific financial value and eligibility criteria for P&F bursaries are determined by the P&F association. Further details on how to apply for P&F bursary support are provided to families during the enrolment process and through direct communication from the College and P&F.
eligibility_criteria: Families transitioning to secondary school (Year 7 entry); Families experiencing financial need related to secondary school transition; Further details available from College and P&F
special_conditions: Determined by P&F association. Details provided to families during enrolment process. Scholarship holders must display ongoing commitment to studies and evidence of Mercy values.

overview: Two scholarships worth $1500 over a two-year period are offered by the Diocese to students in Years 7-12 from within the Diocese who wish to join the choir and further their musical and choral talents.
description: Two scholarships of $1500 over a two-year period are offered to students in Years 7-12 from within the Diocese who wish to join the choir and further their musical and choral talents. These scholarships support students who demonstrate commitment to choral music and wish to develop their vocal abilities within a choir setting. The awards are valued at $1500 total over the two-year period of the scholarship. Applicants must demonstrate interest in joining and actively participating in the College choir.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 7-12 from within the Diocese; Wish to join the College choir; Demonstrated commitment to choral music; Interest in further developing musical and choral talents
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship value is $1500 over a two-year period. Students must be from within the Diocese. Applicants must demonstrate commitment to joining and actively participating in the College choir.

overview: Padbury Catholic Primary School offers a Health Care Card Discount Scheme for families holding a means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The discount is applied to tuition fees only for eligible families who present their card to the office.
description: Padbury Catholic Primary School provides tuition fee discounts for families who hold a means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card - Parenting Payment Single allowance. The card must be in the name of the person responsible for payment of fees. If there is joint responsibility for payment, the discounted rate applies only to the eligible card holder. Families eligible for this scheme must present their card to the office and complete a form by end of March each year. Discount through this scheme is given on tuition only and does not cover additional charges, such as excursions, resources, camp, insurance, graduation shirts, swimming lessons, IT devices, and other ancillary fees.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of means-tested Health Care Card; Holder of Pensioner Concession Card - Parenting Payment Single allowance; Card must be in the name of person responsible for fee payment; Must present card to office and complete form by end of March each year; Open to all year levels at school
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only. Does not cover excursions, resources, camp, insurance, graduation shirts, swimming lessons, IT devices, or other additional charges. Card holder must reapply annually by end of March.

overview: Padbury Catholic Primary School provides financial hardship assistance for families experiencing financial difficulties. Families in hardship are invited to make an appointment with the school to discuss support options and payment arrangements.
description: Padbury Catholic Primary School recognises that some families may experience financial hardship and offers a confidential process for discussing support. Families experiencing financial difficulty are asked to make an appointment with the school office to discuss their situation. The school works with families to find appropriate solutions and payment arrangements that may ease the financial burden. This program demonstrates the school's commitment to ensuring all students can access Catholic education regardless of financial circumstances. The nature and extent of assistance is determined on a case-by-case basis through discussion with school leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing financial hardship; Open to all families currently enrolled or seeking enrolment; Confidential assessment required; Must contact school office to arrange appointment; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Appointment required with school office. Confidential assessment and discussion process. Assistance is determined on individual basis. Payment arrangements may be negotiated. Annual review of payment arrangements may be required.

school_name: Our Lady of the Cape Primary School
Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School
overview: Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School provides support for families experiencing genuine hardship with school fees. Families in financial difficulty are encouraged to contact the Principal to discuss their situation confidentially and explore available assistance options.
description: As a Catholic Education Western Australia (CEWA) school, Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School is committed to ensuring no family is denied a Catholic education due to financial difficulty. Families with limited capacity to pay school fees may be eligible for fee concession support. The school treats all requests for fee assistance with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality. Parents experiencing genuine hardship are invited to contact the Principal directly to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options tailored to their specific situation. Support may include partial or full fee remission depending on individual circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing genuine financial hardship; Demonstrated limited capacity to pay school fees; Confidential discussion required with Principal; Applicable to all year levels from Pre-Kindy to Year 6
special_conditions: Applications and discussions are treated with complete confidentiality. Support is determined on individual merit based on family circumstances. No automatic entitlement; each case is assessed individually by the Principal.

school_name: Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School
program_name: CEWA Health Care Card Discount Scheme
overview: Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School is part of the Catholic Education Western Australia network which offers an automatic fee concession to families holding eligible means-tested family concession cards. This scheme provides a discount on school fees as part of CEWA's commitment to making Catholic education accessible to all families.
description: As a CEWA member school, Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School participates in the Health Care Card Discount Scheme introduced in 2005. This scheme provides an automatic fee concession to holders of eligible means-tested family concession cards, including Health Care Cards or Pensioner Concession Cards. The discount applies to all year levels from Kindergarten to Year 12. The discount rate is determined annually by the CEWA system and applied automatically upon verification of card eligibility. No separate application is required; families need only provide proof of their concession card status to the school office. This represents a significant cost saving for eligible families across all education levels.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of valid Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Means-tested family concession card required; Australian resident families; Applicable to all year levels Pre-Kindy to Year 6 at this school
special_conditions: Automatic application upon presentation of valid concession card. Discount determined by CEWA board annually. Families must maintain valid card status throughout enrolment. Sibling discounts may also apply in addition to concession card benefits.

school_name: Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School
overview: Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School, as part of the CEWA system, offers a family discount to families with multiple siblings enrolled in the school. This discount recognizes the financial commitment of families with more than one child attending the same Catholic school.
description: Families with siblings enrolled at Our Lady Star of the Sea Catholic Primary School at the same time are entitled to a level of family discount determined by the school Board. The CEWA system has established this discount policy to support families who carry the cost of educating multiple children. The specific discount percentage is set by the individual school's Board of Management and may vary. This discount applies to families with siblings from Pre-Kindergarten through Year 6 enrolled simultaneously. The discount is applied to the tuition fees at the time of billing. Families should inquire with the school office about the current sibling discount rate and how it will be applied to their family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more siblings enrolled at the school simultaneously; Both students must be actively enrolled Pre-Kindy to Year 6; Applicable to tuition fees only; Discount rate determined by School Board
special_conditions: Discount applied automatically to eligible families upon verification of sibling enrolment. Specific discount percentage set by School Board. Discount applies only while siblings remain simultaneously enrolled. Families should confirm current discount rate with school office.

overview: Our Lady of Fatima School participates in the Health Care Card Discount Scheme, which provides automatic tuition fee discounts to holders of eligible means-tested concession cards. This financial assistance program supports families experiencing financial difficulties in accessing Catholic education.
description: Our Lady of Fatima School participates in the Health Care Card Discount Scheme, which provides automatic tuition fee discounts to holders of eligible concession cards, including Health Care and Pensioner Concession Cards. This discount applies across all year levels from Kindergarten to Year 6. The financial benefit consists of automatic tuition fee discounts applied to eligible families. The scheme operates on a continuous basis for as long as concession card eligibility is maintained. Selection is based on verification of current concession card status through the completion of a form provided by the school. Families must provide their concession card documentation to the School Finance Officer for processing and verification.
eligibility_criteria: Holders of eligible means-tested concession cards including Health Care Cards; Holders of Pensioner Concession Cards; Open to all year levels from Kindergarten to Year 6; Requires completion and submission of verification form
special_conditions: Form must be completed and provided to the School Finance Officer. Discounts are handled with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality. Requires current concession card verification.

overview: Our Lady of Fatima School is committed to ensuring every family seeking a Catholic education is supported, regardless of financial circumstances. Families experiencing financial difficulty may speak to the Principal about the possibility for fee concessions, which are handled with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality.
description: Our Lady of Fatima School recognises that some families may experience financial difficulty in meeting full tuition fees. In alignment with the Bishops' Mandate for Catholic Education Western Australia, families experiencing financial difficulty may speak to the Principal about the possibility for fee concessions. These concessions are handled with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality. The specific financial benefit amount is determined on a case-by-case basis through discussion with the Principal. The support is available for the duration of enrolment, subject to annual review. Selection is based on individual family circumstances and demonstrated financial hardship. Families must initiate contact with the Principal to discuss their circumstances and explore available support options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty; All year levels from Kindergarten to Year 6; Must be enrolled at the school; Assessed on individual family circumstances
special_conditions: Concessions handled with dignity, fairness, compassion and confidentiality. Families must speak with the Principal directly. Individual assessment of circumstances required. Aligned with Bishops' Mandate for Catholic Education Western Australia.

overview: Our Lady of Fatima School offers family discounts for families with more than one child enrolled at the school. This program recognises the financial commitment of families with multiple children in Catholic education.
description: Our Lady of Fatima School provides family discounts for families with more than one child enrolled at the same Catholic school. Based on the 2024 fee schedule, the tuition fees decrease for additional children: the first child pays full tuition fee, the second child receives a 20% discount, the third child receives a 40% discount, and the fourth and subsequent children receive free tuition. The discount structure operates as a percentage reduction applied to the standard tuition fees. The discount applies for the duration of enrolment at the school. Siblings must be enrolled concurrently at the school. Family discounts are automatically applied based on enrolment status and do not require a separate application process.
eligibility_criteria: Families with more than one child enrolled at the school; All year levels from Kindergarten to Year 6; Requires concurrent enrolment of multiple siblings
value_aud: 20% Fee Reduction; 40% Fee Reduction; Full Fee Remission for 4th+ children
special_conditions: Discount is tiered based on child position: 1st child full fee, 2nd child 20% discount, 3rd child 40% discount, 4th and subsequent children free. Requires concurrent enrolment of siblings at the school.

program_name: K-12 Sibling Discount Program (Partnership with Seton Catholic College)
overview: In partnership with Seton Catholic College, Our Lady of Fatima School participates in the K-12 Sibling Discount Program. This initiative supports families with children enrolled across both schools through meaningful financial support and enhanced learning opportunities.
description: Our Lady of Fatima School, in partnership with Seton Catholic College, participates in the K-12 Sibling Discount Program, reflecting the shared commitment of both institutions to supporting families and fostering strong educational pathways. This initiative helps strengthen the connection between the primary school and Seton Catholic College while providing meaningful financial support to families with children enrolled across both schools. The specific financial benefit details are available through discussion with the school's Finance Officer. The program operates continuously for families with children enrolled in both schools. Selection is based on verified concurrent enrolment at both institutions. Interested families should contact the Finance Officer to discuss this program and obtain details specific to their enrolment situation.
eligibility_criteria: Families with children enrolled at both Our Lady of Fatima School and Seton Catholic College; Children at Our Lady of Fatima from Kindergarten to Year 6; Requires concurrent enrolment at both schools
special_conditions: Program requires enrolment at both Our Lady of Fatima School and Seton Catholic College. Contact Finance Officer for specific details and pricing. Designed to strengthen educational pathways between schools and provide financial support.

overview: Parklands School offers Academic Scholarships to encourage and foster scholastic excellence in students. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning.
description: Parklands School offers two academic scholarships available to both currently enrolled students and new enrolments. Scholarships are awarded based on previous academic results and aptitude for studies. The specific financial value, duration, and detailed selection process are not published on the school website. Interested families are directed to contact the school directly for applications and further information about award amounts and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Open to currently enrolled students; Open to new enrolments; Academic performance based on previous academic results; Strong aptitude for studies
special_conditions: Interested families must contact the school directly for complete details on applications, selection criteria, and award specifics.

overview: Parklands School is committed to accessible education and welcomes discussions with families experiencing financial barriers. The school works directly with families to explore flexible options to support their child's enrolment.
description: While not a formal scholarship program, Parklands School states that if finances are a barrier to enrolment, families are welcome to have a confidential discussion with the Principal to explore flexible options. This indicates the school offers some level of financial assistance or fee flexibility for families in financial hardship. The specific terms, amounts, and eligibility criteria for this support are not publicly detailed and require direct contact with the school for more information.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial barriers to enrolment; Requires confidential discussion with Principal
special_conditions: Support is provided through confidential discussion with the Principal. School policy indicates commitment to keeping fees accessible for all families. Family discounts are available for multiple children enrolled.

overview: Perth Montessori offers Bursaries / Financial Aid to families who have demonstrated eligibility for tuition assistance from Year 7 onwards. These bursaries provide a temporary reduction in tuition through an objective assessment of financial position.
description: Perth Montessori defines Bursaries/Financial Aid as a temporary reduction in tuition granted to families who have demonstrated eligibility including an objective assessment of their financial position. These programs are available to families who have demonstrated a positive history with the school. The financial assistance is granted in the form of tuition assistance from Cycle 4 (Year 7) onwards, recognizing families facing genuine financial hardship. The exact amount of reduction is determined based on individual family circumstances and assessment. For complete information on eligibility criteria, application procedures, and specific financial assistance amounts available, interested families should contact the school's administration office directly.
eligibility_criteria: Families must have demonstrated a positive history with the school; Year 7 (Cycle 4) onwards; Objective assessment of financial position required; Financial need must be demonstrated
special_conditions: Bursaries are defined as temporary reduction in tuition. Families must have demonstrated positive history with the College. Contact the Administration office for complete information on Bursaries and Scholarships program and refer to Perth Individual Fee Policy for further conditions.

overview: Perth Montessori offers Scholarships granted as a reduction in tuition to students on the basis of talent desired within the student body from Year 7 onwards. These merit-based scholarships recognize exceptional student abilities without strict regard to family financial position.
description: Perth Montessori offers Scholarships granted as a reduction in tuition to a student on the basis of talent that is desired within the student body, without strict regard to the family's financial position. These merit-based scholarships are available from Cycle 4 (Year 7) onwards to families who have demonstrated a positive history with the school. The school offers multiple scholarship types based on various talents and abilities. The exact tuition reduction amounts and specific scholarship categories are determined based on the school's evaluation of student merit and talents required within the student body. Families should contact the administration office for detailed information on specific scholarship types available and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 (Cycle 4) onwards; Families must have demonstrated a positive history with the school; Based on demonstrated talent desired within the student body; Financial position not a primary consideration
special_conditions: Scholarships are granted as a reduction in tuition on the basis of talent. Multiple scholarship types include various categories of talent. Families must have demonstrated positive history with the College. Contact the Administration office for complete information on Bursaries and Scholarships program and refer to Perth Individual Fee Policy for further conditions.

overview: Perth Montessori offers Temporary Financial Arrangements as a formal agreement with the school board outlining a Schedule of Payment for tuition assistance. This program provides structured payment options for families requiring financial support.
description: Temporary Financial Arrangements are offered in the form of a formal agreement made with the Perth Montessori School Board, outlining a 'Schedule of Payment' for one term with all payments made in advance. This provides families with flexible payment arrangements for one term at a time while maintaining their enrollment. The arrangement requires advance payment of all scheduled fees as outlined in the formal agreement. Failure to comply with the agreed payment schedule will result in removal of the arrangement and potential termination of enrollment. These temporary financial arrangements are designed to provide short-term payment flexibility for families who may face temporary cash flow challenges.
eligibility_criteria: One term payment arrangement only; All payments must be made in advance as agreed; Formal agreement required with school board; Failure to maintain payment schedule results in removal of arrangement
special_conditions: Temporary arrangement for one term only with formal agreement required from school board. All payments must be made in advance. Non-compliance with payment schedule results in removal of the arrangement.

Peel Language Development School - Pinjarra Campus

overview: Parkerville Steiner College offers the Art Scholarship to celebrate and support students who exhibit exceptional creativity, commitment, and skill in various artistic disciplines. The scholarship provides a platform for developing artists to explore and excel in their artistic endeavors, contributing to the vibrant, creative culture of the school.
description: The Art Scholarship is designed for students born in 2009 who demonstrate exceptional ability across various artistic disciplines. Student selection is based on an interview with the student and, where applicable, a portfolio review of their artistic work. The scholarship recognises students who exhibit outstanding creativity, commitment, and skill in visual arts. The specific financial value of the scholarship is not publicly disclosed and must be obtained by contacting the school's admissions office. Scholarships are offered only to new families looking to join Parkerville Steiner College.
eligibility_criteria: Born in 2009; Exceptional artistic ability and skill in various artistic disciplines; Open to new students joining the school; Interview and portfolio review required
special_conditions: Scholarships are only available for new families looking to join Parkerville Steiner College. Selection is based on interview and portfolio review. Specific financial benefits and terms must be obtained from the school's admissions office.

overview: Parkerville Steiner College offers the General Excellence Scholarship to recognise students who showcase talent across multiple areas including academics, music, art, leadership, and community service. The scholarship aims to acknowledge excellence and foster a diverse community characterised by various talents, interests, and backgrounds.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is open to students born in 2009 who showcase talent across multiple areas, including academics, music, art, leadership, and community service. This scholarship programme aims to acknowledge excellence and foster a diverse community characterised by various talents, interests, and backgrounds, reflecting the holistic educational approach of Parkerville Steiner College. The specific financial value of the scholarship is not publicly disclosed and families must contact the school's admissions office for details. Scholarships are offered only to new families looking to join the college.
eligibility_criteria: Born in 2009; Talent across multiple areas including academics, music, art, leadership and/or community service; Open to new students joining the school; Expression of interest required
special_conditions: Scholarships are only available for new families looking to join Parkerville Steiner College. Applicants must demonstrate talent across multiple disciplines. Specific financial benefits and renewal conditions must be obtained from the school's admissions office.

program_name: General Scholarships for Year 7 and Year 11
overview: Peter Moyes Anglican Community School offers a limited number of scholarships for both current and external students entering Year 7 and Year 11. The scholarships reward students demonstrating academic merit, general excellence across many disciplines, or outstanding performance in particular fields, while supporting well-rounded students who embody the school's values of faith, excellence, compassion, respect, responsibility, and courage.
description: Peter Moyes Anglican Community School's scholarship program provides financial support through 40% to 100% remission on tuition fees and other levies and charges, excluding the IT levy. Scholarships are awarded to students displaying academic merit and general excellence, promoting a culture that celebrates personal endeavour and achievement across many different disciplines. All scholarships offered include a financial needs consideration, and families are required to provide financial documents as part of their application. The selection process involves application submission, shortlisting based on academic and co-curricular achievements, and formal interviews with school leadership. Successful candidates demonstrate commitment to contributing positively to the school community and exemplify the school's values. Scholarship offers are formal and must be accepted within stipulated timeframes.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 and Year 11; Open to current and external students; Demonstrate academic merit or general excellence across multiple areas; Outstanding performance in a particular field; Financial needs consideration; Commitment to contributing to school life; Alignment with school values of faith, excellence, compassion, respect, responsibility, and courage
special_conditions: All scholarships include financial needs consideration. Families required to provide financial documents. Selection includes rigorous shortlisting process and formal interviews. Scholarship offers are formal and must be accepted within stipulated timeframes. Recipients must support the school's Christian ethos and community values. Scholarships recognised for academic merit and general excellence across many different disciplines.

school_name: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School - Calista Campus
program_name: Archbishop Dr Peter Carnley Scholarship for Academic Excellence
overview: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School offers the Archbishop Dr Peter Carnley Scholarship for Academic Excellence to reward outstanding academic achievement. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and maintain a high level of academic achievement.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate outstanding academic excellence. The value and duration of the scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal and is subject to the student maintaining a high level of academic achievement throughout their enrolment. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship. Selection is based on demonstrated academic excellence, with applicants typically assessed through Academic Assessment Services (AAS) entrance examination, which comprises numerical reasoning, verbal reasoning, abstract reasoning, reading comprehension and mathematics achievement tests.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Must demonstrate outstanding academic excellence; Must maintain high level of academic achievement; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Value and duration at discretion of the Principal. Recipient must maintain high level of academic achievement throughout enrolment.

school_name: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School - Calista Campus
overview: The Allrounder Scholarship recognises students who add value to school life across multiple areas of excellence. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate all-round excellence in service, culture, sport and academics.
description: The Allrounder Scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who add value to the life of the School in areas of service, culture, sport and academia. All-round excellence will benchmark this student, who will be a strong academic achiever with demonstrated strengths across a number of areas such as sport, drama, music, visual arts and language. The value and duration of the scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship, recognising co-curricular excellence and well-rounded development.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Demonstrates all-round excellence across multiple areas; Strong academic achievement required; Evidence of strengths in sport, drama, music, visual arts or language; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Value and duration at discretion of the Principal. Applicants assessed on demonstrated excellence across multiple disciplines including academics, sport, drama, music, visual arts and language.

school_name: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School - Calista Campus
program_name: Bishop David Murray Scholarship for Leadership/Citizenship
overview: The Bishop David Murray Scholarship for Leadership/Citizenship recognises students who embody the School's core values. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate the values of Spirituality, Excellence, Respect and Belonging.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate the virtues of the School: Spirituality, Excellence, Respect and Belonging. The selected student will also demonstrate a positive contribution to the School and the wider community. The value and duration of the scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship, recognising leadership qualities and active citizenship that contribute positively to both the school and broader community.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Must demonstrate School values of Spirituality, Excellence, Respect and Belonging; Evidence of positive contribution to School and wider community; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Value and duration at discretion of the Principal. Recipient must demonstrate positive contribution to School and wider community throughout enrolment.

school_name: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School - Calista Campus
overview: The Grace and Truth Scholarship provides financial assistance to students facing genuine hardship. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who face economic, social or other disadvantages that would make enrolment at the School very difficult.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who face economic, social or other disadvantages, which would make enrolment at Peter Carnley Anglican Community School very difficult. The value and duration of the scholarship are at the discretion of the Principal, reflecting individual circumstances and need. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship. Applications are assessed based on demonstrated financial or social hardship that genuinely impacts the family's ability to meet school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Must face economic, social or other disadvantages; Enrolment would be very difficult without support; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Value and duration at discretion of the Principal based on individual circumstances. Confidential assessment of financial and social circumstances required.

school_name: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School - Calista Campus
program_name: John Moody Scholarship for Excellence in Sport
overview: The John Moody Scholarship for Excellence in Sport recognises outstanding sporting achievement. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate exceptional sporting ability and active ambassador status for sports.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and are positive and active ambassadors for sports in their school community. The selected student will also demonstrate a high level of participation in State, National or International sports. The value and duration of the scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship, recognising excellence in sporting performance and leadership within the sporting community.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Must demonstrate outstanding sporting ability; Must be positive and active ambassador for sports in school community; Evidence of State, National or International level sports participation; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Value and duration at discretion of the Principal. Recipient must maintain active participation in State, National or International level sports and continue to be positive ambassador for school sports.

school_name: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School - Calista Campus
overview: The Lindbeck Family Scholarship recognises student excellence and leadership in Year 12. This scholarship is awarded to a student in Year 11 for the duration of their Year 12 studies the following year.
description: The Lindbeck Family Scholarship is awarded by the Principal in consultation with School staff. The recipient will have demonstrated sound academic achievement, a positive attitude and strong leadership qualities. The recipient will have complied with the standards and expectations of the School and will be an exemplary role model. The scholarship is awarded to a student in Year 11 for the duration of their Year 12 studies in the following year, recognising demonstrated excellence across academic, behavioural and leadership dimensions.
eligibility_criteria: Current student in Year 11; Must demonstrate sound academic achievement; Must have positive attitude and strong leadership qualities; Must comply with School standards and expectations; Must be exemplary role model
special_conditions: Awarded at the discretion of the Principal in consultation with School staff rather than by direct application. Duration is for Year 12 studies only. Recipient must maintain exemplary conduct and academic standards throughout Year 12.

program_name: The Archbishop Dr Peter Carnley Scholarship for Academic Excellence
overview: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School offers the Archbishop Dr Peter Carnley Scholarship for Academic Excellence to reward outstanding achievement. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate outstanding academic excellence and exceptional commitment to learning.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate outstanding Academic Excellence. The value and duration of the Scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal and is subject to the student maintaining a high level of academic achievement. The financial benefit varies according to the Principal's discretion. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship, and applicants are assessed through an Academic Assessment Services (AAS) written test covering abstract reasoning, verbal reasoning, numerical reasoning, reading, and mathematics. The scholarship requires recipients to maintain academic excellence throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Outstanding academic achievement required; Students from different year levels considered; Subject to maintaining high level of academic achievement
special_conditions: Scholarship value and duration are at the discretion of the Principal. Recipient must maintain a high level of academic achievement throughout the scholarship period.

program_name: The Bishop David Murray Scholarship for Leadership / Citizenship
overview: The Bishop David Murray Scholarship for Leadership / Citizenship recognises students who embody the school's core values. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate the values of the School: Spirituality, Excellence, Respect and Belonging, with a positive contribution to the School and wider community.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate the values of the School: Spirituality, Excellence, Respect and Belonging. The selected student will also demonstrate a positive contribution to the School and the wider community. The value and duration of the Scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship. Recipients are identified for their demonstrated leadership qualities, citizenship values, and commitment to community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Demonstrate values of Spirituality, Excellence, Respect and Belonging; Positive contribution to School and wider community; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Scholarship value and duration are at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must demonstrate positive contribution to both School and wider community.

overview: The All-Rounder Excellence Scholarship recognises students who excel across multiple disciplines. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who add value to the life of the School in areas of service, culture, sport and academia, with all-round excellence as the benchmark.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who add value to the life of the School in areas of service, culture, sport and academia. All-round excellence will benchmark this student. This student will be a strong academic achiever, with demonstrated strengths across a number of areas, such as sport, drama, music, visual arts and language. The value and duration of the Scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship. The scholarship recognises those who combine academic achievement with excellence in co-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Strong academic achievement required; Demonstrated strengths in multiple areas (sport, drama, music, visual arts, language); Add value to School life through service and culture; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Scholarship value and duration are at the discretion of the Principal. Recipients must demonstrate excellence across multiple disciplines including sport, drama, music, visual arts and language.

overview: Peter Carnley Anglican Community School offers a Scholarship for Assistance to support students facing economic or social disadvantage. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who face economic, social or other disadvantages which would make enrolment at Peter Carnley Anglican Community School very difficult.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who face economic, social or other disadvantages, which would make enrolment at Peter Carnley Anglican Community School very difficult. The value and duration of the Scholarship are at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship. The school recognises financial barriers to education and provides support to deserving students to ensure all families can access quality education regardless of financial circumstance.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Face economic, social or other disadvantages; Demonstrate genuine financial hardship; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Scholarship value and duration are at the discretion of the Principal. Assessment of financial hardship may require disclosure of family financial information.

program_name: The John Moody Scholarship for Excellence in Sport
overview: The John Moody Scholarship for Excellence in Sport recognises outstanding athletic ability and commitment to sports. This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and are positive and active ambassadors for Sports in their school community.
description: This scholarship is awarded to current or prospective students who demonstrate outstanding sporting ability and are positive and active ambassadors for Sports in their school community. The selected student will also demonstrate a high level of participation in State, National or International sports. The value and duration of the Scholarship is at the discretion of the Principal. Students from different year levels will be considered for this scholarship. Recipients are recognised for their elite sporting achievements and leadership in promoting sports participation within the school.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students; Outstanding sporting ability demonstrated; High level of participation in State, National or International sports; Positive and active ambassador for sports in school community; Students from different year levels considered
special_conditions: Scholarship value and duration are at the discretion of the Principal. Applicants must provide evidence of State, National or International sporting participation and achievements.

overview: The Lindbeck Family Scholarship is awarded to outstanding Year 11 students to support their final year at school. This scholarship is awarded to a student in Year 11 for the duration of their Year 12 studies the following year.
description: The Lindbeck Family Scholarship is awarded to a student in Year 11 for the duration of their Year 12 studies the following year. This scholarship is open to current students and is awarded at the discretion of the Principal rather than by direct application. The recipient will have demonstrated sound academic achievement, a positive attitude and strong leadership. They will have complied with the standards and expectations of the School and will be an exemplary role model. The value and duration of the Scholarship covers the Year 12 enrolment period and is awarded based on the Principal's assessment of student merit.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 11 students only; Sound academic achievement demonstrated; Positive attitude required; Strong leadership demonstrated; Must comply with School standards and expectations; Must be exemplary role model
special_conditions: This scholarship is open to current students only and is awarded at the discretion of the Principal rather than by direct application. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated academic achievement, positive attitude, strong leadership qualities and exemplary role model status. Scholarship covers Year 12 enrolment.

overview: Piara Waters Senior High School offers the Academic Excellence Program to engage, challenge, and inspire exceptionally able students. The program provides enrichment, extension, and acceleration opportunities in Mathematics, English, Science, and Humanities and Social Sciences. It recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic abilities and a strong commitment to academic excellence.
description: The Academic Excellence Program is a highly selective program designed to provide enrichment, extension, and acceleration opportunities for exceptionally able students in Mathematics, English, Science, and Humanities and Social Sciences. Admission is based on the Higher Ability Selection Test (HAST), administered by the Australian Council for Education Research (ACER), to identify students who demonstrate exceptional abilities and strong commitment to academic excellence. The program embraces self-directed and deep learning, leveraging technology to enhance the development of 21st-century skills including character, citizenship, collaboration, communication, creativity and critical thinking. Teachers within the program have undergone specialised training at the Gifted Education Research, Resource, and Information Centre (GERRIC). There is no disclosed financial benefit, tuition reduction, or fee assistance associated with this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7, 8, 9, or 10; Must demonstrate exceptional academic abilities; Must demonstrate strong commitment to academic excellence; Selection based on Higher Ability Selection Test (HAST) by ACER; Consideration given to positive disposition towards English/Humanities or Mathematics/Sciences; Open to all students regardless of school sector background
special_conditions: Program is part of the school's commitment to academic excellence from the foundation year. The school emphasises self-directed learning and deep understanding of key concepts. Students are expected to apply their knowledge to real-world problems and engage in critical thinking, effective communication, and peer collaboration.

overview: Piara Waters Senior High School offers the Instrumental Music School Services program as one of the highly regarded music programs in public schools. The program provides selected students with free instrumental tuition and access to music enrichment activities. It recognises students who demonstrate musical aptitude and talent.
description: The Instrumental Music School Services program is a unique and highly regarded program for students in public schools, run through Piara Waters Senior High School. Selection into this program is based on musical aptitude testing and other criteria. Selected students will receive free instrumental tuition and the opportunity to participate in a range of music enrichment activities. Students will be given the opportunity to join school ensembles and to perform at concerts and school-based events. The teaching standard is extremely high, with instrumental teachers being qualified musicians and registered teachers. The financial benefit is free instrumental tuition.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Must demonstrate musical aptitude; Selection based on musical aptitude testing; Must meet other selection criteria as determined by the program; Open to students with passion for music
special_conditions: Selection is based on musical aptitude testing and other criteria. Students are expected to participate in school ensembles and perform at concerts and school-based events. Teaching is provided by qualified musicians and registered teachers.
