Port Hedland School Of The Air

overview: Pioneer Village School offers the Alumni Scholarship to celebrate families' ongoing loyalty and commitment to the school. This scholarship welcomes second generation students who wish to attend Pioneer Village School.
description: The Alumni Scholarship is offered to students new to Pioneer Village School who are second generation members of the school community. This scholarship recognises and celebrates families' ongoing loyalty and commitment to Pioneer Village School. The scholarship offers half tuition remuneration, providing financial support for eligible families. Applications for each school year are accepted between January 1st and April 24th in the year prior to the year of commencement. This scholarship is only available to new families.
eligibility_criteria: Second generation family member; Open to students new to Pioneer Village School; Applications must be submitted between January 1st and April 24th in the year prior to commencement
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to students new to Pioneer Village School only. Recipients must be second generation family members.

overview: Pioneer Village School offers the Indigenous Scholarship to support students of Indigenous Australian descent. This scholarship recognises and provides full tuition support to Indigenous students joining the school community.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship is offered to students of Indigenous Australian descent who are new to Pioneer Village School. This scholarship provides full tuition remuneration, offering comprehensive financial support to eligible Indigenous students. The scholarship is designed to enhance access and opportunity for Indigenous Australian students within the school community. Applications for each school year are accepted between January 1st and April 24th in the year prior to the year of commencement. This scholarship is only available to new families.
eligibility_criteria: Students of Indigenous Australian descent; Open to students new to Pioneer Village School; Applications must be submitted between January 1st and April 24th in the year prior to commencement
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to students new to Pioneer Village School only. Applicant must be of Indigenous Australian descent.

overview: Pinjarra Senior High School offers the Academic Extension Program to students in Years 8 to 10 who demonstrate exceptional engagement with the learning environment. The program is designed for students keen to be challenged and committed to achievement at high levels.
description: The Academic Extension Program is delivered to students in Years 8 to 10 based on the quality of their engagement with the Pinjarra SHS learning environment. Students are identified as potential candidates for this program if they have demonstrated their ability through state and national testing and other school achievements, as well as their approach to learning. Students may be transferred into or out of this program according to how effectively they respond to the expectations and opportunities available during any semester. The program seeks to offer talented students the opportunity to apply their talents in a broad and enriched curriculum. Financial benefit: None specified as this is a selective entry program within the government school system.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8, Year 9, Year 10; Demonstrated ability through state and national testing; Strong school achievement and engagement; Positive approach to learning; May be transferred based on semester performance
special_conditions: Students may be transferred into or out of this program according to how effectively they respond to the expectations and opportunities available through the program during any semester.

overview: The Pinjarra Football and Netball Academy is a specialist sports program that has operated since 2008. It provides intensive football and netball training and development for students in Years 8 to 10, supported by partnerships with local and professional football clubs.
description: The Pinjarra Football and Netball Academy (PFNA) has been a part of Pinjarra Senior High School since 2008 and is delivered over 3 years, from Year 8 to Year 10. The program is committed to shaping students into self-motivated, disciplined individuals who develop into leaders within the school and in the football and netball clubs in the community. PFNA students have the opportunity to represent the school through interschool football and netball competitions to develop specialist skills. The program is supported by significant community partnerships with Pinjarra Football Club, Peel Thunder and Fremantle Football Club. Academically, PFNA students complete all MESH subjects (Maths, English, Science, HASS) within their regular school timetable. Financial benefit: Not specified as a fee-paying program; integration into regular government school curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Year 8, Year 9, Year 10; Selection based on sporting ability and commitment; Keen to be challenged; Committed to achievement; As of 2019, program extended to include girls in Years 8 and 9
special_conditions: Program delivered over 3 years from Year 8 to Year 10. Students remain together as a cohort during Mathematics, English, Humanities and Social Sciences, and Specialist Football delivered through Health and Physical Education. Supported by significant community partnerships with Pinjarra Football Club, Peel Thunder and Fremantle Football Club.

overview: The Pinjarra Music Program offers students from Years 7 to 12 a range of musical opportunities. Students receive instrumental instruction and participate in classroom music and ensemble activities including a swing band.
description: The Pinjarra Music Program offers students from Years 7 to 12 a range of opportunities. Students in Lower School (Years 7 to 9) continue their lessons with their instrumental teachers and round-out their musical training within the Classroom Music Program, studying how to analyse and understand music, read and write both traditional notation and tablature, and use musical software to generate, record and produce music. Students in Year 10 further these skills in preparation for their study of a Certificate II in Music in Years 11 and 12. The program equips students for a career in the Music Industry or further study at TAFE (higher Certificates and Diploma), or University. Instrumental music students are required to join one of the school's bands or ensembles where attendance at weekly rehearsals is expected. Financial benefit: None specified as integration into regular government school curriculum.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12; Interest in learning a musical instrument; Commitment to ensemble participation
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Music students in the Instrumental Program are required to join one of the school's bands or ensembles where attendance at weekly rehearsals are expected. Students in Year 10 can prepare for Certificate II in Music in Years 11 and 12.

overview: Prendiville Catholic College, in partnership with MADALAH (a not-for-profit organisation), offers the Lennox-Bradley First Nations Bursary to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The bursary provides tuition fee scholarships for First Nations students from Years 7 to 12.
description: The Lennox-Bradley First Nations Bursary is administered in partnership with MADALAH Limited, a not-for-profit organisation that specialises in educational scholarships for First Nations students, primarily from remote and regional communities. The bursary provides tuition fee scholarships to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students undertaking secondary education at Prendiville Catholic College. These scholarships are administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). The College is a partner school with MADALAH, and enrolled First Nations students may be eligible to apply. The Yirri Yaarkin Program, established in 2018, provides additional support services for First Nations students within the College community.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Open to students from Years 7 to 12; Primary consideration for students from remote and regional communities; Enrolled or seeking enrolment at Prendiville Catholic College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Initial enquiries should be directed to Mrs Cheryl Lennox-Bradley, First Nations Liaison Officer. Scholarships are administered by MADALAH on behalf of the National Indigenous Australians Agency (NIAA). Recipients are expected to participate in the Yirri Yaarkin Program support services.

overview: Prendiville Catholic College offers the Specialised Band Scholarship Programme for musically talented students entering Year 7. The scholarship enables students to participate in the Prendiville Specialised Music course, which provides instruction in instrumental and vocal performance alongside the regular curriculum.
description: The Specialised Band Scholarship Programme is a dedicated music scholarship for students with instrumental or vocal proficiency entering Year 7. Students in the Specialised Band Scholarship Programme are automatically enrolled in the Prendiville Specialised Music (PSM) course, a comprehensive programme that covers musicianship, composition, music knowledge and performance. The PSM course consists of a 55-minute curriculum class weekly, individual or group lessons with specialist instrumental or vocal tutors on a rotating timetable, participation in large ensemble rehearsals, and musicianship rehearsals. The programme is structured to extend students' musical skills and understanding and supports students wishing to pursue Music as a subject in upper school. Co-curricular musical activities, instrumental and vocal tuition, and involvement in various ensembles are integrated components of the programme.
eligibility_criteria: Currently learning a musical instrument or voice (within or outside the College); Either highly skilled musicians, wishing to pursue Music as a subject in upper school, or having a strong interest in or passion for Music; Seeking entry to Year 7
special_conditions: Students in the Specialised Band Scholarship Programme will be automatically placed into the Prendiville Specialised Music course. Continued participation requires maintenance of musical proficiency and engagement with the co-curricular programme. Instrumental and vocal tuition are charged on an individual basis by specialist tutors.

program_name: Health Care Card (Concession Card) Discount Scheme
overview: Queen of Apostles School offers fee discounts to families who hold an eligible Centrelink Concession Card. This program provides financial assistance to eligible families managing school costs.
description: Holders of Health Care Card (Concession Card) from Centrelink may be eligible for a discount on their child's school fees. Families must complete the Concession Card Discount Application form available from the school's front office or website and return it with a copy of their card. The application process requires submission to the school's front office. The school provides flexible payment options and monthly billing. Families experiencing financial hardship are also encouraged to make an appointment with the Principal to discuss additional discount options.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold an eligible Centrelink Concession Card (Health Care Card); Application form and copy of card must be submitted to the school office
special_conditions: Application forms available from front office or school website. Copy of Concession Card required with submission.

overview: Queen of Apostles School provides financial hardship assistance to families experiencing genuine difficulty in paying school fees. Families can discuss their circumstances confidentially with the Principal.
description: Families experiencing financial hardship who do not qualify for the Health Care Card discount scheme are encouraged to make an appointment with the Principal to discuss their options and potential fee discounts or payment arrangements. The school recognizes that families may face temporary or ongoing financial difficulties and offers a confidential discussion process. Contact the school's Reception to arrange a meeting with the Principal. The school aims to work with families to find sustainable payment solutions.
eligibility_criteria: Must be experiencing genuine financial hardship; Must contact the school to arrange appointment with Principal; Families not qualifying for Health Care Card scheme
special_conditions: Confidential appointment required with Principal; Families must contact Reception to arrange meeting; Assistance available on case-by-case basis; School will discuss individual payment options and potential discounts.

overview: Rehoboth offers the Principal's Scholarship to reward outstanding achievement in prospective students. The scholarship is merit-based and recognises students who demonstrate exceptional ability across a range of areas, while also expressing what it means to be a committed Christian. Scholarships are offered to assist families in transitioning to the College and to encourage students to achieve their best.
description: The Principal's Scholarship is a merit-based award offered to new students entering Years 6-12. Scholarships take the form of a $2,500 per year reduction in tuition fees, deducted from annual College fees. Selection is based on demonstrated high achievement in one of a number of areas including academic excellence, music, performing arts, visual arts, sports, or community service. Applicants must also demonstrate how Jesus is real in their life and express what it means to be a Christian. Applications normally open in September each year. Families are encouraged to request the Prospectus and scholarship pack for additional information.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Year 6; Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12; Must demonstrate high achievement in at least one area; Must demonstrate commitment to Christian faith; Families must meet enrolment requirements including at least one parent being a committed Christian
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are intended to assist families in transitioning to the College. Selection is merit-based and applicants must demonstrate high achievement in one or more areas such as academics, music, performing arts, visual arts, sports, or community service, as well as expressing their Christian commitment. Families are required to meet the College's enrolment requirements which include at least one parent being a committed Christian and an active member of a protestant Christian church.

overview: Rehoboth offers an innovative Trainee Teacher Scholarship program for Year 12 students pursuing tertiary education in teaching. The scholarship supports students who intend to study teaching at university and have a desire to return to Rehoboth as teachers following their studies. It recognises the College's commitment to developing future educators within the Christian school movement.
description: The Trainee Teacher Scholarship is an innovative program designed to support current Year 12 students who intend to study teaching at a tertiary level and who have a desire to return to Rehoboth as a teacher at the conclusion of their studies. The scholarship takes the form of a reduction in tuition fees to help offset costs. This program reflects Rehoboth's commitment to developing and nurturing future generations of Christian educators. Selection involves discussion with the Enrolments Officer who can provide specific details about the program structure, financial benefits, and renewal conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Must be currently enrolled in Year 12; Must intend to study teaching at a tertiary level; Must have a commitment to return to Rehoboth as a teacher following completion of studies; Must meet the College's standard enrolment requirements
special_conditions: Recipients must be committed to studying teaching at tertiary level and to returning to Rehoboth as a teacher upon completion of their studies. This is a conditional scholarship tied to the student's commitment to contribute to the College's educational mission as a future teaching staff member.

overview: Rehoboth Christian College offers the Principal's Scholarship to students newly enrolled at the College entering Years 6-12. The scholarship is merit-based and recognises high achievement in one of a number of areas, combined with demonstrated Christian faith and commitment to the College's values.
description: The Principal's Scholarship is offered to students in Year 6-12 who are newly enrolled at Rehoboth. These scholarships are aimed primarily at new students to the College and are to the value of $2,500 per year which is deducted from the annual College fees. Scholarships are merit-based and awarded to students who demonstrate their high achievement in one of a number of areas, as well as demonstrating how Jesus is real in their life and expressing what it means to be a Christian. The scholarships are intended to assist families in transitioning to the College and to encourage a commitment amongst students to achieve their best. Round 1 applications normally open in September each year.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Years 6-12; Merit-based; Must demonstrate high achievement in one or more areas; Must demonstrate Christian faith and understanding of what it means to be a Christian
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships are intended to assist families in transitioning to the College. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to achieve their best. Scholarships usually take the form of a reduction in tuition fees. Existing families are encouraged to discuss these opportunities with friends and family who may be interested in enrolling.

overview: Rehoboth Christian College offers the Trainee Teacher Scholarship to current Year 12 students who intend to study teaching at tertiary level. The scholarship recognises students' commitment to the teaching profession and desire to return to Rehoboth as educators after completing their tertiary studies.
description: The Trainee Teacher Scholarship is an innovative program offering scholarships for Year 12 students who intend to study teaching at a tertiary level and who have a desire to return to Rehoboth as a teacher at the conclusion of their studies. The scholarship is designed to help offset tuition fees for qualifying students. Applicants must be current Year 12 students with demonstrated commitment to teaching as a profession and a genuine intention to return to Rehoboth to teach after completing their tertiary teaching qualifications. Conditions apply to this scholarship, including a commitment to serve as an educator at the College upon qualification.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 12 students; Must intend to study teaching at tertiary level; Must have a desire to return to Rehoboth as a teacher after completing studies; Conditions apply
special_conditions: Recipients must have a desire to return to Rehoboth as a teacher at the conclusion of their tertiary studies. Conditions apply. Scholarship intended to help offset tuition fees.

overview: Ridge View Secondary College offers a range of Instrumental Music scholarships through a partnership with Instrumental Music School Services. The scholarships support students who demonstrate musical ability across multiple instrumental and vocal disciplines including brass, woodwind, percussion, and guitar.
description: Ridge View Secondary College provides Instrumental Music scholarships through its partnership with the Instrumental Music School Services. The programs include training and support in Vocal, Brass, Woodwind, Percussion, Classical Guitar and Contemporary Guitar. Students selected for this program develop their musical skills in preparation for performances in the college's Performing Arts Theatre. The scholarship recognises students who show commitment to instrumental music development and provides structured tuition through the partnership arrangement. Selection is based on musical ability and demonstration of talent in the chosen instrumental discipline.
eligibility_criteria: Interest in instrumental music; Demonstrated musical ability; Selection through audition or musical assessment by Instrumental Music School Services; Commitment to continued study of chosen instrument
special_conditions: Students participate through partnership with Instrumental Music School Services. Ongoing participation requires continued commitment to instrumental music study and regular lesson attendance.

overview: Ridge View Secondary College offers an Academic Achievement Program for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. This program provides targeted support and extension opportunities for high-achieving students across the college.
description: The Academic Achievement Program at Ridge View Secondary College is designed to support and extend students who demonstrate outstanding academic performance. The program provides differentiated learning experiences and targeted support to help high-achieving students reach their full academic potential. Students in this program receive specialized instruction and extension activities across core learning areas. The program supports academic excellence and prepares students for advanced study pathways, particularly the Academic Enrichment Pathway offered in senior school. Selection involves ongoing academic assessment and teacher recommendation based on demonstrated achievement and learning capacity.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated high academic achievement; Selection through ongoing academic assessment; Teacher recommendation; Commitment to academic excellence
special_conditions: Participation is based on ongoing academic performance and teacher assessment. Students must maintain commitment to academic excellence. Program contact through Compass or direct teacher communication.

overview: Rockingham Beach Primary School offers the Primary Extension and Challenge (PEAC) program for gifted and talented students in Years 5 and 6. The program provides special courses and enrichment opportunities for high-achieving students identified through standardized testing in Year 4.
description: The PEAC program provides special courses for gifted and talented primary school students in years 5 and 6. Students are tested in year 4 and letters offering placements are forwarded to parents. This is a part-time enrichment program that extends learning for identified high-ability students through challenging and intellectually stimulating courses. The program is offered through the Department of Education Western Australia and serves the South Metropolitan Education Region. Selection is based on performance on standardized assessments administered in Year 4, with identification of exceptional learners who demonstrate advanced reasoning, verbal comprehension, and mathematical problem-solving abilities.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 4 at the time of testing; Exceptional academic ability identified through standardized PEAC testing; Currently enrolled at Rockingham Beach Primary School
special_conditions: This is not a competitive scholarship but a Department of Education enrichment program. Selection is universal - all Year 4 students at the school are tested. Students identified as exceptionally able are invited to participate in special off-site courses during regular school hours. Parents receive letters offering placements for selected students.

overview: Rockingham Montessori School offers bursary assistance to eligible families experiencing financial difficulty. The bursary program is designed to support families who may otherwise be unable to afford the school fees, enabling their children to continue their education at the school.
description: The Bursary Policy at Rockingham Montessori School provides means-tested financial assistance to families who are experiencing unexpected or ongoing financial difficulty. Bursary support is available to assist parents in meeting their school fee obligations. The policy outlines eligibility criteria based on family financial circumstances and is administered confidentially. Bursaries are evaluated on a case-by-case basis and are subject to available school resources. Families should contact the school administration to inquire about bursary eligibility and application procedures. The school keeps fees to a minimum, with almost half of the school's funding coming from family fees, reflecting the commitment to making Montessori education accessible.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty; Open to current students; Means-tested based on financial circumstances; Subject to available school resources
special_conditions: Bursaries are awarded based on family financial circumstances and available funding. Applications should be made to the school administration. Assistance is confidential and administered on a case-by-case basis.

overview: Rockingham Montessori School offers a scholarship for former students who wish to pursue tertiary education in Montessori teaching. This scholarship recognizes and supports alumni who are committed to continuing their educational journey within the Montessori framework.
description: The RMS Alumni Montessori Teaching Scholarship, valued at $9,500, is available to former Rockingham Montessori School students who have been accepted into and are pursuing a Montessori teaching degree at tertiary level. This scholarship was approved by the school board and aims to support alumni in becoming the next generation of Montessori educators. The scholarship recognizes the school's commitment to developing passionate educators who will carry forward the Montessori philosophy and contribute to the wider Montessori community. Eligibility requires that applicants be former RMS students and have gained acceptance into an accredited Montessori teacher training program. The scholarship value represents a significant contribution toward tertiary education expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Former RMS students only; Must be accepted into a Montessori teacher training program; Must be pursuing tertiary Montessori teaching qualification
special_conditions: Open to alumni only. Applicants must have completed their education at Rockingham Montessori School and be pursuing approved Montessori teaching qualifications.

school_name: Rockingham Montessori School - Port Kennedy Campus (Secondary)
Roebourne District High School - Ngurrawaana Campus
Rockingham Senior High School Education Support Centre
Rockingham Montessori School - Port Kennedy Campus (Primary)
overview: Rockingham Montessori School offers sibling discounts on school fees to families with multiple children enrolled at the school. This discount applies to the tuition fees for additional children and helps reduce the overall cost of education for families with multiple siblings.
description: Sibling discounts are applied to school tuition fees for families with more than one child enrolled at Rockingham Montessori School. The discount applies to the school fees paid via direct debit on a yearly, term, monthly, or fortnightly basis. Sibling discounts do not apply to Bond Deposits or Enrolment Fees. The discount is an automatic financial assistance measure for families managing education costs across multiple children. Families paying full annual fees in advance receive a separate 2% discount on tuition fees. Sibling discounts represent a standard financial assistance mechanism to support families with multiple enrolments at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple siblings enrolled at Rockingham Montessori School; Applies to tuition fees only; Does not apply to Bond Deposits or Enrolment Fees
special_conditions: Sibling discounts apply only to school tuition fees and not to Bond Deposits. Families paying full annual tuition fees in advance receive an additional 2% discount on those fees.

overview: Sacred Heart College offers the BoodjerMia Bursary Program to support First Nations students in engaging fully with the College's learning community. The program invests up to $100,000 annually to provide financial assistance to eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students, both prospective and current, recognising their dedication and potential.
description: The BoodjerMia Bursary Program is Sacred Heart College's commitment to supporting First Nations youth across Australia. The program provides financial assistance of up to full tuition fee remission for eligible Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Named after the Noongar word meaning 'sandcastle', the bursary supports students to grow in knowledge, culture and community, much like a sandcastle builds strong foundations with care. The College allocates up to $100,000 annually for this program. Where demand is high, the College Principal, Director of Finance and Corporate Services and College Aboriginal Liaison Officer determine the allocation and percentage of support provided, not exceeding the $100,000 commitment. The cost of levies, laptop, uniforms and books remain the responsibility of parents/caregivers. Applications are submitted with a Declaration of First Nations Heritage and uploaded to the enrolment application or delivered in person to the College Registrar.
eligibility_criteria: Be of First Nations (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander) heritage; Have a desire to receive a Christ-centred Catholic education; Support the College ethos; Demonstrate a commitment to learning; Demonstrate regular school attendance; Be eager to participate in College activities; Open to prospective and current students
special_conditions: Applications include Declaration of First Nations Heritage. For Year 7 enrolment and bursary consideration, it is recommended that an Expression of Interest is submitted by the end of Year 4. In January of Year 5, the formal enrolment selection process commences and applications are opened. Students and parents/caregivers will be invited for an interview with the College Principal. Where demand for Bursary support is high, allocation of the bursary and percentage of support provided is at the discretion of the College Principal, Director of Finance and Corporate Services and College Aboriginal Liaison Officer, not exceeding the $100,000 annual commitment. Levies, laptop, uniforms and books are the responsibility of parents/caregivers.

overview: Sacred Heart College's Aboriginal Bursary Program provides up to $100,000 annually to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who would otherwise be unable to afford enrolment. Available to both current and prospective students, this bursary recognises and rewards students' commitment to their education.
description: Sacred Heart College is committed to providing accessible educational experiences for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students while acknowledging, respecting and reflecting on Aboriginal peoples' histories and cultures. The College contributes up to $100,000 annually towards the Aboriginal Bursary Program for Aboriginal students who would otherwise not be able to afford enrolment. This bursary is available to selected current and prospective students, with the purpose of recognising and rewarding a student's commitment to their education. Recipients are invited for an interview with the College Principal following submission of applications. The Sacred Heart College Aboriginal Bursary supports students who have a desire to receive a Christ-centred Catholic education, support the College ethos, demonstrate commitment to learning, demonstrate regular school attendance, and are eager to participate in College activities. Applications require a Statutory Declaration confirming Aboriginality.
eligibility_criteria: Be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Have a desire to receive a Christ-centred Catholic education; Support the College ethos; Demonstrate a commitment to learning; Demonstrate regular school attendance; Be eager to participate in College activities; Open to prospective and current students
special_conditions: Requires completion of Aboriginal Bursary Application Form and Statutory Declaration confirming Aboriginality. For Year 7 enrolment and bursary consideration, it is recommended that an Expression of Interest is submitted by the end of Year 4. In January of Year 5, formal enrolment selection process commences. Students and parents/caregivers will be invited for an interview with the College Principal. College offers circumstantial financial support to enrolled students with genuine financial need. Payment plans available upon successful enrolment. Families may be eligible for additional financial support through Concession Cards, Sibling Assistance, and ABSTUDY Supplement Allowance.

overview: Sacred Heart College offers circumstantial financial support to families of enrolled students experiencing genuine financial hardship. The College works with families to provide assistance and flexible payment arrangements to ensure access to education.
description: Sacred Heart College offers circumstantial financial support to the families of enrolled students who have genuine financial need. The College recognises that some families may face unexpected financial challenges and works collaboratively to provide assistance. Parents/caregivers in genuine financial hardship should contact the Accounts Receivable Officer to discuss their circumstances. Those in this situation will be asked to complete an 'Income Assessment Proforma' to assist the College in deciding on appropriate support in each circumstance, with involvement of the College Principal. Payment plans are also available for all College families upon successful enrolment. The College provides flexible financial arrangements to help families meet their educational costs while remaining committed to providing accessible Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Be an enrolled student at Sacred Heart College; Experience genuine financial hardship or financial need; Be willing to complete Income Assessment Proforma for assessment
special_conditions: Available to enrolled students only. Assessment conducted by completing Income Assessment Proforma with involvement of College Principal. Assessed on individual circumstances. Payment plans available as alternative or additional support. Contact Accounts Receivable Officer on (08) 9246 8208 or fees@sacredheart.wa.edu.au for more information.

program_name: Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme
overview: Sacred Heart Primary School offers a Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme to families experiencing financial hardship. The program provides discounted tuition fees to families who hold a current means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card from Centrelink. This initiative reflects the school's commitment that financial inability should never be a reason for excluding any child from the school community.
description: The Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme provides tuition fee discounts for families holding valid means-tested Health Care Cards or Pensioner Concession Cards issued by Centrelink. To qualify, the card must be in the name of the parent or guardian responsible for paying fees, not the student. Families must present the original card to the school office for photocopying and complete a Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme form. The discount is dependent on maintaining an active and current card; families are responsible for notifying the school when cards expire or are renewed, or fees will be adjusted accordingly. The school's approach reflects its belief that financial constraints should never prevent any child from accessing Catholic education at Sacred Heart Thornlie.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a current means-tested family Health Care Card issued from Centrelink; OR Must hold a current Pensioner Concession Card issued from Centrelink; Card must be in the name of the parent/guardian responsible for fee payment; Card cannot be in the student's name; Families must present card to school office; Must complete Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme form; Card status must remain current throughout enrolment
special_conditions: All information pertaining to the payment of school fees is treated as confidential. Members of the School Board are not advised of individual family names to maintain privacy. Families must keep the school updated with new Health Care Cards or upon expiry, as fees may be adjusted accordingly. The inability to pay school fees should never be the reason for exclusion from Sacred Heart Thornlie. Parents experiencing difficulty with fee payment can make an appointment with the Principal to discuss fees and establish a flexible payment plan.

overview: Roleystone Community College offers the Academic Challenge Program for selected students to study in two areas: Mathematics/Science and English/Humanities and Social Sciences as a unified group from Year 7 to Year 10. The program is designed to engage academically able students with enriching and challenging learning opportunities.
description: The Academic Challenge Program provides an enriching and challenging learning curriculum designed to engage academically able students. Students are provided with opportunities to engage in activities which develop critical and creative thinking skills above and beyond regular classroom learning. Placements are made on the basis of individual performance in benchmark testing completed in Year 6, NAPLAN results, school reports and attendance. Students may enrol in one or both program areas (Maths/Science or English/HaSS) depending on their academic needs. Maintaining a place in the program is based on ongoing assessment throughout the course and maintaining Good Standing within the school. There is no financial value associated with this program.
eligibility_criteria: Selected students; Must complete benchmark testing in Year 6; NAPLAN performance considered; School report performance required; Good attendance record; Commitment to Roleystone's values; Remaining in good standing at school
special_conditions: Maintaining a place in the Academic Challenge Programs is based on ongoing assessment throughout the course in addition to maintaining Good Standing within the school. Continued success in subject areas and commitment to Roleystone's Values and Motto is vital for continued enrolment in the program.

overview: Roleystone Community College's Academy of Digital Media (RADM) is an industry-aligned specialist program for students in Years 8 to 10. This is the only specialist film and digital media school in Western Australia and provides students with training in all facets of digital media production.
description: The Academy of Digital Media is a three-year specialist program commencing in Year 8, with late applications accepted on a case-by-case basis after Year 8. Students receive ongoing support from industry professionals and use industry-standard equipment while developing a full understanding of filmmaking. The program allows students to obtain a Certificate II in Creative Industries (Media) through North Metro TAFE by the end of Year 10. This nationally recognised qualification counts towards WACE points for Year 11 and 12 studies at any school. Entry into the program requires completion of a RADM portfolio review process. There is no direct financial scholarship value associated with this program, though students gain a VET Certificate II credential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from Year 8 onwards; Portfolio review process required; Industry-standard equipment skills needed; Interest in film, television and digital media; Good standing in school
special_conditions: Enrolments for the Digital Media: Film and Television specialist programme are called for in June and are subject to an interview process. Students undergo the RADM portfolio review process once application forms are received. Certificate II course completion counts towards WACE points at schools chosen for Year 11 and 12.

overview: Roleystone Community College offers an Advanced Netball program as a specialist sports opportunity. The program is for selected students who demonstrate excellence and commitment to netball at a high level.
description: The Advanced Netball Program is a specialist sports opportunity offered at Roleystone Community College for students who demonstrate athletic excellence in netball. The program is designed for selected students with advanced netball ability and commitment to competitive sport. While specific eligibility criteria and entry requirements are not detailed on the school website, the program operates as part of the college's specialist sports offerings. There is no direct financial scholarship value associated with this program.
eligibility_criteria: Advanced netball ability; Selected students only; Commitment to competitive netball; Good standing in school

overview: The Roleystone Bushranger Cadets program promotes environmental awareness and self-development for students in Years 7 to 10. This cadet program encourages appreciation and education of the natural environment in which the school is situated.
description: The Roleystone Bushranger Cadets program is a cadet unit for students in Years 7 to 10 that promotes environmental awareness and self-development. The program is based on the college's location in a jarrah forest habitat and includes regular environmental conservation work, including tackling jarrah dieback issues and participating in tree injection projects. Bushranger Cadets are supplied with a cadet uniform (to be returned when the student leaves the program). There is no cost for week-to-week sessions. Camps, incursions and excursions are usually funded by the Department of Communities but may incur a small cost. Students participate in hands-on environmental conservation activities and learn practical outdoor skills.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 10; Interest in environmental conservation; Commitment to self-development
special_conditions: Bushranger Cadets are supplied with a cadet uniform (to be returned when the student leaves the program). There is no cost for week to week sessions. Camps, incursions and excursions are usually funded by the Department of Communities, but may incur a small cost.

overview: Roleystone Community College offers the Duke of Edinburgh's International Award for students in Years 7 to 10. This prestigious youth award is a program of self-discovery and is the world's leading achievement award for young people.
description: The Duke of Edinburgh's International Award is a world-leading achievement award for young people offered to students in Years 7 to 10. The program is designed as a pathway of self-discovery, helping students develop confidence, resilience and practical skills through structured activities and challenges. The award operates at different levels and provides students with opportunities to set and achieve personal goals across multiple areas. There is no direct financial scholarship value associated with this program, though it provides valuable credentials and personal development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 7 to 10; Interest in personal development and challenge activities

overview: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is a Department of Education Western Australia financial assistance program available to eligible secondary students at Roleystone Community College. The scheme provides financial support to assist with school-related expenses for students and families experiencing financial hardship.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is a financial assistance program administered by the Department of Education Western Australia for students in secondary year levels. The allowance consists of two components: a Clothing Allowance of $115 paid directly to the parent/guardian or the school, and an Educational Program Allowance of $235 paid directly to the school. Applications are made by the parent or guardian for students enrolled in eligible year levels. Late applications will only be accepted in extenuating circumstances and must be accompanied with a written explanation. Eligible interstate or overseas students who are enrolled after first term may apply for the allowance. Applications close on 2 April 2026.
eligibility_criteria: Students enrolled in eligible year levels; Demonstrated financial need; Application required from parent or guardian; WA Department of Education eligibility requirements; School enrolment confirmation required
special_conditions: The allowance consists of two components: $115 Clothing Allowance paid directly to the parent/guardian or the school, and $235 Educational Program Allowance paid directly to the school. Personal information collected will only be used for the purpose of managing the SAS and will not be disclosed for any other reason. Late applications will only be accepted in extenuating circumstances and must be accompanied with a written explanation.

overview: Safety Bay Senior High School offers the Pathfinders Academic Excellence Program to challenge high-achieving students academically. The program is designed to build self-esteem, develop leadership skills, and prepare students for tertiary education. The top-ranked 32 students from the incoming Year 7 cohort are selected for this program annually.
description: The Pathfinders Academic Excellence Program is designed to challenge students academically, build self-esteem, build leadership skills and prepare students for tertiary education. Selection is based on Performance Achievement Test (PAT) testing conducted annually in Weeks 7-9 of Term 4. The program identifies and supports the top 32 students from each incoming Year 7 cohort. Students participate in accelerated and enriched coursework with a focus on developing critical thinking, leadership capabilities, and university preparation. The program operates for the full duration of secondary schooling from Year 7 onwards. There is no financial value or fee associated with this program; it is a merit-based selection process that provides enhanced academic opportunities and support.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 7 students entering the school; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Academic performance in top 20% nationally based on PAT testing; Selection occurs in Term 4 of the year prior to Year 7 entry
special_conditions: Selection is based on PAT testing results. Academic progress is monitored throughout the program. Students must maintain satisfactory academic performance and behaviour standards to remain in the program.

overview: Safety Bay Senior High School's Specialist Football Program is one of only three Department of Education-approved football specialist programs in Western Australia. The program combines sporting excellence, academic achievement and personal growth, offering elite professional coaching in a structured pathway towards careers in football. Students are selected through competitive trials held annually.
description: The Specialist Football Program provides an elite professional coaching environment for boys and girls, combining sporting excellence with academic achievement and personal growth. Selection trials are held once annually for current Year 6 students looking to enter Year 7. The program includes 4 x 1-hour practical coaching sessions during the school week, supplemented with practice matches, participation in the Champion Schools Soccer Program, club training and weekend matches. Students have access to FIFA-approved artificial turf facilities and a purpose-built fitness centre. The program has won eleven State Titles since its inception in 2002. Academic progress is monitored closely; students are expected to work consistently across all subjects to maintain high academic performance, behaviour and attendance standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated football ability and talent; Must pass selection trials; High academic achievement and good behaviour required; Commitment to both sporting and academic excellence
special_conditions: Selection trials held once annually. Registrations are essential to confirm place at trials. Students are expected to work consistently across all subjects to maintain high academic performance, behaviour and attendance standards. Participation in additional coaching, practice matches and weekend competition is required alongside school coaching sessions.

overview: Safety Bay Senior High School's Cheer-Dance Specialist Program is one of only two Western Australian schools offering a combined Cheerleading and Dance specialist program. The program develops strong and confident young people who strive for excellence, teaching valuable life lessons including integrity, work ethic, mutual respect, accountability, and commitment. Students are selected through competitive trials.
description: The Cheer-Dance Specialist Program is committed to developing strong and confident young people who strive for excellence and reach their full potential. Students participate in 2x Specialist Cheerleading (Physical Education) and 2x Specialist Dance (Performing Arts) classes weekly. The program teaches valuable life lessons such as integrity, work ethic, mutual respect, accountability, and a high level of commitment. Students participate in performances and competitions throughout the year and have the opportunity to travel and compete nationally. Elite training facilities include a 7-strip Cheerleading floor, 9m air track, and a Dance studio with sprung floor, mirrors and sound system. Trials are open to students currently in Years 6-10. Academic progress is important; students are expected to work consistently across all subjects to maintain high standards of academic performance, behaviour and attendance.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 6-10; Must pass selection trials; Demonstrated ability in cheerleading and/or dance; High academic achievement required; Good behaviour and attendance standards required
special_conditions: Trials are open to students currently in Years 6-10. Registrations are essential. Academic progress is monitored; students are expected to work consistently across all subjects. High standards of behaviour and attendance are required. Students must maintain commitment to both cheerleading and dance components.

overview: Safety Bay Senior High School's Special Music Program aims to nurture and develop students' passion for music with a focus on individual and group music lessons. The program welcomes students who have been learning instruments through the Department of Education's Instrumental Music Program in primary school, and also encourages students who wish to begin their musical journey. Selection is based on audition and demonstrated musical ability.
description: The Special Music Program aims to nurture and develop students' passion for music with focus on individual and group music lessons. Selection trials for current Year 6 students are held once annually; students are encouraged to register for trial appointments. The program includes weekly rehearsals after school for instrumental students who have attained the required level of playing. Students learn to read and use the language of music and utilise technology in developing, recording and performing their own compositions, arrangements and songs. Band or ensemble work enhances performance and group playing skills. Vocal ensemble participation develops essential musical skills including inner hearing and singing in tune. Performances are given at various times throughout the year. The program is open to Year 6 and 7 students who live within the school's local intake area or who are trying out for specialist Cheer-Dance or Football programs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 and Year 7 students; Must live within school's local intake area OR be trying out for Cheer-Dance or Football specialist programs; Demonstrated musical ability or experience; Must pass selection trial; Prior instrumental music learning preferred but not required
special_conditions: Selection trials for current Year 6 students are held once annually. Registrations are essential to confirm place at trials. Program is only open to current Year 6 students who live within local intake area or are trying out for specialist sports programs. Weekly rehearsals are held after school. Individual and group lessons are required.

program_name: Moorditj Djoowak Enrichment and Engagement Program
overview: Safety Bay Senior High School's Moorditj Djoowak Enrichment and Engagement Program is aligned with the school's Reconciliation Action Plan and supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The program provides cultural knowledge, pride, and engagement through activities, films, guest speakers, and encourages students to become voices for their community. It promotes resilience, educational achievement, and cultural respect across the entire school community.
description: The Moorditj Djoowak Enrichment and Engagement Program is dedicated to enriching the lives of all students and developing the school and curriculum culturally. The program provides Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students with knowledge and pride of culture that they can share with peers, friends and family. Students are encouraged to learn through activities, films and involvement with guest speakers, and encouraged to think outside the box and aim high to become voices of the future guiding Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. The program supports cultural respect and promotes resilience through sharing experiences. The program operates through the school's Reconciliation Plan and is embedded across the school community. There is no financial value associated with this program; it provides cultural support and engagement opportunities for Indigenous students. The program supports enhanced learning experiences and cultural development throughout the school year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; All year levels welcome; Open to both enrolled and prospective students; Participation is voluntary but encouraged
special_conditions: Program is embedded within the school's Reconciliation Action Plan. All students are welcomed; program specifically supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Students learn through activities, films, guest speakers and community involvement. Program operates throughout the school year.

program_name: Industry Links Vocational Education and Training Program
overview: Safety Bay Senior High School's Industry Links VET Program enables Year 10, 11 and 12 students to gain nationally recognised qualifications at Certificate II level or higher while completing their secondary education. The program includes school-based courses, structured work placements, and traineeships in fields such as hospitality, business, tourism, disability support, and the public sector. Students complete qualifications that contribute towards their WACE requirements.
description: The Industry Links Vocational Education and Training (VET) Program enables students to gain nationally recognised qualifications at Certificate II level or higher while completing their secondary education. All Year 11 and 12 students may choose to complete a recognised qualification as part of their senior school selection. Year 10 students wishing to join the program must select Industry Links through Subject Selection Online at the end of Term 2. Students will participate in school-based courses and have the opportunity to participate in structured work placements related to their chosen career pathway. Students have the option to complete a school-based traineeship which allows senior secondary students to start a traineeship while completing the WACE. Under these arrangements, students are both full-time students and part-time employees with the same employment and training responsibilities as other trainees. Traineeships are available in diverse fields including Hospitality, Banking, Tourism, Individual Disability Support and the Public Sector. VET qualifications provide students with essential skills transferable across multiple employment and education pathways. There is no financial cost; qualifications are pursued through partnership with TAFE.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 10, Year 11 and Year 12 students; Year 10 students must select through Subject Selection Online at end of Term 2; Willingness to pursue VET qualification; Interest in vocational career pathway; Subject to TAFE application requirements and approval
special_conditions: Students must complete TAFE application process and attend interview with Industry Links Program Coordinator. TAFE offers multiple application rounds; students may apply multiple times. Selection of General Studies as backup option recommended. Once accepted, students are enrolled at TAFE while maintaining full-time student status at Safety Bay SHS. Traineeships involve part-time employment alongside studies.

school_name: School Of Isolated and Distance Education
School of Special Educational Needs: Behaviour and Engagement
School of Special Educational Needs: Behaviour and Engagement - Midland Learning Academy
School Of Special Educational Needs: Medical And Mental Health

School Of Special Educational Needs: Medical And Mental Health - Pathways
School Of Special Educational Needs: Sensory
SMYL Community College - Beale Way Campus
SMYL Community College - Crompton Road Campus
SMYL Community College - Davey Street Campus
SMYL Community College - Seabrooke Way, Medina
South East Language Development Centre - Maddington Campus
South East Language Development Centre - Armadale Campus

overview: South Coast Baptist College offers Secondary Academic Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional academic capabilities. These scholarships recognise outstanding academic achievement and prepare successful applicants for the College's Gifted and Talented Education (GATE) Program.
description: Academic Scholarships at South Coast Baptist College are awarded to students demonstrating exceptional academic capabilities, who complete an ACER Scholarship Test as part of the selection process. Successful applicants will be placed in the College's Gifted and Talented Education (GATE) Program, which provides advanced curriculum with a focus on STEM skills. Scholarships are offered at either 75% or 50% of tuition fees. Applicants must demonstrate an A grade in their chosen field and submit a cover letter outlining reasons for applying, a portfolio with achievement details and references of support, and copies of the student's last 2 school reports and NAPLAN results. Applications for scholarships commencing in 2027 are now open.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating exceptional academic capabilities; Must demonstrate an A grade in their chosen field; Prospective secondary students entering Year 7; Current and prospective South Coast Baptist College students welcome; Good school citizenship required; ACER Scholarship Test completion required
special_conditions: ACER Scholarship Test will be hosted at South Coast Baptist College on Friday, February 27 2026. Applicants progressing to the next stage will be notified of the next steps thereafter. Successful applicants will be placed in the College's Gifted and Talented Education (GATE) Program.

overview: South Coast Baptist College offers Art & Cultural Scholarships to students who demonstrate exceptional musical ability. Successful applicants gain placement in the College's specialist Music Program or Vocal Academy.
description: Art & Cultural Scholarships are open to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability, who must complete a Live Audition as part of the selection process. Successful applicants will be placed in the College's specialist Music Program or Vocal Academy, which provides professional vocal training and musical education. Scholarships are offered at either 75% or 50% of tuition fees. Applicants must demonstrate an A grade in their chosen field and submit a cover letter outlining reasons for applying, a portfolio with achievement details and references of support, and copies of the student's last 2 school reports and NAPLAN results. Applications for scholarships commencing in 2027 are now open.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability; Must demonstrate an A grade in music; Current and prospective South Coast Baptist College students welcome; Good school citizenship required; Live Audition completion required
special_conditions: Applicants are required to complete a Live Audition. Successful applicants will be placed in the College's specialist Music Program or Vocal Academy. Applicants progressing to the next stage will be notified of the next steps thereafter.

overview: South Coast Baptist College offers Secondary Sporting Scholarships to students who demonstrate outstanding ability within one or more sports. These scholarships recognise athletic excellence and commitment to sporting achievement.
description: Sporting Scholarships are open to students demonstrating outstanding ability within one or more sports. Applicants must demonstrate an A grade in their chosen sport field and show high achievement and good school citizenship. Scholarships are offered at either 75% or 50% of tuition fees. Applicants must submit a cover letter outlining reasons for applying, a portfolio with achievement details and references of support, and copies of the student's last 2 school reports and NAPLAN results. Successful Football applicants will be placed in the College Football Academy. Applications for scholarships commencing in 2027 are now open. This scholarship has replaced the previous Football Academy Scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating outstanding ability in one or more sports; Must demonstrate an A grade in their chosen sport; Current and prospective South Coast Baptist College students welcome; Good school citizenship required; High achievement in nominated sport required
special_conditions: Successful Football applicants will be placed in the College Football Academy. The Football Academy Scholarship has been replaced by the Secondary Sporting Scholarship. Applicants progressing to the next stage will be notified of the next steps thereafter. College reserves the right to assess sporting ability through various means.

school_name: South West Community College - Bunbury Campus

overview: Southern River College students have access to scholarships offered through the WA Department of Education. These scholarships are administered by the Department and support secondary school students in Western Australia who demonstrate academic merit and/or financial need.
description: The WA Department of Education offers multiple scholarship programs to eligible secondary school students in Western Australia. These scholarships support students entering secondary school and are administered through an online application process. Applications open in mid-October each year and close in the third week of December, with selection occurring in February through April. Scholarships are judged by a selection committee after the school enrolment census at the end of February. Applicants are notified of outcomes in March or April, with cheques mailed during Term 2 to successful applicants. Financial value and specific eligibility vary by scholarship type. Most scholarships require students to attend a public school.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students who have Australian citizenship or permanent resident status; Must attend a public school in Western Australia; Currently in Years 6 to 11 and will be attending secondary school the following year; Must meet individual scholarship requirements which vary
special_conditions: Scholarships may be renewed for a second year providing academic and other assessment criteria have been met and the student remains enrolled at one of the eligible schools. Selection occurs after the school enrolment census at the end of February.

overview: Bendigo Bank offers scholarship programs through its Community Bank network and national programs to assist students in Australia. These scholarships target disadvantaged and community-minded students seeking to pursue higher education and are available to eligible secondary school students and tertiary applicants.
description: Bendigo Bank administers multiple scholarship programs across Australia through its Community Bank partners and national schemes. These include regional, rural, Indigenous, agricultural, and community-based scholarships. Scholarships are typically available for university, TAFE, and secondary school study, with financial values ranging from $3,000 to $6,500 per year depending on the scholarship type. Applications open annually between August and December, with varying deadlines across different programs. Scholarship offerings and eligibility criteria vary significantly by Community Bank location, so students should check with their local Community Bank branch for specific details.
eligibility_criteria: Varies by scholarship type and Community Bank location; Typically open to Australian citizens or permanent residents; Varies based on age, region, Indigenous status, or agricultural focus depending on scholarship type; First-time students often prioritized
special_conditions: Applications open at various times throughout the year between August and December. Scholarship offerings and eligibility criteria vary by Community Bank. Applicants should contact their local Community Bank for more information.

overview: The City of Gosnells offers scholarships to support local students in the Gosnells area. Southern River College students, as residents of the City of Gosnells, may be eligible to apply for these local community scholarships.
description: The City of Gosnells provides scholarship opportunities to support local students in the area. Specific details regarding the financial value, eligibility criteria, application process, and deadlines for these scholarships are not publicly detailed on the Southern River College website. Students are advised to contact the City of Gosnells directly or check the City's website for current scholarship information, including application procedures, selection criteria, and funding amounts.
eligibility_criteria: Likely requires residency in the City of Gosnells or surrounding area; Specific eligibility criteria not publicly stated on school website
special_conditions: No details available. Students should contact the City of Gosnells directly for comprehensive information about available scholarships.

school_name: South West Community College - Busselton campus
overview: South West Community College provides financial assistance to ensure no students are excluded due to inability to pay tuition fees. The college offers reduced payments for families holding a healthcare card and ensures equitable access to education regardless of financial circumstances.
description: South West Community College offers flexible tuition fee arrangements to support families facing financial hardship. The standard tuition fee is $250 per term ($1,000 per year across four terms). Families holding a healthcare card receive reduced payments on the standard tuition fee. The school's explicit commitment is that no students with inability to pay will be excluded from attending the college. This support acknowledges that accessibility and inclusivity are core values of the institution. The financial assistance is integrated into the school's overall approach to serving students who have disengaged from mainstream education and may face additional barriers to education.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolment at South West Community College Years 10-12; Families holding a current healthcare card eligible for reduced payments; No students with inability to pay excluded from enrolment
value_aud: $250 per term with reduced payments available
special_conditions: Reduced payments available for families with a current healthcare card. The college ensures no eligible student is excluded from enrolment due to financial hardship. Complimentary hoodie and polo shirt provided upon enrolment for all students.

overview: St Anthony's School offers a Health Care Card Discount Scheme for eligible families in Kindergarten to Year 6. This program provides automatic fee reductions on tuition and building levy fees for families who hold a valid Health Care Card. The discount recognises families experiencing financial hardship and supports access to Catholic education.
description: The Health Care Card Discount Scheme provides a reduction in tuition and building levy fees (not other fees) for eligible families. To qualify, families must hold a valid Australian Health Care Card in the name of the parent or guardian responsible for school fees, and the card must list the child's name. Eligible cardholders must complete a discount application form and return it to the school office. The discount applies to tuition and building levy only; amenity fees, excursion fees, technology fees, PE fees, and P&F fees are not included. A current Health Care Card must be provided each year; failure to do so results in fees being charged at full rate. Applications for the Health Care Card discount close on Friday 7 March annually. No specific dollar amount is stated; the discount percentage varies based on the HCC eligibility determination by government authorities.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Australian Health Care Card; Card must be in the name of parent/guardian responsible for school fees; Card must list the child's name; Enrolled in Kindergarten to Year 6 at St Anthony's School; Annual reapplication required with current card
year_levels: Kindergarten, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition and building levy fees only. Amenity fees, excursion and other fees, technology fees, PE fees, and P&F fees are not eligible for discount. Eligible cardholders must provide a current Health Care Card each year or the discount will be removed and fees charged at full rate. Applications close Friday 7 March annually.

program_name: Fee Arrangement and Financial Hardship Assistance
overview: St Anthony's School provides discretionary financial assistance to families experiencing difficulty paying fees. Families may apply to the Principal to discuss fee arrangements and payment plans. The school recognises that financial circumstances vary and works with families to ensure Catholic education remains accessible to all students regardless of ability to pay.
description: Families experiencing difficulties with school fees are invited to apply to the Principal for assistance. Within 14 days of receiving the school account, families should contact the school office to make an appointment with the Principal to discuss fee arrangements and explore payment options. Flexible payment arrangements can be negotiated on a case-by-case basis. The school emphasises that no student will be excluded from education due to inability to pay fees. Assistance may include adjusted payment schedules, extended payment terms, or other fee arrangements determined through discussion with the Principal. This is a needs-based discretionary program; the amount of assistance varies according to individual family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled student at St Anthony's School; Family experiencing financial difficulties with fee payment; Must contact school within 14 days of receiving school account; Must be willing to discuss arrangements with the Principal
special_conditions: Contact with school office must occur within 14 days of receiving school account. Arrangements must be agreed upon and honoured by the family. All accounts must be finalised by Friday 12 September annually unless other arrangements have been agreed with the Principal. Families are expected to meet agreed payment dates.

overview: St Andrew's Grammar offers a limited number of Scholarships for students demonstrating outstanding academic ability and excellence. Scholarships are open to both existing and prospective students who always strive to achieve their best and whose place at St Andrew's Grammar will benefit themselves, the School and the greater community.
description: St Andrew's Grammar offers Academic Scholarships through an ACER examination process designed and graded by the Australian Council of Educational Research. Prospective and existing students demonstrating outstanding academic ability and excellence may be eligible for consideration. Parents receive individual feedback about their child's performance on the examination. The School Board determines the number of Scholarships and the discount to be awarded on each Scholarship each year, with the value and duration varying annually based on academic performance and Board discretion. Registrations for the 2026 exam are now open, with the examination held around March each year.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11; Outstanding academic ability and excellence demonstrated; Prospective or existing students eligible; Candidate must strive to achieve their best; Placement at St Andrew's Grammar must benefit the student, the School and the greater community
special_conditions: School Board determines the number of Scholarships and the discount to be awarded on each Scholarship each year. Scholarships are limited in number. The examination cannot be studied for. Individual feedback is provided to parents about their child's performance.

overview: The Netball Academy at St Andrew's Grammar offers scholarships for eligible candidates who demonstrate exceptional talent and dedication to the sport. The academy provides a comprehensive program for aspiring netball players in Years 4-12 looking to enhance their skills and knowledge of the game.
description: St Andrew's Grammar operates a dedicated Netball Academy with scholarships available for eligible candidates who demonstrate exceptional talent and dedication to the sport. The academy provides a comprehensive program for aspiring netball players in year levels 4-12, offering first-class coaching from experienced professionals, opportunities to participate in various carnivals, and a supportive environment for players to thrive and reach their full potential. Admission to the academy is granted through a trial process. The exact financial value of scholarships is determined on an individual basis according to demonstrated talent and commitment.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Year 4 to Year 12; Exceptional talent in netball; Dedication to the sport demonstrated; Admission granted through trial process
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Admission to the academy is granted through a trial process. Scholarships are available for eligible candidates who demonstrate exceptional talent and dedication to the sport.

overview: St Andrew's Grammar is a Perth Glory Academy School offering opportunities for students to develop their soccer skills under professional coaching. The school hosts an annual Open Day with trials where talented players can impress coaches and develop their potential in this elite academy program.
description: St Andrew's Grammar is Perth's first Perth Glory Academy School, providing students with access to professional soccer coaching and mentorship. Talented players have the opportunity to wear professional uniforms when representing the school in various soccer competitions and new Perth Glory Academy competitions. The program identifies talent at various age groups and provides mentorship to help students succeed further in the sport. Students participate in rigorous training and competitive opportunities as part of this elite partnership. The school hosts an annual Open Day with trials where talented soccer players can demonstrate their abilities and join the academy.
eligibility_criteria: Students interested in soccer at advanced level; Demonstrated soccer talent or potential; Participation in annual trials required; Students must be able to commit to academy training and competitions
special_conditions: Annual Open Day with trials. Students selected wear professional Perth Glory uniforms. Partnership with Perth Glory provides enhanced coaching and competition opportunities. Program is ongoing and growing.

overview: St Brigid's College offers the Myrtle Scott-Tomlinson Scholarship to provide financial assistance to deserving students entering Years 7 and 10. The scholarship recognises exceptional academic achievement and potential.
description: The Myrtle Scott-Tomlinson Scholarship provides full tuition fees over three years for eligible recipients. The scholarship is designed to assist families and support students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to their education. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and demonstrate commitment to the College's Catholic values and mission. The scholarship applies to students entering Year 7 or Year 10 and covers tuition fees for the duration of the recipient's enrolment, subject to annual review and continued academic performance meeting College standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or Year 10; Academic excellence in primary school assessments; Commitment to College Catholic values; Australian residency; Demonstrated potential to maximise learning opportunities
special_conditions: Scholarship covers full tuition fees over three years. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress and demonstrate commitment to the College's Catholic faith and Mercy values. Scholarships are subject to annual review.

overview: St Brigid's College is a partner school with MADALAH, offering secondary education scholarships for Indigenous students from remote and regional communities. MADALAH scholarships support Indigenous students who demonstrate potential to succeed in secondary education.
description: MADALAH Limited is a not-for-profit organisation that offers secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Indigenous students, primarily from remote and regional communities, to Western Australia's leading boarding schools and Australian universities. MADALAH secondary scholarships provide support to students from Year 7 through Year 12. These scholarships are administered by MADALAH on behalf of the Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet. As a partner school, St Brigid's College welcomes MADALAH scholarship recipients and provides support for their academic, cultural and personal development within the College community.
