eligibility_criteria: Indigenous Australian (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent); Year 7 to Year 12 entry; From remote or regional communities; Demonstrate potential to succeed in secondary education; Part of MADALAH cohort selection process
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships administered by MADALAH on behalf of the Department of the Prime Minister and Cabinet. St Brigid's College is a partner school and enrolled Indigenous students may be eligible. For specific eligibility criteria and application information, visit www.madalah.com.au.

program_name: St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School Parish Scholarship
overview: St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School offers the Parish Scholarship to support new students from the Parish community who require financial assistance to attend the school. The scholarship recognises families who are committed to a Catholic education and supports them in accessing quality schooling at the primary level.
description: The St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School Parish Scholarship is designed to provide financial support to new students who are members of St Cecilia's or St John the Baptist Parish communities and require financial assistance. The scholarship covers either 100% or 50% of your child's tuition fees. Costs not covered by the scholarship include booklist, uniform, excursions/camps, swimming lessons, and P&F levies. These scholarships are funded by the generosity of the school and broader community and are available for the duration of a child's education at St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School. Awards are made at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only; Must be part of St Cecilia's or St John the Baptist Parish community; Must demonstrate financial need; Submissions must be accompanied by a letter of support from the parish priest
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships are available for the duration of a child's education at St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School. Awards are made at the Principal's discretion. Scholarships are funded by the generosity of the school and broader community.

overview: St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School partners with the Hanrine Foundation to offer scholarships to Indigenous students. The scholarship recognises and supports Indigenous families seeking a quality Catholic education for their children and assists in removing financial barriers to access.
description: The Hanrine Foundation offers scholarships to Indigenous students from St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School from 2025 to 2027. Two levels of support are available: Full Scholarship if no Land Group contributes to educational expenses, or Part Scholarship if a Land Group contributes but the amount is insufficient to cover expenses. The scholarship amount varies by year group. Only a limited number of scholarships are available each year subject to funding availability. Scholarships usually commence at the beginning of the school year; however, in exceptional circumstances and subject to funding may be provided partway through the school year. Scholarships only cover Part A (Part-Time) of Kindergarten fees. Parents must reapply each year by completing the Scholarship Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Indigenous students; Must be currently enrolled or commencing enrolment at St Cecilia's Catholic Primary School; Families must complete the Scholarship Application Form; Limited to partnership agreement period 2025-2027
special_conditions: Scholarships are valid for the duration of the 2025-2027 partnership agreement with Hanrine Foundation. Following this period, scholarship eligibility will be subject to continued funding from Hanrine Foundation. Parents must reapply each year by completing the Scholarship Application Form. Failure to rectify attendance or conduct issues without valid explanation could result in the scholarship being revoked. Scholarship amount varies in each year group.

overview: St Columba's School offers the Bursary to provide a Catholic education for students whose families do not have the means to access such an opportunity due to their family financial circumstances. The bursary was launched in 2019 to support families facing financial hardship.
description: The St Columba's School Bursary was established in 2019 to provide financial assistance to families who are unable to access a Catholic education due to financial circumstances. The bursary is awarded to students who meet the entrance requirements for the school. The application process requires families to complete a Bursary application form available on the school website, including details of financial circumstances and supporting documentation. The bursary is reviewed annually by a Bursary Committee and awards are shown as a deduction from tuition fees on the annual fee note.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all year levels; Family financial circumstances must demonstrate need; Student must meet school entrance requirements; Must be enrolled at St Columba's School
special_conditions: All bursaries are reviewed by the Bursary Committee on an annual basis. Bursary awards will be shown as a deduction from the tuition fees on the annual fee note. No actual payment will be made to the parent.

overview: St Columba's School offers the Aboriginal Bursary to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in accessing Catholic education at St Columba's. The bursary was launched in 2020 and continues support through secondary schooling at Chisholm College.
description: The Aboriginal Bursary was launched in 2020 to provide financial support to Aboriginal students attending St Columba's School. The bursary is specifically designed to support Indigenous students in accessing quality Catholic education. The bursary extends beyond primary school, with support continuing into secondary education at partner institution Chisholm College. The bursary represents the school's commitment to supporting Aboriginal families in the community and implementing the Mercy Values of the school's founding Sisters of Mercy.
eligibility_criteria: Must be an Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander student; Open to students at St Columba's School; Bursary support continues into secondary schooling at Chisholm College
special_conditions: Bursary support extends beyond St Columba's School into secondary education at Chisholm College. Annual review may apply.

school_name: St Francis of Assisi Catholic Primary School

program_name: Health Care Card Holder Fee Relief
overview: St Francis Xavier Primary School provides fee relief for families holding a Health Care Card. This financial assistance program recognises families experiencing financial difficulty and offers tuition fee discounts to support their children's education at the school.
description: St Francis Xavier Primary School offers fee relief for families who hold a current Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. Eligible families receive a discount on their tuition fees to reduce the financial burden of school costs. This program is designed to support families experiencing financial hardship by making education more accessible. Families must provide documentation of their Health Care Card status to the school's finance office. The discount applies to the standard tuition fees for enrolment from Kindergarten to Year 6. Contact the finance officer on 9923 5800 or admin@sfx.wa.edu.au for specific information about the discount amount and application process.
eligibility_criteria: Currently holding a valid Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Open to all enrolled or prospective students; Must provide card documentation to school
special_conditions: Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card holders must apply. Families experiencing financial hardship should contact the finance officer confidentially.

overview: St Francis Xavier Primary School offers bursaries specifically for Aboriginal families. This program provides financial assistance to support Aboriginal students' access to quality Catholic education at the school.
description: St Francis Xavier Primary School offers bursaries for Aboriginal families to support their children's education. These bursaries are designed to remove financial barriers to education for Indigenous students. The bursary program recognises the importance of providing equal educational opportunities for Aboriginal families in the Geraldton community. Eligible families should contact the finance officer to discuss their situation confidentially. The school works closely with Aboriginal families to ensure they receive appropriate support. For more information about the bursary application process and available support, contact the finance officer on 9923 5800 or email admin@sfx.wa.edu.au.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Currently enrolled or seeking enrolment at St Francis Xavier Primary School; Families should discuss financial circumstances with the finance officer
special_conditions: Applicants must be of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent. Bursaries are awarded based on demonstrated financial need and family circumstances. Confidential discussion with finance officer required.

overview: St Francis Xavier Primary School provides financial support arrangements for families experiencing genuine financial difficulties who do not qualify for Health Care Card discounts. The school works with families to provide immediate assistance with tuition costs.
description: For parents and caregivers who do not qualify for the Health Care Card discount but are experiencing financial difficulties, St Francis Xavier Primary School can arrange immediate support to assist with tuition costs. The school recognises the significant financial pressures facing families and seeks to keep fees as low as possible. Arrangements are handled with confidentiality and sensitivity. Families should contact the finance officer to discuss their specific circumstances and explore available support options. The school aims to ensure that no family is prevented from accessing quality Catholic education due to financial constraints. Contact the finance officer on 9923 5800 or admin@sfx.wa.edu.au to discuss support options.
eligibility_criteria: Experiencing genuine financial difficulties; Do not qualify for Health Care Card discount; Seeking immediate tuition cost support; Open to all students enrolled or seeking enrolment
special_conditions: Applications and discussions handled confidentially and sensitively. Flexible payment arrangements available. Contact with finance officer required to arrange support.

overview: St Francis Xavier Primary School offers a $100 family discount for students with a sibling attending Nagle Catholic College. This cross-institution discount program recognises families with multiple children in Catholic education.
description: St Francis Xavier Primary School has introduced a Kindergarten to Year 12 sibling discount program. If any of your children attending St Francis Xavier Primary School have a sibling currently at Nagle Catholic College, families are eligible for a $100 family discount off their school fees from St Francis. Additionally, families will receive a further $100 family discount off their school fees from Nagle Catholic College. This dual discount system supports families with children across both Catholic education institutions. The discount is automatically applied once sibling enrolment is confirmed at both schools. Families should inform the finance office of sibling enrolments to ensure discounts are correctly processed.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be enrolled at St Francis Xavier Primary School; Must have a sibling currently enrolled at Nagle Catholic College; Discount applies to both institutions
special_conditions: Sibling must be currently enrolled at Nagle Catholic College. Discount is $100 from St Francis Xavier and $100 from Nagle Catholic College. Automatically applied upon confirmation of sibling enrolment at both schools.

overview: St Francis Xavier Primary School offers a $200 credit incentive to existing families who successfully refer a new enrolment from outside of Catholic Education. This community engagement program supports school growth while rewarding families for advocacy.
description: St Francis Xavier Primary School offers a $200 referral incentive to existing families who successfully refer new enrolments to the school, particularly from outside Catholic education. The $200 credit is deducted directly from the referring family's own school fees. This initiative is designed to support the school's growth by leveraging the advocacy and recommendations of current families. By actively promoting the vibrant St Francis Xavier community and encouraging new families to consider joining the school, existing families support the school's capacity to maintain facilities and resources. The referral system rewards families for their support while strengthening the school community. Contact the school office for details on how to register referrals and claim the incentive.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a current family with enrolled students at St Francis Xavier Primary School; Successfully refer a new enrolment from outside Catholic Education; New family must complete full enrolment process
special_conditions: $200 credit is deducted from referring family's school fees upon successful new family enrolment. Referral must be from outside of Catholic Education sector. Credit applies only to existing families.

school_name: St George's Anglican Grammar School - Murray Street Campus
overview: St George's offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 7-11. These scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential as evidenced through the scholarship examination.
description: The Academic Scholarship is available to both current and new students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027. The scholarship recognises demonstrated academic achievement and potential as evidenced in the Edutest scholarship examination. All applicants must sit the Edutest scholarship examination and submit the St George's Scholarship Application Form along with their most recent school reports and NAPLAN results by 1 June 2026. The exact financial value of the scholarship is determined by the school on a case-by-case basis and described as part of the school's 'generous scholarship program'. The scholarship is awarded for the duration of enrolment at the school. Recipients must maintain academic standards and good conduct to retain the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027; Open to both current and new students of St George's; Demonstrated academic achievement and potential as evidenced in the Edutest scholarship examination; Must submit St George's Scholarship Application Form; Must provide two most recent school reports; Must provide NAPLAN results; Applications must be submitted by 1 June 2026
special_conditions: Scholarship testing held on Saturday 6 June 2026 at 9am. All students must arrive by 8:30am. Test is primarily online; students must bring their own charged laptop (Windows 10 or macOS 12 Monterey or later). Photo ID required. Renewal is subject to maintaining academic standards and good standing with the school.

school_name: St George's Anglican Grammar School - Murray Street Campus
overview: St George's offers General Excellence Scholarships to recognise outstanding achievement across multiple domains. These scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate excellence in academic achievement, music, science, visual arts, dance, drama, sport, and/or community service.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is open only to new students entering Year 10 in 2027. The scholarship is awarded to students who would contribute wholeheartedly to the St George's school community and embody the school's values of Wisdom, Grace and Service. Eligibility requires demonstrated academic achievement and potential as evidenced in the scholarship examination, excellence in one or more areas including music, science, visual arts, dance, drama, sport or community service, a high level of application to chosen activities inside and outside school, and leadership potential within their area of excellence. All applicants must sit the Edutest scholarship examination and submit the St George's Scholarship Application Form along with two most recent school reports and NAPLAN results by 1 June 2026. The exact financial value is determined individually and described as part of the school's generous scholarship program.
eligibility_criteria: Open only to new students entering Year 10 in 2027; Demonstrated academic achievement and potential as evidenced in the scholarship examination; Excellence in one or more of: Music, Science, Arts (visual arts, dance or drama), Sport, or Community Service; High level of application to chosen activity inside and outside of school; Leadership potential within demonstrated area of excellence; Must submit St George's Scholarship Application Form; Must provide two most recent school reports; Must provide NAPLAN results; Applications must be submitted by 1 June 2026
special_conditions: Scholarship testing held on Saturday 6 June 2026 at 9am. All students must arrive by 8:30am. Test is primarily online; students must bring their own charged laptop (Windows 10 or macOS 12 Monterey or later). Photo ID required. Renewal subject to maintaining standards and good standing with the school.

school_name: St George's Anglican Grammar School - Murray Street Campus
overview: St George's offers Music Scholarships to recognise outstanding musical achievement and potential. These scholarships are available to students demonstrating high standard instrumental performance and commitment to music.
description: The Music Scholarship is open only to new students entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027. Applicants must be studying at least one instrument to a high standard to be considered. The selection process requires applicants to play two short contrasting pieces of their choice on their chosen instrument. The exact financial value of the scholarship is individually determined and described as part of the school's generous scholarship program. Music is a key co-curricular focus at St George's, with the school offering an Advanced Music Program and diverse ensemble opportunities. Recipients are expected to participate actively in the school's music program. Renewal is contingent on continued high-level musical participation and good standing.
eligibility_criteria: Open only to new students entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027; Must be studying at least one instrument to a high standard; Must be able to perform two short contrasting pieces on chosen instrument; Must submit required audition materials; Applications must be submitted by 1 June 2026
special_conditions: Auditions held on Saturday 6 June 2026. Applicants must play two short contrasting pieces of their choice on their chosen instrument. Renewal subject to active participation in school music program and maintaining good standing. Recipients expected to contribute to school's music ensembles and performances.

school_name: St George's Anglican Grammar School - Murray Street Campus
program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship
overview: St George's offers Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarships to provide financial support and access to education for Indigenous students. These scholarships recognise the importance of Indigenous representation and participation in the school community.
description: The Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship is open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 in 2027. Up to two scholarships are offered annually, providing 50% remission of tuition fees. The scholarship is open to all Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students and is available to both new and current students of St George's Anglican Grammar School. Importantly, students applying for this scholarship are not required to undertake the Edutest scholarship examination. Instead, applications are made directly with the school via the St George's Scholarship Application Form, along with two most recent school reports and NAPLAN results, to be submitted by 1 June 2026. The 50% fee remission provides substantial financial assistance and is maintained for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school, subject to continued good standing.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 in 2027; Open to all Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students; Open to both new and current students of St George's; Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must submit St George's Scholarship Application Form; Must provide two most recent school reports; Must provide NAPLAN results; Applications must be submitted by 1 June 2026; No Edutest examination required
special_conditions: No Edutest examination required for this scholarship stream. Scholarship worth 50% remission of tuition fees annually. Limited to up to two awards per year. Renewal subject to maintaining good standing and positive engagement with the school community.

overview: St James' Anglican School offers the Allan Griffiths Scholarship to recognise students with outstanding performance in non-academic fields. The scholarship honours Mr Allan Griffiths, founding Chair of School Council, and supports students who demonstrate exceptional talent in music, performing arts, or sports while maintaining academic commitment.
description: The Allan Griffiths Scholarship is awarded to students entering Year 7 to Year 11 who demonstrate outstanding performance in at least one non-academic field, such as music, performing arts, or sports. This may be evidenced by inclusion in a State squad or team, or through several awards for excellence in a particular field. The scholarship provides fee remission as determined by the Principal. The successful applicant is expected to work hard and show leadership throughout their time at school. Selection is at the discretion of the Principal following shortlisting and interview. Recipients are expected to contribute to the school community and maintain their achievements across all school areas.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7 to Year 11; Demonstrated outstanding performance in at least one non-academic field (music, performing arts, or sports); Evidence of State squad/team inclusion or multiple awards for excellence; Willingness to show leadership and contribute to school community
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants will be contacted to attend an interview with the Principal. Parents will have the opportunity to tour school facilities and meet with Senior School staff and student leaders. The scholarship is named in honour of Mr Allan Griffiths, first Chair of School Council of St James' Anglican School.

overview: St James' Anglican School offers the Indigenous Scholarship program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The School Council endorsed this scholarship to enable Indigenous students to enrol and experience the full range of opportunities available at SJAS.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship program is available for students currently in Year 6 to Year 11 who are Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander. The School Council has endorsed with enthusiasm the formation of this scholarship program to enable Indigenous students to enrol at the School and experience the full range of opportunities that an SJAS education can provide. The scholarship provides fee remission to support access to education. Selection is at the discretion of the Principal following application and interview assessment. Recipients are expected to contribute positively to the school community and maintain their engagement with school life.
eligibility_criteria: Indigenous students (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander); Currently in Year 6 to Year 11; Evidence of being Australian Indigenous required; External students eligible
special_conditions: Evidence of being Australian Indigenous (Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander) must be provided. The School Council endorsed the formation of this program with enthusiasm to support Indigenous students' access to education. Selection is at the discretion of the Principal.

overview: St James' Anglican School offers means-tested bursaries to support families unable to afford tuition fees. These bursaries are specifically set aside for external students commencing in Year 7 or Year 8 and continue throughout their schooling at the school.
description: St James' Anglican School offers means-tested bursaries for students who commence in Year 7 2025 and/or Year 8 2025. These bursaries are available to external students only and are specifically set aside for families and students who would otherwise be unable to afford the tuition fees and attend St James' Anglican School. Awarded bursaries will run for the entire duration of the recipient's remaining schooling at St James' Anglican School up to and including Year 12, subject to an annual review. Applications for a bursary can be submitted at any time during the school year. The amount of fee remission is determined based on demonstrated financial need through formal financial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: External students only; Commencing in Year 7 or Year 8; Families unable to afford tuition fees; Must demonstrate genuine financial need; At least 12 months attendance required for fee remission eligibility
special_conditions: Available to external students only. Awarded bursaries run for the entire duration of remaining schooling at SJAS up to Year 12, subject to annual review. Applications can be submitted at any time during the school year. Financial information must be provided to the Business Manager to establish financial need. Only students who have been in attendance for at least 12 months are eligible to apply for fee remission.

school_name: St John Paul II Catholic Primary School

overview: St John's School offers a Sibling Discount for families enrolling multiple children. The discount recognises the financial support needed by families with siblings attending the school and seeks to make quality Catholic education more affordable.
description: St John's School has introduced a Kindergarten to Year 12 Sibling Discount for school fees to support families with multiple children enrolling at the school or at Catholic schools within the Diocese of Geraldton. This discount acknowledges the financial demands placed on families and forms part of the school's commitment to keeping fees as low as possible while providing quality Catholic education. The specific percentage or amount of the discount is not detailed on the school website and families are advised to discuss this directly with the school. Families are encouraged to contact the school administration for current details on the discount structure and eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Family must have two or more children enrolled at St John's School or other Catholic schools in the Diocese of Geraldton; Current sibling(s) must be enrolled students at the school
special_conditions: Discount available for families with two or more children. The specific discount structure and amount should be confirmed directly with the school administration.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls - Chidley Campus
overview: St Hilda's offers Academic and General Excellence Scholarships to girls who are committed to their studies and who consistently strive to do their best. These scholarships recognise students who thrive academically and also shine in a particular field, whether that is Music, Sport, Dance, Drama or community service.
description: The Academic and General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to girls who demonstrate exceptional academic ability combined with achievement in a co-curricular field. These scholarships recognise students who excel both in the classroom and in areas such as Music, Sport, Dance, Drama or community service. Applicants must sit a series of tests administered by AAS at St Hilda's. The scholarships are tenable for the duration of a girl's education at St Hilda's, subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. The exact financial benefit and duration are subject to individual assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Demonstrated academic excellence; Achievement in at least one co-curricular field including Music, Sport, Dance, Drama or community service; Satisfactory conduct and contribution to school life required
special_conditions: 2027 Academic and General Excellence Scholarships have now closed. All Scholarships are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Your daughter's enrolment status will not impact the decision to award her a scholarship. Scholarships may be awarded to girls who have already confirmed their place at the school for the future, and to those who have not applied.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls - Chidley Campus
overview: St Hilda's Boarding Scholarships are awarded to girls from rural, regional and remote Western Australia who demonstrate commitment, resilience and a genuine desire to make the most of every opportunity. Boarders become part of a welcoming community where they are supported academically, socially and personally.
description: Boarding Scholarships provide meaningful support to students from rural, regional and remote Western Australia in their pursuit of excellence. These scholarships recognise girls who demonstrate commitment, resilience and a genuine desire to make the most of every educational opportunity. Recipients become part of a welcoming boarding community supported academically, socially and personally. The scholarships are tenable for the duration of enrolment. Applicants must demonstrate both academic ability and strong capacity for involvement in school life. Selection involves written assessments and evaluation.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from rural, regional and remote Western Australia; Seeking to board at St Hilda's; Must demonstrate commitment, resilience and desire for educational excellence; Satisfactory academic performance and conduct required
special_conditions: 2027 Boarding Scholarships have now closed. Scholarships are subject to annual review. All Scholarships and Bursaries are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls - Chidley Campus
overview: St Hilda's is committed to providing educational opportunities for West Australian Indigenous girls with scholarships available for students commencing in Year 7 and continuing through to the completion of their schooling. These scholarships provide exceptional educational opportunities for Indigenous girls with the potential to lead and inspire within their communities.
description: Indigenous Scholarships are designed to support West Australian Indigenous girls from Year 7 through to Year 12. Each scholarship is offered in partnership with MADALAH or Yalari, reflecting the school's commitment to fostering belonging, supporting aspirations and creating pathways for students to thrive within the school community. The scholarships, together with Abstudy and parent contributions, cover tuition and boarding fees from Year 7 through to Year 12. Applications are assessed through a selection process that evaluates leadership potential and demonstrated ability.
eligibility_criteria: Identified as First Nations Indigenous Australian; West Australian residency; Commencing Year 7; Able to continue through to Year 12; Partnership requirement with MADALAH or Yalari; Satisfactory conduct and academic engagement required
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered in partnership with MADALAH or Yalari. Funding includes Abstudy contributions and parent contributions. Tenable from Year 7 through to Year 12 completion. Exclusive to First Nations Indigenous girls from Western Australia.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls - Chidley Campus
overview: The Old Scholars Association (OSA) Entrance Scholarship is awarded to a student entering Year 7 whose mother, grandmother or great-grandmother is a financial member of the OSA. The scholarship is intended for a girl who can demonstrate academic ability and a strong capacity for involvement in school life.
description: The OSA Entrance Scholarship is one of the most significant contributions of the Old Scholars Association to St Hilda's. It supports students with demonstrated academic ability and strong involvement in school activities. The scholarship is tenable for Years 7 to 10, subject to satisfactory academic progress and contribution to the school. It is offered as a fixed financial amount each year, approximating one term of fees. The scholarship recognises both academic excellence and well-rounded engagement with school life.
eligibility_criteria: Daughters, granddaughters or great-granddaughters of financial OSA members; Entering Year 7; Demonstrated academic ability; Strong capacity for involvement in school life; Satisfactory academic progress and school contribution required for continuation
special_conditions: 2027 OSA Scholarships have now closed. Tenable for Years 7 to 10 only, subject to satisfactory academic progress and contribution to the School. Incomplete or late applications will not be considered. Applicant must have a mother, grandmother or great-grandmother who is a financial member of the Old Scholars Association.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls - Chidley Campus
overview: St Hilda's offers Needs-Based Bursaries to girls from low socio-economic backgrounds and rural areas who have the potential to benefit from a St Hilda's education. These bursaries are committed to providing young women from all backgrounds with the opportunity to shine, regardless of their financial circumstances.
description: Needs-Based Bursaries are available to students not currently attending St Hilda's who require financial assistance to pursue their educational aspirations. The school is committed to providing young women from all backgrounds the opportunity to excel, regardless of financial circumstances. Bursaries support girls from low socio-economic backgrounds, rural areas, and those who identify as First Nations. Recipients receive not only academic education but also support in transitioning to school, accessing co-curricular opportunities and building community networks. All students participate in the Year 7 Belonging course focused on well-being and school values.
eligibility_criteria: Not currently attending St Hilda's; Demonstrated financial need; Low socio-economic background or rural residency; May include First Nations applicants; Must demonstrate potential and desire for educational excellence; Satisfactory conduct required
special_conditions: Needs-based bursaries are excluded from paying the standard application fee. Applications assessed based on demonstrated financial need and potential. School provides additional support including transition programs, mentoring, and access to co-curricular programs. These bursaries change lives and are funded through the Ignite a Spark giving campaign and school philanthropy.

overview: St George's Anglican Grammar School offers Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Years 7-11. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning as evidenced by performance on the Edutest scholarship examination.
description: The Academic Scholarship is available to both current and new students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027. All Academic Scholarship applicants are required to sit the Edutest scholarship examination on Saturday 6 June 2026. Selection is based on demonstrated academic achievement and potential as evidenced in the scholarship examination. Applicants must also submit the St George's Scholarship Application Form, together with their most recent school reports and NAPLAN results, by Monday 1 June 2026. The school offers these scholarships as part of its generous scholarship program to encourage excellence in academics. While the exact financial remission value is not publicly stated, the school describes its scholarship program as 'generous' and indicates partial or full remission of tuition fees based on merit.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10 and 11 in 2027; Open to both current students and new students to St George's; Must submit school reports and NAPLAN results; Must sit Edutest scholarship examination
special_conditions: Applicants must provide two most recent school reports and NAPLAN results. Edutest scholarship examination conducted online at school location (441 Murray Street) starting at 9:00am on Saturday 6 June 2026. Students must bring own charged laptop (Windows 10 or macOS 12 Monterey or later) and photo ID. Scholarships are awarded at the school's discretion based on demonstrated academic potential.

overview: St George's Anglican Grammar School offers General Excellence Scholarships to award students who would contribute wholeheartedly to the school community. The scholarship recognises students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement combined with excellence in music, science, arts, sport or community service.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is open to new students entering Year 10 in 2027. Recipients are awarded to students who would contribute wholeheartedly to the St George's school community and embody the school's values. Applicants must demonstrate: (1) demonstrated academic achievement and potential as evidenced in the scholarship examination; (2) excellence in one or more of music, science, visual arts, dance, drama, sport and/or community service; (3) high level of application to their chosen activity inside and outside of school; (4) leadership potential within their demonstrated area of excellence. All applicants are required to sit the Edutest scholarship examination on Saturday 6 June 2026 and submit the St George's Scholarship Application Form with two most recent school reports and NAPLAN results by Monday 1 June 2026. The financial remission value is not publicly specified.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only entering Year 10 in 2027; Demonstrated academic achievement and potential; Excellence in music, science, arts (visual arts, dance, drama), sport and/or community service; High level of application to chosen activity both inside and outside school; Leadership potential within demonstrated area of excellence; Must sit Edutest scholarship examination; Must submit school reports and NAPLAN results
special_conditions: Open to new students entering Year 10 only. Applicants must sit Edutest scholarship examination on Saturday 6 June 2026 at 9:00am at 441 Murray Street. Must provide two most recent school reports and NAPLAN results. Scholarships awarded at discretion of school council based on overall contribution to school community. Students must bring own charged laptop (Windows 10 or macOS 12 Monterey or later) and photo ID.

overview: St George's Anglican Grammar School offers Music Scholarships to recognise students with excellence in instrumental or vocal performance. The scholarship is available to new students entering Year 7 and Year 10 who demonstrate high-standard proficiency in at least one musical instrument.
description: Music Scholarships are available for new students entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027. Applicants must be studying at least one instrument to a high standard to be considered for the Music Scholarship. Candidates are required to play two short contrasting pieces of their choice on their chosen instrument. Vocal Music Scholarships are also available as part of the Music Scholarship Program. All Music Scholarship applicants are required to sit the Edutest scholarship examination on Saturday 6 June 2026 at 9:00am and must apply in writing on the St George's Scholarship Application Form. Applicants must submit their most recent school reports and NAPLAN results by Monday 1 June 2026. The school's Advanced Music Program (AMP) for Years 7-9 supports the development of music excellence. The exact financial remission value is not publicly specified on the main page.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students only entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027; Must be studying at least one instrument to high standard; Required to play two short contrasting pieces on chosen instrument; Vocal Music Scholarships also available; Must sit Edutest scholarship examination; Must submit school reports and NAPLAN results
special_conditions: Applicants must bring scores and prepared musical pieces for audition/performance. Edutest scholarship examination conducted online at 441 Murray Street starting 9:00am on Saturday 6 June 2026. Students must bring own charged laptop (Windows 10 or macOS 12 Monterey or later) and photo ID. The school supports music excellence through its Advanced Music Program (AMP) for Years 7-9. Scholarships awarded at discretion of school based on demonstrated musical proficiency and potential.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship
overview: St George's Anglican Grammar School offers Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarships to support Indigenous students with significant financial assistance. The scholarship provides up to two students annually with 50% remission of tuition fees.
description: The Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Scholarship is open to Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students entering Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 in 2027. This scholarship offers up to two students 50% remission of tuition fees annually. The scholarship is open to both new and current students of St George's Anglican Grammar School. Unlike other scholarships, students applying for this scholarship are not required to undertake the Edutest scholarship examination. Applications are made directly with the school via the St George's Scholarship Application Form, together with their two most recent school reports and NAPLAN results, by Monday 1 June 2026. This scholarship recognises the school's commitment to Indigenous inclusion and provides substantial financial support to eligible students.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students; Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10 in 2027; Open to both new and current students; No scholarship examination required; Must submit school reports and NAPLAN results; Up to two scholarships available annually
special_conditions: Limited to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Up to two scholarships awarded annually. Students are not required to sit Edutest examination. Provides 50% remission of tuition fees annually. Applications submitted directly to school via Scholarship Application Form. Selection based on application materials including school reports and NAPLAN results.

overview: St Jerome's Primary School offers a Health Care Card Tuition Discount for families holding current Health Care Cards or Pension Concession Cards. This program provides tuition fee reduction support to eligible families experiencing financial hardship.
description: Parents holding a current Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card may be eligible to apply for a Health Card Tuition Discount on their tuition fees. Eligible families must ensure a current copy of their card is held at the school office at all times. The discount is applied to the tuition component of school fees. Concession card holders must maintain current documentation with the school. Application and eligibility assessment are conducted by the school administration upon enrolment or during the year.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian holds current Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card; Copy of current concession card must be provided to school office; Must be maintained throughout enrolment period
special_conditions: Concession card holders must ensure a current copy of their card is held at the school office at all times. This is a means-tested assistance program available to families demonstrating financial hardship through government concession card eligibility.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls
overview: St Hilda's Academic and General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to girls who are committed to their studies and who consistently strive to do their best. These scholarships recognise students who thrive academically and also shine in a particular field, whether that is Music, Sport, Dance, Drama or community service.
description: The Academic and General Excellence Scholarship programme at St Hilda's recognises girls demonstrating exceptional commitment to academic excellence combined with strength in Music, Sport, Dance, Drama or community service. These scholarships will vary in value and will cover up to 50% of the tuition fees. All Academic and General Excellence Scholarships are tenable for the duration of a girl's education at St Hilda's, provided the recipient maintains satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Applicants will be invited to sit a series of tests conducted by AAS (Academic Assessment Services) at St Hilda's. Remote and overseas testing applicants will be contacted by AAS to confirm testing details. Scholarship recipients must demonstrate sustained academic achievement and engagement in their nominated field of excellence throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate academic excellence in top percentile; Must show excellence in Music, Sport, Dance, Drama or community service; Must maintain satisfactory academic performance and conduct throughout enrolment; Scholarship subject to annual review based on academic progress and contribution to school life
special_conditions: All Scholarships and Bursaries are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Applicants with special circumstances (illness with doctor's certificate or school camp attendance) must notify their first-preference school immediately. Additional online testing fee of $150.00 applies for special circumstances testing. Remote testing available for applicants living more than 400km away. Enrolment status does not impact scholarship decision. Scholarships may be awarded to girls who have already confirmed their place and to those who have not applied.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls
overview: St Hilda's offers Boarding Scholarships to girls who are ready to thrive in a nurturing boarding environment and embrace all that the school has to offer. These scholarships support students from Years 7 onwards to access the unique boarding experience at the school.
description: The Boarding Scholarship at St Hilda's provides financial assistance to girls entering the Senior School who demonstrate the potential to excel in a boarding environment. These scholarships will vary in value and will cover up to 50% of the tuition fees. Boarding Scholarships are tenable for the duration of a girl's education at St Hilda's, subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Selection requires applicants to sit a series of tests conducted by AAS (Academic Assessment Services) at St Hilda's. The scholarship recognises girls who can thrive academically and who show strong capacity for involvement in boarding house life and the broader school community. Renewal is subject to continued satisfactory performance and engagement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7 or continuing students applying for boarding; Must demonstrate academic ability; Must show capacity for involvement in boarding house life and school community; Must maintain satisfactory academic progress and contribution to the School; Boarders accepted from Year 7 onwards
special_conditions: All Scholarships and Bursaries are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Additional online testing fee of $150.00 applies for special circumstances testing. Remote testing available for applicants living more than 400km away. Enrolment status does not impact scholarship decision. Scholarship recipients must support the boarding community ethos.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls
overview: The OSA Entrance Scholarship is awarded annually to a student entering Senior School in Year 7 whose mother, grandmother or great-grandmother is a financial member of the Old Scholars Association. The scholarship is intended for a girl who can demonstrate academic ability and a strong capacity for involvement in school life.
description: The Old Scholars Association awards a scholarship to a student entering the Senior School in Year 7 whose mother, grandmother or great-grandmother is a financial member of the Association. The OSA Entrance Scholarship is intended for a girl who can demonstrate academic ability and a strong capacity for involvement in school life. The scholarship is offered as a fixed financial amount each year, approximating one term of fees. The scholarship is tenable for Years 7 to 10, subject to satisfactory academic progress and contribution to the School. Incomplete or late applications will not be considered.
eligibility_criteria: Daughters, granddaughters and great-granddaughters of financial OSA members only; Open to students entering Year 7; Must demonstrate academic ability; Must show strong capacity for involvement in school life; Must maintain satisfactory academic progress and contribution to the School; Limited to family members of OSA financial members
special_conditions: Tenable for Years 7-10 only. Applications must be received by the closing date; incomplete or late applications will not be considered. Scholarship subject to satisfactory academic progress and contribution to the School.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls
overview: The OSA Ammonite Scholarship provides support in Years 11 and 12 to daughters, granddaughters or great-granddaughters of financial OSA members. The scholarship recognises a girl who is an all-rounder, rather than selecting a winner based solely on academic achievement.
description: The OSA Ammonite Scholarship provides financial support to a student in Years 11 and 12 who is a daughter, granddaughter or great-granddaughter of a financial member of the Old Scholars Association. This scholarship is a fixed financial amount per annum that approximates one term's fees per year. The scholarship recognises an all-rounder, valuing academic achievement alongside contributions to the broader school community, leadership, co-curricular participation and personal qualities. Selection is determined by a panel of three Financial Members of the OSA based on a written application followed by an interview. The scholarship supports girls who demonstrate excellence across multiple dimensions rather than specialisation in a single area.
eligibility_criteria: Daughters, granddaughters and great-granddaughters of financial OSA members only; Open to students entering Year 11; Must demonstrate all-rounder qualities across academics and co-curricular activities; Selected based on written application and interview process; Must maintain satisfactory conduct and contribution to school life
special_conditions: Tenable for Years 11 and 12. Selection based on written application and panel interview with three Financial Members of OSA. Recognises all-rounder excellence rather than single-area specialisation. Scholarship subject to satisfactory academic performance and contribution to school life.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls
overview: St Hilda's is committed to providing educational opportunities for West Australian Indigenous girls, with scholarships available for students commencing in Year 7 and continuing through to the completion of their schooling. These scholarships aim to provide exceptional educational opportunities for Indigenous girls with the potential to lead and inspire within their communities.
description: St Hilda's offers Indigenous Scholarships in partnership with MADALAH or Yalari to support West Australian Indigenous girls. Each Indigenous Scholarship is offered in partnership with either MADALAH or Yalari, depending on the student's location and circumstances. The scholarships, together with Abstudy and parent contributions, cover tuition and boarding fees from Year 7 through to Year 12. These scholarships provide comprehensive financial support enabling Indigenous girls to access quality education at St Hilda's and develop into leaders within their communities. Recipients must demonstrate academic potential and a commitment to making positive contributions to their communities. Financial support extends throughout the student's entire secondary schooling journey at the school.
eligibility_criteria: West Australian Indigenous girls only; Open to students commencing in Year 7; Must continue through to Year 12 completion; Partnerships with MADALAH or Yalari (location dependent); Must demonstrate academic potential; Must show commitment to community leadership; Combined support through scholarship, Abstudy and parent contributions
special_conditions: Needs-based bursaries and Indigenous Scholarships are excluded from paying application fees. Scholarships offered in partnership with MADALAH or Yalari. Combined funding includes Abstudy assistance and parent contributions to cover tuition and boarding fees from Year 7 to Year 12. All Scholarships and Bursaries are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life.

school_name: St Hilda's Anglican School for Girls
overview: St Hilda's offers needs-based bursaries to provide educational opportunity to girls who would not otherwise have the opportunity to attend due to financial means. These bursaries are fundamental to the school's commitment to accessibility and equity in education.
description: St Hilda's Needs-Based Bursaries provide financial assistance to girls who demonstrate genuine financial need and have the potential to thrive at the school. The bursaries support both day and boarding students, with the school aiming to raise funding for multiple recipients each year. Bursary recipients are selected based on financial assessment and demonstrated potential. The school encourages donors to consider pledging recurring donations over six years to ensure the sustainability of these bursaries. All Needs-Based Bursaries are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Bursary applications are excluded from paying the standard application fee, ensuring accessibility for families in financial hardship.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated financial need as primary criterion; Girls with potential to thrive academically and in school life; Open to both day and boarding students; Families unable to afford fees due to financial hardship; Application assessment considers family financial circumstances; Must maintain satisfactory conduct and contribution to school life
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Needs-based bursaries are excluded from paying application fees. Applications open and closing dates vary by year and are available in the scholarship table on the website. All Scholarships and Bursaries are subject to satisfactory academic performance, conduct and contribution to school life. Families are encouraged to consider pledge donations over six-year periods. Support available for both day and boarding students.

overview: St Joseph's College provides financial hardship support to eligible families experiencing genuine difficulty meeting tuition and boarding fees. The program assists families to access quality Catholic education at the College.
description: St Joseph's College offers financial hardship assistance to families who demonstrate genuine financial need. The program aims to support new and continuing students who would otherwise be unable to attend the College due to financial circumstances. Families can apply by contacting the College administration to discuss their specific situation. The amount of assistance varies based on individual circumstances and available funding. Applications should be made directly to the College office for assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating genuine financial hardship; Open to new and continuing students; Assessed on individual circumstances; Requires documentation of financial need
special_conditions: Assessed on individual family circumstances and documented financial need. Families should contact the College directly to discuss eligibility and the application process.

program_name: Boarding Support - Living Away from Home Allowance
overview: St Joseph's College assists eligible regional students with boarding costs through government living away from home allowances. The College partners with the Albany Residential College to support students from remote and rural areas.
description: St Joseph's College students who reside more than 56 kilometres from the nearest government secondary school may be eligible to apply for a living away from home allowance. This government allowance covers costs associated with boarding accommodation. Students can board at the Albany Residential College and access public transport to attend classes. The amount of allowance and eligibility criteria are determined by government funding policies. Families should contact the Albany Residential College directly for eligibility assessment and application procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Reside more than 56 kilometres from nearest government secondary school; Must apply directly to Albany Residential College for assessment; Current or prospective student of St Joseph's College
special_conditions: Eligibility assessed by Albany Residential College based on residential distance criteria. Applications must be made directly to the Residential College, not the school.

program_name: Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme
overview: St Joseph's School offers the Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme to support families experiencing financial hardship. This program provides tuition fee assistance to families who hold a valid Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card.
description: St Joseph's School provides tuition fee discount assistance through the Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme. Eligible families who hold a valid Health Care Card (including Pensioner Concession Card coded PPS) can access reduced tuition fees by completing a simple form available at the school office. Card holders whose cards are due to expire prior to the end of term must show their new card once issued for discounts to continue. The discount amount and specific fee reduction details are available from the school office. This is an ongoing assistance program for qualifying families throughout their child's enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold a valid Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card (code PPS); Must complete a simple form available at school office; Card must be current and valid
special_conditions: Card holders whose cards are due to expire prior to the end of term must show their new card once issued for discounts to continue.

overview: St Dominic's School offers a Year 6 Scholarship to recognise outstanding academic achievement in students currently enrolled in Year 5. The successful recipient will receive full fee remission for their Year 6 studies in 2026.
description: A scholarship is available to students currently enrolled in Year 5 at St Dominic's School, recognising their academic excellence and commitment to learning. The successful recipient will receive a 100% remission on the tuition fee associated with Year 6 in 2026. To apply for this scholarship, students are required to complete an application form. The scholarship selection process involves reviewing the application form submissions. This award supports one student per year level and is offered on a competitive basis.
eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 5 at St Dominic's School in 2025; Open to all students at the school; Academic performance excellence required
special_conditions: The scholarship applies specifically to Year 6 tuition fees in 2026. Recipients must remain enrolled at the school to maintain the scholarship benefit.

program_name: Health Care Card Fee Discount Scheme
overview: St Joseph's School offers fee discounts for families who hold eligible concession or health care cards. This CEWA-administered scheme provides financial support to assist eligible low-income families access Catholic education.
description: St Joseph's School, as part of the Catholic Education Western Australia system, provides fee discounts to families who hold a current means-tested family Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Department of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card (Blue Card). The discount applies to tuition fees and specified amenity fees (part of amenities fees, IT fee, and building levy). Families must present their valid current card to the school to benefit from the scheme. No additional financial information is requested from eligible card holders, as the income test has already been performed by Centrelink. The discount applies on a pro-rata basis if the card expires during the school year. Application and enrolment fees are not covered by the scheme, however families experiencing financial hardship may apply to the Principal for discretionary assistance with these additional charges.
eligibility_criteria: Parents or guardians responsible for payment of school fees; Must hold current means-tested family Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Department of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card (Blue card only); Must present valid card to school; Open to all year levels from Pre-Kindergarten to Year 6
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees and part of amenity fees (IT fee and building levy) only. Application fees and enrolment fees are not covered by this scheme but may be subject to principal discretion for hardship cases. Discount is applied on a pro-rata basis if the card expires during the school year. Families must maintain current card status throughout the year to retain the discount.

program_name: St Jude's Catholic School Scholarship Program
overview: St Jude's Catholic School offers a Scholarship Program to support families who are active parishioners of St Jude's Parish. The program recognises the importance of Catholic education and supports eligible families in accessing the school.
description: St Jude's Catholic School Scholarship Program is available for children who are active parishioners of St Jude's Parish. The scholarship program is designed to make Catholic education more accessible to parish families. Applicants must download the application form or contact the School Office on 6350 2500 for more information. Specific details regarding the financial value of the scholarship, whether it offers full or partial fee reduction, and the exact selection process were not publicly available on the school website at the time of extraction. The program appears to be an ongoing offering for qualifying parishioner families.
eligibility_criteria: Active parishioner of St Jude's Parish; Must contact school office for specific eligibility requirements
special_conditions: Open only to children who are active parishioners of St Jude's Parish. Application form must be downloaded or requested from the school office.

overview: St Kieran Catholic Primary School offers an automatic sibling discount to families with multiple children enrolled at the school. The discount applies to the tuition component of school fees for each subsequent child in the family.
description: Families with siblings enrolled and currently attending Year 4 Old Kindergarten to Year 6 at St Kieran Catholic Primary School are automatically entitled to a discount on the tuition component of the children's school fees. The discount is automatically deducted for each subsequent child from the tuition fee on the same tuition statement. This program recognises the financial commitment of families with multiple children and aims to make Catholic education more accessible. There is no application process required—the discount is automatically applied upon enrolment of multiple siblings.
eligibility_criteria: Multiple children enrolled and currently attending Year 4 Old Kindergarten to Year 6 at St Kieran Catholic Primary School; Children must be in the same family
special_conditions: Discount is automatically deducted on the same tuition statement. Applies to tuition component of fees only.

overview: St Kieran Catholic Primary School provides automatic fee concessions for families holding eligible government concession cards. This program assists families experiencing financial hardship to access Catholic education.
description: Parents and/or guardians with children in Year 4 Old Kindergarten to Year 6 with an eligible means tested Family Concession Card (health care card or pensioner concession card) have an automatic entitlement to a fee concession. The Card must be in the parent or guardian's name and the child(ren) must be listed on the card to be eligible. Concession card discounts are applied on a pro rata basis for newly issued or cancelled cards. The current discount includes a discounted tuition fee and building levy, and the waiving of the amenities fee, IT Levy and Kieran Connect levy. This program ensures that families in financial need can afford to enrol their children at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Children in Year 4 Old Kindergarten to Year 6; Family holds eligible means tested Family Concession Card (health care card or pensioner concession card); Concession card must be in parent or guardian's name; Child must be listed on the card
special_conditions: Concession card discounts applied on a pro rata basis for newly issued or cancelled cards. Discount includes discounted tuition fee and building levy, and waiving of amenities fee, IT Levy and Kieran Connect levy.

overview: St Joseph's School offers a limited number of Aboriginal Scholarships for students in Years 5-12. The scholarship recognises students of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent who demonstrate a strong interest in learning and commitment to Catholic education.
description: A limited number of Aboriginal Scholarships are available for students in Years 5-12 who meet the specified criteria. The scholarship covers textbooks from the booklist, a full set of school uniforms from the uniform shop, and school tuition fees. The purchase of an iPad for school is not covered. Applications and an interview with the Principal is a requirement of the process, with Application Forms available from the School. The scholarship recognises students who are of Aboriginal (or Torres Strait Islander) descent, identify as Aboriginal, and are accepted as Aboriginal by the Aboriginal community in which they live.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Desire of family for Catholic education; Demonstrates strong interest for learning; Demonstrates regular school attendance; Aspires to attend Catholic School; Student's application is endorsed by member of Catholic Education Aboriginal Committee (CEAC) or Aboriginal Corporation; Official definition of Aboriginality: is of Aboriginal (or Torres Strait Islander) descent AND identifies as Aboriginal AND is accepted as Aboriginal by Aboriginal community in which student lives
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications and interview with Principal are required. iPad purchase is not covered by the scholarship. Families must demonstrate ongoing compliance with eligibility criteria.

overview: St Joseph's School offers a Boarding Bursary to assist with boarding costs at the Northam Residential College. The bursary provides financial support to eligible students who wish to board while attending the school.
description: The Boarding Bursary is designed to assist with the boarding costs associated with a student enrolled at St Joseph's School who wishes to board at the Northam Residential College. The Bursary provides a contribution of $2,500.00 towards the boarding fees of the Residential College, paid directly to the Residential College upon receipt of proof of enrolment. The Bursary will be awarded to a student for a one-year period. Families are required to submit an annual Application Form for renewal of the Bursary. Funds for the Boarding Bursary are limited. Parents are expected to cover all other associated fees and charges beyond the bursary contribution.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be enrolled at St Joseph's School; Student must wish to board at Northam Residential College; Student must display expected behaviour at School and at Residential College; Annual application and renewal required
special_conditions: Bursary awarded for one-year period only. Families must submit annual Application Form for renewal. Renewal is determined by meeting eligibility criteria including maintaining expected behaviour standards. Funds for the bursary are limited. Parents must cover all other boarding and tuition fees.

program_name: Health Care Card (HCC) Tuition Fee Discount Scheme
overview: St Joseph's School offers a tuition fee discount for parents or guardians who hold a current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card. This scheme provides financial assistance to eligible families.
description: St Joseph's School offers a discount on tuition fees for parents/guardians who are responsible for the payment of school fees and hold a current Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card or means-tested Family Health Care Card or Department of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card. Parents must complete the discount form available from the school. The scheme provides financial assistance to eligible families in Years 7-12. Application forms and further details about the discount amount are available from the Finance Department or school office.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/guardian must hold current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card; Parent/guardian must be responsible for payment of school fees; Applicable to Years 7-12
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Eligible families must complete and submit the HCC Tuition Fee Discount Scheme Form available from the school. Form must be witnessed by the Business Manager at the beginning of the year.

overview: St Joseph's School facilitates the Secondary Assistance Scheme, a government-funded program for eligible families holding specific government concession cards. The scheme provides cash assistance and clothing allowance to support secondary students.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is made available through the Education Department to holders of a current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The scheme provides $235 assistance for eligible students in Years 7-12. The scheme also provides each student a Clothing allowance of $300, paid directly to the family's nominated bank account. Forms must be filled out and witnessed by the Business Manager at the beginning of the year. The 2025 closing date is April 11 (end of Term 1). This is a government program administered by the school.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be in Years 7-12; Parent/guardian must hold current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card; Application form must be completed and witnessed by Business Manager at beginning of year
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Government program administered through the school. Eligible families receive $235 cash assistance plus $300 Clothing allowance. Annual application and witness by Business Manager required. Closing date April 11 (end of Term 1).

overview: St Luke's College administers the Yara Pilbara Tertiary Scholarship, a tertiary education award available to Year 12 students. The scholarship recognises and supports students pursuing higher education by providing financial assistance to cover tertiary education costs.
description: The Yara Pilbara Tertiary Scholarship is valued at $20,000 and is available each year to Year 12 students at St Luke's College. The scholarship is designed to assist students with the cost of tertiary education at university or higher education institutions. Application forms are made available by the College towards the end of each year, with the selection process involving a merit-based assessment. The scholarship supports students transitioning from secondary to tertiary education and recognises academic achievement and potential for success at university level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be enrolled at St Luke's College; Tertiary education pathway required; Applications submitted towards end of year
special_conditions: Application forms made available by the College towards the end of each year. Funds intended to assist with cost of tertiary education.

overview: St Luke's College students may be eligible for the UWA Hackett Scholarship, administered by The University of Western Australia. This merit-based scholarship is designed to assist and encourage high-achieving Year 12 WACE school leavers with exceptional potential to undertake a bachelor's degree at UWA.
description: The UWA Hackett Scholarship is valued at up to $20,000 and aims to assist and encourage Year 12 WACE school leavers with great potential to undertake a bachelor's degree at The University of Western Australia. Students are automatically awarded the scholarship if they achieve an ATAR in the top 10 per cent of their school cohort (ATAR greater than 60), list a UWA course as their first preference through TISC in the December round, complete Year 12 at a regional, remote or targeted metropolitan school, are Indigenous, or participated in Fairway UWA. The scholarship selection process is based on ATAR performance and university course preference registration.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 WACE students; ATAR in top 10% of school cohort (ATAR greater than 60); Listed UWA course as first preference through TISC December round; Completed Year 12 at regional, remote or targeted metropolitan school; OR Indigenous; OR participated in Fairway UWA
special_conditions: Automatic award if criteria met. Students must list UWA course as first preference through TISC in December round. Regional, remote or targeted metropolitan school attendance, Indigenous status or Fairway UWA participation may qualify.

overview: St Luke's College students may be nominated for the UWA Principal's Citizenship Award, recognising outstanding Year 12 WACE students nominated by their principal. The award recognises exceptional resilience, scholarly attributes and contributions to the school and broader community.
description: The UWA Principal's Citizenship Award recognises an outstanding Year 12 WACE student who is nominated by their principal as most likely to succeed at university due to his or her resilience, exemplary scholarly attributes and/or contributions to the school and broader community. The selection process involves the principal nominating a student based on demonstrated resilience, exemplary scholarly traits including positive work ethic and clarity of educational goals, active citizenship or leadership through contributions to school and community wellbeing, or being the first in their family to attend university. The nomination is submitted directly to UWA by the school principal.
eligibility_criteria: Year 12 WACE student; Principal nomination required; Demonstrated resilience; Exemplary scholarly attributes; Contributions to school and/or broader community; May be first-generation university student
special_conditions: Nomination by school principal required. Selection based on resilience, scholarly excellence, and community contributions. Award recognises potential for success at university.

overview: St Mark's offers a limited number of Academic Scholarships to reward outstanding achievement in students entering Year 6. The scholarships recognise students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning, encouraging excellence in education.
description: St Mark's will be offering up to four Academic Scholarships for students entering Year 6 in 2027. These scholarships provide partial tuition fee remission and will be awarded for the student's entire secondary school career at St Mark's, subject to ongoing annual review. The scholarships are awarded based on performance in the scholarship examination, which assesses abstract reasoning and problem-solving, mathematics achievement and reasoning, and reading comprehension. Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed prior to final selection. The number of scholarships awarded each year and the percentage of tuition fee remission is at the discretion of the School Council. Testing is conducted independently by Academic Assessment Services.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 6 in 2027; Must be currently enrolled in Year 5 in 2026; Students aged between 8 and 11; International students applying through Anglican Schools Commission International are not eligible
special_conditions: Scholarships are awarded for the entire secondary school career at St Mark's, subject to ongoing annual review. The percentage of tuition fee remission is at the discretion of the School Council. Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed prior to final selection. A registration fee of $120 will be charged by Academic Assessment Services for each child sitting the examination. An additional fee of $100 will be incurred for late registrations.

overview: The St Mark's Old Scholars' Association awards a scholarship each year to an eligible Year 9 student. The scholarship recognises well-rounded students who excel and demonstrate commitment to sporting, cultural, extra-curricular and co-curricular pursuits, community service, and academic excellence.
description: The Old Scholars' Association Scholarship is awarded to a Year 9 student who excels and commits to several sporting, cultural, extra and co-curricular pursuits, community engagement, academic commitment, and specialist areas. The scholarship seeks a well-rounded student who embodies the spirit of St Mark's Anglican Community School. The scholarship provides a partial fee remission of 25% for the successful applicant's final three years at St Mark's (Years 10 to 12). The scholarship will continue for three years or until the student leaves St Mark's, whichever occurs first. The OSA Scholarship is managed directly by the Old Scholars' Association.
