eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 9 students; Demonstrate excellence in sporting pursuits; Demonstrate excellence in cultural or performing arts pursuits; Show strong academic commitment; Demonstrate community engagement; Active participation in extra and co-curricular pursuits; Embody the values of St Mark's Anglican Community School
special_conditions: The scholarship provides partial fee remission for three years or until the student leaves St Mark's. Recipients must continue to demonstrate the values and commitment expected at St Mark's. The OSA Scholarship is managed directly by the Old Scholars' Association. Applications must be structured to demonstrate the student's well-rounded excellence across multiple disciplines.

overview: St Mark's offers the Sister Eileen Heath Scholarship to provide financial support to the most disadvantaged children in the school community. This scholarship aims to ensure families experiencing significant financial hardship can access St Mark's education.
description: The Sister Eileen Heath Scholarship is designed to support the most disadvantaged children in the St Mark's community. It provides financial assistance to families experiencing significant hardship who may otherwise be unable to afford tuition at St Mark's. Applications are submitted via nomination, and the Principal must sign off on all applications before submission. This scholarship recognises the school's commitment to making education accessible to all students regardless of financial circumstances, aligned with the Anglican Schools Commission's principles of providing affordable independent education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to any current St Mark's students; Family must demonstrate significant financial hardship; Student must demonstrate potential to benefit from St Mark's education; Applications must be nominated and signed off by the Principal
special_conditions: Applications must be signed off by the Principal before submitting. The scholarship is to support the most disadvantaged children. Families are encouraged to apply in confidence. All applications and circumstances are treated confidentially.

program_name: St Mark's Tuition Fee Assistance Plan
overview: St Mark's offers the Tuition Fee Assistance Plan to help families experiencing financial challenges due to unforeseen personal and economic circumstances. The plan provides flexible payment arrangements and financial support to ensure access to education.
description: The St Mark's Tuition Fee Assistance Plan was introduced to assist families who may experience financial challenges due to unforeseen personal and economic circumstances. All school families are able to approach the school in confidence at any time to enquire and/or request support through the plan. The school is willing to consider arrangements for alternate payment plans during short-term financial difficulties. Once a request for assistance is received, a confidential appointment will be set up with the Business Manager to discuss the family's personal circumstances. Following this meeting, a formal request for support may be submitted. The family may be required to provide supporting documentation to fulfil a means test. A decision on the request will be made as quickly as possible, and an appropriate Assistance Plan will be set up for the family.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all St Mark's enrolled families; Experiencing financial challenges due to unforeseen personal or economic circumstances; Willing to provide financial documentation for means testing
special_conditions: All applications and consultations are confidential. Families must approach the Business Manager in writing or confidentially. Supporting documentation may be required to fulfil a means test. Appropriate assistance plan will be set up following approval. The school reserves the right to assess eligibility based on individual circumstances.

overview: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is available to parents and guardians with children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 who hold an eligible concession card. This government-supported scheme provides financial assistance to families receiving government support.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme is available to parents and guardians with children enrolled in Years 7 to 12 who hold an eligible concession card (a Pensioner Concession Card or a Health Care Card). This scheme provides government-supported financial assistance for eligible families. As a registered approved school, St Mark's participates in this scheme and can process applications from families who meet the eligibility criteria. Families holding eligible concession cards are encouraged to contact the school to enquire about available assistance under this government scheme.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian must hold a Pensioner Concession Card or Health Care Card; Child must be enrolled in Years 7 to 12; Child must be attending St Mark's Anglican Community School
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Parent or guardian must hold an eligible concession card (Pensioner Concession Card or Health Care Card). This is a government-supported scheme and assistance amounts vary based on government guidelines. Eligible families should contact the school directly for application details and current entitlements.

school_name: St Mary Star of the Sea School - Secondary Campus
St Mary Star of the Sea College

overview: St Joseph's School Northam offers Aboriginal Scholarships to Indigenous students in Years 5-12. The scholarship recognises and supports Aboriginal students by providing comprehensive educational support, including textbooks, school uniforms, and tuition fee coverage.
description: The Aboriginal Scholarship is available to a limited number of Aboriginal students in Years 5-12 who meet the eligibility criteria. The scholarship provides coverage of textbooks from the booklist, a full set of school uniforms from the uniform shop, and full school tuition fees. The purchase of an iPad for school is not covered under this scholarship. Applicants are required to complete an application form and participate in an interview with the Principal as part of the selection process. The scholarship continues for the duration of the student's enrolment at the school, provided they maintain satisfactory academic progress and conduct.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander student; Years 5-12; Eligible students must meet identified selection criteria; Interview with Principal required; Application form completion required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available. Applications and interview with the Principal is a requirement of the process with Application Forms available from the School. iPad purchase is not covered by the scholarship.

overview: St Joseph's School Northam offers a Boarding Bursary to assist students who wish to board at Northam Residential College. The bursary provides financial support towards boarding costs for eligible students who meet specific criteria.
description: The Boarding Bursary is designed to assist with the boarding costs associated with a student enrolled at St Joseph's School who wishes to board at the Northam Residential College. The bursary provides a contribution of $2,500.00 towards the boarding fees of the Residential College, which is paid directly to the Residential College upon receipt of proof of enrolment. Eligibility requires that students display expected behaviour at both School and the Residential College. This bursary supports student access to boarding facilities and is available on a needs basis. Application forms and further information are available from the School and can be downloaded from the boarding facilities page.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled at St Joseph's School; Wishes to board at Northam Residential College; Displays expected behaviour at School and at the Residential College; Meets financial need assessment criteria
special_conditions: Bursary is paid directly to the Residential College upon receipt of proof of enrolment. Student must maintain expected behaviour standards at both School and Residential College. Application and Information Form must be completed. Bursary is subject to annual review and may be withdrawn if behaviour expectations are not maintained.

program_name: Health Care Card Tuition Fee Discount Scheme
overview: St Joseph's School Northam provides a tuition fee discount for families holding a current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card. This scheme assists eligible families in reducing their tuition costs.
description: The Executive Director of Catholic Education has implemented a discount on tuition fees for parents and guardians who are responsible for the payment of school fees and hold a current CentreLink Pensioner Concession Card, a means-tested Family Health Care Card, or a Department of Veterans Affairs Pensioner Concession Card. To obtain this discount, parents must make an appointment with the Business Manager at the beginning of the school year to verify their eligibility. Forms must be filled out and witnessed by the Business Manager. The discount applies to tuition fees and is processed at the beginning of each school year. This assistance programme helps ensure families with demonstrated financial need can access education at the school.
eligibility_criteria: Current holder of a Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card; Parent or guardian responsible for payment of school fees; All year levels eligible; Must apply through Business Manager at beginning of school year
special_conditions: Parents must hold current eligibility documentation. Forms must be completed and witnessed by the Business Manager at the beginning of the school year. Discount is applied to tuition fees only. Parents must make an appointment to verify eligibility and complete documentation.

overview: St Joseph's School Northam participates in the Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) administered by the Education Department. This scheme provides financial assistance to eligible secondary students in Years 7-12 from eligible families.
description: The Secondary Assistance Scheme (SAS) is made available through the Education Department to holders of a current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card. The scheme provides $235 assistance for eligible students in Years 7-12 and also provides each student with a Clothing allowance of $300, which is paid directly to the family's nominated bank account. Forms must be filled out and witnessed by the Business Manager at the beginning of the school year. The 2025 closing date for applications is April 11 (end of Term 1). This scheme provides both financial assistance for educational costs and clothing support to eligible families.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Years 7-12; Holder of current Health Care Card, Veterans Affairs Card, or Pensioner Concession Card; Application and witness by Business Manager required; Annual application required at beginning of school year
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Forms must be filled out and witnessed by the Business Manager at the beginning of the school year. Payment is made directly to the family's nominated bank account. Annual re-application required. Closing date for 2025 is April 11 (end of Term 1). Additionally provides $300 clothing allowance per student.

overview: St Mary MacKillop College offers Aboriginal Student Bursaries to recognise commitment to schooling and assist Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who might otherwise be denied access to Catholic secondary education due to financial circumstances. The bursaries assist with the cost of tuition fees.
description: The Aboriginal Student Bursaries are available to students entering secondary school at St Mary MacKillop College. These bursaries assist with the cost of tuition fees and are intended to recognise commitment to schooling as well as support Aboriginal students who might be denied a Catholic secondary education because of financial circumstances. Applicants must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander and have a desire to be educated in the Catholic education system. The bursaries are provided at the school and do not cover boarding fees. Applicants are expected to have established strong foundations for learning and aspire to complete Year 12. Parents and guardians of eligible students are invited to lodge applications early as the number of bursaries is limited. The financial value varies based on individual circumstances and applications. Selection is based on demonstrating Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent and a commitment to Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; Must have a desire to be educated in the Catholic education system; Must have established strong foundations for learning; Must aspire to complete Year 12; Open to secondary school entry
special_conditions: Bursaries do not cover boarding fees. Number of bursaries available each year is limited. Early applications are encouraged. Selection based on commitment to schooling and financial need.

program_name: CEWA Health Care Card Discount Scheme
overview: St Marcellin Catholic College provides automatic fee concessions to families holding eligible means-tested Health Cards and Pensioner Concession Cards through the CEWA Health Care Card Discount Scheme. The scheme assists families in managing school fee costs based on their financial eligibility.
description: The CEWA Health Care Card Discount Scheme provides an automatic fee concession for holders of eligible means tested Health Cards and Pensioner Concession Cards. The concession amount varies based on the specific card held and is determined by Catholic Education Western Australia. The card must be in the name of the parent or guardian responsible for payment of school fees and must be submitted to the College in January. Concessions can only be applied during the validity period of the card. Eligible families should contact the College's Enrolment Officer at enrolment@stmarcellin.wa.edu.au or (08) 9537 4701 to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian must hold an eligible means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card; Card must be in the name of the person responsible for school fee payment; Card must be valid and submitted to College in January; Concession applies only during validity period of card
special_conditions: Card must be current and valid at time of submission. Concessions are automatically applied by the College upon receipt of valid card documentation. Eligible cards include Centrelink Health Care Card, Centrelink Pensioner Concession Card, and Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Concession Card.

overview: St Mary's Catholic School offers significant discounts on school fees for families who hold eligible Health Care Cards. This scheme recognises families experiencing financial hardship and ensures no student is excluded from enrolment due to inability to pay.
description: St Mary's Catholic School provides significant fee discounts for health care card holders as part of the Health Care Card Discount Scheme in accordance with CEWA (Catholic Education Commission of Western Australia) guidelines. The scheme applies to students enrolled from Kindergarten to Year 10. Parents or guardians who hold eligible means-tested family concession cards receive discounts on tuition fees. The school's policy ensures that financial grounds shall never be the reason for non-enrolment or exclusion of any child from the school. Additionally, the inability to pay school fees will never result in the non-enrolment or exclusion of any student from St. Mary's school. The discount amount varies based on the type of concession card held.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold eligible means-tested family concession card (Health Care Card); Student enrolled in Kindergarten to Year 10; Must provide concession card documentation at time of enrolment
year_levels: Kindergarten, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only, not to other annual fees and charges. Health care card holders must provide proof of concession card at enrolment. In accordance with CEWA policy, financial grounds shall never be the reason for non-enrolment or exclusion of any child from Catholic School.

overview: St Mary's Catholic School offers sibling discounts on school fees for families with multiple children enrolled at the school. This program recognises the increased financial burden on families with multiple students and provides structured fee reductions.
description: St Mary's Catholic School provides sibling discounts on school fees for families with multiple children enrolled at the same time. The discount scheme is applicable as multiple children from one family enroll. The sibling discount applies to tuition fees and is designed to make Catholic education more accessible to larger families. The school recognises that families with multiple students face greater financial burden and offers structured reductions to support them. These discounts are part of the school's commitment to ensuring financial grounds never prevent a child from attending St. Mary's.
eligibility_criteria: Two or more children from same family enrolled at St. Mary's simultaneously; Applies to tuition fees only
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only, not to other annual fees and charges. Applicable when multiple children from the one family are enrolled at the same time. Families must be enrolled in school fee payment plans.

overview: St Mary's Catholic School provides financial hardship assistance to ensure that no student is excluded from enrolment or school attendance due to inability to pay school fees. The school recognises that financial circumstances may change and makes assistance available to families in need.
description: St Mary's Catholic School has a formal policy that the inability to pay school fees will never be the reason for non-enrolment or exclusion of any student from the school. While school fees are an important source of income for the school, the institution is committed to ensuring all families can access Catholic education regardless of financial capacity. Families experiencing financial difficulty can apply to the Principal to discuss fee discounts and payment arrangements. The school acknowledges that changing economic circumstances may warrant assistance during the school year. This assistance ensures equal access to curriculum and pastoral provision for all students regardless of fee payment capacity.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship or difficulty paying school fees; Must apply to Principal to discuss assistance options; Open to all students at all year levels
special_conditions: Application by discussion with Principal. Financial grounds shall never be the reason for non-enrolment or exclusion. The withholding of access to students from normal pastoral and curriculum provision is not permitted on financial grounds. Parents with discounted or no fees retain equal rights to involvement in their child's education as full fee-paying parents.

overview: St Mary's College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognise and support learning innovation and academic achievement. The scholarship is available to prospective and existing families who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and commitment to College values.
description: St Mary's College encourages learning innovation and academic achievement through its Academic Scholarship program. Students will be considered based on their demonstrated academic performance and results as well as their commitment to the College values. The scholarship is designed to support families and remove financial barriers to accessing the College's educational programs. The specific financial value of the scholarship and duration have not been publicly disclosed on the College website. Applications for 2027 open in March 2026, and eligible students both new to the College and existing students may apply.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective and existing students; Demonstrated academic performance and results; Commitment to College values

overview: The Catholic Scholarship is an award of financial assistance to enable students to access Catholic education at St Mary's College. The scholarship ensures all students have the opportunity to benefit from a Catholic education regardless of a family's capacity to meet associated costs.
description: The Catholic Scholarship has been established to ensure that all students have the opportunity to benefit from a Catholic education at St Mary's College, regardless of a family's capacity to meet the associated costs. This financial assistance program recognizes that not all families can afford full fees, and aims to reduce barriers to Catholic education. The scholarship is open to Catholic students from Year 7 to Year 10, who are either new or existing Catholic students of St Mary's College. Applications for 2027 open in March 2026. The specific financial value, duration, and renewal conditions have not been publicly disclosed on the College website.
eligibility_criteria: Catholic students; Year 7 to Year 10; Prospective or existing students of St Mary's College
special_conditions: Scholarship is established specifically for Catholic students to ensure access to Catholic education regardless of family financial circumstances.

overview: St Mary's College offers the First Nations Secondary Bursary to support Indigenous students. The bursary recognises commitment to schooling through regular attendance, learning engagement, and participation in College and community activities.
description: St Mary's College has a strong commitment to working in partnership with First Nations people to provide assistance and increase positive life choices and career opportunities for First Nations youth. The College offers First Nations Bursaries to both students new to the College and existing students each year. The purpose of awarding the Bursary is to recognise and reward commitment to schooling, evidenced by regular attendance at school, a commitment to learning, and participation in College and community activities. Applications for 2027 open in March 2026. The specific financial value and duration of the bursary have not been publicly disclosed on the College website.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations students; Commitment and motivation in learning; Demonstrated regular school attendance; Participation in College and community activities
special_conditions: Bursary recipients must demonstrate commitment to schooling through regular attendance, commitment to learning, and participation in College and community activities.

overview: St Mary's College partners with MADALAH Limited to offer Secondary Education Scholarships for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from Year 7-12. The scholarship provides educational opportunities for Indigenous youth from remote and regional communities across Western Australia.
description: The College is committed to providing educational opportunities to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from Year 7 to Year 12 through MADALAH Secondary Education Scholarships. MADALAH Limited is a not-for-profit organisation that offers secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from remote and regional communities to Western Australia's leading schools. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who enrol at the College or who are already enrolled can apply for these scholarships. These scholarships are open to Indigenous students who demonstrate commitment and motivation in areas of learning, leadership, and community. Scholarship recipients are selected based on academic potential, financial need, and personal circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Year 7 to Year 12; Demonstrable commitment and motivation in learning, leadership and community; Students from remote and regional communities; Academic potential and financial need
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients are monitored to ensure they meet academic and personal goals and have access to support needed to succeed. Students must enrol or be enrolled at St Mary's College. MADALAH works closely with schools to provide ongoing support.

overview: St Mary's College offers Academic Scholarships to reward learning innovation and academic achievement. The scholarship is available to prospective and existing families who demonstrate exceptional academic performance and commitment to the College values.
description: St Mary's College encourages learning innovation and academic achievement and offers the opportunity for prospective and existing families to apply for an Academic Scholarship for their child. Students will be considered based on their demonstrated academic performance and results as well as their commitment to the College values. Applications for 2027 open March 2026. The scholarship recognises students who display commitment and motivation in the areas of learning, creativity, leadership, collaboration and community. Specific financial benefits and year levels are not detailed on the public scholarship page.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective or existing students; Demonstrated academic performance and results; Commitment to College values; Applications open March 2026

overview: The Catholic Scholarship is an award of financial assistance established to ensure that all students have the opportunity to benefit from a Catholic education, regardless of a family's capacity to meet the associated costs. It is offered to Catholic students from Year 7 to Year 10.
description: The Catholic Scholarship is an award of financial assistance to enable a student to further their education at St Mary's College. This Scholarship has been established to ensure that all students have the opportunity to benefit from a Catholic education, regardless of a family's capacity to meet the associated costs. The Scholarship is open to Catholic students from Year 7 to Year 10, who are either new or existing Catholic students of St Mary's College. Applications for 2027 open March 2026. The scholarship recognises students who display commitment and motivation in the areas of learning, creativity, leadership, collaboration and community.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Catholic; Year 7 to Year 10; New or existing students of St Mary's College; Applications open March 2026

overview: St Mary's College is committed to working in partnership with First Nations people to provide assistance and increase positive life choices for First Nations youth. The First Nations Secondary Bursary is available to both students new to the College and existing students who demonstrate commitment and motivation.
description: St Mary's College has a strong commitment to working in partnership with First Nations people to provide assistance to increase positive life choices and help further possible career opportunities for First Nations youth. As part of this commitment, St Mary's College offers the opportunity for prospective families to apply for a First Nations Secondary Bursary. Each year the College offers First Nations Bursaries to both students new to the College, and existing students. The purpose of awarding the Bursary is to recognise and reward commitment to schooling, evidenced by regular attendance at school, a commitment to learning and participation in College and community activities. Applications for 2027 open March 2026.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations heritage; Regular school attendance; Commitment to learning; Participation in College and community activities; New or existing students; Applications open March 2026
special_conditions: Regular attendance at school is required; Commitment to learning must be demonstrated; Participation in College and community activities is expected.

overview: St Mary's College is committed to providing educational opportunities to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from Year 7-12 by offering MADALAH Secondary Education Scholarships. MADALAH Limited is a not-for-profit organisation that partners with Western Australia's leading schools to support remote and regional Indigenous students.
description: The College is committed to providing educational opportunities to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from Year 7-12 by offering MADALAH Secondary Education Scholarships. MADALAH Limited is a not-for-profit organisation that offers secondary and tertiary education scholarships for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students from remote and regional communities to Western Australia's leading schools. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who enrol at the College or who are already enrolled at the College can apply for these Scholarships. These scholarships are open to Indigenous students who demonstrate commitment and motivation in areas of learning, leadership and community. MADALAH provides ongoing support including tutoring, academic support, counselling and career advice.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage; Year 7 to Year 12; Commitment and motivation in learning, leadership and community; From remote and regional communities; Can be new or existing enrolees
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Selection based on academic potential, financial need, and personal circumstances. MADALAH provides ongoing support and mentoring throughout the scholarship period. Scholarship includes access to work experience placements, cultural events, and leadership development programs.

overview: St Matthew's School offers full or partial scholarships to families seeking Catholic Education for their children. These scholarships provide substantial financial benefits and are available to both current and prospective students in the early primary years.
description: St Matthew's School Scholarship Program provides substantial financial benefits to support families who desire a Catholic Education for their children. The scholarships are offered as either full or partial remissions of tuition fees. Students eligible for this program include both current students at the school and prospective students seeking entry. The application process requires families to contact the Principal or Finance Officer directly to arrange an application form. Scholarships are available for students in Kindergarten to Year 2, recognizing the importance of educational access during foundational primary years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current students at St Matthew's School; Open to prospective students; Students entering Kindergarten to Year 2; Application by request from Principal or Finance Officer
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarships are available for current and prospective students. Interested families must contact the Principal or Finance Officer to request and complete an application form.

program_name: Health Care Card / Pensioner Concession Card Discount
overview: St Matthew's School provides a significant discount on tuition fees for families holding a current means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. This financial assistance program recognizes families facing financial difficulties and supports their access to Catholic education.
description: St Matthew's School offers substantial tuition fee discounts to parents and guardians who are responsible for school fee payments and hold a current means-tested family Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The discount provides significant financial relief to eligible families. To access this assistance, families must contact the Finance Officer at the school to arrange an application form. This program is designed to ensure that families experiencing financial hardship can maintain their children's enrolment at the school. The card must be current and means-tested to qualify for the discount.
eligibility_criteria: Parent or guardian responsible for school fee payment; Must hold a current means-tested family Health Care Card; OR must hold a current Pensioner Concession Card; Contact Finance Officer to arrange application form
special_conditions: Parent/guardian must be responsible for payment of school fees. Must provide evidence of current means-tested Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. Application form required from Finance Officer.

overview: St Matthew's School recognizes that genuine financial hardship may arise and provides fee relief options for families experiencing difficult circumstances. Parents and guardians in genuine financial hardship are encouraged to contact the Principal to discuss available options.
description: St Matthew's School acknowledges that circumstances may arise requiring families to seek changes to how fees will be paid. The school supports families experiencing difficult financial situations and encourages any parent or guardian in genuine financial hardship to contact the Principal, Susan Milton, to discuss what fee relief options are available. The school also offers alternative payment options including payment plans that can be set up via Direct Debit through the Finance Manager. This program demonstrates the school's commitment to ensuring that financial barriers do not prevent families from accessing Catholic education. Contact with school leadership is the first step to exploring available support.
eligibility_criteria: Family experiencing genuine financial hardship; Student currently enrolled or accepted into school; Demonstrated financial need; Contact required with Principal to discuss options
special_conditions: Must demonstrate genuine financial hardship. Alternative payment options available including Direct Debit payment plans. Direct contact with Principal Susan Milton required to discuss options. School encourages families in financial difficulty to reach out rather than withdraw from school.

overview: St Patrick's School offers the Parish Scholarship to students from any year level as a form of financial assistance. The scholarship is awarded for use towards school fees in the following year, recognising families seeking support with educational costs.
description: The Parish Scholarship is available to school students from Kindergarten to Year 6 and supports families with fee payment assistance. The scholarship is awarded for application towards fees in the following school year, providing partial fee reduction support. The selection process involves application by the family to the school administration, with consideration given to demonstrated financial need. The scholarship operates on an annual basis with recipients required to reapply each year to continue receiving support.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students from any year level; Must be currently enrolled at St Patrick's School; Demonstrated financial need required; Available for use towards fees in the following year
special_conditions: Scholarship is awarded for use towards fees in the following year. Annual application and review process required. Recipients must remain enrolled at the school.

overview: St Patrick's School presents the Achievement Award to recognise outstanding academic and personal achievement across all year levels. The award is presented annually at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night to students who demonstrate excellence in their learning and development.
description: The Achievement Award is offered to all children in each year level from Kindergarten to Year 6, recognising students who have demonstrated outstanding achievement in their academic work and personal growth. Recipients of this award receive a Book Prize presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night, providing formal recognition of their accomplishments. The award is determined by teacher assessment of student performance and achievement across the school year. This is one of three major awards (along with Encouragement and Sister of Our Lady of the Mission Religious Award) offered annually at each year level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in each year level; Must be currently enrolled at St Patrick's School Kindergarten to Year 6; Selected based on demonstrated achievement in learning
special_conditions: Award is presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night. One award offered per year level. Recipients receive a Book Prize. Selection is at the discretion of school staff based on achievement.

overview: St Patrick's School presents the Encouragement Award to recognise students who demonstrate improvement, effort and perseverance across all year levels. The award is presented annually at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night to students who show commitment to their personal development.
description: The Encouragement Award is offered to all children in each year level from Kindergarten to Year 6, recognising students who have demonstrated significant effort, improvement and commitment to their learning and personal growth. Recipients of this award receive a Book Prize presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night, providing formal recognition of their endeavour and progress. The award is determined by teacher assessment of student effort and growth across the school year. This is one of three major awards (along with Achievement and Sister of Our Lady of the Mission Religious Award) offered annually at each year level to celebrate various aspects of student development.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in each year level; Must be currently enrolled at St Patrick's School Kindergarten to Year 6; Selected based on demonstrated effort and improvement
special_conditions: Award is presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night. One award offered per year level. Recipients receive a Book Prize. Selection is at the discretion of school staff based on effort and improvement demonstrated throughout the year.

program_name: Sister of Our Lady of the Mission Religious Award
overview: St Patrick's School presents the Sister of Our Lady of the Mission Religious Award to recognise students who demonstrate outstanding faith, spiritual commitment and values aligned with the Catholic mission of the school. The award is presented annually at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night to students in all year levels.
description: The Sister of Our Lady of the Mission Religious Award is offered to all children in each year level from Kindergarten to Year 6, recognising students who have demonstrated outstanding commitment to living out the values and faith of the Catholic community at St Patrick's School. Recipients of this award receive a Book Prize presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night, providing formal recognition of their spiritual contribution to the school community. The award is named in honour of Sr Mary Bernadette McLeod, a former St Patrick's teacher remembered for her passion for educating young people with encouragement and desire to achieve greatness. This is one of three major awards (along with Achievement and Encouragement) offered annually at each year level.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students in each year level; Must be currently enrolled at St Patrick's School Kindergarten to Year 6; Selected based on demonstrated faith commitment and living school values
special_conditions: Award is presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night. One award offered per year level. Recipients receive a Book Prize. Named in honour of Sr Mary Bernadette McLeod of the order of Our Lady of the Missions. Selection is at the discretion of school staff based on demonstrated faith and values alignment.

overview: St Patrick's School presents special awards to the graduation class (Year 6 students) recognising their achievements, leadership and contribution to the school community. These additional awards complement the standard Achievement, Encouragement and Religious awards offered to all year levels.
description: St Patrick's School offers additional awards specifically for the graduation class (Year 6 students) beyond the standard Achievement, Encouragement and Sister of Our Lady of the Mission Religious awards offered to all year levels. These graduation class awards provide special recognition of the final year students' contributions and achievements at the school. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated excellence across academic, personal and community dimensions. The awards are presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night ceremony. Recipients receive recognition through the presentation process, acknowledging their leadership and service to the school community during their time at St Patrick's.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 6 students; Must be graduating from St Patrick's School; Selected based on demonstrated achievement and contribution to school community
special_conditions: Awards are exclusive to the graduation class (Year 6 students). Presented at the End of Year Concert and Prize Night. Selection is at the discretion of school staff. Additional awards beyond the three main awards offered to all year levels.

overview: St Paul's Primary School offers an Enrichment Program to selected students in Years 3-6 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. The program provides extension investigations incorporating multiple learning areas and participation in academic competitions including Have Sum Fun Maths Competition, Tournament of Minds, Write a Book in a Day, and ICAS Assessments.
description: St Paul's Primary School offers an Enrichment Program specifically designed for identified gifted and talented students in Years 3-6. Students who score extremely highly on the ACER General Ability Test (AGAT) are selected for the program. Alternative entry into the program is also possible. The program is divided into two groups: Years 3-4 and Years 5-6. Students participate in extension investigations that incorporate multiple learning areas and are offered additional enrichment initiatives throughout the year. Selected students are invited to participate in Have Sum Fun Maths Competition, Tournament of Minds, Write a Book in a Day, and ICAS Assessments. The program aims to provide advanced learning opportunities for high-ability students and recognises their need for differentiated and challenging curriculum. This is a non-monetary program that provides academic acceleration and enrichment rather than financial assistance.
eligibility_criteria: Years 3-6; Scoring extremely high on ACER General Ability Test (AGAT); Alternative entry possible based on school assessment
special_conditions: Students are identified through the ACER General Ability Test (AGAT). Alternative entry into the program is possible. Please refer to the school's Gifted and Talented policy for more information on entry requirements.

overview: St Paul's Primary School offers Music Scholarships to recognise students who demonstrate musical talent and commitment to the school's music program. As part of the school's commitment to developing the School Band, music scholarships are offered and students have access to afterschool instrumental lessons provided by Keyed Up Music.
description: St Paul's Catholic Primary School's Music Scholarship program supports talented musicians through the provision of instrumental lesson access and opportunities within the school's music ensembles including the School Choir and School Band. The school works in partnership with Keyed Up Music, which is a full teaching member of ASME (Australian Society of Music Educators) and provides qualified instrumental instruction. While the specific financial benefit amount is not detailed in publicly available materials, the scholarship includes access to afterschool instrumental lessons. Students receiving the scholarship are expected to participate actively in the school's music program, including ensemble participation. Selection is based on demonstrated musical ability and commitment to musical development. The scholarship continues for the duration of a student's enrolment at the school, provided they maintain active participation in the music program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students enrolled at St Paul's Primary School; Demonstrated musical ability and talent; Commitment to ongoing participation in school music ensembles and lessons; Active membership in School Choir and/or School Band required
special_conditions: Recipients must participate actively in the school's music program and maintain membership in school music ensembles. Instrumental lessons are provided through partnership with Keyed Up Music (external provider). Students are required to continue instrumental instruction and support the development of the School Band.

overview: The St Paul's ASPIRE program is for high-potential learners in Years 3-6 to assist and support them through opportunities that acknowledge and maximise their individual potential. The program provides enrichment and extension activities designed to challenge gifted students.
description: The St Paul's ASPIRE program is an extension program designed specifically for high-potential learners in Years 3 through Year 6. Appropriate identification of these students is achieved through diagnostic testing undertaken by the school to identify students with exceptional ability across academic domains. The program aims to acknowledge and maximise each student's individual potential through carefully designed enrichment opportunities and extension activities that challenge advanced learners. While not formally designated as a scholarship with direct financial benefit, the ASPIRE program provides specialized educational opportunities and support tailored to gifted and talented students. The identification process is rigorous and ongoing, ensuring students selected for the program demonstrate genuine high-potential learning characteristics. Participation supports student development across intellectual, creative and leadership dimensions.
eligibility_criteria: Enrolled in Years 3-6; Identified as high-potential learner through diagnostic testing; Demonstrated exceptional ability in academic domains; Selection based on school's identification process
special_conditions: Participation in the ASPIRE program is based on diagnostic testing and identification by the school. Selected students benefit from enrichment and extension activities designed to maximise their individual potential.

school_name: St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School
program_name: PCC and SSP Sibling Discount Program
overview: St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School and Prendiville Catholic College offer a collaborative Sibling Discount Scheme to provide financial relief for families with multiple children enrolled at both schools. The scheme reduces tuition fees for students at St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School who have siblings currently enrolled at Prendiville Catholic College.
description: The PCC and SSP Sibling Discount Program is designed to make quality Catholic education more accessible and affordable for families with children attending both Prendiville Catholic College and St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School. Families who have one or more children enrolled at Prendiville Catholic College and another at St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School will qualify for a 20% discount on tuition fees for their SSPCPS student. The discount applies to students in Years Pre-Primary through Year 6 at St Simon Peter. To be eligible, families must have school fees up to date across both schools, or have an authorized payment plan in place. To apply, families must download and complete the Sibling Discount Application Form and return it via email to ssp.fees@cewa.edu.au. Once enrolment across both schools is verified, the discount will be automatically applied to tuition fees at St Simon Peter.
eligibility_criteria: Children concurrently enrolled at both Prendiville Catholic College and St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School; School fees must be up to date across both schools or authorized payment plan in place; Open to all year levels Pre-Primary to Year 6
special_conditions: Families must have children concurrently enrolled at both schools. School fees must be up to date or an authorised payment plan must be in place. Discount is applied to the St Simon Peter Catholic Primary School tuition fees only. Application Form must be completed and submitted via email to ssp.fees@cewa.edu.au.

overview: St Stephen's School's Academic Scholarship recognises students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. This scholarship supports recipients' studies from Years 7 to 12 and empowers them to reach new heights in their educational journey.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded annually to students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and commitment to learning. The scholarship provides financial support for the duration of enrolment from Year 7 to Year 12. Selection is determined through a formal assessment examination scheduled for March, followed by interviews with shortlisted candidates. The specific financial value of the scholarship is not publicly disclosed on the website, though it provides meaningful fee assistance to support eligible students throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; External and internal applicants eligible; Must demonstrate exceptional academic ability
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to both internal and external applicants. Recipients are chosen based on academic achievements and alignment with the school's values. Online application form with parental consent required.

overview: The Community Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, or 10 who demonstrate extraordinary pathways to personal excellence through community involvement and leadership. Recipients embody the spirit of community involvement and serve as role models for their peers, showing that academic success and social responsibility go hand in hand.
description: The Community Excellence Scholarship recognises students in Years 7-10 who seek extraordinary pathways to personal excellence through demonstrated commitment to community involvement, leadership, and service. Applicants must demonstrate enthusiasm, participation and exceptional commitment in diverse activities including community involvement, leadership, extra-curricular involvement, persistence in academic pursuits and service. The selection process includes submission of a handwritten letter to the Principal outlining achievements and reasons for applying, along with a character reference letter from a teacher, coach or community member. Shortlisted applicants then undergo an interview with a selection panel. The specific financial value is not disclosed publicly.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9, or 10; Must demonstrate enthusiasm and exceptional commitment in diverse activities; Must demonstrate community involvement, leadership, or service; External and internal applicants eligible
special_conditions: Handwritten letter to Principal and character reference letter required. Interviews conducted with selection panel. Recipients chosen based on community involvement and alignment with school values.

overview: The Instrumental Music Scholarship is awarded to students in Years 5-10 who are outstanding musicians who have attained a high level of performance on their chosen instrument. The scholarship recognises musical excellence and encourages talented musicians to explore their musical passions and contribute to the school's vibrant music community.
description: St Stephen's School offers the Instrumental Music Scholarship to nurture and recognise future musicians in Years 5-10 who have attained exceptional performance levels on their chosen instrument. The scholarship covers a student's music tuition fees and is not a full fee scholarship. In 2027 at Carramar campus, available instruments include trumpet, trombone, viola and cello. Selection is determined through audition, where students must demonstrate high-level instrumental proficiency. The scholarship supports the development of talented musicians within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10; Must have attained high level of performance on chosen instrument; Must be studying one of the available instruments (trumpet, trombone, viola, cello at Carramar); External and internal applicants eligible
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Scholarship covers music tuition fees only; it is not a full fee scholarship. Auditions held in Term 2. Available instruments vary by campus and year.

overview: The Instrument of Need Scholarship is awarded to students in Years 5-10 who are outstanding musicians and whose families demonstrate financial need. This scholarship recognises musical talent and provides financial support to remove barriers to musical education.
description: The Instrument of Need Scholarship is specifically designed for students in Years 5-10 who demonstrate outstanding musical performance on their chosen instrument and whose families require financial assistance. The scholarship covers music tuition fees for students where cost may otherwise prevent them from continuing their musical development. Selection is based on demonstrated musical proficiency through audition and assessment of financial need. Available instruments at Carramar campus in 2027 include trumpet, trombone, viola and cello. This scholarship supports talented musicians from all socioeconomic backgrounds.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10; Must have attained high level of performance on chosen instrument; Financial need demonstrated; Must be studying one of the available instruments (trumpet, trombone, viola, cello at Carramar); External and internal applicants eligible
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10
special_conditions: Scholarship covers music tuition fees only. Auditions held in Term 2. Financial assessment required. Available instruments vary by campus and year.

overview: St Stephen's School's Regional Scholarship is designed specifically for students relocating from regional areas to Perth for their secondary education. The scholarship aims to enhance access to quality education and recognise academic excellence, community involvement and good conduct.
description: The Regional Scholarship programme is specifically designed for students from regional and remote areas of Western Australia who are relocating to Perth to attend St Stephen's School. The school recognises the significant transition regional students face and offers scholarships that provide financial support to enhance access to quality education. Scholarships recognise and reward academic excellence, community involvement and good conduct. Applications are accepted year-round with no closing date, allowing regional families flexibility in their application timing. The school values the unique perspective and contributions that regional students bring to the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Must be relocating from regional or remote areas of Western Australia; Demonstrate academic excellence; Demonstrate community involvement and good conduct; External and internal applicants eligible; No closing date for applications
special_conditions: Applications accepted year-round with no closing date. Specifically for students relocating from regional areas to Perth for secondary education. Selection based on academic excellence, community involvement and good conduct.

overview: St Luke's Catholic Primary School offers the Mater Dei Sibling Discount Scheme to support families with children enrolled at both Mater Dei College and St Luke's. This program provides a fee discount on tuition for eligible families, making education more accessible and affordable.
description: The Mater Dei Sibling Discount Scheme provides a 20% discount on tuition fees for St Luke's Catholic Primary School students whose families have a sibling enrolled at Mater Dei College. The discount applies to tuition fees only and is available for the duration of concurrent enrolment at both schools. Families must have one child enrolled at Mater Dei College and another child enrolled at St Luke's Catholic Primary School to qualify. This ongoing program is designed to recognise and support multi-school family commitments within the Catholic education system.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families with children enrolled at both Mater Dei College and St Luke's Catholic Primary School; Applicant must be a current or future student at St Luke's Catholic Primary School; Sibling must be concurrently enrolled at Mater Dei College; No residency restrictions; Program available for duration of concurrent enrolment
special_conditions: Discount applies to tuition fees only. Enrolment at both Mater Dei College and St Luke's Catholic Primary School must be concurrent. The discount continues for as long as both children remain enrolled at their respective schools.

overview: St Luke's Catholic Primary School offers the Health Care Card Assistance Scheme to support families holding eligible means-tested Centrelink cards. This program provides fee concessions to qualifying families, ensuring that Catholic education remains accessible to those experiencing financial hardship.
description: The Health Care Card Assistance Scheme provides fee concessions for families who present an eligible means-tested Centrelink card to the school's administration office. Eligible cards include Health Care Cards and Concession Cards as determined by Centrelink criteria. A Concession Card Discount Scheme form must be completed and submitted to the administration office annually to access the discount. The card must be in the name of the parent or guardian responsible for fee payment. Digital Wallet cards are acceptable via email submission. The discount amount and fee remission details are determined by the school based on individual circumstances, with variations possible year to year.
eligibility_criteria: Must hold an eligible means-tested Centrelink card (Health Care Card or Concession Card); Card must be in the name of the parent/guardian responsible for fee payment; Eligible card must be presented to administration office annually; Concession Card Discount Scheme form must be completed each year; Open to all current and prospective students at St Luke's Catholic Primary School
special_conditions: Form must be completed and resubmitted to administration office annually to maintain eligibility. Card presentation and completed form required each year. Card must be in the name of parent/guardian responsible for fee payment. Digital Wallet cards are acceptable. The card will list children covered by concession. Concession does not apply to Pre-Kindy program fees.

overview: St Stephen's School offers Academic Scholarships to recognise students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability. This scholarship supports studies from Years 7 to 12 and empowers recipients to reach new heights in their educational journey.
description: The Academic Scholarship is designed for students entering Year 7 who have demonstrated exceptional academic performance and intellectual capability. Recipients receive tuition fee reduction as part of their scholarship award and are supported throughout Years 7 to 12. The selection process involves an academic examination where students must demonstrate strong performance. Scholarships are available to both internal and external applicants, and students are selected based on academic achievements and alignment with the school's values. The application process requires completion of an online application form with parental consent and all required documents must be submitted by the published deadline.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7; Exceptional academic ability demonstrated through examination; Open to internal and external applicants; Alignment with school values required
special_conditions: Scholarships are available to both internal and external applicants. Recipients must align with the school's values and be committed to their studies throughout the scholarship period.

overview: St Stephen's School offers Community Excellence Scholarships to recognise students entering Years 8, 9, or 10 who demonstrate exceptional commitment to community involvement, leadership, and service. This scholarship celebrates those who seek out extraordinary pathways to personal excellence beyond academic achievement.
description: The Community Excellence Scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate enthusiasm, participation and exceptional commitment in a diverse range of activities including community involvement, leadership, extra-curricular involvement, persistence in academic pursuits and service. The application process requires students to submit a handwritten letter to the Principal outlining their achievements and reasons for applying, along with a character reference letter from a teacher, coach or community member. Applicants undergo an interview with a selection panel where they must articulate their contributions and values. Recipients are chosen based on community involvement, leadership demonstrated, and alignment with the school's values, ensuring selection of those who will thrive in the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 8, 9, or 10; Demonstrated enthusiasm and exceptional commitment to community involvement; Evidence of leadership and persistence in academic pursuits; Service-oriented mindset required; Character reference from teacher, coach or community member required

overview: Swan Christian College offers the Academic Scholarship Program for students entering Year 10. This Senior School Scholarship Program recognises students who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and provides significant financial support for their studies in Years 10-12.

Applicants must demonstrate strong academic performance in the Australian Core Skills Framework Level 2 examination, which consists of three subtests in the areas of Humanities, Mathematics/Science and Written Expression. The exam lasts approximately 2.5 hours with a short break. Applications are made through the ACER scholarship portal at scholarships.acer.edu.au. Successful applicants will be selected based on their test performance demonstrating outstanding academic ability. The scholarship may be renewed annually subject to satisfactory academic progress.

Applications are made by submitting a letter of application addressed to the Principal via the Principal's PA, Mrs Natalee Friend. Applicants must have a high level of attendance and be in good academic and behavioural standing. The scholarship application requires candidates to address: name of applicant, category of sport, individual goals for the sport, how the candidate believes they would benefit from the scholarship, and why the candidate believes they are worthy of the scholarship. This scholarship was initiated by Swan alumnus and professional cyclist Luke Durbridge in 2015.

The application process involves completing and submitting the relevant SAS Non-Government Application Form. Parents/guardians receiving the ABSTUDY School Fees Allowance from Services Australia (Centrelink) are not eligible for the SAS. The scheme provides financial support based on demonstrated financial hardship and need.

eligibility_criteria: Currently enrolled in Year 6; Must be applying for entry to Year 7; Attending The King's College primary school or external primary schools; Must demonstrate high academic achievement
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered to students entering the Year 7 Catalyst: Academic Extension Program. Recipients must demonstrate ongoing commitment to academic excellence and participation in the extension program.

overview: The Beehive Montessori School offers a Bursary Scheme to assist families who demonstrate a commitment to Montessori education. The bursary is designed to support families experiencing temporary financial difficulties that threaten their children's continued attendance at the school.
description: The Beehive Montessori School's Bursary Scheme aims to assist parents with a demonstrated commitment to a Montessori education for their children who are experiencing temporary financial difficulties which threaten their children's continued attendance in the School. The Bursary Committee, composed of the Principal, the Treasurer, and the Chair of the Board or his or her nominee, considers applications and recommends to the Board an amount and a specific period for which a bursary may be granted. The exact financial value is determined on a case-by-case basis according to individual family circumstances and the availability of funds. The scheme acknowledges that families' financial situations may change and aims to provide temporary support during periods of hardship. Applications are considered individually, and bursaries are awarded for specific periods as determined by the committee.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated commitment to a Montessori education for their children; Currently experiencing temporary financial difficulties that threaten continued school attendance; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: The Bursary Committee considers applications and recommends to the Board an amount and a specific period for which a bursary may be granted. Bursaries are awarded on a temporary basis to assist families through periods of financial difficulty. The scheme is intended to support families with demonstrated commitment to Montessori education.

overview: Toodyay District High School offers scholarships to students continuing at the school. These scholarships promote the continuation of schooling by subsidising the recipient's school fees for Year 7 and Year 10 studies.
description: Toodyay District High School awards scholarships to continuing students as part of its commitment to supporting educational access in the Wheatbelt region. The scholarship provides school fee subsidies for Year 7 and Year 10 students. Scholarship winners are announced at the annual awards presentation night. The school aims to support students who wish to continue their education at the institution through financial assistance with fees. Specific details regarding the exact financial value, selection criteria, eligibility requirements, and application procedures are not detailed on the scholarship page.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students continuing at Toodyay District High School; Year 7 or Year 10 students
special_conditions: Scholarship winners are announced at the annual awards presentation night.

program_name: Tom Price SHS Follow the Dream Program
overview: Tom Price Senior High School partners with the Polly Farmer Foundation to deliver the Follow the Dream program for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in Years 7–12. The program supports students who aspire to successfully complete their secondary schooling through literacy and numeracy support, specialist tutoring, and personalised learning plans.
description: The Tom Price SHS Follow the Dream program is a voluntary support initiative designed for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students in years 7 through 12 who demonstrate commitment to their education. The program provides no direct financial scholarship value in terms of fee remission; rather, it offers in-kind educational support including afternoon tutoring sessions, literacy and numeracy specialist support, and individualised learning plans. Students benefit from enrichment activities, mentoring, and case management services aimed at enabling completion of Year 12 and transition to tertiary studies, apprenticeships, traineeships or employment. The program operates in partnership with the Polly Farmer Foundation and the Department of Education WA, with funding provided for tutoring and support services rather than direct student scholarships.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander background; Years 7-12 enrolment; Minimum 90% attendance record; Positive behaviour and conduct; Demonstrated commitment to learning; Capability and aptitude for learning
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Applications are assessed based on 90% Attendance, Positive Behaviours, Commitment, and Capability for learning. Information and Application Forms are provided to all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at the school. Interested students should return completed forms to the Program Coordinator.

overview: Tranby College offers the Academic Scholarship to recognize and support students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement and potential. The scholarship provides significant tuition fee reduction to high-performing students across Years 5-12.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students with outstanding academic performance and strong school reports. Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) and are awarded based on the applicant addressing specific criteria, current school report performance, and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview. Students are assessed on their academic merit and commitment to excellence. The scholarship supports students from Year 5 through Year 12, recognizing sustained academic achievement across secondary years.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Outstanding academic performance required; Current school report performance assessed; Must participate in scholarship testing process; Must attend interview
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) and will be awarded based on the applicant addressing the specific criteria, current school report performance and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview.

overview: The Bright Futures Scholarship recognizes general excellence in Years 1-4 students, celebrating well-rounded achievement across academic and co-curricular pursuits. This scholarship supports students demonstrating comprehensive excellence in their learning journey.
description: The Bright Futures 'General Excellence' Scholarship is designed for Years 1-4 students who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas of school life. Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) based on the applicant addressing the specific criteria, current school report performance, and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview. This scholarship celebrates students who balance academic achievement with involvement in co-curricular activities and demonstrate the college values of respect, integrity, service, commitment and collaboration.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 1-4; Demonstrated general excellence across academic and co-curricular areas; Strong school report performance; Alignment with college values required
special_conditions: Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) and will be awarded based on the applicant addressing the specific criteria, current school report performance and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview.

overview: The Fulfilling Futures Scholarship recognizes general excellence in Years 5-12 students, supporting those who demonstrate well-rounded achievement and commitment to the college community. This scholarship celebrates comprehensive student development beyond academics.
description: The Fulfilling Futures 'General Excellence' Scholarship is designed for Years 5-12 students who demonstrate excellence across multiple dimensions of school life. Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) based on the applicant addressing specific criteria, current school report performance, and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview. This scholarship recognizes students who balance strong academic performance with meaningful involvement in co-curricular activities and exemplify the college values of respect, integrity, service, commitment and collaboration.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Years 5-12; Demonstrated general excellence across academic and co-curricular areas; Strong school report performance; Commitment to college values required
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) and will be awarded based on the applicant addressing the specific criteria, current school report performance and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview.

program_name: Sport, Performing Arts, Visual Arts and Music Scholarship
overview: Tranby College offers a comprehensive scholarship for students with outstanding ability in Sport, Performing Arts, Visual Arts or Music. This scholarship celebrates and supports children with exceptional talents and commitment in these specialized fields.
description: This scholarship recognizes students with outstanding ability in, and enthusiasm and commitment to Sport, Performing Arts, Visual Arts or Music. Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) based on the applicant meeting specific criteria, current school report performance, and the results of the scholarship testing process and interview. Applicants must provide evidence of their abilities and achievements through awards, audition videos, or examples of work. A reference or endorsement from a specialist in their field of interest is required, along with demonstrated sound academic performance and evidence of living the college values.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding ability in Sport, Performing Arts, Visual Arts or Music required; Evidence of abilities and achievements required (awards, audition video, examples of work); Reference or endorsement from specialist in field required; Sound academic performance required; Demonstration of living college values required
special_conditions: Applicants must provide evidence of abilities and achievements including awards, audition video, or examples of work. A reference or endorsement from a specialist in their field of interest is required. Recipients of this scholarship will also be considered for the Gifted and Talented Program.

program_name: The Spirit of Tranby - Community Service Scholarship
overview: The Spirit of Tranby Community Service Scholarship recognizes students already enrolled at Tranby College who demonstrate active involvement in local community service. This scholarship celebrates those who exemplify the college's commitment to service and community contribution.
description: The Spirit of Tranby Community Service Scholarship is open to current Tranby College students only who can demonstrate their active involvement in local community service activities. Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) based on demonstrated service contribution and alignment with college values. Applicants must evidence sound academic effort and application to their studies, together with examples of how they live the values of respect, integrity, service, commitment and collaboration. The scholarship recognizes the importance of service leadership within the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Tranby College students only; Active involvement in local community service activities required; Sound academic effort and application to studies required; Evidence of living college values required
special_conditions: Open to Tranby College students only. Applicants must demonstrate active involvement in local community service activities and evidence sound academic effort along with examples of how they live the college values.

overview: The First Nations Scholarship provides exceptional educational opportunities for children of First Nations heritage who have the potential to lead and excel within their community. This scholarship recognizes and supports Indigenous students across academic, sporting and other areas of excellence.
description: The First Nations Scholarship is designed to provide exceptional educational opportunities for children of First Nations heritage who have the potential to lead and/or excel within their community, whether in academics, sport or other areas. Scholarships provide a reduction in tuition fees (up to 100%) and are awarded based on demonstrated potential and commitment. The scholarship supports students across all year levels and operates as an open year-round application process, allowing flexibility for First Nations families. Recipients of this scholarship will also be considered for the college's Gifted and Talented Program.
eligibility_criteria: First Nations heritage required; Demonstrated potential to lead and/or excel in community required; Achievement across academics, sport or other areas recognized
special_conditions: The First Nations Scholarship is open all year round. Recipients of this scholarship will also be considered for the Gifted and Talented Program. The scholarship intends to provide exceptional educational opportunities for children of First Nations heritage.

program_name: Talkabout Program Bursaries and Sibling Discounts
overview: TSH offers limited bursaries and fee discounts for families enrolling in the Talkabout program. Sibling discounts of 5% are provided for second and third children, while families facing financial hardship can apply for bursary support to access the program.
description: TSH's Talkabout program, designed for children aged 18 months to 6 years with speech and language delays, offers flexible fee arrangements to support families. The standard fee structure provides full fees for the first child, with 5% discounts for second and third children. Limited bursaries are available to provide financial assistance to families experiencing hardship, ensuring equal access to this comprehensive speech and language program. The bursaries support program access for the duration of a child's enrollment. Families must contact the Enrolment Officer or visit the TSH website to complete bursary applications and discuss their specific circumstances. The program combines educational and therapeutic services delivered by specialists including speech pathologists, occupational therapists, psychologists, and audiologists.
eligibility_criteria: Families with financial need; Open to children aged 18 months to 6 years; Multiple class options available for different age groups
special_conditions: <cite index="42-1,42-2">First child pays full fees; second and third children receive 5% discount. Limited bursaries available. Contact the Enrolment Officer or visit the website for bursary applications.</cite>

overview: Trinity College offers Academic Scholarships to students in Year 3 applicable for entry to Year 4. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate an excellent work ethic, passion for academic performance, and exemplary conduct.
