description: Trinity College is offering Academic Scholarships to boys currently in Year 3 who wish to enter Year 4. Students selected must have an excellent work ethic, demonstrate a passion for academic performance and display exemplary conduct. Applicants are required to complete a free Trinity College aptitude examination. The scholarship provides financial support through tuition fee reduction, though the exact remission percentage is not specified on the current page. Applications for 2024 are now closed, and interested families should contact the Registrar for future application dates and details.
eligibility_criteria: Currently in Year 3; Excellent work ethic; Passion for academic performance; Exemplary conduct; Must sit free Trinity College aptitude examination
special_conditions: Applications for 2024 are now closed.

overview: Trinity College proudly offers Academic Scholarships available to boys entering Year 7. These scholarships provide up to 75% remission of tuition fees and are awarded based on performance in the free Trinity Scholarship Examination.
description: Trinity College offers scholarships to boys entering Year 7 which provide up to a 75% remission of tuition fees, representing a significant financial benefit for families. The scholarship is offered based on performance in the free Trinity Scholarship Examination, which all applicants must complete at no cost. Students must demonstrate strong academic ability through this assessment. The scholarship provides substantial fee reduction for the duration of the student's enrolment at the College, making it accessible to talented students regardless of financial circumstances. Applicants should contact the Registrar for specific examination dates and application deadlines.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Must sit free Trinity Scholarship Examination; Academic performance in examination

overview: Trinity College offers Music Scholarships to boys who are talented instrumentalists and/or vocalists entering Years 4, 7, or Year 11. Applications are welcomed from both current students and external applicants.
description: Trinity College offers Music Scholarships available to boys entering Years 4, 7, or Year 11 who demonstrate talent as instrumentalists and/or vocalists. Applications are welcomed from current students as well as external applicants, providing flexible entry pathways. Auditions for Music Scholarships are held at the College by arrangement with the Director of Music. For students applying for the Pipe Organ Scholarship specifically, a 3rd-grade AMEB standard on piano is required as a prerequisite. The scholarship provides tuition fee reduction, though the exact percentage is not specified. Students interested should contact the Director of Music to arrange an audition at a mutually convenient time.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Years 4, 7, or 11; Talented instrumentalist and/or vocalist; For Pipe Organ: 3rd-grade AMEB standard on piano required; Current or external applicants eligible
special_conditions: Auditions held at the College by arrangement with the Director of Music. A 3rd-grade AMEB standard on piano is required for students applying for the Pipe Organ Scholarship.

overview: The Edmund Rice Bursary is awarded to students entering Year 7 who may not be able to attend Trinity College due to extenuating family circumstances. The student's academic records and school engagement records are reviewed as part of the assessment.
description: The Edmund Rice Bursary is designed to support boys entering Year 7 who face financial barriers to attending Trinity College due to extenuating family circumstances. The bursary recognises that financial hardship should not prevent deserving students from accessing a Trinity education. Applications are assessed holistically, with consideration given to the student's academic records and school engagement records, ensuring that both potential and circumstances are evaluated. The specific financial value of the bursary is determined on a case-by-case basis depending on individual family circumstances. Interested families should contact the Registrar at registrar@trinity.wa.edu.au to inquire about eligibility and the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Entering Year 7; Extenuating family circumstances; Academic records reviewed; School engagement records reviewed
special_conditions: Academic records and school engagement records will be reviewed. Applicants must demonstrate extenuating family circumstances.

overview: The Refugee Bursary is available to boys of Refugee background entering Year 7 who are able to demonstrate financial need and would benefit from a Trinity education.
description: Trinity College offers a Refugee Bursary specifically designed to support boys of refugee background who are entering Year 7. The bursary is intended for students who can demonstrate financial need and would benefit significantly from a Trinity College education. This initiative reflects the College's commitment to providing educational opportunities to students from disadvantaged backgrounds and aligns with the Edmund Rice tradition of supporting those in need. The specific amount of financial assistance is determined based on individual circumstances and family need. Interested families should contact the Registrar to discuss eligibility and begin the application process.
eligibility_criteria: Boys of Refugee background; Entering Year 7; Able to demonstrate financial need; Would benefit from Trinity education
special_conditions: Refugee background required. Financial need must be demonstrated.

program_name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Bursary
overview: The Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Bursary is available to boys of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background entering Year 7 who are able to demonstrate financial need and would benefit from a Trinity education.
description: Trinity College offers the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Bursary, established by the Trinity College Foundation in 2012, to support Indigenous boys entering Year 7. The bursary is designed for students who can demonstrate financial need and would benefit from a Trinity College education, ensuring that Indigenous students have access to quality education regardless of financial circumstances. The program recognises the importance of educational opportunity within Indigenous communities. The specific financial support provided is tailored to individual family circumstances and need. For more information about this bursary, students and families should contact the Trinity College Business Manager, Jan Hurley on (08) 9219 1126, or visit the Scholarships and Bursaries page.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background; Entering Year 7; Able to demonstrate financial need; Would benefit from Trinity education
special_conditions: Established by the Trinity College Foundation in 2012. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background required.

overview: The TOBA (Trinity Old Boys Association) Bursary is available to boys entering Year 7 in 2025 who are the sons or grandsons of Old Boys and who would otherwise be unable to attend Trinity College for financial reasons.
description: The TOBA Bursary is a specialised financial assistance program administered by the Trinity Old Boys Association, designed to support the sons and grandsons of Trinity alumni. The bursary is available to boys entering Year 7 in 2025 who would otherwise be unable to attend Trinity College due to financial constraints. The TOBA Bursary covers 50% of tuition fees, providing substantial relief for families and ensuring that the children of Trinity Old Boys have the opportunity to benefit from a Trinity education. This program strengthens the intergenerational connection to the school while maintaining commitment to financial accessibility. Eligible families should contact the Registrar to inquire about application procedures and requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Sons or grandsons of Trinity Old Boys; Entering Year 7 in 2025; Unable to attend Trinity College for financial reasons
special_conditions: Applicant must be son or grandson of Trinity Old Boys. Available for 2025 entry to Year 7.

school_name: Ursula Frayne Catholic College - Primary Campus

overview: Ursula Frayne Catholic College offers the Aboriginal Student Bursary to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families seeking Catholic secondary education. The bursary recognises commitment to schooling and assists students who might otherwise be unable to access a Catholic secondary education.
description: The Aboriginal Student Bursary is designed to assist Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families and helps with the cost of tuition fees. The bursary value and duration vary based on individual circumstances and school determination. Applicants must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander and demonstrate a desire to be educated in a Catholic school. Applicants are expected to have established strong foundations for learning and aspire to complete Year 12. Applications are processed on a rolling basis with limited bursaries available in each year level. Bursaries do not cover boarding fees. The selection process involves submission of an application form with supporting documentation demonstrating educational commitment and family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent; Desire to be educated in a Catholic school; Established strong foundations for learning; Aspiration to complete Year 12; Open to secondary year levels at Ursula Frayne Catholic College
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Limited number of bursaries available per year level. Bursaries do not cover boarding fees. Applications are invited from Aboriginal families seeking Catholic education. Early application encouraged due to limited availability.

program_name: Health Care Card / Concession Card Tuition Fee Discount
overview: Ursula Frayne Catholic College provides automatic tuition fee discounts to holders of eligible Centrelink or Department of Veterans' Affairs concession cards. The discount recognises families with demonstrated financial need and assists with access to Catholic education.
description: Eligible holders of Health Care Cards, Pensioner Concession Cards, or other Centrelink Concession Cards receive either a substantial discount for Health Care Card holders or a 15% tuition fee reduction for other concession card holders. Students holding valid Concession Cards also qualify for a 15% reduction (conditions apply). The discount is automatically applied once the cardholder presents their card and completes a simple form during the first term of each year. For students in Years 7-12, the discounted tuition fee is $1,500 per annum ($37.50 per week on a standard 40-week year). For students in Years K-6, the discounted tuition fee is $300 per annum ($7.50 per week). Other specific charges still apply separately, including Building Levy, subject levies, excursions, camps, and resources levy.
eligibility_criteria: Holder of a Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card (for maximum discount); Holder of other Centrelink Concession Cards or Department of Veterans' Affairs Pensioner Concession Card (15% reduction); Student Concession Card holders (15% reduction with conditions); Card must be presented and form completed during first term
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12, Kindergarten, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Discount requires presentation of valid concession card and completion of simple form during first term each year. Health Care Card holders receive higher discount than other concession card types. Student Concession Card reductions have specific conditions. Discount applies only to tuition fees; other charges still apply separately.

program_name: Application for Review of School Tuition Fees
overview: Ursula Frayne Catholic College offers families the opportunity to apply for a review of school tuition fees if they experience financial hardship. This discretionary program provides individual assessment of exceptional financial circumstances to support continued access to education.
description: An Application for Review of School Tuition Fees form is available to parents who feel they need special assistance to meet their financial commitments to the College. The application process requires parents to provide details about their income and expenditure to support the request for fee assistance. All applications are treated confidentially. Approval and fee reduction amounts are determined on a case-by-case basis following individual assessment of the family's financial circumstances. Application forms are available from the Front Office of either campus (Balmoral Street for primary or Duncan Street for secondary). The school encourages families experiencing financial difficulty to contact the college directly to discuss options.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship; Inability to meet regular tuition fee commitments; Willingness to provide detailed financial information; Open to all year levels
special_conditions: Requires submission of detailed income and expenditure information. All applications treated confidentially. Approval determined on case-by-case basis. Applications available from Front Office at either campus.

overview: Waddington Primary School offers an Educational Scholarship through The Smith Family to eligible families. The program provides two annual payments to assist with purchasing school-related items such as stationery and uniforms. This program recognises families who require financial support to access educational resources.
description: The Smith Family Educational Scholarship is available to all Waddington Primary School families with a Health Care Card. The scholarship provides two payments per year to help families purchase school stationery, uniforms, and other educational necessities. The amount varies based on the number of children in the family, as multiple children can enrol in the program. Eligibility requires only a valid Health Care Card, making this accessible to families requiring financial assistance. Students in Kindergarten to Year 6 are eligible to participate in this program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students in Kindergarten to Year 6; Family must hold a valid Health Care Card; Open to all Waddington families
year_levels: Kindergarten, Year 1, Year 2, Year 3, Year 4, Year 5, Year 6
special_conditions: Multiple children from the same family can all enrol in the program. Interested families should contact Ms Chivers or Amy (Chaplain) at the school for more information and to apply.

overview: Waddington Primary School offers a Concession/Pension Card Primary Scholarship to students whose carers hold a valid Concession or Pension Card. The scholarship provides annual payments to support school costs and continues into secondary school if attendance requirements are maintained. This program recognises students from families with reduced incomes.
description: The Concession/Pension Card Primary Scholarship provides direct financial support to eligible students at Waddington Primary School. Primary students receive $478 per year, and if attendance is maintained at 85% or above, the scholarship continues throughout high school with an increased payment of $772 per year. The scholarship supports students whose carers hold a valid Concession or Pension Card, which indicates eligibility for financial assistance. Annual payments are made to help offset school-related expenses. To maintain the scholarship, students must ensure attendance remains at or above the 85% threshold.
eligibility_criteria: Students whose carer holds a Concession Card or Pension Card; Attendance must be maintained at 85% or above; Open to all students at Waddington Primary School
value_aud: $478 per year for primary students; $772 per year if continued to high school
special_conditions: Attendance must be maintained at 85% or above to retain the scholarship. If attendance is maintained at the required level, the scholarship will continue throughout the student's high school years with an increased annual payment of $772. Failure to maintain the attendance requirement may result in loss of the scholarship.

school_name: Warnbro Community High School Education Support Centre

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College offers the Specialist Australian Rules Football Program to provide male and female students with ongoing development in the skills and attributes needed to play Australian Rules Football. The program aims to develop each student's individual strength of character and personal skills which are essential in emerging as leaders, both on and off the field as well as within the College.
description: The Wanneroo Secondary College Specialist Australian Football Program is designed to provide male and female students with ongoing development in the skills and attributes needed to play Australian Rules Football. The program aims to develop each student's individual strength of character and personal skills which are essential in emerging as leaders, both on and off the field as well as within the College. Wanneroo Secondary College has a strong reputation in the football community. The program is one of a select few endorsed by both the Department of Education and the West Australian Football Commission. In addition, the school has strong links with the West Coast Eagles, Fremantle Dockers and West Perth Football Clubs which provides students with many outstanding opportunities in community football. The program is not a scholarship and offers no direct financial value, but provides elite coaching and training.
eligibility_criteria: Students applying for the program must participate in selection trials; Open to male and female students; Students must be residing within Western Australia; Selection based on skills assessment during trial sessions; In Area applicants must complete a Specialist Program Application form and a General Enrolment form; Out of Area applicants must complete a Specialist Program Application form and an Application to Enrol form
special_conditions: Registrations for the Australian Football Specialist Program Trials open in March and close end of April each year. Shortly after the registration process closes, all applicants are emailed a designated trial timeslot. The trials are conducted at Wanneroo Secondary College during which each students' skills are assessed. The selection process is detailed, and the selectors' decision is final. All applicants are advised via email of the outcome of their trial session. Player development is designed to complement participation in community football, WAFL Club Development Programs and/or State 15's/16's and 18's Program.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College's premier Specialist Performing Arts (SPA) Program offers students the chance to hone advanced performance skills and take their training to the next level whilst completing their regular secondary studies. The program focuses on training individuals to be well-rounded performers with opportunities in dance, drama and music disciplines.
description: Wanneroo Secondary College's premier Specialist Performing Arts (SPA) Program offers students the chance to hone advanced performance skills and take their training to the next level whilst completing their regular secondary studies. The program provides no direct financial value but offers elite training in three disciplines: dance, drama, and music. Students commence studying two out of the three disciplines with a combined performing arts class, and in years 9 and 10 some students may study all three disciplines along with their combined specialist class. The program extends the boundaries of the regular curriculum by deep diving into the world of performing arts and working with a variety of styles and practitioners. Students are trained to be triple threat performers with consistent learning and performance opportunities. Selection is through audition process held in April each year.
eligibility_criteria: Intake is at the commencement of Year 7; Applications submitted when students are in Year 6 for enrolments commencing the following calendar year; Open to students living anywhere in Western Australia, school boundaries do not apply; In Area applicants must complete a Specialist Program Application form and a General Enrolment form; Out of Area applicants must complete a Specialist Program Application form and an Application to Enrol form; Students must specify their subjects and instrument choice on the application sheet; Selection based on audition performance
special_conditions: Registrations for the Specialist Performing Arts Auditions open in March and close end of April each year (unless otherwise specified). Shortly after the registration process closes, all applicants are emailed a designated audition timeslot. Auditions are conducted at Wanneroo Secondary College during which each student's skills are assessed. The auditions for all three disciplines take place on the same day, within the allocated timeslot. Students will be assessed individually and also in group settings by staff to evaluate their suitability for the program. This usually occurs on the same day as academic extension testing.

overview: The Academic Extension (AE) Program at Wanneroo Secondary College aims to encourage and stimulate students to excel at school. The program provides no financial value but offers enriched curriculum and extended learning opportunities for academically advanced students.
description: The Academic Extension (AE) Program aims to encourage and stimulate students to excel at school. The program places particular emphasis on developing an understanding of different learning styles and skills in inquiry, analytical, critical and creative thinking. Classroom teachers aim to provide a stimulating environment to give students the skills and the time to explore an issue, text or problem in more depth and communicate their conclusions more effectively. The curriculum studied by AE students is greatly enriched and extended to provide learning opportunities that assist them to maximize their talents and abilities and open pathways to University. Entry can be gained into the Academic Extension High Performance program by completing a selection test.
eligibility_criteria: Entry to the program requires completion of a selection test; Open to students entering Year 7; Test-based selection process; Applicants must demonstrate academic aptitude
special_conditions: Academic Extension testing usually occurs on the same day as the Specialist Performing Arts auditions, typically in April. Selection is based on performance on the selection test. Program provides enriched and extended curriculum focusing on inquiry-based learning, analytical, critical and creative thinking skills.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College offers access to the Instrumental & Vocal Music (IMSS) program, providing students the opportunity to master their chosen instrument and delve into the intricacies of musical theory. This is a high-performance music program offered to students who demonstrate musical ability.
description: Wanneroo Secondary College offers access to the renowned Instrumental & Vocal Music School Services (IMSS) program, providing students the opportunity to master their chosen instrument and delve into the intricacies of musical theory. Students in the specialist music program are immersed in contemporary, jazz and western art music, as well as learning sound engineering, song-writing and stage-craft. Students also have access to playing in various ensembles like rock bands, concert band, guitar ensemble, funk band and choir. The program focuses on providing performance opportunity at all times, with students performing on campus at lunch time concerts, formal showcase events and summer concerts. There is no financial value provided by this program, but students receive high-quality instrumental instruction.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students demonstrating musical aptitude; Students must be able to play an instrument or sing; Selection based on musical ability and audition; Open to students in Years 7 onwards
special_conditions: Selection into the IMSS program is based on musical ability and audition. Students have access to Department instruction on one instrument only. If a student is learning from a private teacher they may not learn the same instrument from a Department teacher. Students are expected to attend weekly instrumental lessons and participate in school ensembles.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College offers a Robotics and Coding high-performance program for students interested in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. This program provides no financial value but offers enriched learning opportunities in coding and robotics.
description: Wanneroo Secondary College offers a Robotics and Coding high-performance program for students interested in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. The college has dedicated facilities for robotics and coding and offers extended curriculum opportunities in these areas. Students learn practical coding skills including JavaScript and Python programming, and gain hands-on experience with robotics projects. The program includes intensive workshops such as MadMaker, a six-week intensive coding program in partnership with Curtin University. The program provides no direct financial value but offers elite-level STEM education and expert instruction.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students interested in STEM disciplines; Selection based on aptitude and interest; Available to students from lower school onwards
special_conditions: The college has dedicated facilities for robotics and coding. The program includes partnerships with external organizations such as Curtin University for intensive learning experiences.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College is the host school for the Follow the Dream program known locally as Wanna Kaditj, which supports the aspirations of Aboriginal students to successfully complete their secondary schooling. The program provides specialist tuition support and mentoring.
description: Follow the Dream is delivered at Wanneroo Secondary College as the Wanna Kaditj program and supports the aspirations of Aboriginal students to successfully complete their secondary schooling. The program is delivered by a committed team of tutors who provide specialist tuition support and mentoring to Aboriginal students. While not a traditional scholarship or bursary, this program provides targeted academic support and mentoring specifically for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students, supporting their retention and success in secondary education. The program offers no direct financial payments but provides essential educational support services.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students; Must be enrolled at Wanneroo Secondary College; Program aims to support completion of secondary schooling
special_conditions: The program is delivered by a committed team of tutors who provide specialist tuition support and mentoring. Follow the Dream supports the aspirations of Aboriginal students to successfully complete their secondary schooling.

overview: Waroona District High School students whose families hold Centrelink or Veterans' Affairs cards may be eligible for the Secondary Assistance Scheme, which is provided by the Western Australian Department of Education to assist with secondary schooling costs.
description: The Western Australian Department of Education provides financial assistance to eligible families with secondary (Year 7 to Year 10) schooling costs. The allowance consists of two components: Educational Program Allowance ($235) and Clothing Allowance ($300), which is paid directly to the parent/carer for other expenses such as uniforms or stationery supplies. To be eligible, the parent/carer must hold a Centrelink or Veterans' Affairs card which lists the student as a dependant. Applications must be submitted by the specified deadline each year. Parents/carers in receipt of the ABSTUDY School Allowance from the Department of Human Resources (Centrelink) are not eligible for this scheme.
eligibility_criteria: Parent/carer must hold a Centrelink or Veterans' Affairs card listing the student as a dependant; Student must be enrolled in secondary (Year 7 to Year 10); Parents/carers receiving ABSTUDY School Allowance are not eligible
special_conditions: Applications cannot be processed after the specified deadline. Scholarship payment is made directly to parent/carer. Students receiving ABSTUDY School Allowance are not eligible but may apply for the ABSTUDY Supplement Allowance instead.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College's Academic Extension Program aims to encourage and stimulate students to excel at school. The program is designed for gifted and talented students entering Year 7 who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and strong learning potential.
description: The Academic Extension (AE) Program places particular emphasis on developing an understanding of different learning styles and skills in inquiry, analytical, critical and creative thinking. The curriculum studied by AE students is greatly enriched and extended to provide learning opportunities that assist them to maximize their talents and abilities and open pathways to University. Entry can be gained into the Academic Extension High Performance program by completing a selection test. Classroom teachers aim to provide a stimulating environment, to give students the skills and the time to explore an issue, text or problem in more depth and communicate their conclusions more effectively. A number of places will be reserved for students whose NAPLAN results are meritorious.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Selection test required; Some places reserved for students with strong NAPLAN results; Open to all school sectors
special_conditions: Audition/testing usually occurs on the same day as the Specialist Performing Arts auditions.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College's premier Specialist Performing Arts (SPA) Program offers students the chance to hone advanced performance skills and take their training to the next level whilst completing their regular secondary studies. The program focuses on training well-rounded and experienced performers in dance, drama and music disciplines.
description: The Specialist Performing Arts Program offers exciting and consistent learning and performance opportunities in the dance, drama and music disciplines. The program nurtures talent and hones skills for success in the performing arts industry. Students are assessed individually and in group settings by staff to evaluate their suitability for the program. The program provides performance opportunities including the annual Choreography Night, Dance Showcase, and invitations to Nationals. Students train in a broad range of genres and perform many times throughout their time in the program. There is no specific financial value listed; this is a non-monetary specialist program. Registrations open in March and close end of April each year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Audition required; Students can come from any school; In Area applicants need Specialist Program Application form and General Enrolment form; Out of Area applicants need Specialist Program Application form and Application to Enrol form
special_conditions: Audition requirements are specific to each discipline (dance, drama, music). All dance students complete the same audition as will all drama students. Discipline must be specified on application. Instrument choice must be identified on application for music students.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College's Specialist Australian Rules Football Program is designed to provide male and female students with ongoing development in the skills and attributes needed to play Australian Rules Football. The program aims to develop each student's individual strength of character and personal skills which are essential in emerging as leaders.
description: The Specialist Australian Football Program is one of a select few endorsed by both the Department of Education and the West Australian Football Commission. The program is designed to complement participation in community football, WAFL Club Development Programs and/or State 15's/16's and 18's Program. The college has strong links with the West Coast Eagles, Fremantle Dockers and West Perth Football Clubs which provide students with outstanding opportunities. The program is committed to providing students with the highest standard of coaching and teaching by expertly qualified and experienced staff and provides players with a holistic footballing education including pathways post school. Player development includes day trips to primary schools, gym sessions, beach sessions and other activities. Intake is at Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 and school boundaries do not apply.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9 or 10; Trial and application process open to students living anywhere in Western Australia; School boundaries do not apply; In Area applicants need Specialist Program Application form and General Enrolment form; Out of Area applicants need Specialist Program Application form and Application to Enrol form
special_conditions: Registrations for trials open in March and close end of April each year. Shortly after registration closes, all applicants are emailed a designated trial timeslot for the day. A reply email serves as official acceptance of the offer of placement in the program.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College offers a High Performance Program in Instrumental Music aimed at students who show a keen interest and aptitude in playing and performing. The program provides regular instrumental lessons, ensemble rehearsals and performance opportunities at school events, public venues and competitions.
description: This program is aimed at students who show a keen interest and aptitude in playing and performing and are prepared to make an ongoing commitment to musical activity. Students are given weekly small-group instrumental lessons from the Instrumental Music Schools Services (IMMS), weekly ensemble rehearsals and perform regularly at school events and public venues and competitions. Instruments offered through IMMS include clarinet, saxophone, flute, classical guitar, bass guitar, contemporary guitar, percussion and vocals. Students participating in this program gain access to the school's music facilities and are part of the instrumental music community. Application involves submission of the Specialist Instrumental Music Application Form.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students with keen interest and aptitude in instrumental music; Prepared to make ongoing commitment to musical activity; Weekly lessons and rehearsal participation required
special_conditions: Students approved for this program are guaranteed a place at the school even if they live outside the local-intake area. This program is administered through the High Performance Programs division.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College offers a Robotics and Coding Program as part of its high-performance programs in the Lower School. The program is designed to challenge and extend students' intellectual abilities through practical exploration of coding and robotics technology.
description: The Robotics and Coding Program is one of several high-performance programs available at Wanneroo Secondary College designed to challenge and extend a student's emotional and intellectual intelligence. This program provides students with dedicated facilities and expert instruction in robotics and coding. Students gain hands-on experience with coding languages and robotics platforms, developing problem-solving and computational thinking skills. The program complements the regular curriculum and is available as part of the school's enrichment offerings. Year 9 students can take Robotics as a year-long course, and Computer Coding is available as an elective subject. The program aims to prepare students for technology careers and higher education pathways in STEM fields.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Lower School students (Years 7-10); Interest in robotics and coding required; Selection varies by year level and course structure
special_conditions: Robotics (Year long) subject carries a charge of $66. Computer Coding is available as an elective subject with associated charges.

overview: Wanneroo Secondary College is the host school for the Follow the Dream program (known locally as Wanna Kaditj). The program supports the aspirations of Aboriginal students to successfully complete their secondary schooling through specialist tuition support and mentoring.
description: Follow the Dream supports the aspirations of Aboriginal students to successfully complete their secondary schooling and is delivered by a committed team of tutors who provide specialist tuition support and mentoring. Achieving academic success lies at the heart of the program. Follow The Dream encourages students to 'Dream Big' with purpose and in a setting that creates a sense of belonging. The program provides sustained academic support throughout students' secondary education. On completion of school, Follow The Dream students are well prepared to embark on successful career pathways and to identify as leaders and role models amongst their peers, families and communities. Students receive dedicated tutor support, mentoring and guidance to maximize academic achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Aboriginal students; Enrollment at Wanneroo Secondary College required; Commitment to the program and academic pathways required
special_conditions: This is a support program administered through the college's Aboriginal Education division. Students receive tuition support and mentoring from a committed team of tutors. For more information visit https://pff.com.au/programs/programs/follow-the-dream/

overview: The Department of Education's Secondary Assistance Scheme is available to eligible families through Wanneroo Secondary College. This scheme provides financial support for secondary schooling costs to help families meet the costs of education.
description: Eligible families can access the Department of Education's Secondary Assistance Scheme, which provides financial support for secondary schooling costs. The college recognizes that some families may require assistance with college payments and offers this state-funded program as one option. A link to application forms and eligibility requirements is emailed to families and made available on the school webpage. Families holding a Health Care or Pensioner Concession card that complete the Secondary Assistance forms receive $115 per child specifically for dress code/uniform costs. The scheme helps manage overall educational costs and may include support for contributions, charges and other schooling expenses.
eligibility_criteria: Eligible families as determined by Department of Education; Health Care or Pensioner Concession card holders eligible for uniform assistance; Application required; Form available from Administration Office or school webpage
special_conditions: Concession card holders receive $115 per child for uniform costs. Applicants must complete Secondary Assistance forms available from Administration or the school website. Payment plans are also available to all families to spread payments over time.

school_name: West Coast Language Development Centre - Poseidon Campus
West Coast Language Development Centre - East Hamersley Campus
West Coast Secondary Education Support Centre

program_name: Specialist Australian Rules Football Program Scholarship
overview: Warwick Senior High School offers scholarships for the Specialist Australian Rules Football Program to recognise students demonstrating exceptional talent in Australian Rules Football. These entry-level scholarships support Year 7 students entering the Department of Education approved specialist program.
description: The Specialist Australian Rules Football Program provides entry scholarships valued at $100.00 each for Year 7 students. Two scholarships are awarded annually to students selected through the General Aptitude Test, football trials/skills assessment, and primary school reports. The program runs over six years and allows students to balance their football commitment with both VET and ATAR pathways. Selection is based on demonstrated sporting ability, aptitude testing results, and staff references from primary schools. Recipients must meet criteria for selection and maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated exceptional sporting ability in Australian Rules Football; General Aptitude Test completion required; Primary school report required; References from primary school staff considered
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program. Scholarship is for entry to Year 7 only. Students are expected to become affiliated with the football program and may continue with their local football teams.

overview: Warwick Senior High School offers scholarships for its Department of Education approved Specialist Netball Program to support talented Year 7 netball players. The school is one of only 6 tier 1 specialist netball schools in Western Australia.
description: The Specialist Netball Program offers entry scholarships valued at $175.00 each for Year 7 students entering one of Western Australia's premier netball programs. Two scholarships are awarded annually based on skill trials, General Aptitude Test results, and primary school reports. The program provides students with a minimum of 4 hours per week of netball practice and study, access to experienced coaching staff, and WACE examiners. Students must be willing to affiliate with Netball WA and contribute to the wider netball community. Selection is based on demonstrated netball talent and academic potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated exceptional netball ability and skill; General Aptitude Test completion required; Primary school report required; Willingness to affiliate with Netball WA required; References from primary school staff considered
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program. Students must be willing to become affiliated with Netball WA and are expected to make contributions to the wider netball community. Skill trial required as part of selection process. Annual review of program participation.

overview: Warwick Senior High School offers scholarships for its Dance Elite Program to recognise students with exceptional talent and dedication in dance. The program supports both foundational and advanced dance training across multiple genres.
description: The Dance Elite Program offers entry scholarships valued at $150.00 each for Year 7 students with demonstrated dance ability. Two scholarships are awarded annually to students selected through the General Aptitude Test, dance auditions/trials, and primary school reports. The program builds fundamental movement skills and teaches students various dance genres including Jazz, Tap, Contemporary, Hip Hop, Cheerleading, Folk, Creative, and Gymnastics from Years 7-10. Students develop strength, flexibility, coordination, and musicality while using their body as a medium for artistic expression. Years 11-12 students continue with Dance as a General course with both theory and practical components. Selection is based on demonstrated dance ability and artistic potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated exceptional dancing ability; General Aptitude Test completion required; Dance audition/trial required; Primary school report required; References from primary school staff considered
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program. Dance audition is a key component of selection. Students participate in the annual Dancefest event where approximately 30 routines are performed by over 100 students. Scholarship is for entry to Year 7 only.

overview: Warwick Senior High School offers scholarships for its Music Elite Program to support students with exceptional musical talent and commitment. The program provides practical and theoretical music training across a range of instruments and vocal studies.
description: The Music Elite Program offers entry scholarships valued at $100.00 each for Year 7 students demonstrating exceptional musical ability. Three scholarships are awarded annually to students selected through the General Aptitude Test, music auditions, and primary school reports. The program encourages students to enjoy making music through practical and theoretical music training. Students may continue IMSS (Instrumental Music Support Scheme) lessons from primary school or audition for the IMSS Beginner Instrument Program. Instruments offered include Flute, Saxophone, Clarinet, Trumpet, Trombone, Guitar, Bass, Drums, and Vocals. Classroom Music is a compulsory component for all students from Years 7-10. Selection is based on demonstrated musical ability and artistic potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Must be currently enrolled in Year 6; Demonstrated exceptional musical ability; General Aptitude Test completion required; Music audition required; Primary school report required; References from primary school staff considered; Students must commit to IMSS lessons or beginner instrument program
special_conditions: Recipients must maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program. Music audition is required for selection. IMSS lessons are mandatory for participants. Students who continue IMSS lessons from primary school or participate in the IMSS Beginner Instrument Program (Year 7 only) are eligible. Classroom Music is compulsory. Scholarship is for entry to Year 7 only. To schedule an audition, interested students/families should email warwick.shs.enquiries@education.wa.edu.au.

overview: Warwick Senior High School offers the Academic Extension Program to recognise and support students demonstrating exceptional academic ability. The program provides acceleration and enrichment in English, Mathematics, Science, and Humanities and Social Sciences.
description: The Academic Extension Program is a focus area at Warwick Senior High School providing accelerated learning for high-achieving students. In Years 7, 8, 9, and 10, students are tested and placed in Academic Extension classes in English, Mathematics, Science, and Humanities and Social Sciences. Extension work, programs, and activities are embedded in the teaching and learning programs to accelerate and challenge students to achieve beyond their limits. The program prepares students for ATAR courses in Years 11 and 12. Selection is based on testing results, and students must meet criteria for selection. Recipients must maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program throughout their secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7; Academic testing required for placement; Demonstrated academic ability in top achievement levels; Must maintain academic and school behaviour standards; Can be entered via Expression of Interest followed by testing at Years 7, 8, 9, and 10
special_conditions: Selection is based on testing results and academic ability. All students must meet criteria for selection and maintain academic and school behaviour standards to remain in the program. The initial application for enrolment is by Expression of Interest followed by testing. Students can apply at multiple year levels (7, 8, 9, 10). Contact the school on 08 9345 6430 to request an application package and testing information.

school_name: West Coast Steiner School - Mayfair Street Campus
West Coast Language Development Centre - Quinns Beach Campus
Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Morawa
overview: The MFIG Year 10 Scholarship recognises outstanding achievement and potential in students entering Year 10 at Morawa College of Agriculture. This scholarship is funded by the Mingenew Irwin Group and provides financial support to deserving students who demonstrate strong academic and behavioural commitment.
description: The MFIG Year 10 scholarship of $2,000 is available to students entering Morawa College of Agriculture. The funds are held by the college and can be used for expenses such as uniforms, excursions, camps, boarding fees and course fees. Selection is based on academic achievement according to school reports, attitude and behaviour from previous school reports, enthusiasm for agriculture as a career pathway, and student willingness to become a residential boarder. The scholarship recognises students who have worked to the best of their ability and demonstrates a commitment to agricultural education. Recipients must show likelihood of continuing a career in agriculture after school completion.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 10; Must be currently enrolled in Year 9; Demonstrated academic achievement according to school reports and principal's comments; Positive attitude and behaviour based on most recent school report from previous school; Must demonstrate enthusiasm for agriculture as the main reason for attending Morawa College of Agriculture; Must be planning to be a residential boarder at the campus
special_conditions: Scholarship funds will be held by the college. Recipient must be planning to be a residential boarder. Funds can be used for uniforms, excursions, camps, boarding fees and course fees. The recipient must, in the opinion of staff, be likely to continue a career in agriculture after the completion of school.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Morawa
overview: The RABOBANK Scholarship is available to students entering Morawa College of Agriculture who demonstrate exceptional commitment and potential in their agricultural studies and personal development.
description: The RABOBANK Scholarship is offered in partnership with RABOBANK and is available to students entering Morawa College of Agriculture. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate exceptional potential in agriculture and related industries. Funds can be applied toward various educational expenses including uniforms, excursions, camps, boarding fees and course fees. Selection is based on academic performance, demonstrated attitude and behaviour, and clear enthusiasm for pursuing an agricultural career. Students must demonstrate the willingness to persist through challenges and commitment to 100% effort in their work.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 10; Must demonstrate academic commitment; Must show positive attitude and behaviour; Must demonstrate enthusiasm for agriculture; Must be willing to commit to residential boarding; Must show likelihood of continuing agricultural career pathway
special_conditions: Funds held by college for educational expenses. Recipient must demonstrate willingness to persist against adversity and maintain 100% effort in completion of work. Recipient must show likelihood of continuing in agriculture career after school completion.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Morawa
overview: The James and Rose Coombe Scholarships are designed to support promising students from country public schools who face financial barriers to secondary education. These scholarships provide assistance to students whose families are unable to meet the cost of secondary schooling without additional support.
description: The James and Rose Coombe Scholarships are offered to students who attend public schools situated outside a radius of 65 kilometres from Perth. The scholarships are specifically awarded to help promising country public school scholars whose parents or carers are unable, without assistance, to meet the cost of secondary school education. These scholarships recognise the potential of rural students and provide financial support to ensure that geographic and economic barriers do not prevent access to quality agricultural education at Morawa College of Agriculture. The funds support essential educational expenses including accommodation, uniforms, course materials and other costs associated with residential study.
eligibility_criteria: Must attend a public school situated outside a radius of 65 kilometres from Perth; Must be a promising student demonstrating academic potential; Parents or carers must be unable to meet secondary school education costs without assistance; Must meet entry requirements for Morawa College of Agriculture
special_conditions: Must reside outside 65km radius from Perth. Assessment based on financial need and demonstrated academic potential. Funds used to cover secondary school education costs that families cannot otherwise meet.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Morawa
overview: The Resilience and Perseverance Award recognises students who demonstrate exceptional determination and the willingness to overcome adversity. This award celebrates students who persist through challenges, complete their work with dedication, and show commitment to agricultural careers.
description: This award celebrates the willingness of the recipient to keep on going against adversity, to stay behind and finish jobs that need doing and to give 100% effort in the completion of work. The award recognises students who demonstrate exceptional determination and resilience in the face of challenges. Selection is based on demonstrated perseverance, the ability to overcome obstacles, commitment to completing tasks thoroughly, and overall academic and practical performance. The recipient must also, in the opinion of staff, be likely to continue a career in agriculture after the completion of school. This award is intended for students who exemplify the qualities valued in the agricultural industry: persistence, work ethic, and dedication.
eligibility_criteria: Must be enrolled at Morawa College of Agriculture; Must demonstrate willingness to persist against adversity; Must show 100% effort in completion of work; Must be likely to continue career in agriculture after school completion; Selection based on staff assessment of demonstrated resilience and perseverance
special_conditions: Award is based on demonstrated resilience and willingness to persevere through adversity. Staff assessment used to identify recipient. May include non-monetary recognition or prize. Recipient must show likelihood of agricultural career continuation.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Cunderdin
overview: WACoA Cunderdin offers GPG Naturgy Scholarships to students transitioning into Year 12. These scholarships recognize reasonable achievers in agricultural studies and are funded by GPG Naturgy community partnership.
description: The GPG Naturgy Scholarships provide financial assistance for Year 11 students entering Year 12 at WACoA Cunderdin. The program offers multiple scholarship levels: 2 scholarships valued at $1,000 each, 3 scholarships valued at $2,000 each, and 4 scholarships valued at $3,000 each, for a total of 9 scholarships available annually. Recipients are selected based on being reasonable achievers in their academic and practical work. Scholarship funds are applied directly to each recipient's college fees account and deducted from their Year 12 College fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students transitioning from Year 11 to Year 12; Must be classified as a reasonable achiever; Current enrolment at WACoA Cunderdin or accepted for entry into Year 12
special_conditions: Scholarship funds are applied to each student's account and deducted from the student's Year 12 College fees.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Cunderdin
overview: WACoA Cunderdin presents the ADF Future Innovators Award to Year 12 graduating students. This award is donated by the Australian Defence Forces and recognizes outstanding achievement and leadership potential.
description: The ADF Future Innovators Award is presented annually at the college's valedictory ceremony to graduating Year 12 students who demonstrate exceptional potential for innovation and leadership. The award is donated by the Australian Defence Forces and recognizes students who show promise for future careers in military or defence-related fields. While the exact monetary value is not specified, the award carries prestige as a premier valedictory award. Recipients are selected based on demonstrated leadership qualities, innovative thinking, and overall achievement throughout their Year 12 year.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be graduating from WACoA Cunderdin; Selected based on leadership potential and innovation demonstrated during Year 12
special_conditions: Awarded at the end of Year 12 as part of the valedictory ceremony. Recipients are selected by school leadership.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Cunderdin
overview: WACoA Cunderdin presents the Best All Rounder Award to graduating Year 12 students. Donated by the Lions Club Cunderdin in memory of Robert Jenzen, this award recognizes students who excel across academic, practical, and co-curricular activities.
description: The Best All Rounder Award is presented annually to a Year 12 student who demonstrates excellence across multiple areas of college life including academics, practical agricultural and trades training, and co-curricular involvement. The award is generously donated by the Lions Club Cunderdin in memory of Robert Jenzen, reflecting the community's commitment to recognizing well-rounded student achievement. While the exact monetary value is not specified in available records, the award is considered a prestigious recognition. Selection is based on demonstrated ability and contribution across all aspects of student experience at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be graduating from WACoA Cunderdin; Must demonstrate excellence in academic, practical, and co-curricular pursuits
special_conditions: Awarded at the end of Year 12 as part of the valedictory ceremony. Donated annually by the Lions Club Cunderdin in memory of Robert Jenzen.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Cunderdin
overview: WACoA Cunderdin awards the Dux Award to the highest-achieving Year 12 student. This premier award recognizes the overall academic and practical excellence of the top graduating student.
description: The Dux Award is the highest honour bestowed upon a graduating Year 12 student at WACoA Cunderdin, recognizing the student who has achieved the highest overall standard across all areas of their studies and college experience. The award is donated by the college itself, reflecting the institution's commitment to recognizing academic excellence and achievement. While the specific monetary value is not detailed, the Dux Award carries significant prestige within the college community. Recipients are typically recognized at the valedictory ceremony and their names are recorded in college records as part of the institution's honour roll.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be graduating from WACoA Cunderdin; Selected based on highest overall academic and practical achievement across all subjects and courses
special_conditions: Awarded at the end of Year 12 as the premier honour at the valedictory ceremony. Reflects overall excellence in academic studies, practical training, and college contribution.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Cunderdin
overview: WACoA Cunderdin awards the VET Dux Award to the highest-achieving Year 12 student in Vocational Education and Training programs. This award recognizes excellence in trades and technical qualifications.
description: The VET Dux Award is presented annually to the top-performing Year 12 student in the Vocational Education and Training (VET) pathway at WACoA Cunderdin. This award specifically recognizes students who have demonstrated exceptional achievement in nationally accredited Certificate II and III qualifications in agricultural and trades fields. The award is donated by the college and recognizes achievement across various VET disciplines including automotive, engineering, building, construction, furniture making, and primary production. Recipients are honoured at the valedictory ceremony, reflecting the college's commitment to recognizing practical and technical excellence alongside traditional academic achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be enrolled in VET programs at WACoA Cunderdin; Selected based on highest achievement in Certificate II or III qualifications; Must be graduating from the college
special_conditions: Awarded at the end of Year 12 to recognize excellence in VET pathways. Recipients are selected by college leadership and honoured at the valedictory ceremony.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Cunderdin
overview: WACoA Cunderdin awards the Runner-Up to Dux Award to the second-highest-achieving Year 12 student. Donated jointly by the college and the Agricultural Advisory Committee, this award recognizes exceptional overall achievement.
description: The Runner-Up to Dux Award is presented annually to the Year 12 student who demonstrates the second-highest level of overall achievement at WACoA Cunderdin. The award is jointly donated by the WA College of Agriculture - Cunderdin and the Agricultural Advisory Committee, reflecting both institutional and community recognition of academic and practical excellence. While not specified in available information, the award carries significant prestige as a top honour. Recipients are recognized at the valedictory ceremony as part of the college's celebration of academic achievement and overall student excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Year 12 students; Must be graduating from WACoA Cunderdin; Selected based on second-highest overall academic and practical achievement; Must be currently enrolled full-time at the college
special_conditions: Awarded at the end of Year 12 at the valedictory ceremony. Jointly donated by the college and Agricultural Advisory Committee.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
overview: The Shire of Denmark Agricultural Scholarship is available to Year 9 or 10 students planning to attend the WA College of Agriculture - Denmark. The scholarship supports students from the Shire of Denmark who wish to pursue agricultural education.
description: The Shire of Denmark Agricultural Scholarship provides financial assistance valued at $1,500 to successful recipients. The scholarship is available to Year 9 or 10 students who reside in the Shire of Denmark and plan to attend the WA College of Agriculture - Denmark. Selection is based on application form criteria determined by the college. The scholarship is awarded once per year, subject to suitable applicants being identified. Recipients must maintain the eligibility criteria throughout their enrolment.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 or 10 student; Must reside in the Shire of Denmark; Must plan to attend WA College of Agriculture - Denmark
special_conditions: A scholarship will not be awarded if there are no suitable applicants. Selection determined by WA College of Agriculture - Denmark based on application criteria.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
program_name: Denmark Senior High School Area Scholarship
overview: This scholarship is available to Year 9 or 10 students who reside in the catchment area of Denmark Senior High School. The scholarship supports students transitioning to agricultural education at the WA College of Agriculture - Denmark.
description: The Denmark Senior High School Area Scholarship supports Year 9 or 10 students from the Denmark Senior High School catchment area. Selection criteria are detailed in the application form available on the college website. The exact financial value is not specified in available documentation. Students must meet residency requirements within the designated school catchment area. Application forms and selection criteria are available through the college.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 or 10 student; Must reside in the catchment area of Denmark Senior High School
special_conditions: Applicants must review the application form and selection criteria provided by the college. Selection is merit-based according to criteria outlined in the application.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
overview: This scholarship is available to Year 10 students with a demonstrated interest in horticulture. The scholarship recognises and supports students pursuing horticultural studies at the college.
description: The Horticulture Interest Scholarship is designed for Year 10 students currently enrolled at the WA College of Agriculture - Denmark who have a demonstrated interest in horticulture. The exact financial value of the scholarship is not specified in available documentation. Selection is based on evidence of genuine interest in horticultural studies and engagement with the college's horticulture enterprise. Recipients must maintain good academic standing and continue their involvement in horticulture programs.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 student; Must be currently enrolled at the college; Must have an interest in Horticulture
special_conditions: Must be a current student of the college with demonstrated interest in horticulture. Selection based on interest and engagement in horticultural studies.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
overview: This scholarship is available to Year 10 or 11 students to support extracurricular agricultural activities. The scholarship recognises students who demonstrate commitment to agricultural industry engagement beyond classroom learning.
description: The Agricultural Industry Extracurricular Activity Scholarship provides $500 worth of funding to Year 10 or 11 students currently enrolled at the college. The funds are intended to support students in undertaking extracurricular activities related to the agriculture industry. Applicants must explain how they would use the $500 funding to pursue agricultural activities outside regular class time. Recipients must demonstrate genuine commitment to agricultural industry engagement and provide a clear plan for using the funds.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 or 11 student; Must be currently enrolled at the college; Must explain intended use of $500 funding for agriculture industry extracurricular activities
special_conditions: Applicants must provide a written explanation of how the $500 funding would be used for extracurricular agricultural activities. Selection based on demonstrated commitment to agriculture industry.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
program_name: Phillip Marshall Scholarship for Excellence in Horticulture
overview: The Phillip Marshall Scholarship for Excellence in Horticulture is awarded to Year 11 students who demonstrate exceptional performance and commitment to horticultural studies. The scholarship recognises students pursuing careers in the horticultural sector.
description: The Phillip Marshall Scholarship for Excellence in Horticulture is awarded to Year 11 students who have demonstrated a strong level of interest and performance in the college's horticulture enterprise. The scholarship is intended to support students looking toward a career in the horticultural sector. Recipients must show exceptional achievement in horticulture studies and a clear commitment to pursuing a horticultural career. The exact financial value is not specified in available documentation.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 student; Must have demonstrated strong level of interest and performance in the college's horticulture enterprise; Must be looking toward a career in the horticultural sector
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated strong interest, academic performance in horticulture, and career goals in the horticultural sector.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
overview: This scholarship is available to Year 11 students intending to enrol in the Year 12 trades program. The scholarship supports students pursuing vocational and trades education pathways.
description: The Year 12 Trades Program Scholarship is available to Year 11 students who are intending to enrol in the Year 12 trades program at the WA College of Agriculture - Denmark. The scholarship recognises and supports students pursuing vocational and trades education in agricultural and related fields. The exact financial value is not specified in available documentation. Recipients must maintain good academic standing and complete the full Year 12 trades program.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 student; Must be intending to enrol in the Year 12 trades program
special_conditions: Applicants must intend to enrol in the Year 12 trades program. Scholarship conditional on successful enrolment and progress through the program.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
overview: This scholarship is available to Year 11 or 12 students who demonstrate outstanding interest and aptitude in sheep and wool handling, shearing, and shed handling. The scholarship supports students pursuing careers in the sheep and wool industry.
description: The Sheep and Wool Industry Scholarship is awarded to Year 11 or 12 students who show outstanding interest and potential in sheep and wool handling, shearing, and shed handling. Recipients must have demonstrated significant aptitude and ability in these specific areas and be genuinely interested in pursuing a career in the sheep and wool sector. The exact financial value is not specified in available documentation. Selection is based on demonstrated skills, aptitude, and career aspirations in wool and sheep management.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 or 12 student; Must show outstanding interest and potential in sheep and wool handling, shearing, and shed handling; Must have demonstrated aptitude and ability in these areas; Must be interested in a career in sheep and wool
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated aptitude in shearing, shed handling, wool handling, and genuine interest in career in sheep and wool industry.

school_name: Western Australian College Of Agriculture - Denmark
overview: This scholarship is available to Year 11 residential students who demonstrate strong potential for a career in agriculture. The scholarship recognises students showing promise in agricultural studies and leadership.
description: The Agricultural Career Potential Scholarship is awarded to Year 11 residential students who demonstrate the potential of being involved in an accepted career in agriculture. The scholarship is intended for students living on campus who show genuine commitment to pursuing agricultural careers. The exact financial value is not specified in available documentation. Recipients must reside at the college's residential facility and maintain good academic and behavioural standing.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 student; Must be a residential student; Must demonstrate potential of being involved in an accepted career in agriculture
special_conditions: Must be a residential student. Selection based on demonstrated potential in agricultural career pathways.
