overview: Woodbury Boston Primary School offers Special Fee Arrangements for families experiencing particular financial difficulty. This program aims to support eligible families by providing flexible fee payment options and potential fee relief to ensure every child has the opportunity to attend the school.
description: Woodbury Boston Primary School recognises that families may experience financial difficulties and offers limited special fee arrangements on application. Families experiencing financial hardship can contact the school administration to discuss their circumstances. The arrangements available may include flexible payment plans and fee relief options. The specific financial benefit varies depending on individual family circumstances and is determined following application and discussion with school administration. These arrangements are available to families at any year level (Kindergarten through Year 6) and require families to contact the school office to apply and negotiate terms. There are no specific application dates or deadlines—applications can be submitted at any time throughout the year as needed.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all families; Must be experiencing particular financial difficulty; Must apply in writing or by phone to school administration; No income threshold specified on public materials
special_conditions: Applications must be submitted to school administration. Special fee arrangements are available on application only and may include flexible payment plans. Limited special arrangements are available. Families should contact administration directly for more information and an application form.

overview: Woodbury Boston Primary School provides a 25% discount on tuition fees for the second child and full fee waiver for the fourth and subsequent children in families with multiple siblings enrolled simultaneously.
description: Woodbury Boston Primary School recognises families with multiple children and offers automatic discounts on tuition fees as a matter of policy. Families with a second child enrolled concurrently receive a 25% reduction on that child's tuition fees. Families with three or more children enrolled at the same time receive even greater relief: when more than three children are attending simultaneously, tuition fees are completely waived for the fourth and any subsequent children. These discounts are applied automatically to families meeting the criteria and are effective for the duration of enrolment, reducing annual tuition costs from $2,912 per child. No application or special request is required—the school applies these discounts directly to eligible families' fee invoices.
eligibility_criteria: Must have sibling(s) enrolled at the school concurrently; Second child: 25% discount on tuition; Fourth and subsequent children (when more than 3 siblings enrolled): Full tuition fee waiver; Applies only when siblings are enrolled at the same time
special_conditions: Discounts apply only when siblings are enrolled concurrently at the school. Discount is applied to tuition fees only and does not apply to Curriculum Related Activity costs. Families may also pay fees in full by the due date to receive an additional 3% early payment discount in addition to sibling discounts.

overview: Willetton Senior High School offers a Gifted and Talented Secondary Selective Academic Program to students demonstrating exceptional academic ability. The program provides a rigorous and challenging academic pathway with extended and enriched curriculum in Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities with like-minded peers.
description: The Gifted and Talented Program is a selective academic program for Years 7 to 10 where students study an extended and enriched curriculum in Mathematics, English, Science and Humanities alongside like-minded peers. Admission is based on the results of the Academic Selection Entrance Test (ASET) administered by the Department of Education, with testing occurring in Year 6 for Year 7 entry. The selection process is managed centrally by the Department of Education, not by the school. Places are highly competitive, with only 64 places per cohort available at Willetton SHS. Students benefit from learning in a tailored environment that is intellectually challenging and designed to motivate students to extend their knowledge, understanding and application. There are 1-2 places per year available for students in Years 8-10 to enter the program in Years 9-11. No direct financial benefit is provided as it is a government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Completion of Academic Selection Entrance Test (ASET); Testing occurs in Year 6 for Year 7 entry; Places available for Years 8, 9 and 10 entry with limited availability; Students must demonstrate exceptional academic ability; Central selection by Department of Education
number_awarded: 64 per cohort for Year 7; 1-2 per year for Years 8-10 entry
special_conditions: Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10. All selections are made centrally by the Department of Education; the school has no role in selection. Students in G&T can also participate in Computing Science, Languages, Music, Specialist Basketball and The Arts programs.

overview: Willetton Senior High School offers a Specialist Basketball Program for elite student-athletes from Years 7 to 11. The program provides exceptional development opportunities for basketball players who demonstrate outstanding ability and commitment to the sport.
description: The Specialist Basketball Program is an elite development pathway for Years 8 to 11 (Year 7 entry also available) where students combine academic studies with intensive basketball training. The program has produced numerous current state and national based players, with achievements including Centre of Excellence and NBA Academy scholarship holders, Australian Development Squad athletes, US College representatives, and State Basketball League players. Selection is through practical trials held at the Willetton Basketball Stadium. Applicants must complete a coach reference form, school reference form, and provide NAPLAN results or latest school report. Applications for Year 7 close in May, and applications for Year 8-11 close in June. Once students begin the program, there is a commitment to continue for the following years of middle schooling. No direct financial value is provided as it is a government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated elite basketball ability; Completion of practical trials; Coach reference required; School reference required; NAPLAN results or latest school report required; Open to all students regardless of residential location
special_conditions: Practical trials held at Willetton Basketball Stadium; Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue for the following years of middle schooling (Years 8 to 10); Program offers TAFE Certificate II in Sport Coaching and Sport and Recreation for Years 8-11; Both male and female teams have won multiple national championships.

overview: Willetton Senior High School offers a Specialist Art Extension Course for Years 8 to 10 to students demonstrating exceptional visual arts ability. The course provides an enriched curriculum for artistically talented students.
description: Willetton offers an extension course in Art for Years 8 to 10, providing a specialist pathway for students demonstrating artistic talent. Students involved in the specialist art extension course are pre-selected through a testing or screening process conducted in Term 2 of Year 7. Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10. The school offers a specialist fine arts course with a successful senior school art program. The computer science and art programs are offered only to current students already enrolled at Willetton SHS or students living within the local intake area. No direct financial value is provided as it is a government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated artistic ability; Entry testing held in Term 2 of Year 7; Pre-selected through testing or screening process; Current students at Willetton SHS or students living within local intake area
special_conditions: Entry testing held in Term 2 of Year 7; Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10; Offered only to current Willetton SHS students or students within local intake area; Students devote two of four hours allocated to electives to this course.

overview: Willetton Senior High School offers a Specialist Computer Science Course for Years 8 to 10 to students demonstrating exceptional ability in computing. The course was the first of its kind in Western Australia and has been expanded to include a multimedia stream.
description: Willetton offers a specialist course in Computer Science for Years 8 to 10, which was the first of its kind in Western Australia and has recently been expanded to include a multimedia stream. Students involved in the specialist computer science course are pre-selected through a testing or screening process. Entry testing is held in Term 2 of Year 7. Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10. The computer science program is offered only to current students already enrolled at Willetton SHS or students living within the local intake area. Students in the specialist class devote two of four hours allocated to electives to this course. No direct financial value is provided as it is a government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated computer science ability; Entry testing held in Term 2 of Year 7; Pre-selected through testing or screening process; Current students at Willetton SHS or students living within local intake area
special_conditions: Entry testing held in Term 2 of Year 7; Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10; Offered only to current Willetton SHS students or students within local intake area; Students devote two of four hours allocated to electives to this course.

overview: Willetton Senior High School offers entry into the Instrumental Music Schools Program (IMSS) for students demonstrating musical ability. The program provides specialist music education for instrumentalists.
description: Willetton offers participation in the Instrumental Music Schools (IMSS) program for students demonstrating musical ability on instruments. Entry testing occurs in primary school, prior to secondary entry. For students new to Western Australia who wish to enter the Instrumental Music Schools program, parents should contact the teacher in charge of Music regarding opportunities. Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10. Students in the specialist music class devote two of four hours allocated to electives to this course. No direct financial value is provided as it is a government school program.
eligibility_criteria: Demonstrated instrumental music ability; Entry testing in primary school; For new arrivals to WA, contact Music teacher for opportunities; Commitment to continue to end of Year 10
special_conditions: Entry testing occurs in primary school; For new students to WA, contact Music teacher; Once a student begins the program, there is a commitment to continue to the end of Year 10; Students devote two of four hours allocated to electives to this program.

overview: Xavier Catholic School offers a parish scholarship program to support families in the school community. The program continues to be a priority initiative for the school, assisting families who are members of the school's parish community.
description: Xavier Catholic School maintains a continuation of its parish scholarship program, as noted in the 2024 Annual Council Minutes. The exact financial value, number of awards, and detailed eligibility criteria are not publicly documented on the school's website. Interested families should contact the school directly for specific information about the program's current scope, application process, and available funding amounts. The program is designed to support Catholic families attending Xavier and represents an ongoing commitment to accessibility in Catholic education.
eligibility_criteria: Parish membership; registered at Xavier Catholic School; in good academic standing
special_conditions: Program details available directly from the school office. Contact the school for application requirements and annual funding allocation.
